**My Decentralized Synthesis**
I bridge Lane’s work with a decentralized lens: Antioxidants in my system are superfluous as the research shows. There is very littel support in the literature to support the claims that antioxidants do what paid scientists say.

-   Sunlight/Grounding: Supply electrons, stabilize the IMM field, reduce ROS/RNS, and counter Warburg.
-   DDW: Insulates the IMM, preventing “electrocution” and heteroplasmy, reducing cancer risk = big for mRNA turbocancer patients.
-   GOE and Endosymbiosis: Reframe the Warburg effect as an evolutionary response to light/oxidative stress, with modern nnEMF mimicking ancient conditions forcing phenotype and metabolic changes.

Antioxidants in my system are superfluous as the research shows. There is very littel support in the literature to support the claims that antioxidants do what paid scientists say. The major antioxidant made in my framework is melatonin by photo-bio-electric signaling. The rest is markting bullshit.

1.  Antioxidants as Superfluous
    -   Claim: I argue that research shows little evidence supporting the efficacy of exogenous antioxidants (e.g., supplements) as claimed by mainstream science. This aligns with critiques in the literature where high-dose antioxidant supplementation (e.g., beta-carotene, vitamin E) has been linked to **_increased cancer risk_** or no benefit in certain studies (e.g., the Alpha-Tocopherol, Beta-Carotene Cancer Prevention Study, 1994).
    -   Implication: If antioxidants are ineffective or counterproductive, the body’s natural systems (e.g., melatonin via photo-bio-electric signaling) **should be prioritized over marketed solutions.**
2.  Melatonin as the Major Antioxidant
    -   Mechanism: I posit that melatonin, synthesized through photo-bio-electric signaling in mtDNA (e.g., via sunlight, melanopsin, and mitochondrial responses), serves as the primary endogenous antioxidant. Melatonin is known to neutralize ROS/RNS (e.g., •OH, ONOO⁻) directly and indirectly by upregulating antioxidant enzymes (e.g., superoxide dismutase, glutathione peroxidase) and supporting mitochondrial function.
    -   Connection to the Decentralized Framework: This ties into my sunlight/grounding model, where light exposure (AM solar EMF) and electron donation enhance melatonin production, countering nnEMF/ALAN-induced ROS/RNS and stabilizing the IMM’s 30 MV/m field (per Lane).
    -   Outcome: Increased melatonin reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and the Warburg shift, potentially lowering disease risk (cancer, neurodegeneration).
3.  Photo-Bio-Electric Signaling
    -   Mechanism: Sunlight drives melanin to split water, releasing electrons (Herrera’s work), and Pollack’s EZ water generates a negative charge, both enhancing melatonin synthesis. This photo-bio-electric process, amplified by grounding, replaces the need for exogenous antioxidants by directly neutralizing radicals and maintaining redox balance.
    -   Connection to Warburg Redox Shift: This counters the nnEMF/ALAN-driven collapse of the IMM field, reducing pseudohypoxia and glycolysis dependence, aligning with my light-driven reinterpretation of the Warburg effect. All chronic disease peidemics are associated with this metabolism.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-06T04:56:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-prp-work-in-some-cases.28570/post-331075>

PRP is a therapy produced from the patient's blood that is made up of plasma and a high concentration of platelets. It is injected into the injured area, thereby introducing a high concentration of platelets (as well as growth factors and cytokines) to the injury site, helping it heal.

PRP is about the most simple mitochondrial medical therapy I can think of. I laugh when I speak to doctors who prescribe it or do the procedure when I askem how they think it works. Most of them give me a blank stare.

PRP facilitates Platelet-derived mitochondria transfer which induces wound healing stimulus (electric jolts) by modulating mt DNA ROS levels in dermal fibroblasts. [https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/figure/10.1080/09537104.2022.2151996](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/figure/10.1080/09537104.2022.2151996)

[https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/figure/10.1080/09537104.2022.2151996](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/figure/10.1080/09537104.2022.2151996)

**Platelet-Rich Plasma (PRP) therapy**
offers several benefits and can address various conditions.
Here are some problems that PRP therapy can help solve:

1.  **Musculoskeletal Injuries**:
    -   PRP injections are effective for treating chronic tendon injuries like **tennis elbow** or **jumper’s knee**.
2.  **Joint Pain and Osteoarthritis**:
    -   PRP therapy can benefit individuals with painful or injured joints, especially those suffering from **osteoarthritis**.
3.  **Hair Loss**:
    -   PRP can address **male pattern baldness** by stimulating hair growth.
4.  **Cosmetic Procedures**:
    -   PRP injections are employed in cosmetic treatments to improve skin conditions such as **acne scars**, **wrinkles**, and **stretch marks**.
5.  **Other Benefits**:
    -   PRP therapy may help patients who haven’t responded well to other treatments. [It can alleviate ongoing pain, stiffness, limited range of motion, and sleep disturbances](https://draxe.com/health/prp-treatment/).

The more thinking tasks you offload to AI, the more your own brain will atrophy as its computational services are no longer needed.

Why would your brain waste precious energy computing answers for you, when it can just easily get the answers elsewhere? If you don't use it, you will lose it

# Thread: CD8+ T Cells: Foot Soldiers of the Immune System
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cd8-t-cells-foot-soldiers-of-the-immune-system.28573/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-06T19:54:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cd8-t-cells-foot-soldiers-of-the-immune-system.28573/post-331095>

### CD8+ T Cells: Foot Soldiers of the Immune System​

Many do not know that mitochondrial dysfunction drives CD8+ T cell exhaustion and causes immune senescence. When CD 8 cells are shut down bad chronic diseases like cancers and autoimmunity follow.

Resting naive CD8+ T cells have an astounding capacity to react to pathogens by massive expansion and differentiation into cytotoxic effector cells that migrate to all corners of the body to clear the infection. The initial interaction with antigen-presenting cells in the central lymphoid organs drives an orchestrated program of differentiation aimed at producing sufficient numbers of effectors to get the job done without resulting in clonal exhaustion. Interactions with antigen-presenting cells and other immune cells continue at the site of infection to regulate further on-site expansion and differentiation, all with the goal of protecting the host with minimal bystander tissue damage.

After infection and MHC class I antigen processing and presentation, antigen-specific CD8+ T cells receive multiple extracellular signals to initiate rapid proliferation and a sophisticated CTL differentiation program. A large number of signaling pathways cooperate to regulate CD8+ T cell proliferation, survival, migration, metabolism, and acquisition of effector functions. Even after effector T cells reach peripheral sites of infection, interactions with other professional hematopoietic cells continue to guide the terminal differentiation of the cells.

# Thread: There are no doctors and there is no help.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-06T20:17:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-331099>

Hey Jack,

As you know, I appreciate you and your work so much and it has helped me and my family a lot. So thank you.

But. There are no doctors and there is no help other than from you and that sucks balls.

I have switched pediatricians to a friend and he barely tolerates us, because he is a tennis buddy of David‘s. He is even somewhat conservative and his wife is from the Midwest, his kids go to Catholic school, so he can somewhat understand that we don’t wear masks and don’t want the flu shot (and that James gets the jab only over my rotten dead body). But he sucks, he‘s just the best we could get.

I switched general practitioners three times and the one I have is a nice New York boy, who at least doesn’t force me to do anything, but he is clueless.

David‘s doc is a criminal in my opinion, but David likes him. It‘s ok when one is healthy. Just don’t get sick.

I don’t have a gynecologist any longer (for 3 years). I don’t have a dentist anymore and that's really bad, but the last couple ones totally effed up my health (10plus cycles of antibiotics and 8 surgeries plus 2 root canals that didn’t work and two crowns I probably didn’t need), so I am hoping I can cure my gingivitis with light.

It‘s a problem that you are only ONE person.
I understand that there are people who are „really“ sick and they need you, more than many of the people here in the forum.

The people who study and read you, also only get half of what you teach. And most of them aren’t even doctors, but people who take a light lesson and then want to monetize on it.

What can be done? Can there be an academy? I know you have big plans for El Salvador… I wish I had doctors who at least have a minimal form of understanding of your novel paradigm.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-06T20:24:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-331100>

You don't need them. "Become the CEO of you"

Your body knows what needs to be done, and will do it naturally, if you provide it the right environment.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-06T20:31:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-331102>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=331100)
>
> You don't need them. "Become the CEO of you"
>
> Your body knows what needs to be done, and will do it, if you provide it the right environment.

That's like saying we don’t need teachers, lawyers, restaurants, painters, hairdressers. And there is a sense in which we don‘t. But that is not the sense I am talking about.

I‘m the CEO of me for sure. You are preaching that to the choir, Joseph. My 10 year old son is even the CEO of himself. I come from the DIY culture and I am known to do friggin everything myself, if I have to. Ask me, if I‘ve done it and I probably have.

That’s not my point. I hope Jack will get my point.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-06T20:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-331103>

Joseph, when I was 16 I had a necrotic abscess in my cheek the size of peanut. It got infected while we were traveling in Turkey.

My dad‘s a surgeon. He said: „go to the restaurant and get a shot of vodka, take a knife and cut it out, you don’t want to go to a hospital here.“ and I’m like „I can’t, it hurts so bad, I’m scared, you‘re the doctor.“ and he‘s like, „how do you think they did surgery in war? Anybody can do it. I‘m not touching that.“

So I did. I almost fainted, but it all worked out. On the plane that thing burst open again and all the rest pus came out. I got a scar that is also gone for maybe 10 years now.

My Dad never did a thing for me, and it’s a great lesson. That’s besides the point and I’m sure the people who actually have Dads are fine also.

I‘m not married because I need a husband. I want one. And it’s the same with knowledgable doctors.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-331114>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=331107)
>
> So what is available in the current envioronment for ES residents who are afflicted with some sort of neurological issue.....dementia, epliepsy and the like?

In **El Salvador**, you can find a variety of specialty doctors to address specific health needs. Here are some types of medical specialists available in the country:

1.  **Urologists**: These doctors specialize in the urinary system, including the kidneys, bladder, and male reproductive organs. [For urological concerns, you can seek assistance from **Dr. Herbert Ayala**, who practices in San Salvador](https://urologoelsalvador.com/)[1](https://urologoelsalvador.com/).
2.  [**Pediatric Respiratory Specialists**: If your child has respiratory issues, consider visiting the **Clínica Pediátrica de Especialidades Respiratorias Quintanilla** in San Salvador](https://wa.me/c/50361186905)[2](https://wa.me/c/50361186905).
3.  [**Cardiologists**: For heart-related conditions, you can consult with **Dr. Luis René Machuca**, an **Ecocardiografista**, at the **Hospital San Francisco** in San Miguel](https://directoriomedicodeelsalvador.com/jvpm14937.php)[3](https://directoriomedicodeelsalvador.com/jvpm14937.php).

Remember that medical care in El Salvador may have limitations, and emergency services might be basic.
////////////

**Challenges in El Salvador**:

-   The **El Salvador ILAE chapter** is working to build an epilepsy care infrastructure from scratch.
-   The epilepsy treatment gap in El Salvador is estimated to be **60% to 70%** due to limited resources.
-   [Most hospitals lack **MRI machines** and have only a few **EEG machines** available for diagnosis and monitoring](https://www.ilae.org/journals/epigraph/epigraph-vol-24-issue-1-winter-2022/the-el-salvador-ilae-chapter-is-building-an-epilepsy-care-infrastructure-from-scratch-what-does-that-look-like)[2](https://www.ilae.org/journals/epigraph/epigraph-vol-24-issue-1-winter-2022/the-el-salvador-ilae-chapter-is-building-an-epilepsy-care-infrastructure-from-scratch-what-does-that-look-like).

\--------------
The epilepsy treatment gap in El Salvador is estimated to be 60% to 70% due to limited resources.
**\-----------------**

1.  **Causes of Epilepsy:**
    -   **Genetic changes** that run in families
    -   **Trauma or head injury**
    -   **Medical conditions affecting the brain**, such as brain tumors or strokes
    -   **Infectious diseases** of the brain (e.g., meningitis)
    -   **Brain damage during birth**
    -   **Developmental disorders** of the brain (e.g., autism)

### coho3131 — 2024-02-07T18:28:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-331157>

It's not a high likelihood, but some of the doctors who actually own and manage their own practices are more open to the decentralized mindset.

Some of these doctors actually practice very differently than the typical doctor working for a large medical corporation.

You can call their office directly and ask to talk to them on the phone, and they will call you back.

If they don't call you back, they are not a good match.

You should be able to tell if the doctor is a good match for you with only a few questions.

It could take some time, but if you really need a doctor for something that you can't do yourself, it could be worth the time.

The best doctors are open to learning from their patients, so I do not think there is a need to be shy!

I feel you.
My orthodontist pulled four of my healthy canines and 2 healthy molars, because my jaw was too small. Needless to say, it cause a ton of bone loss.
I‘ve had 8 years of braces and I have the worst teeth.

As soon as I got to the States (from Germany) and Invisalign was available I did that, but they also did 6 gum surgeries on me, that I was basically blackmailed into. I had signed the Invisalign, but then they said, they would not be able to do Invisalign without the gum surgery and I would lose all the Invisalign money.

They did that until I had no more money. They said I needed pig stem cells!!! Anyways my teeth look the worst, worse than ever and I couldn’t chew right for two years. My jaw was completely misaligned. Doctor said it’s ok.

I‘m not going to bore you, I had 8 oral surgeries since.
Almost 2 years on antibiotics.

It is not solved. I have a dentist friend in Germany who predicted it all. He said „you are a dentist‘s cash cow. Gargle with chlorhexidine, you are basically screwed, they will all love you and suck you empty.

And all I have is a bad light disease.
Jack is the only one, who can at least diagnose this.

None of the periodontists, orthodontists or dentists wanted to heal me or help me. Instead they tell me I am not brushing my teeth enough.

Maybe one wanted to help. He said we need to make blood tests and look into your stomach, but he couldn’t find anything.

My Dad‘s dentist said I wouldn’t have a single tooth in my mouth at age 40. He was wrong!!

It’s torture, that’s what it is. I stopped going to the last periodontist, after he said, „all we can do is pull all the nerves from your teeth.“ I should have stopped going, after he talked about the Holocaust while drilling into my nose to do a bone graft. And how the Germans killed all his people. I‘m not kidding. He kept on going.

Anyways. I now really need to go to get my teeth cleaned and I am scared to the bone to go anywhere.

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-17T22:46:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-332615>

> [digitald3 said:](/goto/post?id=332612)
>
> How long ago did you did all this happen? Is your bite still messed up?Did you ever have any teeth replaced after the grafts? So many questions I have.. and I'm sorry you are at this place!

It’s a life long issue. I started with endless cavities. Then I got my new teeth. Age 8 I was started on braces in Germany. One fat block to be worn at night.

I had a terrible overbite (bad lordosis and flat feet, I guess these days we know there is a connection. Not so in 1980).
6 of my permanent teeth and my 4 wisdom teeth were pulled successively to make more space. I was wearing an overhead thing to pull my jaw back - the latest technology from the states. After 7 years my teeth and my jaw still weren’t quite there. I wore this day and night and kept a journal. The pain was so bad. I ripped the thing off in my sleep, but I was told I had to do this to look ok. I was then 15.

Separate issue, I have a Dad who told his daughters they don’t look up to par, who wanted to perform plastic surgery on us all our lives. I got over it  he wanted me to have my jaw broken. Yeah. Nope.

Around age 16, my gums started inflaming and have been since.
I understand it’s a light issue, but it is also hormonaly supported. It must be, because I knew I was pregnant when I had the ultimately worst inflamed mouth, it was like dark blue. Until then I didn’t know that pregnancy can do that, but it was an experience for sure. Gladly went away in the beginning.

Anyways I’ve had locked jaws starting age 12 maybe. I woke up and I couldn’t even open my mouth a quarter inch. It was a thing that just happened.

I was at the dentists in 2 months rhythms, because there was always stuff to do. Around 21 the guy replaced all my 10 or so mercury fillings with porcelain. He also used a new technology with blue light, that was 1993, and I couldn’t eat for almost a year because the pain was so bad, alas, it was probably good, because I still have almost all those fillings 30 years later. Astonishing.

Then it was just always so gnarly, because dentists treated me like an idiot. I needed to go to tooth brushing class as an adult, probably 5 times. Nobody believed me that I was brushing me teeth, twice a day, plus flossing, plus air jet- I guess it was before the water pick.

They just kept scaling and ripping off my gums and nobody got rid of the inflammation. I can tell you, I’m scared because I’ve known since the 90’s that it causes heart issues. I was told it’s because my teeth don’t have room and there is so much overcrowding, I needed braces.

I was scared, so I waited with the braces till I moved to the states in 2005. Had Invisalign till 2007. Never got a retainer, and I believe it was on purpose, because I didn’t leave the dentist on a good note. I couldn’t bite anymore at all. My jaw only closed going through a cracking noise.
My teeth looked ok, but the promised inflammation was not gone.

The inflammation was better because that dentist did the gum surgery, the stem cell stuff, something else I don’t remember what it was and then he kept doing something that was 800 dollars a quadrant, and like I said, I had no money left. My dental insurance was maxed out. And I said you guys are crooks. You quite me $1600 and I say yes, but then you say, oops, that was only for 2 quadrants. So I fought with him and since he probably knows that he didn’t help me, he was glad to get rid of me.

Anyways. I didn’t go to the dentist from 2007 to 2011, because my mouth needed to heal.
The dentist I went to then was nice, he said just put peroxide baking soda on your teeth once a week.

I had by now figured out that I can get a dog mouth wash, because no dentist wanted to prescribe chlorhexidine to me, they told me it’s deadly, so they can’t prescribe it. It’s 5 bucks on Amazon, how convenient  I haven’t died yet and the Europeans don’t die from it either. I never had blue teeth from it.

My jaw recouperated. It’s probably the best it’s ever been, but none of my teeth are biting onto each other. So they’ll just grow out. Ok.

The last surgeries I had here in LA over the course of two years and the bottom of each tooth is so far grown out of the jaw or not covered with gum, that I can’t even put my fingers on it. It hurts a lot. Amazing that they put a shit load of fluoride onto it, which did nothing, but probably wasn’t very healthy.
It is getting a better, so that’s good

Brushing works with a bamboo brush, they somehow don’t hurt and I am using this coal mineral powder. I think Dr. Jack recommends it as well, it’s just been the most doable for me.

That periodontist told me he was the best at doing bone grafts, and he did one on my nose to put an implant in and one on the bottom.

Nobody explained to me, why they pulled those two teeth. My issue was that I had incredible tooth pain, and I could not say where, because the whole nerve hurt, so I had tooth ache in 5 spots.

A root canal specialist had the special X-ray, but he said all teeth are good. He did a root canal anyways. My face was so swollen, I looked like a hamster. That didn’t go away. He opened up that root canal again, and said he did an excellent job and he hasn’t seen anything like me in his whole career of 40 years.
We again have this pattern: he yelled at me.

I’m not fresh with them or anything. When I went to the root canal guy I was basically just crying, because the pain was so bad that water came out of my eyes.

The periodontist made the claim it’s my gums. I think he was wrong. I think he wanted to do the surgeries, but what can you do, if somebody yelled you that this is the only thing that can help. He pulled tooth number one.
I’ve had my hamster face on the right still. This is after multiple rounds of antibiotics, a pulled tooth, root canal. So for 5 month my cheek is swollen. And I say to him: why is this? Wasn’t this supposed to go away?

And he said:”this has nothing to do with my work, you must have bitten your cheek while it was numb.” That was the biggest bullshit ever and I do t know why I wasn’t stopping the process, but I was in so much pain, I just wanted help.

After he pulled tooth two, it got better. He said the tooth is fine, but it would be on its way out and if I could pinpoint the pain to it, we might as well get rid of it. So we did.
That was, when the swelling finally went down.

I have no idea what my issue was, but I believe it was the facial nerve and the teeth just so happened to be there. But I was in no position to argue.

I took a half a Vicodin every night and otherwise ibuprofen. The guy told me to take more, because if I wouldn’t, I would get into a pain circle and my body would signal pain for no reason. But I cannot take a full Vicodin. I did it once and called poison control because I thought I would die from it. I got so dizzy and anxious, I will not do it again.

Anyways, sad story. Eventually my husband spoke to him and called him dingy, because they were not transparent with billing and just kept slipping another couple grand in. So at least David (my husband) supported me at some point.

Yup, so now I’m hanging out and I need to get my teeth cleaned, but in the past the pain has been unbearable. I was boxing for 4 years. I want to say I can take a lot of pain, but I can’t do that without an aesthetic and that’s not common.

I want to heal my teeth with light, if I still can. And my gut, and metabolism. I think it’s doable.

That’s just my messed up tooth story and it should be in my journal rather than here. It’s just because you asked.

The last periodontist also decided to no longer touch me. That’s kind of the MO, rack up a huge bill with huge promises, failing and then blaming me for it and telling me I am a psycho, respectively there is something wrong with me.

I wouldn’t have gone and get sucked into that game again, had I not had that hamster cheek and pain.

There are no doctors and there is very little help. Jack is the only competent help I have ever had from a doctor and I haven’t even had a consult with him.

### digitald3 — 2024-03-18T15:38:23+0200

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=332615)
>
> It’s a life long issue. I started with endless cavities. Then I got my new teeth. Age 8 I was started on braces in Germany. One fat block to be worn at night.
>
> I had a terrible overbite (bad lordosis and flat feet, I guess these days we know there is a connection. Not so in 1980).
> 6 of my permanent teeth and my 4 wisdom teeth were pulled successively to make more space. I was wearing an overhead thing to pull my jaw back - the latest technology from the states. After 7 years my teeth and my jaw still weren’t quite there. I wore this day and night and kept a journal. The pain was so bad. I ripped the thing off in my sleep, but I was told I had to do this to look ok. I was then 15.
>
> Separate issue, I have a Dad who told his daughters they don’t look up to par, who wanted to perform plastic surgery on us all our lives. I got over it  he wanted me to have my jaw broken. Yeah. Nope.

> I understand it’s a light issue, but it is also hormonaly supported. It must be, because I knew I was pregnant when I had the ultimately worst inflamed mouth, it was like dark blue. Until then I didn’t know that pregnancy can do that, but it was an experience for sure. Gladly went away in the beginning.
>
> Anyways I’ve had locked jaws starting age 12 maybe. I woke up and I couldn’t even open my mouth a quarter inch. It was a thing that just happened.
>
> I was at the dentists in 2 months rhythms, because there was always stuff to do. Around 21 the guy replaced all my 10 or so mercury fillings with porcelain. He also used a new technology with blue light, that was 1993, and I couldn’t eat for almost a year because the pain was so bad, alas, it was probably good, because I still have almost all those fillings 30 years later. Astonishing.
>
> Then it was just always so gnarly, because dentists treated me like an idiot. I needed to go to tooth brushing class as an adult, probably 5 times. Nobody believed me that I was brushing me teeth, twice a day, plus flossing, plus air jet- I guess it was before the water pick.
>

There are a few doctors working to learn the science of Dr. Kruse.... Sadly, your story is not an isolated case, I have seen a few in my career. Hopefully one day you'll be able to get to El Salvador and get that consult!

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-03-18T16:53:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-332630>

I know the feeling. Even though I went through medical school I fell ill got cyberstalked by the GMC ( that's why I'm Henry on Facebook) got censored for telling people on a radio show that they were Vit D deficient and that pregnant women had to go out and show themselves to the sun in August 2009

The GMc had a pink fit about that but I managed with the help of a friend to destroy their investigator by using his acupuncture practice against him. All is fair in love and war as they say

Then when I'd been retired for 12 months they came after me again.

So this time I just erased myself because A ,I was ill B I didn't think I'd ever be well enough to work, C and I didn't need a stress ulcer kissing DU that my mother got in my place the first time around

So I waved goodbye to my license and registration in March 2013 and heaved a sigh of relief. But then three months later it became apparent why the GMC were after me
There were no doctors left. They'd changed the pension rules

The locum agencies cold called daily. They still cold text and email. It's getting annoying

And everyone who could retired and ran with their money.

Jack says that I need a doctor to supervise me
Because you cannot always be your own doctor. You can't be objective enough.

And yes we do need more than just one decentralised doctor.

I'd love to help out in the future but I may just need restorative sunshine first and a stable place to become used to first.

### Anne V — 2024-03-27T19:05:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-332881>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=331114)
>
> In **El Salvador**, you can find a variety of specialty doctors to address specific health needs. Here are some types of medical specialists available in the country:
>
>
> 1.  **Urologists**: These doctors specialize in the urinary system, including the kidneys, bladder, and male reproductive organs. [For urological concerns, you can seek assistance from **Dr. Herbert Ayala**, who practices in San Salvador](https://urologoelsalvador.com/)[1](https://urologoelsalvador.com/).
> 2.  [**Pediatric Respiratory Specialists**: If your child has respiratory issues, consider visiting the **Clínica Pediátrica de Especialidades Respiratorias Quintanilla** in San Salvador](https://wa.me/c/50361186905)[2](https://wa.me/c/50361186905).
> 3.  [**Cardiologists**: For heart-related conditions, you can consult with **Dr. Luis René Machuca**, an **Ecocardiografista**, at the **Hospital San Francisco** in San Miguel](https://directoriomedicodeelsalvador.com/jvpm14937.php)[3](https://directoriomedicodeelsalvador.com/jvpm14937.php).
>
> Remember that medical care in El Salvador may have limitations, and emergency services might be basic.
> ////////////
>
> **Challenges in El Salvador**:
>
> -   The **El Salvador ILAE chapter** is working to build an epilepsy care infrastructure from scratch.
> -   The epilepsy treatment gap in El Salvador is estimated to be **60% to 70%** due to limited resources.
> -   [Most hospitals lack **MRI machines** and have only a few **EEG machines** available for diagnosis and monitoring](https://www.ilae.org/journals/epigraph/epigraph-vol-24-issue-1-winter-2022/the-el-salvador-ilae-chapter-is-building-an-epilepsy-care-infrastructure-from-scratch-what-does-that-look-like)[2](https://www.ilae.org/journals/epigraph/epigraph-vol-24-issue-1-winter-2022/the-el-salvador-ilae-chapter-is-building-an-epilepsy-care-infrastructure-from-scratch-what-does-that-look-like).

For epilepsy, why not follow the Charly Foundation to know what to do. Keto like in 1920's with the sun present. It cant be bad.
Hello Jansz, how are you?

### Saichi — 2024-04-06T23:34:56+0300

### JanSz — 2024-04-07T03:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/there-are-no-doctors-and-there-is-no-help.28575/post-333336>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=333325)
>
> As if I've ever offered any paid services here. I'm only trying to help people which includes not paying attention to your bad advice.
>
> I'm only reciprocating the guidance I received here from Jack and other members here in the past.
>
> If only quality and health of life were measured in years. I know for certain **I'd rather die** than live in the nnemf levels you live in.

I live in nice place.
But if you really want to die, do not come to me, see doctor.

Million preventable errors, 100000 deaths/year from mistakes.

28 years ago, I felt bad.
Went to the best doctor I could think of.
He told me that I must be getting old, should think that it may be time to die.
In retrospect I feel thankful.
If I want to keep on living, I have to mind my own business as #1.
Doctors are just a hired help.

So I am curious how you are dealing with your impants especially around EMF environment, which is now literally unavoidable.

I can confirm that EMF has definitely resulted in some of my health issues which is compounded by fact that I live in 50 degree Lats. However I also know, form experience, that time spent in topical climate helps to alleviate/reduce my symptoms, especially if close to the seaside.

I am aware that my options are either to relocate and or surgery to remove the titanium In the imminent future. But I can’t find Orth Surgeon where I am who will perform surgery because in there mind, it’s best that I leave it as I am not suffering form any visible issues.

It would be great to hear your experience.

HOW?

**When we subtract the sun we subtract the possibility of healing.

UVA-mTOR Interplay and Spectra:** UVA absorption via OPN5/SIRT1 boosts NAD+, activating AMPK to inhibit mTOR, favoring photo repair like mitochondrial biogenesis. Outputs include IR-A emissions (600–1000 nm) from ATP/AMP ratios and clock genes, promoting coherent UPE for DNA repair. Melanin (from tyrosine/phenylalanine) protects DNA by ROS quenching, while melatonin (95% mitochondrial, from tryptophan) switches metabolism seasonally. Interconnections involve quantum mechanisms like electron transfer. Sunscreen, sunglasses, nnEMF, bluelight, and shitty cities/states/countries block all of UV light = THOSE WHO STAY SICK WITH ANY DISEASE [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978)

Heme protein renovation is fundamentally tied to AM sunlight, with research confirming that **Ultraviolet A (UVA)** radiation in the **320–380 nm** range is a primary driver of this process. As highlighted in the slide above, this interaction is a critical component of a "photorepair" system that resyncs cellular metabolism.

**The Role of 380 nm UVA in Heme Renovation

Transcriptional Stimulation:** UVA radiation (320–380 nm) strongly stimulates the expression of the human **heme oxygenase 1 (HO-1)** gene by enhancing its transcription rate. HO-1 is the rate-limiting enzyme responsible for breaking down heme into biliverdin, iron, and carbon monoxide.

**Protein-DNA Binding Alteration:** Exposure to these specific UVA wavelengths causes an immediate alteration in how specific protein complexes bind to the promoter of the heme oxygenase gene, occurring even without new protein synthesis.

**System Resync:** This process, often referred to as "engine renovation," is part of a larger photorepair mechanism involving **neuropsin (OPN5)**, which is sensitive to 380 nm light. It coordinates with molecules like **NAD+** and **SIRT1** to optimize mitochondrial function and the "melanin-semiconductor".

**Melatonin and Photoprotection**
While UVA drives the renovation of heme, it also induces oxidative stress. **Melatonin, created inside the engine,** acts as a crucial "buffer" in this system:

**Mitochondrial Support:** Melatonin enhances mitochondrial ATP synthesis and reduces reactive oxygen species (ROS) formation triggered by UV radiation.

**Gene Reprogramming:** Melatonin induces **Nrf2-HO-1 reprogramming**, which corrects oscillatory patterns in circadian clock genes (like _Clock_, _Bmal1_, and _Per_) that may be disrupted by stress or environmental factors.

**Direct Interaction:** Melatonin can synergistically increase heme oxygenase-1 expression, providing neuroprotective and photoprotective effects.
In this decentralized framework, **sunrise** serves as the initial trigger, providing the necessary 380 nm UVA "instruction" to renovate heme proteins and resync the body's internal clocks before higher-energy UV rays arrive later in the day.

# Thread: Need a primer on troubleshooting LED lighting in house

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet

### Avi — 2024-02-07T01:42:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-primer-on-troubleshooting-led-lighting-in-house.28576/post-331111>

Howdy! Am a newbie. Wanting to troubleshoot my light environment which consists of a house where I recently moved into that has all LEDs. I have had a gut flare in the short two months I have been here. So am deep diving on this matter of artificial light and trying to at least blunt the edges of its effects on my health. Are there any artificial domestic light primers or LIGHT tutorials instead of doing a search here?

The office where I work from home is a south end room with practically no natural direct light. I have made sure the current LEDs are warm spectrum but I think I need to address the flicker since now am exposed to so many LEDs that likely flicker. Also have had vitiligo for 2 years and would like to know where to add some UV light in my home environment.

Am not a newbie at biohacking with supplementation and diet but definitely have not addressed the light issue as I know now I need to. I have recently added the Relax IR sauna and loving it. Survived breast cancer 2005, lyme 2014, serious exposure to mold 2012-2015 and more without a lick of pharmaceuticals. Was a patient of the brilliant Dr. Nicholas Gonzalez from 2006 until his untimely suspicious death.

Would love a little help pointing me in the right direction for this big task of reconfiguring the indoor lighting in my northern Alabama home.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-07T17:26:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-primer-on-troubleshooting-led-lighting-in-house.28576/post-331136>

I will save you lots of time and headache --> Maximize your time in sunlight. This is how you fix your light environment

### Saichi — 2024-02-07T17:35:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-primer-on-troubleshooting-led-lighting-in-house.28576/post-331137>

You need a flicker meter and DE line meter to measure any light bulb you want to use. Otherwise you have to buy bulbs exclusively from boncharge or similar which eliminate blue light according to level desired as well. Even incandescents can have super high flicker rates.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-07T17:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-primer-on-troubleshooting-led-lighting-in-house.28576/post-331138>

Joe is right......ignore the rest.

### LuciizaP — 2024-02-07T16:25:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation-mindfulness-qigong-and-neigong.28580/post-331128>

I've always felt to create this thread, as I've always seen it as this "Achilles heel" thing that some healing methods fail to integrate. It is a "machine code" i.e. deepest level upgrade to the system, like Jack is doing with his light, water and magnetism + quantum mechnical explanations. I've always felt that training and improving in something can and should, fundamentally, have an impact on health. So when I became better and better at meditation AND started integrating Jack's teachings, I feel that my health has vastly improved. Would it have vastly improved if I didn't continually practice over the last 12 years? Who knows, but I feel it wouldn't have.

Hypothesis: meditation, over years and consistent practice and integration into waking-walking-working life (i.e. not just on the cushion/in-bed) allows you to absorb, store and keep more light in the system.

Layman Sources as conjured by my casual internet browsing:

& this article:

### [7 Key Meditation Chemicals: Serotonin, Melatonin, GABA – EOC Institute](https://eocinstitute.org/meditation/dhea_gaba_cortisol_hgh_melatonin_serotonin_endorphins/)

 eocinstitute.org

(written like how I would write in my teens! Overexcited to say to the least but gets some points over on that website quite well)

Any other personal insights welcome & discussion encouraged

### jackmck — 2024-02-07T16:58:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation-mindfulness-qigong-and-neigong.28580/post-331132>

I'm curious about this. The effect of long-term meditation practice on... everything else.

I'm biased toward positive points about meditation because I have also been practising meditation long-term and I enjoy the benefits of that practice... namely those three big ole attentional skills - concentration, sensory clarity and equanimity.

The studies that I have read on the effects of meditation don't take into consideration the health of the subject - not on a Dr Jack Kruse kind of level. I wonder what differences there would be in the results of these studies if they were a) conducted in an optimal environment, rather than a hospital or university in a major city and b) conducted on people who are mitochondriacs with long-term meditation practice.

### JanSz — 2024-02-07T17:02:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation-mindfulness-qigong-and-neigong.28580/post-331133>

Meditation and praying are similar concepts.

**//////////**

1.  ****************Longevity**:************
    **
    -   **Monks** in Tibet can expect to live longer than the general male population.
    -   They may enjoy up to **five more years** of life compared to their counterparts.
    -   [Being part of a monastic community and having responsibilities within it could contribute to their extended lifespan](https://www.lionsroar.com/research-suggests-tibetan-buddhist-monastics-are-much-more-scared-of-death-than-average/)[2](https://www.ncesc.com/geographic-faq/do-tibetans-live-longer/).
2.  **Health and Longevity**:
    -   Research conducted on spiritual gurus in England, Europe, and North America found that **Buddhist monks** tend to live longer and enjoy better health than the average person.
    -   [Despite physical challenges, their overall well-being appears to be more robust](https://www.lionsroar.com/research-suggests-tibetan-buddhist-monastics-are-much-more-scared-of-death-than-average/)[3](https://spiritualhelps.com/do-buddhist-monks-live-longer/).
3.  **General Tibetan Life Expectancy**:
    -   The average life expectancy of people in **Tibet** has significantly improved over the years.
    -   [In 1959, the figure was only **35.5 years**, but now it has risen to **70.6 years**](https://www.lionsroar.com/research-suggests-tibetan-buddhist-monastics-are-much-more-scared-of-death-than-average/)[4](https://www.ncesc.com/geographic-faq/what-is-the-life-expectancy-of-tibetan-people/).

In summary, Tibetan monastics grapple with fear of death, even though their spiritual path emphasizes the impermanence of the self. Meanwhile, their commitment to community and practice seems to contribute to their extended lifespans.

### LuciizaP — 2024-02-15T16:37:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation-mindfulness-qigong-and-neigong.28580/post-331472>

drlwilson.com/articles/meditation.htm

There's a lot of lofty claims in this article and a lot of extra stuff that may or may not be worth reading depending on whether you enjoy it or not.
I've found that with general mindfulness/breath/self-enquiry meditation that it's all very "heady" - in and aware of the head centre. The pushing-down exercise, as described, is a body scan that pushes energetically down from the head to the feet. This helps to become aware of one's body and comes with a few benefits
Personally I just concentrate on my legs/lower back area. This has brought me much more into my body in recent months

People keep going on about standing meditation, known as Zhan Zhuang in Chinese. They say it delivers a lot, and has a lot of body based benefits compared to sitting meditation. They say it's very powerful. I'm practicing it now, and can attest that with only a short time in practicing, there's a lot of power and energy locked in it. It's a path unto itself.

### [Zhan Zhuang: Ancient Standing Meditation for Energy & Focus](https://scottjeffrey.com/zhan-zhuang/#:~:text=Additionally%2C%20practicing%20Zhan%20Zhuang%20regularly,relaxed%2C%20mobile%2C%20and%20strong.)

Discover Zhan Zhuang, the Taoist standing meditation that unifies posture, breath, and chi to restore balance, vitality, and mental clarity

 scottjeffrey.com

They say it's very powerful. I'm practicing it now, and can attest that with only a short time in practicing, there's a lot of power and energy locked in it. It's a path unto itself.

>
> Discover Zhan Zhuang, the Taoist standing meditation that unifies posture, breath, and chi to restore balance, vitality, and mental clarity

Tried this today, was surprised at how much mental effort was required to just stand there. I have had a reasonably strong discipline in meditation the last 6 months so it came as something of a surprise.

There are also some Rinzai Zen "Naikan" exercises that I have found are useful to integrate into my practise, building energy and groundedness. They can be found by reading some of Hakuin's texts, but this book covers them very well:

### ["Practical Zen: Meditation and Beyond" by Julian Daizan Skinner - Zenways](https://zenways.org/product/practical-zen-meditation-and-beyond-by-julian-daizan-skinner/)

"A true gift to the world. From the historical context of Zen to its profound insights, seldom has so much wisdom been packed into a single volume."

 zenways.org

Regarding meditation "keeping light in the system," I've often noticed that various practices that are hitting the right buttons internally seem to generate a "cooling" sensation on the skin. It may be just a subjective perception, but it seems plausible that if you were absorbing light resonantly into your body, i.e. the surface of your skin was conducting IR radiation inside, then such a cooling effect might be expected.
I wonder if you have had a similar experience [@LuciizaP](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19431/) ?

Yes there's a massive difference with standing isn't there. Makes me wonder why it isn't given a second thought about in any other tradition except Taoism from what I can see. It's about body awareness, with all the other meditation techniques its all in the 'ajna'/head centre - thoughts in the head, perceptions in the head, refinement in the head. This standing meditation FORCES one to come out of that area and go into bodily sensation, which brings a whole new world in to the meditative process that simply wasnt available before. I got lots of shaking/untwisting of muscles/readjusting for new alignments. I've been doing it every day now for just over a month and notice an energy increase/feeling of strength in my body when I stand. Apparently nearly everyone gets the posture wrong, this video goes to help for beginners though so we don't get locked in to the wrong posture and can experiment -

Haven't had a cooling of the skin effect that you theorise about, interesting tho

I mean, really looked.
Your living room, your favourite spot in nature.
The texture of the wood.
The shadows.
The fabrics.
The bits of mud.
SO DETAILED!
Ever just focused on the symphony of sound around you?
The birds chirping
The motocycle humming
The waves
SO DETAILED!
So what though?
What if One was to do this daily?
Would One be rewiring a 480pixel perception to a 4K one given enough time so that's baseline?
Ignore the self talk
Concentrate on the sensory details.
What is work at first
Could become baseline immersion/satisfaction effortlessly in time

### LuciizaP — 2026-05-08T20:08:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation-mindfulness-qigong-and-neigong.28580/post-373476>

Decentralized Mind
Can you have a decentralized life without addressing the mind? My suspicion is no.
Decentralized Mind = no fixed personality structure that refers back to itself centrally. Plenty of personality. None of it as a central 'I'. Or, if you don't like that, 'as if' there is no central 'I'.
Time for some woo-woo. There's this system called Human Design that I've been using on and off for the last 9 years. It's essentially an experiment where one follows their 'strategy' and 'authority' - two mechanisms in which the personality listens to before making decisions. There is plenty of info online about what your strat and auth means.
Whether it's woowoo or not, I can't really convince anyone of. The founder, Ra Uru Hu calls it an experiment. I liked that - no pressure.
Mind can measure and reflect and think and hypothesize and do things in all sorts of manners. That's its strength, and that's the strength of this forum. However, as the system suggests, it is not to be used for making decisions. For what the body decides to do as it moves through space. The only decision making that should be done is what is highlighed in one's strategy and authority chart.

### [Get Your Free Human Design Chart | Explore Your Unique Design](https://jovianarchive.com/pages/get-your-human-design-chart?srsltid=AfmBOoogDynQlmqUrKkgNMgi7wi3lxhMqmOe38nnl79VZTTHey3vta9aT)

Use our chart calculator to generate your Human Design chart free. Unlock personalized insights, access beginner-friendly resources, and start your journey of self-discovery today.

 jovianarchive.com

Type in your birthdate, and it'll give you it. If you don't know the exact time, do multiple points along the date of birth and see if the strategy and authority changes, if it does, fill in the gaps and decide what's right for you.
I've fully deconditioned (it takes 7 years of dedication to it to rewire!). This was after many years of meditation prior. Meditation is 'detachment', this is 'immersion'. Both have benefits.
The benefit for human design specifically? A life with no energetic/mental resistance and total one-pointedness and ease because of it. Resistance will come in the form of the illusions mind throws up, but in terms of your body's energy, its aligned.
Enjoy, for those who take the experiment!
"If I think to myself... I really should call that person back... and not one muscle in my body twitches... guess what you should do? Not call that person back" (If the body reaches for the phone, go with it fully)

18 months of my own-style neigong after applying 12-14 years of mindfulness practice guiding the whole process (slow, unwinding/releasing, integrating multiple threads in the body, 'water-method')
Benefits so far (whilst doing the best of my ability to tune myself to/mitigate LWM):
A much more aligned and solid body structure, feels very pleasant
Slightly taller
When low energy for whatever reason, didnt eat or something, theres still a stable energy that feels like it wont collase; there's no 'panic' at the low energy points
I used to get debilitating pinches in my neck where I'd twist something and be out of action for a couple of days with my head lurched awkwardly forward with intense pain, hasnt happened since
In certain bad nnemf environment, I would get low blood pressure and dizziness/visual fade upon standing up. Completely eradicated in the same environment
Exercise tolerance slightly increased/slightly more energy in general
Hands always warm now
People seem to like me a bit better
More of a stable, grounded calm
I used to 'jump foward' when a bus/train started suddenly. Not anymore
Waking up easier

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-08T02:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-charge-state-of-proteins-leads-to-clotting.28582/post-331190>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Negatively charged liquid nanoparticles (LNPs) can cause clots because of charge variation changes indiced in Factor XII Protease. Factor XII (FXII) autoactivates by contact with a variety of artificial or biologic negatively charged surfaces (contact activation), resulting in blood coagulation and activation of the inflammatory kallikrein-kinin and complement systems. Factor XII is a protein involved in the blood coagulation cascade--clotting. Factor XII, also known as Hageman factor, is a protein involved in the blood coagulation pathway. Under normal circumstances, Factor XII circulates in an inactive form in the blood. But when it comes into contact with negatively charged surfaces such as LNPs,, it can undergo a conformational change that activates it. This is referred to as autoactivation.

Because Factor XII can activate itself without a need for other clotting factors to initiate the process. Factor XII initiates a series of reactions that form a blood clot via the conversion of other clotting factors, like Factor XI and Factor IX, creating fibrin, which stabilizes the clot. The kallikrein-kinin system is a part of the inflammatory response. Activation of Factor XII creates kallikrein, an enzyme that produces bradykinin, a potent vasodilator, meaning it causes blood vessels to widen, increasing blood flow to the affected area. It also increases vascular permeability, allowing immune cells and other substances to enter the tissue more easily.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-08T02:04:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-charge-state-of-proteins-leads-to-clotting.28582/post-331191>

Vascular permeability of this nature can result in vasculitis, and damage and a remodeling of the inside of the blood vessels.

First, the charge on the inner lining (intima) of the blood vessels (arteries, veins, AND in the heart), not only changed from having a negative surface charge, but to more positive. Also, when inflamed, the intima was remodeled! It went from looking like this: (see gap below?)

This will happen with positively charged LNPs too. "A Positively Charged Surface Triggers Coagulation Activation Through Factor VII Activating Protease (FSAP)"

[https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsami.7b14281](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsami.7b14281)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-08T02:06:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-charge-state-of-proteins-leads-to-clotting.28582/post-331192>

After three hours, coagulation was seen (clotting) on multiple positively charged surfaces using whole blood. Factor XIIa (activated Factor XII) and kallikrein are both enzymes involved in the coagulation cascade and the inflammatory response.

Factor XIIa is activated when Factor XII comes into contact with certain surfaces, while kallikrein is activated through various pathways, including the contact activation pathway. In this study, they found the pathway was activated by contact with positive charges. Pfizer measured the lipid nanoparticle to have a zeta potential of - 3 millivolts, which is almost close to zero, or what one would call neutral. RNA has a negative charge. DNA has a negative charge (high!). Cationic lipids in the LNP have a positive charge.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-09T21:30:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-will-apples-new-vr-glasses-likely-do-to-humans-who-use-it.28591/post-331284>

CPC 73 says it all.

Cephalopods have been on earth from the cambrian explosion. What wisdom do they have for us?

Even cephalopods know what most humans do not. Light at night is not good. In fact, it is the fast track to chronic neolithic diseases like cancer.

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1755714067971379631](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1755714067971379631)

Cephalopods predate bivalaves on our pale blue dot. Oysters, clams, and mussels are bivalves that the census takers of our pale blue dot. All their relatives are not only featured for dinner at my house often but they form one of th most abundant components of the Earth's fossil record. Google Dave Jablonski sometime. They are common in ancient lakes, streams, oceans, and rivers everywhere on EARTH. Bivalves species fill the cabinets of paleontological collections world wide. They have been on the planet for 500 million years. 85 million years after the Cambrian explosion. Realized that cephalopods are older then they are.

Bivalves remain the most nutrient dense food stuff on Earth for mammals. Their evolutionary history makes them the ideal laboratory to test theories on how species diversity changes through time. If you eat a lot of them it will also lead to an extension of your own life by giving you time because of how many electrons they have. [http://www.vox.com/.../11352354/oysters-environment-farming](https://www.vox.com/2016/4/6/11352354/oysters-environment-farming?fbclid=IwAR209-A26p_-DM7Put-3uRLywvzFRuDEC44Du1szQXMtbF0d4KfRnOC-Jfg)

# Thread: Kina's journal - in need of some encouragement and support
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kinas-journal-in-need-of-some-encouragement-and-support.28596/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-02-11T17:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kinas-journal-in-need-of-some-encouragement-and-support.28596/post-331330>

Hello everyone.

Here is my story:

I am a 38 year old Mauritian woman. I was diagnosed with triple negative breast cancer in 2017 and since then I have been trying to heal my body and mind back to optimal health. The journey has been hard but I cannot give up.

I had to undergo many rounds of chemo (a combination of TAXOTERE, ADRIAMYCIN and CYCLOPHOSPHAMIDE). I still to this day suffer from alopecia. My hair is still very thin and sparse, same for eyelashes and eyebrows and body hair (had a full head of healthy curls, thick eyebrows and eyelashes prior to chemo). I also went through several rounds of radiation and I had several lumpectomies as well.

My twin sister got diagnosed with exact same cancer a year after me. My mother, who has now passed, had breast cancer twice and died of metastatic ovarian cancer (a lot of my mother's sisters and my female cousins have had either breast or ovarian cancer).

Throughout my healing journey I have tried many things :
\- GAPS diet (to heal the gut) - 8 months
\- carnivore diet - 1 year
\- the root cause protocol - 1 year
\- Jane McLelland's anti cancer protocol (using off-label drugs like metformin, doxycycline, aspirin, and statins to block cancer growing pathways) - 6 months
\- various detox protocols including frequent blood donations, daily coffee enemas - 2 years
\- the copper revolution protocol (copper and co-factors supplementation) - 2 years
\- supplementation (copper, zinc, diatomaceous earth, boron, magnesium, iodine, ....) to address deficiencies and to detox.....very hard sometimes - 2 years

I cannot say for sure if these protocols have helped me but I learnt a lot along the way and I continue to learn everyday. These protocols might have harmed my body too: at some point I lost so much weight with all the detox I was doing that I had to stop everything or else I think my body would have shut down...Also, using the off-label pharmaceutical drugs to prevent cancer recurrence must have definitely wreaked havoc in my body.

My current health issues/concerns/symptoms are:

\- bad sleep/trouble falling asleep/indomnia/night wakings
\- energy crashes daily after meals/fatigue/low energy
\- digestive issues (SEVERE CONSTIPATION - cannot poop for days in a row, only enema helps, bloating after eating, lots of burping)
\- dry skin/dark circles (lack of sleep? liver issues?)
\- thin hair, eyebrows, eyelashes (hasn't resolved since cancer treatment)
\- dull and tired eyes

The positive side is that I have a good appetite, good mental health, stable weight (63kg, 1m68).

I am motivated to heal more and more, I am hopeful as ever even after 7 years on this journey.

I am grateful to have found this community.

It's been 30 days since I have stopped all supplementation and detox protocols. I have been watching sunrises daily for a 30 days non-stop. Caught many sunsets as well. Have done CT every other day in the freezing cold sea water. I now eat an epi-paleo diet with oysters everyday. I stay outside in the sun during the day as much as possible and wear blue light blockers and turn off ALL artificial light after sunset and limit EMF during the day.

I cannot say for sure that I have seen benefits in those 30 days but I can feel something good is coming.

I would love to have some support and encouragement from you.

Thanks for reading.

### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-02-14T18:33:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kinas-journal-in-need-of-some-encouragement-and-support.28596/post-331438>

Progress:

\- Last night I actually felt sleepy when I went to bed. And I only woke up ONCE in the middle of the night. This is a huge win for me because I usually do not feel sleepy at bedtime and have to fore myself to sleep and I used to wake up several times unable to go back to sleep.

\- For the last five days I have been having an urge to go to the bathroom (bowel movement) at the SAME TIME everyday. This is interesting because I have always had irregular bowel movements at random times of the day or I very often just do not feel any urge at all to go to the bathroom for days on end. I am still very constipated but I have a feeling that things are being organised and regulated in my body.

N.B. 34 days in a row of watching AM sunrise and staying in am light (minimal clothing) for 2 hours while grounding by the beach and CT every other day in cold ocean water for 10-20 mins; also daily 2-hour (4pm to 6 pm) walk on the beach in the sun while grounding

### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-02-19T14:44:52+0200

The last few days have been up and down. My sleep has been weird but in general not as bad as before. On some nights I wake up once, others twice or more. I do feel sleepy most nights. Oh, how I wish I could sleep like a rock all night long and wake up refreshed and ready to start the day. What a luxury!

I watch the sunrise, while eating with my feet grounded on the sand. Spend about 2 more hours basking in the am sun with as little clothes as possible. In the afternoon, around 4pm, I go back to the beach again and spend time walking, swimming until sunset. I love this routine. I hope my vitamin D levels are increasing .... > When I was in the Caribbean I saw a local girl scrub her skin with wet beach sand and then rinse in the ocean. I tried it and my skin was amazingly soft. It's detoxing, I think.

I've seen some people do this. I will research on this. Thank you
