# Thread: My wild journey : my EHS, losses, a domestic case, observations, El Zonte, a new beginning and more. Pt. 1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:33:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331488>

Hello all,

It’s been a while since I ‘ve ‘met & greeted’ but I’m finally ready to share.

My journey actually starts by me having to leave Amsterdam Airport by car and seeing flight UA21 leaving without me.

I had planned to go to El Zonte, El Salvador to get some 13th latitude sun & UV, clear my head, check out job- and life options to possibly restart my masseuse practice there. Cause I am tired, weary and done with this latitude, this version of society and its scrambled head inhabitants. I want a completely new life. I had planned to write this whole extensive, detailed journey while laying on Pacific sand. I figured I’d be inspired to write when I’m the right environment. So this is the relatively short 52nd-latitude version of my journey.

I have been staying with my older brother in “5G” The Hague City (Den Haag) in the Netherlands / Holland since summer last year. It’s no NY or Amsterdam even, but there are still 600k idiotic users packed together, using technology. My brother will turn 51 this year, has had two TIA’s, has nerve pains in hands & feet, high blood pressure, diabetes type II and works at night as a taxi driver, driving a Tesla. And doesn’t get outside much.

I’ve bought him two Ra Optics glasses, but ofcourse that’s not enough. To me that’s an ending case in all honesty. Once Holland goes into phase 2 of the 5G roll out. That’ll probably happen next year. I’ve already been preparing myself for the worst, emotionally. But he’s not taking me with him. That’s for sure.

So I basically been running away for 7 - 8 months, renting out places in the south of the Netherlands/ Holland and Belgian/ Flemish Ardennes. Mainly to better my environment, health, biology and overall outlook on life. And get my head clear, my thinking up and my cell charge up. ‘Cause I have (some form of) EHS. But I feel like I’m running out of time. And money. At the same time my experiences and observations in these last couple of months have enriched me in ways I could’ve never imagined. I have used the power of fire, in- & outside, grounding in a.m. & p.m. light, grounding knee deep in the Northsea along the Belgian coast, even during a storm, cold exposure in- & outside, use of near & infrared light , been blocking blue light with several Ra Optics glasses, scarfs, cotton gloves, wooly hats and cotton clothing to protect my skin, neck & brain and of course being in nature, naked, barefoot and/or barechested. Everything has been an experience to write about. I actually found a surprisingly good friend in CT. Not right away but quicker than I expected. I have been in cold showers for the last 2 years,CT-ing 3 -4 times a week for the last 4 months at 8 - 12 C (46 - 54F)The last 3 weeks I was able to get in cold everyday straight. I did my first 75 min bath at 15 C (59F) a few days ago. Couldn’t get it any colder unfortunately. And been outside in 5-6 C (41 - 43F) water for 10 - 15 minutes. I remember sitting at the bottom of that natural pool, thinking, ‘Well this ain’t even that bad!” Of course, after 15 minutes, things got different. I haven’t missed a single sunrise or sunset in more than two years.

But renting all those houses, cabins and cottages has been a very costly endeavor. But I believed it was necessary to get better. And I have been in hell the last 8 years. Basically longer, but let me just stick to 8. And El Salvador then wasn’t as appealing as it is now. My mind wasn’t ready,

Before living with my brother I’ve had a terrible ending to my fam life, being forced to say goodbye to two ADHD, suïcidal kids and a depressed, anxiety disordered, down, stressed out, melatonin addicted, catatonic, non thinking, low dopamine, pale white, non supportive, medical paradigm addicted, ex partner. All three were and probably still are addicted to their phones too. With no respect for or understanding of my EHS. I basically had to fight my way out of there ending in a minor domestic case and a short stay in jail. That’s how far I had to take after being together for 28 years.

Which does not describe who I am. At all. I’ve been having arguments with grandmothers, friends, teachers, ADHD coaches, doctors, countless psychologists, mental coaches, Corona vaccine administers and psychiatrists, all eating from the same bullshit pie and spitting all that shit at me. And an ex partner who completely sided with all of them without being critical or thinking for herself. Those multiple stabs in the heart and back will probably never fully heal.

No one or no kid should be taking methylfenidate. Taking something exogenous that raises your blood pressure enormously probably isn’t the best way to better your health. I showed them side effect of Ritalin & Concerta but it’s like talking to a statue.

So I’ve cut most ties with family and I have been staying far away from so called ‘friends’. Even my best friend, who I’ve known for over 30 years.

I am a L0a1a haplotype, 45% Liberian & 45% Nigerian with 4% Scandinavian sprinkled on top. 6% unknown. Born in Surinam, 5/6th latitude, but been living in Holland since I was about 9 months.

I’ ll be turning 50 this year and have been able to stay away from doctors, prescriptions, medicine or faulty medical advice for almost 12 years. And of course, no Corona/SV-40 vaccine injection. Ofcourse no, are you crazy.

I think I’m doing ok..ish.

My earlier mentioned EHS, which I discovered on November 22nd 2020, that’s when I first heard of this dude named Jack Kruse, has been under control since 2021 and has been ‘evolving’ ever since. In a good way I think.

Evolving ‘cause it has become more than just ‘electro’ now. When I really think about it, it’s not. But it’s different for sure.

I can feel, detect and sense everything. And I literally mean EVERYTHING’. And it has not been irritating, annoying, painful or bothersome but feeIs more like sensing. Or having a compass even. It always feels very subtle. Sometimes very locally depending on the strenght of the field. Or size.

My fascia changes at very specific places, whenever I’m around nnEMF, for example tissues around my right adductor magnus, left IT/glute max, deep gut/upper pelvic region, right upper part of my semimembranosis (hamstrings) upper and lower thorax area, occipital ridge, right platysma, fascia running up my left triceps, shoulderblade & down to my left rhomboid and spine. But mostly the left part of my thoracolumbar fascial area. It’s never muscle but always fascial tissue. Changes are always felt across the whole fascial system but certain area just light up more.

### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:40:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331489>

### ​

Pt. 2 / 4
My wild journey : my EHS, losses, a domestic case, observations, El Zonte and more.

Here we go :
I feel every 4/5G tower, powerlines, trains, phones, electrical wiring or appliances, planes flying over, Tesla’s and GLC300’s driving behind me. Everything new built by the German Volkswagen & BMW Group feels like a disaster on wheels. Deleting any file on my phone is a change, even adjusting the volume.

But it goes further than that. I feel a distinct difference between a Bluetooth and a WIFI signal. Or when I enter a room where my phone is charging I know and feel that it’s at a 100%. Or the switch from 2% to 1% battery power. Winding, curling or changing the shape of a disconnected iphone cable, I sense those changes too.

I can tell you by placing my hand against a wall how the electrical wiring runs. If it’s a place not stuffed with nnEMF though. Dimmer switches are the worst to stand next to. Or bad wiring, dirty electricity. It does not bother me, it’s just there. On the outskirts of my senses.

But its gone crazier than that. I can feel a plant’s energy. Watering plants is a joyful moment as it induces deep breathing and it makes my eyesight clearer. I feel a warm tingle across ny ribcage, overall it just makes me feel good. I can tell by touch but also by intensely looking if a plant has energy in its roots, therefore knowing that it’s dead or not dead. So I have been sleeping with plants around my bed. No sex ;-) but i think we are enjoying each others company. I can feel a difference in energy between the roots, stems and leafs. If I open a window up more to let some more sun in or put them outside everything I just mentioned just get heightened.

The same goes for water. l feel lakes, pools and forests at a distance. I can sense ocean water at approximately 3 km/2 mi distance. I can feel trees or forests at a km/0,6 mi distance. I can even ground through trees or thick branches by holding /touching a tree trunk or standing on the roots of a tree barefoot. It’s almost the same surge of energy as standing on grass, barefoot. Especially when it’s raining.

But that’s not all. I can feel or sense bio energy coming from food. Every tomato, onion, egg, oyster. ounce/gram of kale, red cabbage, curcumine, red meat, animal fat, shrimp or fish. Just by holding it in my hand, or looking at it intensely, or even standing next to whatever it is, I can feel slight changes in my eyes, eyesocket, eyebrow, brow ridge, nose, chest and my deep gut. There are actual physical changes I detect. Red cabbage, kale especially fish have got the strongest signal or energy. So of course, those I eat the most.

Again, very very subtle but it’s definitely detectable.

I’m able to feel colours too. Hovering my hand over a white, blue and red piece of paper with my eyes closed I can tell you which is which. With the colour red having the strongest signal. I sense the difference between 100% cotton and 50% polyester/ 50% cotton. Or when a cotton shirt has 5% elastine.

But during these last couple of weeks it went even further than that. I can see and feel deuterium in food. When splitting egg yolk from eggwhite I can feel deuterium ‘burning’ on my fingertips. I boldy assume it’s deuterium. I get the same signal from all those packaged deuterium bombs in our local supermarkets. Oatmeal, potato chips, chocolate bars, cookies, some are way worse than others but they’re basically all the same. McDonalds is at the top of that list by the way.

I can look at and through a pack of eggs and determine how much energy is in the yolk and or how much deuterium is its surrounding white. So I know which eggs are best. Free roam with added vitamine D are horrible plus there is little to no energy in its yolk. Compared to normal free roam eggs. Which I eat almost daily.

Looking at a bowl of eggwhite ‘burns” fascia in and around my eyes.

But.. when eating oysters, that’s when the party starts, especially French Gillardeau’s. The bio energy makes me do stupid happy dances while holding empty shells like Spanish castanets. And no, you will never see me do that. I can tell you which oysters are best. To me. The French lead the race, Irish are usually good too, not all of them. Dutch ones taste horrible but they do the job. Makes a lot of sense…

The water inside an oyster also gives off a signal which resembles deuterium in some cases. I once had Spanish oysters which gave me the same signal as eggwhites. I ate a few of them and threw the box away. Never again.

But it goes even further than that. I can now distinguish local food from this latitude. from let’s say Morocco or Spain. 90% of all fruits and vegetables get imported from these countries.

I only have to look at a tomato and know it’s from Spain. Or at least not local. One day there selling you kale from Dutch soil, but will switch to kale from Spain the very next day. I wasn’t paying attention one day, I just grabbed a pack of kale and brought it home. But during my first bite or chew I felt changes in my (upper)gut immediately. Bad ones. All I should have done was be aware and pay attention to signals. Which obviously I didn’t. Never made that mistake again.

I stopped eating sugar in October. Ate a Dutch tomato in december. My eyes said ‘ok’ but my deep gut said ‘no Sir!’ I cant even try to explain how that mismatch feels. So I haven’t touched tomatoes since.

Just for jokes I walk by a tropical fruit & vegetable section in a supermarket just to feel and confirm all that awful and mismatching energy coming from those mangoes, kiwi, pineapples in January. Just to confirm in my tissues what I already know.

### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331492>

Pt.3 / 4

My most recent discovery is seeing, sensing and feeling people. I can look at a human being as a whole but I can also zone in on specific parts of the body. So I have been doing that with people’s heads, hearts , chest and brains. This all happens through clothing. The signals I get by doing this are by far the most interesting of them all, ‘cause I don’t exactly know what I’m looking at. Or receiving rather. Plus there is so much happening inside and I can’t be staring at people for 5 minutes. Magnetic fields, infrared light, other types of light people are sending out, ELF’s or some other form of energy I’m not aware of. What am I receiving ?

When I focus in on somebody’s brain, the signal I get from them is ‘good’, ‘bad’ or ‘distorted’. Good’ relaxes fascia in my eyes & socket, especially my left eye, brightens up my vision, implodes my gut and reenergizes or resets my whole abdomen which subsequently makes me roll my shoulders back and basically shifts my whole posture into a more relaxed state. Again this is all very subtle. ‘Bad’ tightens up my gut & eyes, making me want to pinch my eyes and look away. People with headphones on or looking at their phones will give me a scrambled signal. It makes me think of distortion or white noise. A very annoying signal.

Kids mostly send out a good signal , from babies to 9 / 10 year-olds. With adults you never know what you’re getting. A mom walking her kid(s) to school could give me very strong brainsignal while a father walking behind them is totally bad. Old people sometimes surprise me with a very strong brain signal, just strolling or walking their dog. Women usually have a stronger heart & brain signal than men. What I’ve been noticing too is that I’m able to sense the combined light or energy of a crowd. Whether it’s at the airport or at a food plaza, of course there’s nnEMF around, but it’s very manageable. It’s almost the same cloud of energy that hits me when I walk into a forest. But slighty different. I think that’s what the Dutch call ‘gezelligheid’ (cosiness, fun).

I’m leaving out a whole lot I would have wanted to talk about. A whole goddamn lot. About the correlation between the shape and size of an eye and human structure and the rotations in the spine. Or how a smaller eye correlates to your gait cycle. You can learn a lot about people’s health by this observing. And watching them stand ir walk. Maybe I will give you the extended version once I’m feeling inspired more. But I have been wallowing in sorrow, regret, pain, loss, loneliness & disappointment for many months and I feel like I’m just getting out of a hole. So this is basically what I was able to squeeze out of my brain and hand. I would have wanted to talk in depth about those countless podcasts I listened to, my experiences, especially my observations in the last 4 - 5 years and the millions of questions I have because of these observations. Or buying a spectrometer and having my eyes opened. Wow.

Or the people, strangers, not friends, people I didn’t know and introduced to Jack Kruse. Or the measurements of my TriMeter I was able to show folks. There are many, many, amazing moments in there.

Goddamn. I just HAVE to talk about a few.
While sitting barechested and grounded in grass in a public park enjoying the Dutch summersun last year at UV index 7,5 I had a little brown bird sitting in the grass just 1 meter (3 ft.) away from me, wings spread, obviously to get some sun. She hopped out of the bushes and literally squatted down like she came home from a hard day’s work and crashed on the couch. She turned her head and looked at me like: ‘oh shit dude, you here too huh ?’ , looked away and just relaxed. Again, wings spread with the shiny, purple, reflective part in her feathers exposed.

Both of us just being there for a good 3 minutes was just beautiful, with me glancing over every 20 seconds. 2 animals with a similar goal. All I could do was smile. And chuckle. Softly ofcourse. One of the funniest and at the same time most beautiful things I’ve ever experienced. Amazing.

Talks with strangers in that park. Eating oysters attracts all kinds of people. An Indian dude and a dude from Curacao starting talking to me. After 30 minutes I showing them measurements of my spectrometer and TriMeter. Again, oysters were there. 2 friends, IT guys being outside on sickleave, suffering from stomach and gut issues who I convinced to go barechested. Which eventually they did after I told them about he gut and the brain and skin surface. I threw a little POMC and melanin in there without knowing all the details or quantum processes. I politely asked them to turn there phones off which they did.

After that I had the pleasure of sharing my oysters with them, which they had never had. I had draped theirs with lemon, red onions, curcumine, ginger and some nutmeg. After the first ones, they both nodded and smiled, which in turn made me smile from ear to ear. After a lot of ‘hmmmm’s’ and ‘oooh’s’ we shared about 24 oysters between the 3 of us. It’s wonderful and very fullfilling to open eyes that have been shut for years. If you are willing and/or interested, I can sense that.

### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331493>

Pt. 4 / 4 (last part)

I have got a whole bunch of those stories and experiences. Like, real magical experiences, like me magnetically and energetically having a lemon stuck to my wrist for a second or my left leg being magnetically drawn to an active dishwasher, which is by far the most out of this world experience I’ve ever had. And it scared the shit out of me. That would be my first question to Jack.

My medical history, my future plans, there is so much. My analysis of Dutch general health, using figures provided by the Central Bureau of Statistics. It tells you a story that should scare all. But not when you’re blue light poisoned. And profiting from that. I want to tell that story someday.

Once I get my head clear, have a new place to call home and I’ve got my legs and feet sturdy up under me I would like to take down those holy and sacred cows which Holland is filled with. And they’ve got a whole lot of cows.

I don’t want to go back to my brother, ever, ‘cause that’s straight hell. I found a place to stay with a lost friend, a real friend who just became my soulmate. It’s not ideal, but of course I can’t complain. When I think about it, I’m ashamed I haven’t kept in touch with him.

He’s always had a special place in my heart. Not because he offered to let me stay at his place, but the warmth and energy he always exuded. And still does. He is a really smart dude, critical thinker, muslim of Turkish descent, doesn’t look his age, has a positive outlook on life and makes connections easily. But is still finding his way in life.?It’s fun to hear him connecting the dots when hearing something for the first time. I have never met anyone like him. He is interested in life, biology and in one of our of conversations about going back home, sun & grounding, my life, quantum biology and energy he casually dropped that he was born in Brunei, 4/5th latitude and spent the first 8 years of his life in Brunei. At the beach! That just shocked the shit of me and makes our consersations even better. I jumped up out of exitement and had to walk it off. The Turkish are generally, mostly J2 so I’m curious to know what those 8 years did to him, his mom anhis sisters and their mitochondria. It obviously gave him some form of extra energy compared to most I know. Or knew. That’s what I detect when I’m around him. No wonder I love him. With the knowledge I have about circadian rhytm, strong UV, apoptosis and autophagy and how melanine plays a role in transforming light to energy, I’m trying to explain to him (and myself!) why he is who he is. This is a dude for life now so I hope he’ll adapt and incorporate the things we talk about in his personal life. I think he will. He just joked that he’s basically blacker than I am cause he’s been born closer to the equator and been there for a much longer time. Good one, but there might be some thruth in there. This is the only dude that I would invite over if I ever get the chance to get settled in El Salvador.

So I just joined as a Gold Member and could definitely use some help. Or a hand. Or a place to stay abroad, with respect to nnEMF. And I need some new friends, preferably, energetic & non idiotic.

There was a validity problem with my passport so I can’t travel and come to El Salvador. Upon entry the Salvadoran government demands a passport to be valid for at least 6 months. Mine expires on June 18 of this year. Getting a new passport would initially take at least 6 weeks. Which really fucked with me. Really. Seeing that flight leave out of Amsterdam Airport kept me up at that night. Not long. But it did. Through perseverance I still managed to submit my passport application today, so fortunately I was able to cut those 6 weeks back to just 1. Hope is back.

Dear Jack, if you’re reading this, this is for you :

You’ve really given me a purpose and a direction. A forever seemingly vagus mind that lost its way finally knows what it wants. You took crazy away and replaced that with quantum science and facts

I’m still that little 7 year old melanine boy that deliberately broke his birthday toy cars just to see how they work. Only to use those little engines/batteries in his own Lego built helicopter. That little dude’s curiosity and intererest in general mechanics are still there. The kid’s got some grey in his beard now, his left eye has a slighty changed shape, his structure is also affected a bit, but the hunger has never left. You gave me an ok to be curious without boundaries. It’s like getting that splinter in your feet removed that’s been bothering you for 3 years or getting rid of a dead weight that you’ve been carrying around for way too long.

I’m eternally grateful, really,

I just wish I had the vocab to really express how I’m feeling inside.

You changed my whole outlook on human biology, human nature, nature and just life period. For the better. Which has severely altered all life choices I’m making. Nobody has ever done that for me. The shit I’ve been going through in the last 8 years, especially the last 2 years is nothing compared to what people and society must’ve dragged you through for the last 20 years. But you still persevered and landed on top. Only to have a good view of the shit they got themselves in. Chuckling, drinking wine and eating oysters in the sun.

Your pursuit of truth is inspiring and to me addictive. You’ve inspired me to do the same. And I already know how. I know I have like years and years of reading & learning but I’m willing. Reading is a problem right now, but we’ll discuss that some other time. You have answered a million questions for me , without you knowing and subsequently made me think of 2 million more. Like what happened with me and that lemon. Or what happened with me and that dishwasher.

Besides massages there’s nothing else I really place value in. Anymore. Valence electrons in groups 1 and 2, making use of the photoelectric effect just make sense. I see that. I want more of that. That’s why I became a member. I don’t feel alone anymore. I just feel by myself now. And at the same time part of something bigger, a community of thinkers. In all honesty I’ve shed many tears writing this all this down. But I have a feeling it’ll all be ok eventually.

I thank you for making my life more meaningful. From the bottom of my heart. Or mitochondria. You helped me to really accept myself for everything that I am. On all levels, even the ones I can’t explain. Yet. And I haven’t even jumped into bitcoin yet.

Thank you for being Jack Kruse, Jack Kruse.

If we ever get to meet in person I’m sure you’ll feel and see the gratitude in my eyes. I might even give you a big hug too.

Hope to hear from anyone soon.

I appreciate you all.

Love,

Leonaero

Welcome. Expedite the passport. You get it real fast.

### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-02-18T21:29:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331602>

> [Leonaero\_Light said:](/goto/post?id=331488)
>
> Hello all,
>
> It’s been a while since I ‘ve ‘met & greeted’ but I’m finally ready to share.
>
> My journey actually starts by me having to leave Amsterdam Airport by car and seeing flight UA21 leaving without me.
>
> I had planned to go to El Zonte, El Salvador to get some 13th latitude sun & UV, clear my head, check out job- and life options to possibly restart my masseuse practice there. Cause I am tired, weary and done with this latitude, this version of society and its scrambled head inhabitants. I want a completely new life. I had planned to write this whole extensive, detailed journey while laying on Pacific sand. I figured I’d be inspired to write when I’m the right environment. So this is the relatively short 52nd-latitude version of my journey.
>
> I have been staying with my older brother in “5G” The Hague City (Den Haag) in the Netherlands / Holland since summer last year. It’s no NY or Amsterdam even, but there are still 600k idiotic users packed together, using technology. My brother will turn 51 this year, has had two TIA’s, has nerve pains in hands & feet, high blood pressure, diabetes type II and works at night as a taxi driver, driving a Tesla. And doesn’t get outside much.
>
> I’ve bought him two Ra Optics glasses, but ofcourse that’s not enough. To me that’s an ending case in all honesty. Once Holland goes into phase 2 of the 5G roll out. That’ll probably happen next year. I’ve already been preparing myself for the worst, emotionally. But he’s not taking me with him. That’s for sure.
>
> So I basically been running away for 7 - 8 months, renting out places in the south of the Netherlands/ Holland and Belgian/ Flemish Ardennes. Mainly to better my environment, health, biology and overall outlook on life. And get my head clear, my thinking up and my cell charge up. ‘Cause I have (some form of) EHS. But I feel like I’m running out of time. And money. At the same time my experiences and observations in these last couple of months have enriched me in ways I could’ve never imagined. I have used the power of fire, in- & outside, grounding in a.m. & p.m. light, grounding knee deep in the Northsea along the Belgian coast, even during a storm, cold exposure in- & outside, use of near & infrared light , been blocking blue light with several Ra Optics glasses, scarfs, cotton gloves, wooly hats and cotton clothing to protect my skin, neck & brain and of course being in nature, naked, barefoot and/or barechested. Everything has been an experience to write about. I actually found a surprisingly good friend in CT. Not right away but quicker than I expected. I have been in cold showers for the last 2 years,CT-ing 3 -4 times a week for the last 4 months at 8 - 12 C (46 - 54F)The last 3 weeks I was able to get in cold everyday straight. I did my first 75 min bath at 15 C (59F) a few days ago. Couldn’t get it any colder unfortunately. And been outside in 5-6 C (41 - 43F) water for 10 - 15 minutes. I remember sitting at the bottom of that natural pool, thinking, ‘Well this ain’t even that bad!” Of course, after 15 minutes, things got different. I haven’t missed a single sunrise or sunset in more than two years.
>
> But renting all those houses, cabins and cottages has been a very costly endeavor. But I believed it was necessary to get better. And I have been in hell the last 8 years. Basically longer, but let me just stick to 8. And El Salvador then wasn’t as appealing as it is now. My mind wasn’t ready,
>
> Before living with my brother I’ve had a terrible ending to my fam life, being forced to say goodbye to two ADHD, suïcidal kids and a depressed, anxiety disordered, down, stressed out, melatonin addicted, catatonic, non thinking, low dopamine, pale white, non supportive, medical paradigm addicted, ex partner. All three were and probably still are addicted to their phones too. With no respect for or understanding of my EHS. I basically had to fight my way out of there ending in a minor domestic case and a short stay in jail. That’s how far I had to take after being together for 28 years.
>
> Which does not describe who I am. At all. I’ve been having arguments with grandmothers, friends, teachers, ADHD coaches, doctors, countless psychologists, mental coaches, Corona vaccine administers and psychiatrists, all eating from the same bullshit pie and spitting all that shit at me. And an ex partner who completely sided with all of them without being critical or thinking for herself. Those multiple stabs in the heart and back will probably never fully heal.
>
> No one or no kid should be taking methylfenidate. Taking something exogenous that raises your blood pressure enormously probably isn’t the best way to better your health. I showed them side effect of Ritalin & Concerta but it’s like talking to a statue.
>
> So I’ve cut most ties with family and I have been staying far away from so called ‘friends’. Even my best friend, who I’ve known for over 30 years.
>
>
>
> I am a L0a1a haplotype, 45% Liberian & 45% Nigerian with 4% Scandinavian sprinkled on top. 6% unknown. Born in Surinam, 5/6th latitude, but been living in Holland since I was about 9 months.
>
> I’ ll be turning 50 this year and have been able to stay away from doctors, prescriptions, medicine or faulty medical advice for almost 12 years. And of course, no Corona/SV-40 vaccine injection. Ofcourse no, are you crazy.
>
> I think I’m doing ok..ish.
>
> My earlier mentioned EHS, which I discovered on November 22nd 2020, that’s when I first heard of this dude named Jack Kruse, has been under control since 2021 and has been ‘evolving’ ever since. In a good way I think.
>
> Evolving ‘cause it has become more than just ‘electro’ now. When I really think about it, it’s not. But it’s different for sure.
>
>
>
> I can feel, detect and sense everything. And I literally mean EVERYTHING’. And it has not been irritating, annoying, painful or bothersome but feeIs more like sensing. Or having a compass even. It always feels very subtle. Sometimes very locally depending on the strenght of the field. Or size.
>
> My fascia changes at very specific places, whenever I’m around nnEMF, for example tissues around my right adductor magnus, left IT/glute max, deep gut/upper pelvic region, right upper part of my semimembranosis (hamstrings) upper and lower thorax area, occipital ridge, right platysma, fascia running up my left triceps, shoulderblade & down to my left rhomboid and spine. But mostly the left part of my thoracolumbar fascial area. It’s never muscle but always fascial tissue. Changes are always felt across the whole fascial system but certain area just light up more.

Hi. I have sent you a couple of private messages. Are you receiving them?

# Thread: Is history repeating in the US?  Was Caeser's Rome a warning?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-history-repeating-in-the-us-was-caesers-rome-a-warning.28617/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-19T03:17:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-history-repeating-in-the-us-was-caesers-rome-a-warning.28617/post-331617>

Is history repeating in the US right now in real time? Was Caeser's Rome a warning?

It´s astounding how close our time is to the conditions that led to the rise of Julius Caesar in Rome - Rome was being fleeced by a greedy elite (called the Optimates) who were monopolizing land with usury and slave labor - These elites were migrating huge volumes of slaves to work the fields for low wages (sound familiar to WEF agenda or the open border plan everywhere?) - This drove down the price of labor and ended up pushing native Romans out of work and off their land - The elites would buy up these vacant family farms and move more slaves onto them - Today we have farmers under attack, and the Fed raising rates and BlackRock buying homes and now stacking BTC to buy it all when things in real estate really blow post US election.

These same elites would then give out loans to these broke Romans to help them "set up a new life in the city", which would put them into debt slavery even further - This is why car loans now go out to 96 months and home loans out to 50-75 years. That is a life of debt slavery. The city of Rome itself swole up with a huge disgruntled class of poor veterans, native Romans, and recently migrated slaves (sound familiar?) - These formed a jaded underclass while the elites lived in slave supported luxury - Roman soldiers would go abroad and risk their lives conquering territory for Rome in hope of "making it", only to get underpaid, and then come back to find their families replaced by slaves from the place they just conquered (sound familiar?) - Caesar was one of the elites who sided with the native Romans - known as a Populist - He used "the People" as a force to pushback against the elites and drive through reforms that limited debt slavery - Through decades of ruthless civil war he came out the winner, but was infamously assassinated by the elites for subverting their grip on Rome. History is repeating and few see it.

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1759072760066965979](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1759072760066965979)

Is this why some of us have risked moving out where freedom is sprouting?

According to Bobby Kennedy, BlackRock, Vanguard, and State Street will own 60% of America’s single-family homes by 2030. They also own 88% of the companies on the S&P 500. This is a massive problem if you are a US passport holder with fiat only in the bank and one zero in Bitcoin.

### ['They Can Outbid Your Children': RFK Jr. Warns That Corporations Are 'Trying To Buy Every Single-Family Home' In America — And They Are On Track To Own 60% Of Homes By 2030](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/outbid-children-rfk-jr-warns-164021972.html)

Housing affordability has been a pressing issue in America. While many people point to supply shortages, rising material costs and stagnant wages as drivers behind the crisis, Democratic presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr. believes there's another culprit — corporations. "The real...

# Thread: Blue Light Therapy for Hyperbilirubinemia in Neonates
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-20T18:04:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331707>

I understand this practice is used to break down elevated levels of bilirubin, but I would assume, based on what I understand from Kruse, that this practice could cause serious, irreversible damage to the eyes and also to brain development of preterm babies.

I have a friend that works in the NICU. He is the one who brought this to my attention, bc he knows my thoughts on blue light hazard. He doesn't believe blue light is a problem and says the neonates are perfectly fine. Also, explains how this practice has been used for decades.

He uses this as a major example as to why blue light is no biggie and that I "overreact" to its "dangers." I know better though.

Is this practice really necessary? I do know babies would be much better off in sunlight, but I also understand that American hospitals aren't going to start putting babies in the sun any time soon. So, is blasting them with this blue light the next best thing?

He also explained to me that babies sometimes soak in this intense blue for up to a week straight which blows my mind!

Anyone have any additional info regarding this practice? I am very curious bc I do plan to have kids soon

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-20T18:40:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331708>

Ah, I realize now that eye protection is used for the infants when this is being done. That is good to know, but there is still the issue of their skin getting blasted with blue after sunset...

### jules3791 — 2024-02-20T21:51:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331712>

I seem to recall that when my first born had jaundice, I was told (unsure by whom) to take him outside in the sun. That was almost 30 years ago.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-21T18:18:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331739>

Yep, sun exposure will fix this unless it's an extreme case. Bilirubin lights are no good. Screencaps from "How To Raise A Healthy Child In Spite of Your Doctor" by Robert Mendelsohn, an excellent book for any parent:

So, you think their light cannot alter things genetically?

Blue light given to babies for circadian jaundice caused by their parent's malillumination is a light vaccine that will affect them.

Blue light is being used to alter chickens genetically. This shows that light is above genetic controls. Scientists are using blue light to "gene edit" chickens. The person posting the article to the group praised the scientists for their innovative idea that "doesn't use genetic engineering." "It's just blue light," they said. # Thread: NAD+ is the key marker for aging of the Quantum Cell.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T16:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331793>

I have spent a lot of energy in the Quantum Engineering series to explain to you how central NAD+ is in the quantum state of the cell. Sunlight has to charge up the mitochondrial membranes to get NAD+ to recycle to NADH; without the 30 million volt charge, NAD+ drops, and the animal ages in that tissue with low NAD+. NAD+ captures electrons and protons.

Without sunlight, using supplements to augment the NAD+ cycle is dangerous.

The QE 65 blog gives your the 80,000 ft view. But the other blogs in the series really get into the weeds of this key TCA metric.

Be aware of this.

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1760361719728415116](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1760361719728415116)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:02:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331795>

Hypoxia is a cellular state that disrupts regular oxygen supply to the tissue (mitochondria), causing cellular dysfunction. Examples of this are altitude sickness at high elevations and clots in a blocked artery in an organ, causing an organ to fail and die. Apoptosis and autophagy allow cells to adapt over their lifespan to many situations. Hypoxia is directly toxic to mitochondrial energy production via a lack of beta-oxidation. When oxygen is in short supply, humans can shift to anaerobic energy production. Still, it is not as efficient as mitochondrial energy production in the human brain and heart because of how many mitochondria are found in these two organs.

When mitochondrial oxygen consumption shows in any human tissue, dissolved oxygen in the cell rises, and this sets the stage for excessive ROS production, which ruins optimal TCA function. This is when NAD+ is being lowered.

Due to pseudohypoxia, NAD+ levels drop, and this funnels back to how the aromatic amino acid tryptophan is catabolized in cells. This affects NAD+ creation and melatonin production at the mitochondrial level. Tryptophan has multiple metabolic pathways it can travel, and the pathway chosen will lead to the cell's fate. That case was made deeply in the Hypoxia series, and collateral effects were laid out in the Quantum Engineering series. Hypoxia one covered this in detail.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331796>

Taking NAD supplements is a waste of money. Using thiamine to raise NAD+ is Black Swan-wise. [https://www.washingtonpost.com/life...c95704-07c4-11ea-818c-fcc65139e8c2\_story.html](https://www.washingtonpost.com/lifestyle/wellness/do-nad-boosting-supplements-fight-aging-not-according-to-current-research/2019/11/26/ffc95704-07c4-11ea-818c-fcc65139e8c2_story.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331797>

Methylene blue is capable of oxidizing NADH into NAD+ and increases the NAD+/NADH ratio, provided some redox metrics are intact. If they are not MB can be a problem. The key most mitohacks miss is not the ratio that matters but the fact that the H in NADH comes from the TCA cycle, and it must be H+ and not deuterium. If it is deuterium, the NADH/NAD+ couple at cytochrome one is destroyed, and this slows ECT speeds and ruins the Q-cycle as the respiratory proteins get further apart because of the atomic radius of deuterium compared to H+. Moreover, it tells you there is too much deuterium in the matrix indirectly and explains why ATP production is slowed and why there are unusual free radical signals being made. This is why Sinclair's Dec 2013 paper on NAD+ and pseudohypoxia are forever linked to aging and mitochondrial pathology. This does not mean I like or respect Sinclair. I think his 2013 paper was the only decent thing he has published in science. The rest is marketing to sell NAD supplements now that he exhausted the reservatrol train. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17928358](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpubmed%2F17928358%3Ffbclid%3DIwAR1Py1KI35m_hxnDrRSqhHxoBYxOZqndTud7AnMFZZjGQ3djV27A3Xi7Bf4&h=AT3jbRQsQD3vB5G0qFOLuM-0LDz9SNFRpkDadAFmOAhhPFnPeNwxwYGFfwZCMLYn1cBmgPiZNV42XPbg0yqO3Xa0FRn9Qt8OTia3uBFlSI8CSZpd0J-xapdP8XvjEedCajnF8f4&__tn__=-UK-R&c[0]=AT0yTSOR8mpZxDB-HzEOmmOXiF9ilsl3v50H0QsIgJhRQt34EmQhCJZZHZdPlUA5wjDTGct3C6-53H7PGM_jFFjnNYCr9X-RMAR1tq1mbTXS_Xvtrnrs6zQXF-_HLrn5eaVCqJQelW4cC0mTuwu2hfyE37OUECUXIWsS)

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1765781462706491669](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1765781462706491669)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331798>

Purines are a natural substance found in the body. They are also found in many foods such as liver, shellfish, and alcohol. They can also be formed in the body when DNA is broken down. When purines are broken down to uric acid in the blood, the body gets rid of it when you urinate or have a bowel movement. This removal process allows humans and birds to conserve mitochondrial water.

Parkinson's disease patients suffer from hypoxia of their pigmented neurons in the brain. This leads to melanin breakdown and lowered levels of endogenous dopamine. Uric acid, a metabolite of purine biochemistry protects neurons from melanin degeneration and dopamine loss. In primates, having a higher level of uric acid is protective of hypoxia in dopamine-producing neurons. It appears evolution knew what it was doing when it altered the uric acid pathways in the big-brained primate clade.

Parkinson's Disease (PD) patients have been found to have significantly lower levels of serum Uric Acid than controls (p = 0.000) in published research. This fact is not well-known in clinical medicine. In fact, in patients on L-dopa, the situation is even more dire and shows us why Big Pharma solutions often run contrary to nature's recipes. In patients with PD, serum UA levels were much lower in the groups under L-Dopa + dopamine agonist treatment. This means L-DOPA will accelerate PD disease.

### stel — 2024-02-22T17:18:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331800>

Thank you Dr Kruse. Curious how G6PD deficiency affects NAD+ ..

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:20:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331801>

All nnEMF use and abuse causes tissue levels of hypoxia.

Never forget the story of a Chris Kresser Institute patient who spent 147K in 2 years and never got better. His pic is below. Functional medicine done by an acupuncturist has risks.

When the patient arrived at my facility, I asked pointed questions about their work environment after looking in their ears. Upon looking in the ears, I saw cerumen accumulation on one side compared to the other, but there was significant erythema in both eardrums. He was also sensitive to artificial light I shined into his eyes. I removed the ear wax and noted the area underneath it was pale. I also noted the pinna and external auditory canal were very pale and devoid of pigment in some areas. I examined the skin around his head and neck and noticed some uneven melanosis in places.

Immediately, I asked about their use of cell phones, blue tooth, and nnEMF. Then I got my answer. The patient told me he used to use his cell phone to the ear 100% of the time, but then his ENT physician from California convinced him to use Apple's wireless earbuds because of his ear problems to avoid putting the cell phone to his affected ear. Within 3 months of using the ear pods, the patient's symptoms of mild tinnitus progressed to mild Meniere's disease with vertigo, hearing loss, and balance issues. This got worse when he shifted to nighttime consulting work. During this time, he developed abnormal white skin patches over his torso and legs.

NAD+ accepts two electrons and one proton to be reduced to NADH.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:33:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331808>

NAD+ is reduced using 2 hydrogen atoms. The two hydrogen atoms can come from one reactant in lactic acid fermentation or from two (in a reaction of glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate. Never forget that the human brain runs on lactate and not on glucose as centralized science beliefs and repeats in textbooks.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:35:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331809>

Phosphate, on its own, has one hydrogen at cytosolic pH because of the pKA. Thus in this reaction, there are two hydrogen atoms. One hydrogen atom is in the form of a hydride (a proton and two electrons) from the aldehyde group. This hydrogen atom is used to reduce NAD+ to NADH. A second hydrogen atom (in the form of a proton) comes from the phosphate. In the bisphosphoglycerate form, the proton dissociates from the phosphate group and is in solution.

The eventual fate of the electrons that were used to reduce NAD+ to NADH is the electron transport chain (in the mitochondria). In some cases, though, for example, muscle cells in an anaerobic environment or other species that don't have an electron transport chain, the electrons are used in fermentation of pyruvate to alcohol or lactic acid. When NADH donates its electrons in either case, it is then oxidized back to NAD+.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:40:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331810>

As you can see from the slide below, the reduction reaction transfers proton (H+) and hydride electrons (H- or 2e-) to NAD+. One electron goes to the nitrogen, and the other to the carbon, where the proton binds.

A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. # Thread: Focal Dystonia - where to start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/focal-dystonia-where-to-start.28631/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JPlessis — 2024-02-22T16:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/focal-dystonia-where-to-start.28631/post-331794>

Hello everyone,

My name is Jesse Plessis, I'm new here after listening to Dr. Kruse's episode of Tetragrammaton with Rick Rubin and Dr. Huberman, which was my first encounter with his ideas which Iinterested me very much.

I'm 36 years old, and in the autumn of 2020 I developed focal dystonia (as I was undergoing some pretty heavy life stresses, as seems to be the case often with this condition), which as you can imagine is very distressing for a pianist: my right hand cramps involuntarily when I try to play the piano (index finger pulls up, middle pulls toward the palm), and sometimes when I type, and my right knee, hip, lower back, scapula, shoulder, elbow, and jaw all have a baseline of co-contraction tension that is quite high. Experientially it feels like there are areas of my body I'm not in touch with, or can't feel, or have a confused relationship with.

I have tried neurofeedback on the advice of a neurologist (who took an EEG and some questionnaires and told me I had C-PTSD that she suspected was linked to the dystonia, which seems to follow logically from a pretty rough start on this planet), 8 months of weekly brainspotting therapy, 6 months of compassionate inquiry therapy, 5 months of hypnotherapy, and I have been retraining movement at the piano for two years with a student of Dorothy Taubmann (a New York pianist who was well known for her wise physical approach to piano playing).

I have also made some big lifestyle changes: reducing emotional stressors, exercising six days a week (including swimming, running, bodyweight, yoga, and kung-fu), eating a whole foods diet, and taking care of my sleep including yoga nidra several times weekly. I'm feeling much better in general, and I would definitely say that I'm not living with trauma anymore (at least not in a way that affects my daily life in an extremely negative way as before).

I have made some progress in dystonia recovery, and have regained a lot of my previous dexterity and reduced my performance anxiety quite a lot, but it is still present enough to encumber my relationship with music and my ability to be my best.

I will post this in another appropriate forum to have guidance and feedback from the other members on this site, but if you could supply any blog posts, protocols, readings, books, and other avenues that would be particularly relevant and best to start with I would really appreciate it.

Thank you very much for your cameraderie and support - I get a good vibe from the posts I've been reading. And take care of yourselves!
Jesse

### Esg999 — 2024-02-24T15:46:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/380nm-light-bulb.28637/post-331891>

I know using lamps/hacks is not recommended here, and there is no replacement for the sun, but thought I’d ask if anyone has experience with this bulb in combo with some IR/Red panel and natural sunlight (not for vit D, for melanin renovation). I know the wattage is low but could buy a few. It shows the spectrum and includes a lot of 380. Assuming they have too much flicker due to being LED?

### [UVA Grow Light: Ideal for Indoor Plant Growth Needs](https://forevergreenindoors.com/products/fgi-uva-385nm-led-bar-single)

Illuminate your indoor garden with the UVA Grow Light, built for durability and optimal plant growth. Order now and see the results thrive!

 forevergreenindoors.com

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-24T19:58:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/380nm-light-bulb.28637/post-331903>

A 30watt LED light puts out the equivalent light as a 240watt incandescent bulb. They vary but LED are about 8x more efficient. Not all LED’s flicker, just have to test.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-24T20:55:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/380nm-light-bulb.28637/post-331912>

Remember 42% of sunlight is IR and only about 5% UVA (depends on location/time of year etc). IF you want to try to duplicate full spectrum natural light - keep these ratio’s in mind. Some “full spectrum” LED grow, aquarium, reptile lights have IR and UV built in.

# Thread: Morphogenesis links to mitochondria via light.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-25T04:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morphogenesis-links-to-mitochondria-via-light.28641/post-331935>

Morphogenesis links to mitochondria via light.

The DC electric current is the intermediary that does the morphogenesis and regeneration job in cells.

The TCA cycle is one level of energy generation in cells whose stochiometry cannot account for the ATP made in a cell. So what processes in man provide the rest? Semiconduction and melanin biology are two others. In total, I believe there 4 others system that help the TCA cycle keep the ATPase spinning.

When we lose metabolic water in our colony of mitochondria, we lose superconduction in nanotubes, time speeds up for life, and it first ages quickly and then dies. This is the key metric to TBI and many collagen vascular diseases. This is one of the cornerstone mechanisms of how all neolithic diseases today are created. When water is part of a semi-conductive and coherent energy transfer system, there is little or no free diffusion in such a system. It has a zero entropy. A biophysicist named Herbert Fröhlich pointed out in the 1960s that energy transfer by using water’s molecular resonance or coherent excitations has to occur through large distances, activating entire populations of similar molecules that are in different parts of the cell or different parts of the body, so long-range coordinating of function can happen instantaneously. Proton jump-conduction occurs along the long fibers of collagen and the water bound to it and belongs to a form of semi-conduction in the condensed matter called a liquid crystal. This allows for instantaneous action within the living organism. This proton conduction is much faster than the conduction of electrical signals by the nerves in the peripheral nervous system. The collagen fibril cytoarchitecture of the entire body has a much better intercommunication system than can be provided by the peripheral nervous system alone. That may help explain the observations of why lower animals, like a hydra, that do not have a nervous system are nonetheless sensitive and responsive.

Physics considered Fröhlich ideas radical in the 1960s because instrumentation was so poor it could not prove his theories. Most of his theories are true today because our testing technology is far superior. In QED, there is something called the [Bose-Einstein Condensate.](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9RDocoSWqPY) Fröhlich believed that living biological systems are “open” and very far from equilibrium and have considerable amounts of energy available, built into their design, that could cause non-linear changes in molecules and larger biological subsystems. He was the first biophysicist to believe that biology works at a distance because it too was far from equilibrium, and he felt it might work like the Bose-Einstein condensate principles in quantum electrodynamic theory (QED). He proposed long-range coherent action in living tissues in 1967 at the L’Institut de la Vie.

This idea was backed up fully by Becker, who confirmed that a body plan charge polarization that seemed to direct regeneration and healing was found in all the animals he tested. Water chemistry is key to this mechanism. It won't surprise me if it is holographic and light trapped in the living state by the delta psi on the mitochondrial membrane built by solar exposure controls everything.

[https://www.cell.com/current-biology/fulltext/S0960-9822(20)31073-3](https://www.cell.com/current-biology/fulltext/S0960-9822\(20\)31073-3)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-25T04:16:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morphogenesis-links-to-mitochondria-via-light.28641/post-331936>

When you read Turing paper on morphogenesis things really heat up.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-25T15:04:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morphogenesis-links-to-mitochondria-via-light.28641/post-331939>

The TCA is like water.....it is not homogenous in all tissues. Water networks are not always H20, as the textbooks say.

Why must we see the sunrise? Sunlight must hit the membrane that houses Kreb's cycle to get it to spin correctly to innovate life. The TCA cycle has different carbon and hydrogen movements in different tissues. This means the TCA cycle is highly dynamic in different tissues, but this point is never amplified in biochemical texts. If the TCA does not spin properly, disease or death results. It is that simple. What does sunlight do to Kreb's cycle? Every sunrise, they all spin in a clockwise direction in unison to remove H+ and move carbon from the chemical intermediates to supply the mitochondrial matrix with its fuel source. Istopic LIGHT Hydrogen. Mitochondria are solar hydrogen furnaces.

Lipogenesis occurs in most tissues to maintain organelle membranes and growth. Still, it is most active in the liver and adipose during feeding to convert excess carbohydrates to lipids for long-term calorie storage. The circadian biology of the skin control liver TCA dynamics. Lipogenesis occurs in the cytosol through the condensation of consecutive units of acetyl-CoA, most of which is derived from mitochondrial PDH during carbohydrate metabolism. For mitochondrial acetyl-CoA to be trafficked to the cytosol, it is first condensed with OAA to generate citrate in the first step of the TCA cycle, and it is then actively transported by the citrate-isocitrate carrier (CiC). Cytosolic ATP–citrate lyase regenerates OAA and acetyl-CoA in the cytosol. These steps remove citrate from mitochondria and require continuous anaplerosis to maintain lipogenesis.

Interestingly, in vitro characterization of the CiC indicates that it couples citrate transport with malate antiport. Thus, cytosolic OAA could be converted to malate and exchanged back into the mitochondria with no further carbon transitions, obviating the need for other anaplerotic support. However, loss of CiC causes decreased TCA cycle pool size and lactate accumulation in cell models and humans, suggesting that citrate transport is critical to anaplerotic and cataplerotic homeostasis. It also explains why lack of AM sunlight leads to fatty liver states. Indeed, NAFLD subjects have elevated lipogenesis, coincident with increased hepatic anaplerosis and citrate synthase flux. Isotopic tracer studies indicate that citrate lyase is a significant source of OAA used by PEPCK-C. This pathway contributes to glycerol-3-phosphate for fatty acid esterification via the glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase pathway and is particularly important in adipose tissue, which lacks glycerol kinase.

Insulin is a solar hormone.

During obesity, type 2 diabetes (T2DM), and nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), insulin fails to suppress cataplerotic PEPCK flux to gluconeogenesis. However, PEPCK-C is not overexpressed in all rodent models of diet-induced obesity or in humans with T2DM.m PEPCK seems to be deactivated by deuterium content in the diet or allowed access to the metabolic pathways of man by its high KIE

## ANAPLEROSIS AND CATAPLEROSIS differ in skeletal muscle​

# Thread: Achilles tears.....

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-26T17:20:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/achilles-tears.28644/post-331957>

Why are there so many Achilles tears in NFL players today? Most wear ungrounded shoes and never get solar exposure over their calves where the skin has POMC. Bulk water is a dipole, and dipoles have to remain with a neutral charge. Water becomes a liquid crystal when sunlight hits it. Crystals are capable of nonlinear functions. Liquid crystals elevate the ability to use certain nonlinear processes like second harmonic generation (SHG). SHG in collagen is an exciting science. In the 1970s, G.H. Bardelmeyer found this electric ability in the Achilles tendons of animals. He found the ability was tied to free proton water electricity from water and light. This is why Achilles' tears are always a sign of poor connections to the sun and Earth at some level. His results make sense in the recent observations made in experiments that proton superconduction occurs in nanospace-confined water below 1.6 nm. Water at this scale does not act like modern fluid flow dynamics believes. I cover this in the May 2017 webinar for the skeptics. The critical discovery of superfast quantum tunneling in chiral water polymers was vital. Collagen is also chiral. Around chiral collagen, water hydrates surround this molecule, and you can see how the body plan is built in animals and can be 100% light-activated and controlled.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-27T18:34:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-civilization-fail.28645/post-332002>

Anyone looking for proof that the medical establishment has gone mad need look no further than the University of California at San Francisco. Two weeks ago, an Adjunct Assistant Professor of Medical Education at Mayo Clinic College of Medicine and Science and a guest UCSF faculty member told a 300-person audience that white people are racist because they are genetically predisposed to psychopathy. Need more? How about Dr. Sherita Golden, a professor of medicine and vice president at Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine, who sent out a mass email last month with a list of identities to repent for, including: “white,” “able-bodied,” “heterosexual,” “cisgender,” “male,” “Christian,” “middle or owning-class,” “middle-aged,” and “English-speaking.”

Public outrage led Dr. Golden to apologize and delete her X account. Oh, and this past November, the American Psychiatric Association published Gender-Affirming Psychiatric Care. This ostensibly definitive textbook offered politicized polemics such as “scientific neutrality is a fallacy”, denounced the “mythical man-woman binary”, and used the words “oppress” 60 times, “power” 62 times, “victim” 63 times, “intersectional” 62 times, and “queer” 83 times.

Nearly 7,000 healthcare professionals signed an open letter calling the work unethical and unsafe. What is going on? Once a conservative-to-moderate profession, medicine in America has swung left – very left. And this politicization is alienating mainline America and undermining health care. The transition has come fast. Data from Stanford political scientists Adam Bonica and colleagues shows that over the past 20 years, medicine has gone from 60% Republican to 80% Democrat. This shift is still underway.

### [How America’s medical system has become totally woke](https://nypost.com/2024/02/17/opinion/americas-medical-system-has-become-totally-woke/)

In barely two generations, American medical care has gone from being staffed predominantly conservative practitioners to those who are mostly liberal.

 nypost.com

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T07:42:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334590>

A song can sounds like heaven.......yet it can burn us like hell, why?

Our brains are wired to associate certain songs with specific events, people, or experiences in our lives. These associations can evoke strong emotions, either positive or negative. For example, a song that was playing during a happy moment might evoke feelings of joy and nostalgia, while a song that was playing during a traumatic event might trigger feelings of anxiety or fear. Music has an incredible ability to tap into our emotions, memories, and experiences. When a song resonates with our emotional state, it can be euphoric, making us feel like we're in heaven. Conversely, if a song triggers painful memories or emotions, it can be like a burning fire, causing discomfort and distress.

Music can be incredibly vulnerable, exposing our deepest fears, desires, and vulnerabilities. When we listen to a song that speaks to these vulnerabilities, it can be both cathartic and uncomfortable, like walking through a fiery inferno. Music creates an intimacy with our Nature.
Music can create a sense of intimacy, connecting us to the artist, the lyrics, and the emotions conveyed. When this connection is strong, it can be exhilarating and uplifting, but when it's not what we expected or wanted, it can be painful and disappointing.

Music can be a powerful tool for catharsis, allowing us to process and release pent-up emotions. When we listen to a song that captures our emotions accurately, it can be a liberating experience, like a weight being lifted off our shoulders. Music can be a powerful trigger for memories, both good and bad. When we hear a song that's associated with a painful memory, it can be like reliving the experience all over again, which can be emotionally overwhelming. The context in which we listen to music can greatly influence how we perceive it. For example, listening to a happy song during a difficult time might provide comfort and solace, while listening to the same song during a happy moment might be annoying or overplayed

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T07:45:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334591>

Is there a hidden law of a probable outcome, as Sting wrote in his music?

1.  **The Butterfly Effect**: This concept, popularized by chaos theory, suggests that small changes in initial conditions can lead to drastically different outcomes. While not a direct prediction of a probable outcome, it highlights the sensitivity of complex systems to initial conditions.
2.  **The Monte Carlo method**: This numerical technique uses random sampling to estimate probabilities and predict outcomes. While not a deterministic "law," it's a powerful tool for modeling complex systems.
3.  **The concept of entropy**: In thermodynamics and information theory, entropy measures the uncertainty or disorder in a system. While not directly predicting outcomes, entropy can be used to understand the probability distributions of certain events.
4.  **The concept of emergence**: This idea suggests that complex systems can exhibit properties that are not predictable from the behavior of their individual components. Emergence can lead to unexpected outcomes, but it's not a direct prediction of a probable outcome.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T08:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334593>

When we look at someone's eyes, we can sense that they are looking into ours, and sometimes it feels like they can see into our very soul. This phenomenon is often referred to as "eye contact" or "gazing." It's as if our eyes are open doors, allowing others to peer into our inner world.

There are several reasons why this might happen:

1.  **Emotional resonance**: When we're deeply engaged with someone, our emotions can synchronize, creating a sense of empathy and connection. This emotional resonance can make us feel like the other person is "looking into" our eyes, even if they're not physically doing so.
2.  **Nonverbal communication**: Our eyes are incredibly expressive, conveying emotions like happiness, sadness, fear, or excitement. When we're in a situation where we're feeling vulnerable or exposed, it can feel like our eyes are "open doors" to others' perceptions.
3.  **Psychological intuition**: Some people may possess a natural ability to pick up on subtle cues from others, such as body language, tone of voice, or facial expressions. This intuition can lead them to feel like they're "looking into" someone's eyes, even if they're not consciously aware of it.
4.  **Attention and focus**: When we're fully engaged with someone, our attention and focus are directed towards them. This concentrated attention can create the illusion that our eyes are "open doors," allowing others to see into our inner world.
5.  **Synchronicity**: Sometimes, the timing and synchronization of eye contact can create a sense of connection or shared understanding. When we're looking at someone who's looking at us, it can feel like our eyes are "open doors" to a deeper level of understanding.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T08:18:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334594>

Mother Nature seems to allow life on Earth to swing on the spiral of our divinity and still be a human.

I'm touching on the idea that our existence on Earth is deeply connected to our spiritual nature, and that our humanity is an integral part of the natural world. It's as if we're part of a larger, intricate web of life that is infused with divinity. In many spiritual and philosophical traditions, the concept of "divinity" is not limited to a specific deity or supernatural being, but rather refers to the inherent, sacred quality of existence itself. This perspective suggests that everything in the universe, including human beings, is imbued with a spark of the divine light.

From this perspective, the idea of "swinging on the spiral arm of the galaxy to sample our divinity" is particularly evocative. It implies that our lives are part of a larger, cyclical pattern that is connected to the rhythms of the natural world. The spiral shape is often associated with growth, transformation, and evolution, suggesting that our journey as silly talking monkeys is characterized by a continuous process of unfolding and self-discovery.

The idea also highlights the idea that our humanity is not separate from nature, but rather an integral part of it. This perspective is often referred to as "ecosophy" or "deep ecology," which emphasizes the interconnectedness of all living beings and the importance of preserving the natural world.

By recognizing our place within the natural world and our connection to the divine, we can cultivate a deeper sense of reverence, respect, and responsibility for the planet and all its inhabitants. This understanding can inspire us to live more harmoniously with nature, to prioritize sustainability and environmental stewardship, and to strive for personal growth and spiritual development.

# Thread: Carrageenan is a problem in Franken foods
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carrageenan-is-a-problem-in-franken-foods.28650/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-29T00:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carrageenan-is-a-problem-in-franken-foods.28650/post-332035>

Lots of people have complained about carrageenan as a food additive. I've never found a good reason to be concerned about it, but this is concerning. In this model, carrageenan (CAR) primes white blood cells to overreact to infection (represented by LPS). "Our results demonstrate that activated leukocytes produce a large amount of TNF-a and that this plays an important role in the pathogenesis of liver injury in the CAR-pretreated endotoxin shock model." That could certainly explain the intestinal upset some report. "Carrageenan Primes Leukocytes to Enhance Lipopolysaccharide \[LPS\]-Induced Tumor Necrosis Factor Alpha Production" [http://doi.org/10.1128/IAI.67.7.3284-3289.1999](https://t.co/4pvNKmbirq)

# Thread: EYE FLOATERS:  WHAT IS THE DECENTRALIZED PERSPECTIVE?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-floaters-what-is-the-decentralized-perspective.28655/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-02T15:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-floaters-what-is-the-decentralized-perspective.28655/post-332125>

Blue Light is Giving You Eye Floaters More and more young people are reporting eye floaters. Why? Because Blue light accelerates vitreous degeneration in the eye! The vitreous has a lot of melanopsin it > This leads to vitreous opacity and increased floaters. "While blue light helps to maintain normal biological rhythms, excessive blue light radiation may induce fundus lesions." Studies show Vitreous floaters are higher among digital device users. Protect your vision. Limit screen time and wear blue light blockers. Study: [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32244150/](https://t.co/3sWk9ZcWTb)

travels to Equator, should he spend lots of time looking toward the sun?

# Thread: Ladies your reproductive system ages at a different rate than you do.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-03T17:04:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332161>

### 1 in 6 people globally affected by infertility. The number is close to 2.5 out of six in Americans. I consider infertility in humans as I would consider a mutation in DNA or mutant disease. Today we know we can rescue fruit flies from their genetic fate. This implies that light maybe behind why humans are becoming infertile at record rates in a modern world that is blue lit, filled with RF and microwaves pulse radiation while blocking the healing rays of the sun in the visible spectrum.​

. The reproductive tract of women is one of the first organ systems to show hallmarks of ageing, in comparison to other organs.

Difficulty conceiving and infertility are treated as taboo topics in most nations and lead to significant psychological stress for those experiencing them ([Patel et al., 2018](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0670)). Although assisted reproductive technologies (ART) such as [cryopreservation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/cryopreservation)(the freezing of oocytes, sperm, or embryos) and _in vitro_ fertilization exist, they are not ubiquitously available or successful and require substantial financial investment ([Katz et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0455)). Furthermore, a fertilized oocyte derived from a woman of advanced age has a higher chance of resulting in miscarriage, and/or aneuploid offspring like [trisomy](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/trisomy) of [chromosome 21](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/chromosome-21), commonly known as Down syndrome ([Bittles et al., 2007](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0105)).

With advancing age, a decline in mitochondrial number and function is observed in oocytes. Mitochondria are exclusively maternally inherited and therefore the original population present in the oocyte will give rise to all future mitochondria in the offspring ([Jansen and de Boer, 1998](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0415)). Thus, elucidating the mechanisms underlying the loss of mitochondrial function with ageing and why oocytes age much earlier as compared to other organ systems, may lead to new therapeutic strategies to prolong oocyte fitness and fertility.

nnEMF ages your oocytes faster than it ages you.

Hard truth here.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T03:30:37+0200
