# Thread: Making Change Happen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-change-happen.28834/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2024-04-30T19:41:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-change-happen.28834/post-334141>

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-334020](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-334020)

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I want your message and incredible findings to find as many people as possible. Although helpful for certain people, institutions, pharma, etc, your tone comes across very aggressive and most people cannot handle or do not trust such an approach. I understand that med school is hardcore and we have some serious issues to fix that require brute force, but attacking people who want to help and understand (and who have massive other commitments other than deciphering every message you've ever posted on all of the internet) is counter-productive.

I don't mind being pushed as I enjoy constructive ruthless criticism, but go so far to call people lazy (I have worked my ass off my entire life to get to where I am, including teaching myself 10 programming languages) and be so aggressive when you don't even know them is a bit too far.

“Anger dwells only in the bosom of fools.” - Albert Einstein

I've been digesting and evaluating your teachings and work for 2 years now, and still am trying to find all the pieces to the puzzle, because I have a (self-employed) job that takes much of my time so I can even afford avoiding most EMF and live somewhere with high solar energy. I also didn't go through med school and have had to learn every approach to research and changing my previous mindset along this journey. I and most others have a long way to go in this area. Most people are not as lucky as me with free time, nor as open to aggressive feedback. This is illustrated by the reactions of many people I show your content to (in an effort to get them on board with your concepts and findings), who compare your aggressive tone to the yelling Paul Saladino employs in his videos. It turns a lot of people off to the content.

I believe you grew up in NYC, and I can't imagine you will agree with me on this because of that personal n=1 experience, but my experience has been most of the public will listen to perceived "level-headed" content which comes across most easily as calm and collected (stoicism). I know you're pissed as fuck at our system and I am too, but we can't create large enough change for the people to rise up unless we are approachable to as many of them as possible, while still conveying high quality information and passion. Passion coming across as anger sounds defensive to many people and I don't want your message to be drowned out by that.

I'd be sad to get banned for this post but I want to share this with you because two of your biggest fans and followers (myself and my friend finishing up med school) both see this as the biggest inhibitor to the distribution of this knowledge and your message.

Additionally if you are trying to share your knowledge and mindset approach (nature being the key) on podcasts and social media to get people to see this, share it, and change the world to get rid of our lackluster approach to technology adoption and usage, we don't need every person wasting their time researching all the same stuff. I get it - this stuff is valuable and was hard-found, so you want people to put effort in and work for it. However, most people are not even curious like me and don't want to do all this research because they only care about the end goal, actionable tasks, and over-arching concepts. I am trying to convey as much of this information as possible to other people and my community to get this shit out there, and for you to suggest someone like me is lazy after 2 years of research is pretty frustrating. Your content takes easily 5+ years to absorb, re-read, and fully understand. I have a good grasp on everything, and I wanted to get some additional 5G information from anyone on the forum who can help to spread this message to our communities, as I was requesting.

I personally like your approach in every way except the aggression that I believe should be coming out as a more constructive version of passion.

# Thread: Another new member, From NW Florida. I'm so EXCITED! Long intro - sorry!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-new-member-from-nw-florida-im-so-excited-long-intro-sorry.28835/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MaggieMiller — 2024-04-30T21:40:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-new-member-from-nw-florida-im-so-excited-long-intro-sorry.28835/post-334144>

72yo retired RN, ER/Trauma the last 3 decades. I chafed at the centralized healthcare system since my very first job. When I was 23, I tried to organize the nurses and the DON called each nurse, one by one, into her office to make sure they would NOT attend the meeting. I was asked to resign. I did and returned working for an agency for more than the hospital paid me. Needless to say, I never rose in the ranks to managerial positions. Management was forced to utilize me when they computerized, b/c I had a masters in computer science, but I didn't keep doing that b/c it was easy to see the EMRs is just a massive billing machine with a little bit of patient care thrown on top. It directs EVERYTHING clinicians do, for the benefit of the profiteers.

I'm a single mom again, raising 2 grandchildren I adopted. I joined here because the past 6 years with the kids has taken a toll on me physically. A recent cross-country move caused some sort of depletion that left me a shell of myself. Labwork from a PCP (who I will never see again, but she did order routine labs) is all okay, but she had no interest in addressing my physical exhaustion. I took bovine adrenals from a health food store one day and woke the next day feeling almost like myself again, so now I'm on a mission to heal myself, b/c obviously my body is out of whack.

I wish I had time to research everything, but in order to raise 2 kids with PTSD in a world where everything is poisoned takes TIME. I cook a lot, drive to farms to get my food, have chickens and am working on my new garden. I have almost eliminated all processed foods from the house. It's been gradual, because the kids did better with a slow transition. Everything costs money, and we're living on my Social Security, so I can only do what I can do. I have amber light bulbs in our bedrooms. I get them off electronics a few hours before bedtime. We go to the beach several times a week, no matter the weather. Today we will probably go in the rain.

I try to rise above supplements and the endless micro-managing of biological processes I don't really understand by using my biggest skill. I have meditated over 50 years and now I find it pretty easy to slip into the God zone, connect with source, the Flow, whatever you call it. I do QiGong Microcosmic orbit every morning as the sun rises, outside with bare feet or socks in Winter. I use the Gateway Tapes a few times a week (the CIA connection leads me to take is slowly so I can listen to my gut before I proceed to the next level).I do breathwork. I attend sound therapy when I can find it (harder here than when we lived in Montana). But I stop to meditate in some fashion 2-3 times a day. I feel like I don't have a choice. It feels mandatory. Energy, Light, Vibration. It's why I was attracted to Jack in the first place - he knows about all of that and he's trying to explain it scientifically. I listen to his podcasts every day, trying to learn more and de-program the nurse part of my brain.

I'm still TIRED by mid-afternoon - more tired than I was when I was a mom in my 30s, and that's the physical thing I'm here to address. I want to find out what happened physically to me in the 6 years since I emergently got custody of my grandchildren. We were in crisis mode for so long that I didn't even notice it happening. I'm well-trained to push through crises because it's ingrained in ER/Trauma. But now we are settled and the kids are both older and mentally better, I'm ready to let the "pushing through" go. I want to rest and still have energy to do all the things I want to do. I'm sure some of it's light/outdoors. I hiked a lot, floated down rivers for hours & did Tai Chi outside almost daily before I got the kids. There HAS to be a way, and I'm here to find it.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-01T01:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-new-member-from-nw-florida-im-so-excited-long-intro-sorry.28835/post-334150>

Welcome aboard. I love nurses.

### MaggieMiller — 2024-05-01T05:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-new-member-from-nw-florida-im-so-excited-long-intro-sorry.28835/post-334156>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=334150)
>
> Welcome aboard. I love nurses.

And ER nurses love neurosurgeons, because they sign on to work crazy shifts under less than ideal circumstances, and we can't run trauma centers without you. Neurosurgeons are warriors. But I laugh so hard during your interviews when you get brusque. "Yep! He's a neurosurgeon!" When my mom needed a neurosurgeon, I took her to an asshole. I knew his sister, so I knew he was actually a truly nice man outside of work, but AT work, he was meticulously precise man with zero communication skills. You don't NEED a neurosurgeon to be warm and fuzzy.

# Thread: High UV triggering autoimmune?

### Flynn Jones — 2024-05-02T20:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334224>

Hi,

This may seem a little odd but I am going to put it out there anyway.

I have been following all of JK's work and managed to stabilise my autoimmune disease. I am making it a priority to build my solar callus and get away as much as possible.

What I have noticed is when I spend time in areas of lower latitude, such as Lanzarote, my mouth ulcers start to come back. My theory is that the increased melanocyte stimulation is causing an almost herx/detox reaction. Perhaps It's the deuterium shedding?

Despite flaring up it still makes me feel better, I tan very well and no longer burn.

Wondering if anyone else has experienced similar?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-02T21:53:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334227>

When the redox sucks yes. You can added some bicarb use to improve ferroptosis regeneration

### Flynn Jones — 2024-05-03T14:26:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334247>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=334227)
>
> When the redox sucks yes. You can added some bicarb use to improve ferroptosis regeneration

Great news! I’ll get on the san pellegrino with a bit of baking soda today.

Thanks Doc

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-03T21:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334255>

Why does it work for AI's? It fixes the "Proton problem" in mitochondria. Ya' ever hear of bicarb and carbonic anhydrase? The carbonic anhydrases or carbonate dehydratases enzymes are experts at moving protons to and fro form a family of enzymes that catalyze the rapid interconversion of carbon dioxide and water to bicarbonate and protons (or vice versa). Making water and CO2 is exactly what a HEALTHY mitochondrion does and it is exactly what an AUTOIMMUNE condition comes from. When will we learn? This is a reversible reaction that occurs relatively slowly in the absence of a catalyst. Baking soda is that catalyst. The active site of most carbonic anhydrases contains a zinc ion; they are therefore classified as metalloenzymes. This means nnEMF can affect this transition metal so we have another 5G mitohack.

### [Drinking baking soda could be an inexpensive, safe way to combat autoimmune disease: study](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2018-04-soda-inexpensive-safe-combat-autoimmune.html)

A daily dose of baking soda may help reduce the destructive inflammation of autoimmune diseases like rheumatoid arthritis, scientists say.

 medicalxpress.com

Making water and CO2 is exactly what a HEALTHY mitochondrion does and it is exactly what an AUTOIMMUNE condition comes from. When will we learn? The active site of most carbonic anhydrases contains a zinc ion; they are therefore classified as metalloenzymes. This means nnEMF can affect this transition metal so we have another 5G mitohack.

# Thread: Is it possible that photons actually have mass?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-03T22:04:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334257>

I am reading through the Time series currently for the first time and there is a lot of talk about the momentum of light. I just can't wrap my head around the idea of something having momentum, but no mass. By the equation for momentum, if mass is zero, momentum would be zero.

I've had a feeling that there is a possibility that photons had mass ever since I taught about atomic spectra for the first time many years ago. Thought that maybe we just lacked the instrumentation to detect it bc of how infinitesimally small it likely is (if it exists at all)

Just wondering the thoughts of others. Am I way off base? On to something?

### Brent Patrick — 2024-05-05T03:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334291>

### [How does gravity affect photons (that is, bend light) if photons have no mass? | Astronomy.com](https://www.astronomy.com/science/how-does-gravity-affect-photons-that-is-bend-light-if-photons-have-no-mass/)

categories:Science | tags:Astrophysics, Magazine

 www.astronomy.com

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-05T04:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334294>

Momentum is an energy concept. Not just a mass content. Consider this slide from Vermont 2018

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-06T16:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334348>

I see

E² = p²c² + m²c⁴

By this eqn from Einstein, mass can be zero and there can still exist a relationship between energy and momentum

Seems the momentum eqn, p = mv, as it is taught in grade school, is incomplete bc it doesn't account for energy momentum. I was hung up on this basic eqn (p = mv) bc I thought it was complete and true in all cases

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-07T05:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334380>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=334348)
>
> I see
>
> E² = p²c² + m²c⁴
>
> By this eqn from Einstein, mass can be zero and there can still exist a relationship between energy and momentum
>
> Seems the momentum eqn, p = mv, as it is taught in grade school, is incomplete bc it doesn't account for energy momentum. I was hung up on this basic eqn (p = mv) bc I thought it was complete and true in all cases

not imcomplete. That is macro physics and mass. Photons are quantum mechanical and quantum mechanics has new ways of dealing with momentum of photons.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-07T16:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334389>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=334380)
>
> not imcomplete. That is macro physics and mass. Photons are quantum mechanical and quantum mechanics has new ways of dealing with momentum of photons.

Ah, okay. So, both eqns are true and complete. They are just intended for use in different scenarios. Thanks for the clarification

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-09T19:10:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334524>

# Thread: Thoughts on this grounding device?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 73
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ajohnson90 — 2024-05-05T16:55:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-334300>

I know the optimal way to ground is to… ground. Barefoot on the earth, get back to nature.

As part of my attempt to improve my health I got job outdoors so I can spend more time outdoors than indoors, but the big problem I’m running into is that I’m shoe shod all day long.

Earth Runners, a minimal shoe company, just released this “earthing” kit and I’m wondering what everyone’s thoughts are?

### Osprey1981 — 2024-10-07T22:36:29+0300

After reading Ober's book on "Earthing" it is my opinion that this statement above by Anthony Smith is invalid! Grounding is grounding!!!
The very time you would benefit the most from "Grounding/Earthing" is when you are in any environment that is highly impacted by the emf fields caused by all kinds of equipment and appliances that cause the radiated environment.

After reading all of Mr. Smith's comments on under this subject of "grounding" (earthing) I've gone back are reviewed various comments made by Clinton Ober and in the "Second Edition" of his BOOK ... ("Earthing - The most important health discovery ever!").
I must repeat my comment above! "Grounding is Grounding"! When one connects a grounding mat or grounding sheet to the "Grounding" plug on your bedroom wall, you are NOT connecting to a part of an AC electrical system, you are clearly connecting the mat or sheet to "Mother Earth".... just as if you used a longer wire and fastened it to a "Grounding Rod" that you may have hammered into the ground outside your home. If you just let the 50 ft of wire hang out your 2nd story window, it would indeed act as an antenna, but the instant that wire is connected to the earthed grounding rod it is no longer an antenna, it is a grounding wire! And thus, when you lay down on your "ground-connected" mat or sheet, you get the grounding benefits that you would get if you laid down in the grass where your grounding rod is affixed in your yard.

The earth is an "unlimited" source of negative electrons that are absorbed into the human body immediately upon one becoming "reconnected" to the earth. It restores your body's natural internal electrical stability and rhythms. It remedies an electron deficiency to reduce inflammation, the common cause of disease.

The are some confusing thing said about grounding and the possible need to be away from powered equipment, etc., when you want to be grounded. You should understand that the earth is "ground".... You don't have to go far away to find a place to reconnect with "ground"! Try our own back yard! And, the "resistors" used in the mats and sheets are the correct resistance levels..... they are well designed.

This has been looked into and may be ~ 60% and up to 80% of primary current flow back to earth. ### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-08T21:51:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-340637>

> [Osprey1981 said:](/goto/post?id=340560)
>
> After reading Ober's book on "Earthing" it is my opinion that this statement above by Anthony Smith is invalid! Grounding is grounding!!!
> The very time you would benefit the most from "Grounding/Earthing" is when you are in any environment that is highly impacted by the emf fields caused by all kinds of equipment and appliances that cause the radiated environment.

All the videos I have seen that are selling Earthing/grounding products use a body voltage meter. They get a reading then touch the ground pad and the reading goes down, often to zero. They then claim the ground pad removes the nnEMF field. NOT TRUE the field remains and now YOU are part of the circuit, an AC circuit.

The field follows the path of least resistance to ground. When you are ungrounded that path is only thru the meter, giving an indication of the strength of the field. When you touch the grounding pad you are double grounded and the field is now going to ground thru the pad NOT the meter. The field you are is stays the same. If you had a bedroom reading of 1500mV (not unusual) with a grounding pad you STILL have 1500mV but now you are part of the circuit and rather than just being IN the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field it is now flowing thru you - all night, PLUS any additional ground affects that are part of your house and neighborhood electrical system. Think of a bird on a high power line “unaffected” because they are NOT grounded.

We advise people to get rid of metal bed frames, metal coil mattresses and not wear metal - WHY would you attach yourself to your house wiring? So you throw a 50ft wire out the window and ground outside . . . Now you are attached to a 50ft antenna.

Good Earthing/grounding requires bare feet ON the Earth/ground (direct physical contact) away from buildings and AC fields. To connect your bodies DC electrical circuit to natures DC circuit requires being IN nature and away from AC fields - Your house “ground” is part of an AC electrical system and a wire out the window is an antenna. I get calls all the time from people who spent the $, tried grounding but got worse - this is why. Just turn off the 60Hz (50Hz) field in your bedroom and get outside and Earth/ground IN Nature.

Just a thought -
In home grounding = lots of $ - Grounding in Nature = no $
It’s kind of like thinking “light is light” and artificial light is as good as natural light . . .

### Emily Burgoon D.O. — 2024-10-08T22:15:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-340638>

I had a friend who's a sound engineer recently tell me he dissected one of the grounding pads from the company that puts the earthing book out. First he tested it and found that there was no conduction from the pad to the grounding prong that's supposed to go in the outlet. Then he took it apart and found that there was a hidden resistor embedded in the plug or cord that was too strong to allow any current through. Apparently it's the only way to make the device compliant with regulations, but it also makes the device completely ineffective for its advertised purpose.

Grounding makes you part of the circuit - outside with your bidy in contact with the ground is good BUT inside attached to a wire while in a 50/60Hz field NOT good. Create a nnEMF sleep sanctuary while inside by turning OFF bedroom circuits and ground OUTSIDE with your body on the ground NOT attached to an antenna.

In my experience the people it works for are low on the health scale and it can help them improve, somewhat. For people who are high on the health scale it does not help them improve but can knock them back (this is very general). It also depends on what nnEMF is going on in their space.

Everything affects everyone in different ways. We all have different backgrounds and exposures and sensitivities. Try the grounding while you watch the sun.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-02T06:27:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-346109>

> [Sunny Day said:](/goto/post?id=346085)
>
> What do you mean by double grounded in this context? I've looked up what double grounding means but the definition doesn't seem to correspond with the act of indoor grounding when you are connected to a grounding rod outside. thanks

Electric fields are induced into the body and a body voltage meter works by giving the field a path of least resistance to ground. As it goes to ground through the ground cord the meter measures its strength. If you are double grounded some, if not all, of the field goes though the second ground NOT the meter, so the meter does not give the correct reading.

Any cord that filters out AC ( because it has a resistor in it) also STOPS the negative ions from the ground coming back up from the ground. This makes grounding inside via a wire (with a resistor) a total waste of time. If you want the advantages of grounding (negative ions from the ground) you NEED to be outside in contact with the ground.

Hi Anthony, I did some research on this. Note that I'm from Belgium so different outlets / systems than in the US.

I have a grounding mat which claims to only connect to the grounding system. If your home is configured correctly, the ground wire is different from the AC hot and neutral wires.

Therefore, I can even turn the breaker circuit off, and still enjoy the grounding benefits because it does not rely on electricity.

The mat functions correctly because I feel tingling in my feet when standing on it (Like when I ground on beaches). I also measured EMF with my trimeter and it doesn't measure any electric/magnetic / RF fields.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-14T18:42:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-346732>

If it works for you that is great.
BUT realize you are not in physical contact with the ground like you would be outside. You are in contact with a metallic pad that is connected to a wire that is connected to the ground wire in your home that is connected to the ground. Also connected to the house ground wire is the rest if the electrical system, fixtures and fittings in your home. In a 1,000 ft2 home (100+ m2) that could be 1/2 -1 mile (1+ Km) of wire.
AND if you are in a 50Hz AC field you are now part of that circuit and that field is going through your body to ground.
Also if there is no resistor in the ground wire, if there is a short you could get electricuted. If there is a resistor the negative ions from the ground cannot get to you.
In the same way that artificial light is not the same as natural light, grounding inside to a wire is not the same as grounding in Nature.

### Mat.I — 2025-04-15T08:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-350721>

There are many ways to ground here and you can do it via devices you use (e.g. laptop or chassis of switch or 0v terminal of your power adaptor etc).. As you are looking for something very simple and quick and very DIY friendly.. If you have your ground rod/peg - get pre-terminated alligator jumper leads for the point of attachments (at ground peg/rod and the RJ45 connector aka the shielded ethernet connector). You will need some single building wire likely between 18AWG to 14AWG dependent on length of run to your peg.. Or get DIY friendly alligator clips (with screw terminals to terminate your wire). Or you run the two jumper leads with cable in between as the extension.. Clip onto the metal connector of the shielded ethernet cable and run to the ground. Switch was not necessary unless you were going to ground to the metal chassis or wanted it for network performance management of multiple LAN connections etc.. As you will be introducing some voltage noise on the circuit via the switch with a standard 2 prong AC/DC adaptor - run the shielded cable from the LAN port at router to the switch, then run your hardwired connections from the switch to the devices. Ground the shielded cable feeding the switch, however confirm this has been effective with whatever equipment you are using e.g. BV or multimeter etc. Note: as I am not there to check certain things downstream from your switch (such as continuity between outer shell of each ethernet port and the switch casing) - the outer shell of the ports appear to be metal, however if they are not you will not get the global effect of grounded wholesale across the switch, in which case you will have to bond at the outputs from the switch. Very easy to check if you have a multimeter or just bond as intended and check for a change.. Or screw a very short self tapper in a safe area of the chassis and bond to that..

### Mat.I — 2025-04-15T10:47:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-350724>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=350723)
>
> I was wondering about the ports on the router.....they are plastic. The switch box says they have a metal casing. I got the switch to copy what was at Indias place (alligator clip clipped to the switch) but also means I can manage the cables close to where I am using laptop. I also want to have a setup where I can use the smartphone with ethernet, as now I have a lightning to ethernet adapter.

Don't worry about the router ports as you are addressing it down stream from there.. Yes switch is metal casing, however, I was unsure if the outer shells of each port had a metal surround (see below screen shot). Due to the dark colour.. If you have a BV or multimeter - set it to Ohms and put a probe on the casing and the outer shell of the port if you have a circuit you are good to go. With one bond you should be able to address any connected devices down stream. You could just clip to the casing instead as this would act as a drain.. But the ultimate outcome is having metal outer shells and confirmed continuity between shells and casing as this will earth all down stream devices in addition to addressing the induced voltages..

### Mat.I — 2025-04-15T10:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-350725>

If you have a BV or multimeter - set it to Ohms and put a probe on the casing and the outer shell of the port if you have a circuit you are good to go. With one bond you should be able to address any connected devices down stream. You could just clip to the casing instead as this would act as a drain.. But the ultimate outcome is having metal outer shells and confirmed continuity between shells and casing as this will earth all down stream devices in addition to addressing the induced voltages.. [View attachment 30702](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/30702/)

Also when using a probe on the metal casing find a screw hole or scratch off some paint (by digging the probe in) to get a connection as it will not go through the painted case..

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=351236)
>
> Can’t tell you how many time I have tried to ground something and when measured it was not grounded . . . So many variables . . . If you don’t measure, you don’t know.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=350709)
>
> I believe I was the one that grounded India’s set up. If you have metal on your ethernet plugs, (Cat6A, 7 or 8) any wire connected to it (alligator clip can work) with the other end of the wire to ground will work.

Did you ground Indias this way with an outside rod because of the way they wire homes in El Salvador (TNCS vs TT?) I am trying to figure out how I can best setup movable Ethernet in rentals there by using the wifi router with a switch and then some v long Ethernet cables to take the connection outside so I'm in the sun. I understand unshielded cables don't need grounding and it is claimed the field emanating is very weak and localised but I expect this is still what we consider to be highly damaging. If so a shielded cable setup will need grounding somehow and I just wondered if you chose the rod in El Salvador due to their wiring? Also were the necessary components readily available or hard to find? If the switch I use has metal sockets would this ground this simple setup instead of the rod? Another consideration is that an unshielded setup would presumably be less bad than a shielded cable setup that has been poorly/unsuccessfully configured?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-09-30T19:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-this-grounding-device.28850/post-360203>

You can only ground to the house ground IF the house/circuit has a ground. Many homes/circuits in ES do not.
You ground the ethernet network because the wire can pick up an electric field, not because of the RF. You use shielded ethernet cable because the shielding carries the electric field and the metal plugs allow you to ground the system so it does not ground thru you.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-07T21:49:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-lens-update-as-of-may-2024.28860/post-334408>

[James](https://www.patreon.com/user?u=127719423) said,

Hi Jack, thank you for your response. I found a IOL implant Teleon Lentis that does not block UV. It is made in Germany. I also found an ophthalmologist in Japan who would be able to replace the IOL I currently have with these non UV blocking lens instead of getting the YAG laser surgery. I'm not asking for medical advice. I know that is a no no. I am interested in your personal opinion. Does this sound like something you might do in my situation? I'm I barking up the right tree?

Answer: Does the German lens also block blue light? If so. I'd be careful. You need blue light from the sun to maintain the eye and brain in top working order. Apoptosis and ROS accumulation can keep the lens clear and slow down cataract occurrence and development. In the eye, carotenoid lutein (L) and zeaxanthin (Z) are effective antioxidants and are the only carotenoids found in the lens. They have the characteristics of compounds that absorb short-wave blue light. Research data show that L or Z can protect the lens's proteins, lipids, and DNA from oxidative damage. During oxidative stress, the redox state of these antioxidants can be improved, thus providing protection for the lens.

CITES:

1\. Bai J, Yang F, Dong L, Zheng Y. Ghrelin protects human lens epithelial cells against oxidative stress-induced damage. Oxid Med Cell Longev. 2017;2017:1–8.

2\. Bernstein PS, Khachik F, Carvalho LS, Muir GJ, Zhao DY, Katz NB. Identification and quantitation of carotenoids and their metabolites in the tissues of the human eye. Exp Eye Res. 2001;72(3):215–223.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-08T04:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breastfeeding-and-leptin-rx.28861/post-334426>

During TBI workups we ask mothers about breast milk production and function because it is also tied to the circadian mechanism. Yes, even breast milk has a Circadian diurnal rhythm. All the aromatic amino acids are utilized diurnally when the sun is out and the non-aromatic ones, like methionine used in heavy metal clearance and methylation are used at night. Cortisol is the hormone that wakes humans up. So, more cortisol is made from pregnenolone and the aromatic amino acids for alertness in milk produced during the day. More tryptophan to create melatonin for sleep and regeneration in milk produced during the night. Nature seems to make sure they match the sun's diurnal variations to gain physiologic function to make an optimal child. The human breast has to be programmed by sunlight to make sure adequate milk production is present to deliver DHA and lipids to fuel ketosis to myelinate and develop the brain of the infant via the light and dark cycles. Many women who are blue light toxic or live with heavy amounts of nnEMF cannot make breast milk and have to rely on synthetic foods to feed their kids. These women tend to have a higher C section rate too. No surprise since uterine contraction and the apocrine breast glands use the hormone oxytocin to control this process. Nature clearly does not want to have an upset infant who wants to keep themselves and Mom awake all night long.
[https://www.nature.com/articles/s41390-019-0368-x](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fs41390-019-0368-x%3Ffbclid%3DIwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAAR3P70u6OGJX3t3va7Mrmn1TWFzq1F2o3r6By38kIlBPxD-s2YwnwlSkul8_aem_AUJGwVCoqxq4bjBaQXnpSwMlQ6h4CTtOK-bNCi6HT_PQOevNnUFv1qItSDyVc7gTYugLQ0oKYTxXhZO4uK7uV9Fw&h=AT2P9tNhtBd11gmokkS7kjVSvCVecVLO6lTkZeIkZi5dUWQ5ZmzVz188teR_VLGw3mUPULugTI3jegnhkzaz3UB2k_nu51bi8rmsWsMZRP9wHs4qG8Mx3XHLpTDSgUX77zUEAIU&__tn__=-UK-R&c[0]=AT3N0q0JaWWKWS8OaK8ZnWDrXgndLqqorn-P1ldmc9U7h3J8gTZeDl4edXlExnUb3nkSqA2TBvnzoLQqG24_oCl-xezAtUQSdRCzpirLn6Lkpr9O3YR6C0JKVL6fpfs0TQerZKKM0RwSrichecnGRkIg8zua8JxfLoDkFUfo9B1nqWpeDT5ZOvPGO9XjuBcdVLtIsw)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-08T04:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breastfeeding-and-leptin-rx.28861/post-334427>

What is critical about this paper? It shows that food, even from Mom must have a proper circadian fingerprint to be useful.

Essentially that the concentration of ingredients in breastmilk changes over 24 hours. And that these variations depending on the time of the day are important to the health and development of the baby. For example, the concentration of antibodies in the milk is highest around mid-day. The concentration of cortisol is highest in early morning.

The authors state in the article: “Expressed milk is not necessarily circadian-matched (not necessarily given at the right time during the 24 hour day)…and may disrupt infants’ developing circadian rhythms, potentially contributing to sleep problems and decreased physiological attunement with their mothers and environments.”

“Dysregulated circadian biology may compromise infant health and development.”

Of course, centralized manufactured infant formula is not circadian-matched at all.
Unfortunately, a large part of the article focuses on expressed milk and how, if it is necessary to express milk, the expressed milk should be given to the baby at the same time during the day or night as the milk was expressed, instead of focusing on the importance of the baby getting milk directly from the breast.

I dislike the bottle in the drawing and it is changed to what it could or should have been (on the right). Furthermore, I am not that thrilled with the different elements being placed at different times of the day. The drawing gives the wrong impression. Obviously, the antibodies, for example, can be found in breastmilk throughout the day. As can cortisol. And what time largely depends on the individual circadian biology of the mother and baby. Some babies wake up very early and go to sleep early, and some stay up really late and wake up later. Also babies sleep differently at the age of 2 weeks and the age of 6 months.

When to give expressed milk is not what is truly important, in my opinion. What is truly important about this article is that it adds yet more information to the already abundant information about how different breastmilk is from any formula on the market and how different it will always be. And how discovering something new that is in breastmilk means you can just add it to a new (and more expensive) formula and it will be the same as breastmilk. And even how different milk directly from the breast is from breastmilk in a bottle.

What also bothers me about this study, is that all these fascinating facts are not used to make what I think should be the obvious conclusion-that mothers need better help breastfeeding directly from the breast, because it makes a HUGE difference to the BABY. We have so much proof for this and yet, the focus is "what about expressed milk" and "now we can spends lots of money to try to make formula even closer to breastmilk.

Why does food have to be yoked to your electromagnetic footprint if you are a mammal?

Because Nature wired us this way in our neuroetoderm.

In mammals, the part of the nervous system responsible for most circadian behavior can be localized to the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN). Although previous studies suggest that each SCN neuron may be an independent oscillator, these pacemaker cells must be synchronized to each other as well as to the environment to function adaptively. Therefore, answers to questions about cell-to-cell communication within the SCN lie at the core of understanding how his timing system operates. The daily cycle of light and dark is the dominant environmental cue responsible for synchronizing this biological timing system to the environment. The SCN neurons receive photic information directly from the retinal-hypothalamic tract (RHT). My Vermont 2017 video gets deep into the physics of the retina.

Many of the SCN neurons that receive retinal input from these cells are located in the ventrolateral (or core) region of the SCN and express GABA and, in many cases, vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and the Peptide Histidine Isoleucine. These retino-recipient cells then convey this environmental information to the rest of the SCN. In brain slice preparations, application of VIP alters the firing rate of SCN neurons through a VPAC2 receptor-dependent mechanism and induces expression of mPer1 and mPer2 genes. These two genes are how the circadian cycles yoke directly to the cell cycle and are related to tumor suppressor genes and oncogenesis when mismatches occur chronically in modern man.

Functionally, the administration of VIP, and to a lesser extent PHI, can cause phase shifts of the circadian rhythms in vivo and in vitro in man.

The role of AVP (arginine/vasopressin) in circadian timekeeping has also been well established in the neurosurgery literature. This is why the quantum engineering series has a whole blog dedicated to vasopressin and melanin. Its role in the control of the circadian rhythm of food and water intake has been reported and well documented. Another intrinsic neuropeptide, VIP, acting through a VPAC2 receptor (a type of receptor for VIP), participates in both resetting to light and maintenance of ongoing rhythmicity of the SCN. NPY and GABA seem to be the neurotransmitters in the projection from the intergeniculate leaflet to the SCN adjacent to CN II. Raphe nuclei projections to the SCN contain serotonin as an NT. AVP and prokineticin 2 are seen in the outputs from the SCN as efferents.

NPY, which is an established neurotransmitter of the geniculohypothalamic tract (GHT), was found to regulate SCN neuronal activity and to produce long-lasting suppression of firing rate of SCN neurons. When co-applied with NPY, NT (neurotensin) was found to dampen the profound inhibitory effect of NPY. So when NPY is high, which would be in equatorial or high light conditions, NPY basically makes the SCN less efficient and allows animals to perform outside their normal circadian boundaries. They stay awake longer for eating and for reproduction in high light times during summer.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-which-rx-to-start-with.28863/post-334419>

Hello all, I'm happy to be here. I'm a wife, mom of two, psychotherapist and business owner in the Chicago suburbs. I've been dealing with mystery sx on and off for ten years and doctors have suggested CIRS, adrenal fatigue and chronic fatigue , although I don't really completely fit any of those categories. I am high functioning with extended periods of wellness, but flare every few mos. Sx include brain fog/grogginess, blurred vision, post-exertional muscle fatigue and muscle tightness. Hx of paresthesias which have remitted. My mother passed away from glioblastoma in 2016 at the age of 60 and so I worry about my mitochondrial health and that of my young kids. My husband and I are planning to move South, which will take us some time as our kids are young and I own a therapy practice here. My question is, how can I support my body in the meantime? Would Dr. Kruse recommend the Leptin Rx, cold thermogenesis, and/or something else? I should add that I am underweight at 5/4 105 lbs and so I am not sure that I am leptin resistant. Thanks so much in advance!

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-08T04:06:33+0300

# Thread: HOW TO BEGIN WITH UNCLE JACK?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-08T05:57:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-with-uncle-jack.28864/post-334434>

Start from the beginning. Initially there will appear to be a lot of crazy ideas to you, but you will find many new gems. Eventually, when you persist with my crazy, some of the crazy might be gems you don't understand yet. That is the magics of the Quilt. The Quilt was built to explain how Nature operates. Most of you are centralized and low agency at the beginning. This is good, because those are the folks who look at things the way they are, concretely, and ask why is it this way? You'll find with time, I think and say of things that never were in the textbooks, and ask why not?

This is where the magics begin.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-03T20:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-with-uncle-jack.28864/post-336537>

Membership matters, but you will not be given the rope to ruin the decentralized process on this forum. I have and will continue to prune the tribe that infiltrates it with centralized dogmas.

**RULES TO PROTECT YOUR INNER PEACE AT THIS WEBSITE**

1\. Cut off toxic people in your life. Although it is not easy to do it, for you to have peace of mind, you have to. This includes members who pay you.

2\. Distance yourself from people who only give out negative thoughts and energy. They will never help you grow into a decentralized thinker.

3\. Choose your battles wisely. Not everything requires your energy and reaction.

4\. Be careful in letting people be a part of your life. Not everyone is a friend. Most students are not. Your family/spouse is often an anchor

5\. Unwind. When everything starts to feel tiring, go on a vacation to recharge yourself. You deserve it and your need this biohack.

6\. Learn when to walk away. If it is no longer giving you peace of mind, it is not worth it. Sometimes I have to push members into retirement with a ban.

7\. Always make time for yourself. Being alone feels therapeutic at times. Silence allows you to think

8\. Have less time for social media and more of starting a new hobby. Be productive with your time. Stop wasting it on the superfluous.

9\. Be adaptable to change. Always brace yourself for endless possibilities.

10\. Knowing your triggers is your responsibility. Not all people will adjust to your temperament. Deal with that appropriately. By design, I have fed many trolls here for a long time. Once I realize they add nothing, I will adjust rapidly.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-07T14:48:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-with-uncle-jack.28864/post-350230>

Here is the Decentralized medicine's key thesis. How it all works. The framework to begin your first thinking principled journey to reversing diseases and not treating them.

This is the entire decentralized thesis that I came up with after spending in 18 months in the medical school library putting Nature's recipes altogether 20 years ago. It lays out in detail what I shared with Nick Jikomes in our podcast.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-42-124739549](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-42-124739549)

**Nature is a clever medicine........** Under the wild canopy of Nature, where the river roars and the earth pulses with untamed life, I faced my weakness, a jagged, gnawing disease that clawed at my bones and spirit, threatening to bury me alive. But you, my friend, stared into my soul with eyes like wildfire, demanding forgiveness in a voice that shook the leaves, and together we forged a pact, a sacred vow to banish that cursed word, a sickness, into the abyss, never to haunt us again.

Out here, amidst the tangled roots and relentless winds, we dig at each other’s truths, unearthing the raw, beating heart beneath our scars; when my ego flares like a storm, you summon the me of yesterday, strong, unbroken, yet refusing to let me drown in shadows. Sing it loud, let the branches quake with our anthem, a defiant cry that we’ll always have each other when the world crumbles to ash! Nature’s fierce breath, like moss on my skin, sun igniting my veins, and tears apart the chains of what once held me, reversing the unfairest of fates, while you dig me up from the wreckage, fanning the embers of my better self. Everything else may burn away, but here, under the leaves, our bond is a wildfire, unstoppable, eternal, alive.

**She is clever because she is fully decentralized.......**

# Thread: Fluvoxamine as a potential circadian regulator
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluvoxamine-as-a-potential-circadian-regulator.28869/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Luana Moraes — 2024-05-08T20:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluvoxamine-as-a-potential-circadian-regulator.28869/post-334467>

I've had a lot of difficulty sustaining any improvement for many years. I remember when I took Fluvoxamine for depression in the past it helped me go to sleep earlier (around 10 PM) and wake up early (6 AM) every day. The medication Bromocriptine (Cycloset) is not available in my country and I was thinking about what I could do as a form of specific intervention in my case.
Since Fluvoxamine increases serum melatonin levels I would like to know if anyone has had any positive experience with this medication and if my guess makes sense.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-08T22:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluvoxamine-as-a-potential-circadian-regulator.28869/post-334474>

Fluvoxamine is a FLUORIDE-laced drug used to treat obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD). It belongs to a group of medicines known as selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs). These medicines are thought to work by increasing the activity of a chemical called serotonin in the brain.

What have I said about Fluoride:

SSRI's: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors for depression do not work for many individuals. Circadian rhythms play a role in predicting efficacy. Here is a hint of what mitochondriacs know because I have been teaching this to people for 15 years, and now the data is coming in...........I was right. Thiamine (vitamin B1) is an essential nutrient that serves as a cofactor for a number of enzymes, mostly with mitochondrial localization. Some thiamine-dependent enzymes are involved in energy metabolism and biosynthesis of nucleic acids whereas others are part of the antioxidant machinery. The brain is highly vulnerable to thiamine deficiency due to its heavy reliance on mitochondrial ATP production. Thiamine storage is decreased by technology abuse and the use of coffee and tea.
Thiamine deficiency is more evident during rapid growth with an unmyelinated brain.
Thiamine deficiency contributes to a number of conditions spanning from mild neurological and psychiatric symptoms (confusion, reduced memory, and sleep disturbances) to severe encephalopathy, ataxia, POTS, dysautonomia, congestive heart failure, muscle atrophy, and even death. # Thread: THE BEST SPEECH ON MONEY...........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-money.28875/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-10T01:50:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-money.28875/post-334534>

THE BEST SPEECH ON MONEY...........

Read the money speech some time. Everytime I go to El Salvador and think about the criminals in Washington DC I re read it.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-10T02:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-money.28875/post-334541>

**Today's Lesson:** Health and Wealth are fundamenally linked. They are the product of a man's capacity to think well. It is a decentralized axiom of my website.

Here are some reasons why I think this idea is accurate:

**_Problem-solving:_** Wealth is often created by solving problems that others may not see or haven't thought to address. This requires a high level of cognitive ability, analytical thinking, and creativity.

**_Innovation:_** Many successful entrepreneurs and inventors have created wealth by developing innovative solutions that disrupt existing industries or create new markets. This requires a willingness to think outside the box and challenge conventional wisdom.

**_Risk-taking:_** Building wealth often requires taking calculated risks, which involves thinking critically about potential outcomes and weighing the pros and cons of different options.

**_Decentralized long-term thinking:_** Wealth is often built over time, requiring a person to think about their goals and plans for the future, as well as make decisions that align with those goals.

**_Strategic planning:_** Wealth creation often involves strategic planning, which involves thinking about how to allocate resources, manage finances, and make investments that align with one's goals.

In today's technocratic society, we often see people who are wealthy, but unhealthy, and vice versa. This is because wealth and health are not solely determined by our financial circumstances, but also by our mindset, habits, and lifestyle choices around how we use and abuse light. When we think about it, our thoughts have a profound impact on our physical and mental well-being.

Our thoughts can influence our stress levels, our sleep patterns, our eating habits, and our overall quality of life. When we have a healthy mindset, we are more likely to make choices that promote physical health, such as meditating regularly, eating a circadian diet for our location, and getting enough sleep.

On the other hand, a lack of financial resources can lead to stress, anxiety, and feelings of hopelessness, which can negatively impact our physical health. This is because chronic light stress can lead to a range of mitochondrial health problems, including cancer, hypertension, heart disease, digestive issues, and a weakened immune system.

Similarly, when we think about wealth, it's not just about having a lot of money. Steve Jobs was a billionaire 9 times over but he was an artistocrat of the sewer because his money was built by the technocracy he supported. This has harmed billions. He did not see it this way, but it manifested in how he died. How you make your money says a lot about how you value others.

Wealth is also about having a sense of financial security, being able to provide for oneself and one's loved ones, and having the freedom to pursue one's passions and goals. When we think positively about wealth, we are more likely to attract abundance into our lives, whether that means through our career, investments, or entrepreneurial endeavors.

Wealth is dual edged sword. Use it to gain health and reserve your time. Never trade time for money, for if you do, you will be paid back by Nature for this choice.

Do you agree that health and wealth are fundamentally linked? Are they the result of a person's capacity to think? By cultivating a decentralized mindset and making positive lifestyle choices, might you improve your overall well-being and increase your chances of achieving financial success? You should ask yourself this everyday when you look at the person in the mirror.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-10T03:09:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-money.28875/post-334545>

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1788618906615050312](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1788618906615050312)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-16T00:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-money.28875/post-334757>

I believe fiat's legacy will be viewed as a flawed system that was susceptible to inflation, manipulation, and instability. Here are a few potential ways that history might judge fiat's legacy:

**Inflation and devaluation**: Fiat currency's reliance on central banks and governments to manage its supply could lead to inflation, as more money is printed to finance government spending and debt. This could result in the loss of purchasing power and the devaluation of fiat currency over time.

**Inequality and wealth concentration**: The ability of central banks to create money out of thin air could lead to wealth concentration among those who have access to this newly created money. This could exacerbate income inequality and create social and economic tensions.

**Lack of transparency and accountability**: Fiat currency's opaque nature, with transactions often taking place behind closed doors, could lead to a lack of trust and accountability. This could result in corruption, financial crimes, and market manipulation.

**Limited accessibility**: Fiat currency's reliance on physical infrastructure, such as banks and ATMs, could limit its accessibility to people living in rural or underdeveloped areas. This could exacerbate economic disparities and create barriers to financial inclusion.

**Dependence on government**: Fiat currency's dependence on governments and central banks could lead to a lack of autonomy and freedom for individuals and businesses. This could result in a loss of economic sovereignty and the ability to make decisions based on individual or local needs.

All great empires die from within. All empires come to an end, and the American one is no exception. Empires inevitably fall, and when they do, history judges them for the legacies they leave behind. What will fiat's legacy be when Bitcoin takes over the pantheon, in your opinion?

### Sajid Mahmood — 2024-06-06T23:00:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-money.28875/post-335528>

#Bitcoin     improves the quality of our food by decentralizing its production

A few companies produce just about 90% of the food in grocery stores

If you look at what these companies produce, the majority of it is TRASH

It's processed and has terrible ingredients, preservatives, chemicals, etc

Humans are not supposed to eat this stuff!

These companies control labor, raw materials, and marketing

They have so much power that they can lower their prices to a point where family-owned businesses and farms can't operate anymore

This is the best example of centralization

Most of the world relies on these large food producers to survive

But these companies aren't doing it because they want to provide us with a high-quality product

They're doing it to MAXIMIZE PROFITS FOR SHAREHOLDERS

The leadership at these companies is made up of executives who decide what the companies do

Executives are also shareholders, so they do whatever they can to increase profits and keep market share

This includes lowering the quality of ingredients in their products

Some of these low-quality ingredients are designed to make food more visually appealing and addictive with colors, flavors, and textures that wouldn't occur naturally

Since the foods from these companies are mass-produced, they can offer them to grocery stores at lower prices compared to something that takes time and effort to produce (eg. food from smaller farms and producers)

These companies also aren't worried about how the pesticides and materials they use impact the human body, because profits come first

Since EVERYTHING about our food is centralized today, small farms can no longer thrive

But why is this a result of fiat?

As the value of our savings goes down, demand for cheap sustenance goes up

If you go to a grocery store, whole foods are so much more expensive, while the processed stuff isn't

Why?

Because whole foods can't be mass-produced and engineered, unlike the foods that these companies produce

Whole foods require time, energy, and effort to produce

They require workers (instead of machines) who demand to be paid for their work

Food produced by farmers costs more money because it requires hard work and care

When the value of our money decreases over time and most consumers can't afford whole foods, they move to the cheapest option, which ends up being processed, frozen, toxic sludge

With Bitcoin, savings don't lose value, meaning EVERYTHING gets cheaper over time

If you save in Bitcoin, you can afford the high-quality whole foods that your body desires

With fiat, all you can afford is mass-produced toxic junk

When you can't afford real foods, you unintentionally support the companies in the picture below by buying low-quality foods that hurt your body with diseases

When you save fiat, there's no option but to eat mass-produced junk, because real foods would be double or even triple the price

The mass-produced junk is pumped with sugar, seed oils, and ingredients you can't pronounce

As fiat currencies are devalued, fiat savers can't afford to buy whole foods like steak and potatoes for $10 a meal

Instead, they buy frozen foods, chips, pop, low-quality bread, and all kinds of mass-produced crap that costs less at the cash register, but more in terms of their health

As fiat loses value, demand for low-quality food increases, giving more power to these large corporations and less power to the local farms producing high-quality foods

When more consumers demand low-quality foods, more effort goes into producing them, and more technology is developed to produce them faster and even cheaper (to maximize profits)

When we don't demand high-quality foods, farmers go out of business

Bitcoiners demand high-quality food because they can afford it - their savings aren't being stolen from them to fund wars

Fiat savers CAN'T afford high-quality foods because their buying power is being stolen from them through inflation so they resort to toxic sludge

### lfp620 — 2024-05-10T16:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cellular-dehydration-with-normal-adh.28882/post-334558>

One of my first symptoms ten years ago was extreme, insatiable thirst, which was ultimately managed by salt, electrolyte and carb intake. I saw an endocrinologist who measured my urine output and since it was normal, she concluded that my ADH was normal. While this sx is mostly resolved, I still struggle with it from time to time. Listening to Dr. Kruse's work, I wonder, is it possible that this is a symptom of cellular dehydration, even with normal ADH? Also, does high heteroplasmy follow cellular dehydration or is it the other way around?

### JanSz — 2024-05-10T17:19:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cellular-dehydration-with-normal-adh.28882/post-334565>

> [lfp620 said:](/goto/post?id=334558)
>
> One of my first symptoms ten years ago was extreme, insatiable thirst, which was ultimately managed by salt, electrolyte and carb intake. I saw an endocrinologist who measured my urine output and since it was normal, she concluded that my ADH was normal. While this sx is mostly resolved, I still struggle with it from time to time. Listening to Dr. Kruse's work, I wonder, is it possible that this is a symptom of cellular dehydration, even with normal ADH? Also, does high heteroplasmy follow cellular dehydration or is it the other way around?

> extreme, insatiable thirst,

Think of ADH (AntiDiureticHormone)
as a dam on the lake.
Height of that dam adjusts water volume in that lake.
The level of that dam is adjustable, from 0 to 4.7 units of height.
ADH: 0.0−4.7 pg/mL

The smaller the ADH number, the lower is the dam, the lesser is volume of water level in the lake, the thirstier you are.
\============================
Whatever amounts of water you drink is (relatively) immaterial.
(You will urinate out that water quickly.)
If your ADH is too low, you will continue to stay dehydrated.
\============

In healthy daily life, ADH level vary.
Over the night it goes slightly up, asking lake to accumulate some more water, so when you sleep, it won't wake you up to go to bathroom urinate.
.......
Way back, I think in 2017 or 2018, when we were talking a lot about dr Boros.
Dr Boros was of opinion that we should drink much less water (to induce larger matrix water production.
He was advising drinking less than one liter daily.
In other places, dr Boros was complaining that most people that he came across
drink too much
and (sic) have ADH=~1.
\--
I was waiting, but newer came across him advising supplementation with ADH (desmopressin), to reduce the thirst.
But it suggested to me that
forced drinking may further lower already low ADH.

/////////////////

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heading-to-el-salvador-to-explore-living.28888/post-334599>

Hola mitochondriacs!

I’m Jess (jessica)

I am a homeschooling mom of two teens. The three of us are heading to El Salvador on Monday to explore living there

We will start our journey in the city of San Salvador. Booked a few nights in Zaragoza hoping to run into Jack in a small town ;-) and the down to La Libertad for two weeks.

We speak decent Spanish. We’ve lived in Puerto Rico and Columbia before. My kids are half Colombian on their dad side. He died when they were 5 and 8… a few years after our divorce. But even after divorce and death, our Latino side of the family has totally embraced us and really helped us have a love for the Latino culture and people.

Curious if you have any tips on things to experience while on an exploratory trip?

Definitely want to figure out where the best spots to have my magnetism improved

Also, curious, what meet ups might be happening for people like us who care about our mitochondrial health and are passionate about the light water and magnetism approach to well-being

I appreciate your time. have a beautiful day.

Jess

# Thread: Developing Carpel Tunnel from an NAD+ shot
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-carpel-tunnel-from-an-nad-shot.28890/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### OptimalSkr — 2024-05-11T16:31:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-carpel-tunnel-from-an-nad-shot.28890/post-334603>

So last summer I got an NAD+ shot and it was a Sub Q injection in my left arm. Almost immediately I felt pain in the arm and it got really sore for the rest of the day – almost like someone punched me in the arm.

This lasted for about a week and the pain slowly went away (Used ice packs on it)

However the next time I played guitar (I have been playing consistently for the last 10 years) my fingertips got really tender and there was soreness in my wrist. I thought this was maybe because I had not played in a week or so and it would go away. But I rested and the when I played next the same thing happened, and the more I played the symptoms got worse. The tips of my middle and ring finger got really sore to where I could not press down the string, and my wrist bone area was really sore.

Now this also caused a really annoying tingling and “fall asleep” feeling in my whole arm, especially in bed at night.

I rested for almost 2 months and then when I started playing again it was a little better, but when I played more the same symptoms came back! So I pretty much have not played all winter, and then I started recently again and the same thing again!

I called the place that did the shot but they said it was not possible that this caused anything – and they said my symptoms are carpel tunnel from playing guitar.

However I have been playing for 10 years and my wrist would not get this sore – maybe if I played for a hour a day 30 days in a row. And my fingers NEVER had any nerve issue like this --- So obviously this shot caused the problem!

Anyone have any ideas about what I can do?

Thanks

### JanSz — 2024-05-11T16:42:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-carpel-tunnel-from-an-nad-shot.28890/post-334606>

My left thumb joint sometimes hurts.

This helps:
When lying in the bed, put this light under your blanket, rest your wrist on it.

Note,
I tried bigger lights. But they get too hot and burn.

# Thread: MELANIN'S EFFECT ON RADIATION IS WELL DOCUMENTED JUST NOT WELL KNOWN
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanins-effect-on-radiation-is-well-documented-just-not-well-known.28891/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T18:32:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanins-effect-on-radiation-is-well-documented-just-not-well-known.28891/post-334610>

What does melanin do? In nature, melanin serves diverse functions that range from camouflage and protection to energy harvesting. As pigments, melanins affect visual perception by enhancing or decreasing visual communication (think peacock feathers and chameleons, respectively). In terms of protection, melanins are well-known to protect against ionizing ultraviolet radiation, but that is just one example. Melanization has also been associated with protection against diverse biotic factors, for example, host defense against pathogens, as well abiotic factors, including heat and cold, and osmotic stresses. The immune systems of some insects and nematodes is based on melanin, and some microbial pathogens use melanin to evade host immune defenses. Finally, many melanotic organisms use melanin to capture energy from radiation. Animals, plants, and fungi can use melanin to harvest heat from radiation, influencing their adaptation to different thermal environments (so called thermal melanism). Studies in fungi have also shown that melanin can harvest energy from electromagnetic radiation for metabolic use in a process that involves melanin’s electrical properties referred to as radiosynthesis. For 20 years I have believed that melanin was innovated by the solar wind bombardment of the Earth for 4.6 billion years. Melanin was initially a strategy of bacteria and archea to protect themselves from incoming radiation. Early eukaryotes like fungi then began to use melanin in more novel ways as a source of energy for metabolism.

This thread on Twitter shows melanin at work in the ecosystem

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1781869518958047561](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1781869518958047561)

there is a site of Russian nuclear tests in Kazachstan.
Likely a lot of how nuclear radiation affects biology, can be learned from there.

### JanSz — 2024-05-11T18:54:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanins-effect-on-radiation-is-well-documented-just-not-well-known.28891/post-334612>

Are you sometime thinking of helping Elon Musk with getting humans to Mars?

I have heard Elon worrying about humans existing only on one planet.
All we need is another dinosaur's story to wipe out all humanity.

But, every time I hear about volcanos there,
I am recalling Vesuvius and Pompeii story.

Humans really need to live on more than just one planet.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-29T01:08:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanins-effect-on-radiation-is-well-documented-just-not-well-known.28891/post-341659>

What would happen if you gave yourself permission to do something you’ve never done before? There’s only one way to find out, isn't there?

If you want a new outcome in your life you must begin by doing something you've never done before beginning right now. Get in the sun and turn off the lights at night to protect the regenerative currents in you.

Follow your own passion—not your parents’, not your friends or mentors' — just tap your own instincts.

The art of life lies in a constant readjustment to our surroundings. Adaptability is the simple secret of survival. There are 31,530,000 seconds in a year, a thousand milliseconds in a second, a million microseconds, a billion nanoseconds. And the one constant connecting nanoseconds to years is changing. The universe, from atom to galaxy, is in a perpetual state of thermodynamic flux.

But we humans don't like change; we fight it, it scares us. That fear keeps you from the RIGHT ACTIONS and the right choices. Human nature is like water, not like stone. So we create the illusion of stasis. We want to believe in a world at rest, the world of right now yet our great paradox remains the same. The moment we grasp the now, that now is gone.

We cling to snapshots, but life is moving pictures, each nanosecond different from the last. Our actions and adaptability create opportunities in life. Only by being open to change will you have a true opportunity to get the most from your talent. Time, or the entropy of life forces us to grow, to adapt because every time we blink our eyes the world shifts beneath our feet.

Every day, every moment, every nanosecond the world changes. Electrons bump into each other and some react and some gain/lose their photons; people collide and alter each other's paths. Adaptability cushions the impact of change or disappointment. Change isn't easy, more often it's wrenching and difficult, but maybe that's a good thing because it's change that makes us strong, keeps us resilient, and teaches us to evolve as entropy creates its chaos. The art of life lies in a constant readjustment to our surroundings. Melatonin creation in blood is just one of those things Nature figured out to make life possible.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-09-20T19:46:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-not-rbcs-have-mitochondria.28892/post-359765>

> [Sheen said:](/goto/post?id=349346)
>
> Does the body create pneumonia to slow down oxygen intake like it does with COPD?

Pneumonia is usually infectious etiology. So no, but your question is a good one because it shows me you are think along the correct lines.

Wheezing is protective mechanism for failing mitochondria some where in the system because it can systemically lowering PaO2 to cause a relative hypoxemia.

Expiratory wheezing is caused by airway narrowing, which
can lead to hypoxemia (low PO2) due to ventilation-perfusion (V/Q) mismatch. The severity of the wheezing and the underlying condition determine the extent of the drop in PO2.
How expiratory wheezing affects PO2

Airway obstruction: A wheeze is the sound of air moving turbulently through narrowed or constricted airways. The constriction requires more effort to move air out of the lungs.

V/Q mismatch: This airway obstruction is often not uniform throughout the lungs, which creates a mismatch between ventilation (airflow) and perfusion (blood flow).

Some areas of the lung receive normal blood flow but reduced airflow, leading to blood passing through the lungs without picking up enough oxygen.

This poorly oxygenated blood mixes with oxygenated blood, causing a drop in the overall arterial PO2 (hypoxemia).

Hyperinflation: The trapped air caused by airway closure during exhalation can lead to hyperinflation of the lungs. While this can temporarily improve airway opening, it also increases the muscular work of breathing, which can further tax the respiratory system.

Wheezing and Sleep apnea have a lot of interesting quantum connections because each one point to what organ systems the mitochondrial damage is in. Sleep apnea tells is the problem is really in the brain or vessels of the CNS. Wheezing = lungs and heart.

Severity and PO2 levels
The effect of wheezing on PO2 is not always uniform and depends on the severity of the underlying condition.

Mild to moderate wheezing: In milder cases, the body can compensate by breathing faster (hyperventilating). An arterial blood gas (ABG) test in these cases may show normal or even low PCO2 (partial pressure of carbon dioxide), but the PO2 will likely be decreased (hypoxemia).

Severe wheezing: As the airway obstruction becomes more severe, the patient may no longer be able to compensate with increased breathing. At this stage, the arterial PCO2 will begin to rise (hypercapnia), and the PO2 will drop significantly. This is a sign of impending respiratory failure and a medical emergency.

Silent chest: In extremely severe cases of airway obstruction, the airflow is so limited that no wheezing sound is produced. This "silent chest" is a life-threatening sign of severe airflow obstruction and significant hypoxemia.

Common causes and their effects on PO2
Several conditions can cause expiratory wheezing and lead to hypoxemia:

Asthma: Inflammation, bronchospasm, and mucus production in the small airways cause widespread and non-uniform obstruction. In severe attacks, this leads to significant V/Q mismatch and hypoxemia. Asthma is always linked to low Vitamin D levels or excessive nnEMF.

Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD): Conditions like chronic bronchitis and emphysema cause narrowing and damage to the airways, leading to chronic V/Q mismatch and hypoxemia. This has many causes.

Heart failure: When the heart does not pump efficiently, fluid can build up in the lungs (pulmonary edema), causing narrowing of the airways and wheezing, sometimes called "cardiac asthma." This congestion impairs oxygen exchange and can lead to hypoxemia. This also has many etiologies that lead to lowered cardiac mitochondrial function.
