# Thread: Home Schooling Feedback & Suggestions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mat.I — 2024-05-13T05:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/post-334668>

Hello All,
My Partner & I are considering home schooling in the next year or so. Our Daughter is 6 y/o and has just started Kindergarten this year and we are planning to transition to full-time home schooling for my Daughter & Son.

I assume there are quite a number of other home schooling parents on here or parents that have home schooled in the past. I was hoping to get some insight on the following -

How's home schooling going? do you have any suggestions or feedback?

How is the social aspect managed? I know there are groups here in Australia that meet up once a week for combined learning activities. Is this enough? Do you do further activities in between with groups??

Did some parents find it was too isolating for their kids?

Did you hire a part time teacher / tutor ?

Thank you
Mat

### Anthony Smith — 2024-05-13T08:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/post-334671>

We home schooled our three. There were group activities with other home school children once or twice a week. We were also active in church so another social group for them. Worked for us and them. They are all VERY social and hold their own in ANY situation.

### Mat.I — 2024-05-13T12:42:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/post-334672>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=334671)
>
> We home schooled our three. There were group activities with other home school children once or twice a week. We were also active in church so another social group for them. Worked for us and them. They are all VERY social and hold their own in ANY situation.

Hi Anthony - thank you for your feedback. That is great to hear and very encouraging.

### thejessallen — 2024-05-13T15:02:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/post-334676>

Ive homeschooled my kids since 2018. My son did spend 18 months in India. For the last couple years of his high school. My 15-year-old is still being homeschooled.

It is absolutely the best decision I’ve ever made. It’s definitely the hardest. Although I didn’t have a spouse or partner to help me. which made it extra hard. a full-time government babysitter a.k.a. public school would’ve been a lot easier in some respects

People who interact with my kids can tell they are a very different than traditional teens. They can hold a conversation about many topics. Because I have been self-employed for most of their life. They get a lot of exposure to entrepreneurship. We have been able to travel and live in a variety of places.

My son didn’t have a phone until he was 17.

My daughter still doesn’t have an official phone. She uses a second family phone because the homeschool group we started attending required it for learning - which I think is a totally dumb policy, which is the kind of stupid things schools due to kids.

When my daughter was in the first grade, she came home and asked me what a pussy was. Because some boy teased her about that.

My friend at that time was dealing with her son being offered Adderall by his nine-year-old friend. Who offered to split his Adderall with him

by the third grade, I was sickened by how the school system uses junk food and candy as a way to reward children. However, there is normalizing around school shooter drills and bullying.

When I went to my son’s open house, his teacher said he was doing great. She did complain that he would correct her. I asked if he was accurate and she said yes, but he should be correcting her. Also getting in trouble for making drawings on the back of the test when his tests were over. Even though he would get straight A’s. He stopped doodling after that completely.

With homeschooling, you can choose how to expose your kids. I’m traveling to El Salvador soon. Because I want my kids exposed to the community of decentralized thinkers who live there. Including hopefully Uncle Jack.

There are always ways to create social interaction. I would say that’s the least of your challenges. It can cost extra money. Public schools are free. But homeschool group groups may not be

By homeschooling you can create a learning opportunity every day about things that are truly important in life.

We did borrow the Acton Academy model - where the kids to a lot of self regulating. I’ve taught them how to plan their day. How to schedule their week out after they decide what progress they want to make for the week.

I use free khan academy and a paid online community called Outschool and we attend homeschool meet up groups.

Should my children decide to go to college, both think they want to right now. I’m not a big fan of college anymore. But if they do, they have the practice of being responsible for ensuring their education happens.

Homeschooling gives us the opportunity to be really present parents.

I think it’ll be the best decision you ever make. Especially assuming you are following Jack’s protocols and taking care of your mitochondrial DNA

### Mat.I — 2024-05-14T05:10:42+0300

> It is absolutely the best decision I’ve ever made. It’s definitely the hardest. Although I didn’t have a spouse or partner to help me. which made it extra hard. a full-time government babysitter a.k.a. public school would’ve been a lot easier in some respects
>
> People who interact with my kids can tell they are a very different than traditional teens. They can hold a conversation about many topics. Because I have been self-employed for most of their life. They get a lot of exposure to entrepreneurship. We have been able to travel and live in a variety of places.
>
> My son didn’t have a phone until he was 17.
>
> My daughter still doesn’t have an official phone. She uses a second family phone because the homeschool group we started attending required it for learning - which I think is a totally dumb policy, which is the kind of stupid things schools due to kids.
>
> When my daughter was in the first grade, she came home and asked me what a pussy was. Because some boy teased her about that.
>
> My friend at that time was dealing with her son being offered Adderall by his nine-year-old friend. Who offered to split his Adderall with him
>
> by the third grade, I was sickened by how the school system uses junk food and candy as a way to reward children. However, there is normalizing around school shooter drills and bullying.
>
> When I went to my son’s open house, his teacher said he was doing great. She did complain that he would correct her. I asked if he was accurate and she said yes, but he should be correcting her. Also getting in trouble for making drawings on the back of the test when his tests were over. Even though he would get straight A’s. He stopped doodling after that completely.
>
> With homeschooling, you can choose how to expose your kids. I’m traveling to El Salvador soon. Because I want my kids exposed to the community of decentralized thinkers who live there. Including hopefully Uncle Jack.
>
> There are always ways to create social interaction. I would say that’s the least of your challenges. It can cost extra money. Public schools are free. But homeschool group groups may not be
>
> By homeschooling you can create a learning opportunity every day about things that are truly important in life.
>
> We did borrow the Acton Academy model - where the kids to a lot of self regulating. I’ve taught them how to plan their day. How to schedule their week out after they decide what progress they want to make for the week.
>
> I use free khan academy and a paid online community called Outschool and we attend homeschool meet up groups.
>
> Should my children decide to go to college, both think they want to right now. I’m not a big fan of college anymore. But if they do, they have the practice of being responsible for ensuring their education happens.
>
> Homeschooling gives us the opportunity to be really present parents.
>
> I think it’ll be the best decision you ever make. Especially assuming you are following Jack’s protocols and taking care of your mitochondrial DNA

[@thejessallen](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/30761/) - Thank you for all the details, it is much appreciated.

There is quite a large community of home schoolers where we currently live and we have also noticed the difference in how the kids present, they appear to be very grounded and present. The public & private schooling system is something we want to move away from and concerns us as there is minimal involvement from us as parents and in some cases you have to work very hard to undo misinformation and skewed views being impressed on the kids at school.. Most importantly, we want to avoid the modern tech classrooms blasting nnEMF, unbalanced spectrum lights, no windows open and indoor split units without fresh air also leading to very high CO2 levels..

Great to hear the positive experience.
Thanks
Mat

### Niki Stanton — 2024-05-18T21:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/post-334889>

We are home educating and absolutely loving it. Expect that it will take a while to find your rhythm and discover your style. There are so many incredible resources.

Our kids are 7 and 9, and we do a blend of Charlotte Mason and a bit of the Acton academy approach, tons of outside time, gardening, we get to focus more on sports and extracurricular like tennis, swimming, basketball because they’re not completely burnt by the 7 hour grind under toxic fluorescent lights.

Another huge benefit, we can mitigate some of the absolute emf shitstorm these schools have now. All the classrooms even down to elementary have laptops for each kid. There’s usually cell towers either very close by or a repeater on the property.

It is hard. It is work. But it’s so incredibly worthwhile.

I challenge the status quo saying an 18-year-old is an adult. I think that’s just an excuse to send them off to war and government wanting less dependents

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-20T05:50:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/post-334912>

Our children are like the Library of Alexandria. Do not make centralized decisions with their education that squander the resources they may house. Public education is indoctrination to obedience and compliance with government ideals and beliefs. This is why governments and teacher unions want this indoctrination mandatory.

The Library of Alexandria, one of the most significant centers of learning in ancient Greece, was a repository of knowledge that housed countless scrolls and manuscripts containing the wisdom and discoveries of ancient civilizations.

Just as the Library of Alexandria was a treasure trove of knowledge, our children are a treasure trove of potential, curiosity, and creativity. They are the future leaders, innovators, and change-makers who will shape the world we live in. As parents, caregivers, and educators, we have a profound responsibility to nurture and develop their minds, hearts, and spirits.

The decentralized idea "do not make decisions with them that squander that resource" is a crucial reminder that our children's potential is not a given; it requires careful cultivation, guidance, and support. We must make decisions that prioritize their growth, well-being, and development, rather than sacrificing their potential for fleeting gains or immediate gratification. No one can shepard this plant that came from you, better than you.

This idea should encourage you to think critically about the kinds of values and priorities we choose to instill in our children. Are we teaching them to value wisdom, knowledge, curiosity, and critical thinking? Are we providing them with opportunities to explore, create, and learn? Are we modeling the behaviors and values we want them to adopt?

By considering our children as a precious resource like the Library of Alexandria, we can recommit ourselves to making decisions that honor their potential and invest in their futures. This means prioritizing education, nurturing their creativity, and fostering a love of learning that will stay with them throughout their lives. Putting a kid in public school is akin to lighting the match the burned the Library of Alexandria from existence.

### Mat.I — 2024-10-23T11:51:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/home-schooling-feedback-suggestions.28897/post-341425>

Just to circle back to this thread - I wanted to share my experience of our first home schooling group meet up (just in case this helps others in making the decision to home school their children. Group of ~ 50 or so children ranging from ages 5 or so up to 17, my observations - not one child/teen was misbehaving, all well regulated, all very caring welcoming, all very confident, all very social, very similar character traits across the board, all remained fully engaged/.interested, & all very inquisitive. A bonus the majority of activities were done outside on grass with most kids being barefoot (grounded). It was a great experience and reassuring to see this first hand. Great to get these signs for reassurance that the unfamiliar/new path is the correct one in terms of building healthy, confident and happy kids..

### Justin Goldberg — 2024-05-15T20:56:24+0300

>
> ONLY use MB w a doc that knows what they are doing. That is all

Thanks, but none do. And Dr J seems to think that I can't afford him. Or maybe he is waiting for me to remove wifi first.

Some refs:

### [Methylene Blue : American Journal of Therapeutics](https://journals.lww.com/americantherapeutics/abstract/2003/07000/methylene_blue.9.aspx)

molytic effects. People with G-6-PD deficiency, along with patients exposed to aniline dyes and dapsone, may present with special risks in the treatment of methemoglobinemia....

 journals.lww.com

> Methylene blue finds its major utilization in toxicology in the treatment of methemoglobinemia at a dose of 1 to 2 mg/kg intravenously. By interacting with methemoglobin and the erythrocyte's enzyme systems to reduce back to hemoglobin, methylene blue is a generally safe drug with dose-related hemolytic effects. People with G-6-PD deficiency, along with patients exposed to aniline dyes and dapsone, may present with special risks in the treatment of methemoglobinemia.

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0197458010005294](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0197458010005294)

> [Methylene blue](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/methylene-blue) (MB), the first synthetic drug, has a 120-year-long history of diverse applications

### [Methylene Blue – Existing and Future Research](https://mitolab.com/2017/11/methylene-blue-research/)

Methylene Blue Molecular Formula: C16H18CIN3S.3H2O Appearance and Structure Methylene blue, also known as methylthioninium chloride, is a compound made up of very dark green-blue crystals or powd…

 mitolab.com

> Methylene Blue can be found on the World Health Organization’s List of Essential Medicines; widely regarded as a list of the world’s safest and most effective medicines

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154)

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

# Thread: THE BEST SPEECH ON WHY THE ELITES NEED TO BECOME EXTINCT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-why-the-elites-need-to-become-extinct.28909/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-16T01:27:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-speech-on-why-the-elites-need-to-become-extinct.28909/post-334759>

For too long, a complex network of institutions - from the media to NGOs to corporations to government - has successfully enforced ideological conformity across American life. The DNC royalty have been ensconced in a bubble, surrounded by protective staffers and fawned upon by our newspapers and even late-night talk show hosts. It’s a privileged berth, reinforced by the institutions and safely barricaded from coming into contact with public opinion and the realities of their public policies. Whenever something came close to challenging their cherished orthodoxies, they moved into action. How the Hunter Biden Laptop story was dealt with is a classic example of this. Enter Mr. [@MrWinMarshall](https://twitter.com/MrWinMarshall) on Twitter.

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1788965604096950381](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1788965604096950381)

YOU BETTER LISTEN TO THE WHOLE SPEECH HE GAVE.

It’s hard to avoid the symbolism of a banjo player who made a name for himself playing folk-rock - and folk, as we all know, meaning the common people - coming face to face with a sitting politician who has been at the helm of the Democratic Party for longer than anyone else, taking her to task for the hubris of condescending to the working class and her party for subverting democracy in the name of reinforcing it. And to do it in, of all places, the most hallowed, elite space imaginable. It’s as if there was a glitch in the matrix, and someone from the outside world made a crossover. We don’t see these direct confrontations - not just of people but of realities - happen at all. The numbers don’t lie.

The Democrats in the USA have been leaching support from the working class and now, even racial minorities. The virality of this moment is yet another manifestation of populist frustrations bubbling up. I wonder if, Mr. [musk](https://twitter.com/elonmusk) had not liberated Twitter, how would this have played out on the Oxford Union YouTube channel?

If legacy media refused to cover it? One thing’s for sure, this time, going viral isn’t actually a bad thing. And besides, almost 3 years since his departure from Mumford & Sons, he’s built a life that he can’t be canceled from. It’s a rebirth. The start of a new chapter. It is time to tell the elites to fuck off.

# Thread: New Light Kruser!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-light-kruser.28910/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TXRN2DO — 2024-05-16T03:22:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-light-kruser.28910/post-334771>

So excited to be here! I’d like to start by thanking Dr. Kruse…thank you for sharing your mind with the world and being an absolute inspiration.

He has given me hope that change is possible in the realm of medicine. I am a former ER/ICU nurse (who always refused to work nights  ) turned medical student from Houston, TX. About a year and a half ago, while working on a concussion project in school, I had a realization about the gut microbiome’s influence on mitochondrial respiration and its links to neurotransmitter production via the endocannabinoid system. (Prior to nursing, I obtained a BS in psychology). I tried explaining to my professors for a good six months what I was finding in my hours of reading research outside of class, but it seemed no one could relate to my newfound passion for endocannabinoids.

I’m in osteopathic medical school, which places an emphasis on the importance of fascia. I found myself wondering how the hydrogen bonding in collagen networks and aquaporin channels can be manipulated through touch and am still diving into how semi-conduction is tied to fascia. (Any reading suggestions would be greatly appreciated.) But it seemed even in an “unconventional” osteopathic school, there was little time for diving into the wonders of physiology and all the time for filling the heads of students with prescription drug mechanisms.

I became lost in biophysics and the colors of the rainbow the further I dove into research articles and this, along with changes to my health, led me to take leave from school during my second year. I have been down quite a road since I left school, reading a variety of books on light, yoga, vibrational medicine, and of course Rick Rubin’s on creativity. I have also been on a journey of healing my own gut and migraine issues during this time…blue light blockers have done wonders for me (thanks to a suggestion by my amazing GI doc), along with ditching my sunglasses and the tan I’ve developed after discovering Dr. Kruse.

I have decided to start back up with school next month and will begin clinical rotations, but I am still trying to find a way to align my morality and experience with the world of centralized medicine. I’ve seen firsthand from my years of bedside nursing how prescriptions and hospital stays affect patients and ultimately lead them down a road of prolonged suffering.

I’m very eager to learn all I can here and hope Dr. Kruse starts a training program for future light practitioners! I fear the oath I take one day of “do no harm” will feel like a lie unless I arm myself with true knowledge on the fundamentals of human health which I hope to grasp by joining this community.

Shine On!
Imaan

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-16T05:02:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-light-kruser.28910/post-334782>

If you have the ethics of a real doctor there is no alignment with morality and centralized medicine. Ironically to finish your training you have to be OK with this. Where the rubber meets the road is what you do after you have your degree and you've finsihed your residency. That is when your morality and actions better be in alignment. I believe doctors in training have a duty to learn what they must. But much like a law that is unjust, you need to follow Thomas Jefferson advice. Patriots have a DUTY to not follow tyranny.

### TXRN2DO — 2024-05-16T20:29:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-light-kruser.28910/post-334803>

Thank you for the advice! Even choosing a residency program feels wrong and knowing I have to do so in order to have the “credibility” behind my name has been a tough pill to swallow…I’ve debated just doing an internship year and calling it a day but have been deterred by physician friends from this path.

I’m fascinated with ophthalmology but more-so in a Meir Schneider way and hope to bring some of his principles to mainstream knowledge and treatment…after learning how to slice eyes in all the crazy ways medicine has come up with so far  Curious if you have any thoughts on his work and techniques?

### TXRN2DO — 2024-05-17T01:29:02+0300

Thank you. Please consider starting a training program for future (and current) physicians so we can collectively change the tide in the right direction once we’re unleashed. # Thread: Can Melanin RX cause side effects in the first stages?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lukassss — 2024-05-16T17:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/post-334797>

My short story this spring. I started renovating melanin in March this year, when the sun returned to Poland. I spent the last 6 days in the sun for 8-10 hours (full sun without clouds). My sleep has become amazing and very restorative. The last day was very windy, but I still spent it outside playing with my skin. It was 5 days of 8 hours of sunbathing +CT.

It ended with night vomiting, diarrhea + fever and general weakness.

The question is: why did this happen? My tan is already at a very high level, and the place where I sunbathed was far from nnEMF.

The only thing that changed in my environment was that my body was exposed to strong winds (I was grounded all the time) + the weather changed for the worse.

Why did diarrhea and vomiting occur?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-05-17T01:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/post-334812>

I have heard many healthcare people talk about a “healing crisis” (nausea etc) a detox that can happen when the body finally either gets what it needs that was missing AND/OR we remove something negative (nnEMF) that was in the way. Is there anything to this? I don’t know but I have wanted to ask Dr Jack about it for a while.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2024-05-17T15:51:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/post-334825>

I'd suspect you did too much too soon - work on your redox more.

### NikkiYork — 2024-05-18T00:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/post-334844>

> [Lukassss said:](/goto/post?id=334797)
>
> My short story this spring. I started renovating melanin in March this year, when the sun returned to Poland. I spent the last 6 days in the sun for 8-10 hours (full sun without clouds). My sleep has become amazing and very restorative. The last day was very windy, but I still spent it outside playing with my skin. It was 5 days of 8 hours of sunbathing +CT.
>
> It ended with night vomiting, diarrhea + fever and general weakness.
>
> The question is: why did this happen? My tan is already at a very high level, and the place where I sunbathed was far from nnEMF.
>
> The only thing that changed in my environment was that my body was exposed to strong winds (I was grounded all the time) + the weather changed for the worse.
>
> Why did diarrhea and vomiting occur?

Could it be you just got sick? I mean, sometimes you eat the wrong thing, and bang, you throw up. Why would it have to be related to sun bathing?
Have you had diarrhea and vomiting before in your life? Probably, right?

Also, the sun in Poland is really not strong this time of year.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-21T03:58:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/post-334950>

Or there is a problem with dopamine/melatonin/melanin levels......could cause the gut problem because of the NTS.

### Lukassss — 2024-05-22T10:56:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/post-334988>

I care about what I eat and drink. I can control it. So it wasn't poisoning. The sun didn't do it either!! Today I am sure of it.

The episode of vomiting and diarrhea lasted one night. Nothing in the sensation of my body indicated that this would happen any time soon. I just woke up with a stomach ache after 4 hours of sleep.

I came back to life quickly, after 8 hours. The CT scan and heating helped me most when I was shivering in the sun.

Entering the cold water after a night's fever was extremely unpleasant, but the effect of restoring energy and well-being was immediate.

After a few days, my daughter and wife had the same symptoms.

Many of my friends often have such symptoms when their environment changes, i.e. from September, children start going to schools and kindergartens with a lot of blue light + nnEMF (nobody cares about it).
Another thing I noticed is the winter and summer solstice periods + plane flights.

I think that in my case and that of my family, it was some element of the environment that influenced my entire family. It wasn't food.

We always have one thing in common: we sleep in the same bed, on a magnetic pad.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) I wonder why the body reacts this way, what does it want to achieve from the quantum point of view by creating such a package: vomiting>fever>diarrhea.?
Does it want to get rid of excess deuterium where it shouldn't be?

### JanSz — 2024-05-22T15:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-melanin-rx-cause-side-effects-in-the-first-stages.28915/post-334992>

> [Lukassss said:](/goto/post?id=334988)
>
> vomiting>fever>diarrhea.?
> Does it want to get rid of excess deuterium where it shouldn't be?

vomiting, diarrhea---->>getting rid of undesirable

fever----->increasing metabolism, removing (quicker than usual) garbage (so there is room for agreeable stuff)
\=============================

> [Lukassss said:](/goto/post?id=334988)
>
> Many of my friends often have such symptoms when their environment changes, i.e.

When I lived in Poland, remember similar reactions, (on the beginning of warmer weather).

### [Brain clearance is reduced during sleep and anesthesia - Nature Neuroscience](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41593-024-01638-y)

It has been widely believed that a key function of sleep is to actively clear metabolites and toxins from the brain. Miao, Luo et al. show in mice that brain clearance is markedly reduced—not increased—during sleep and anesthesia.

The truth is often a powerful force that can be uncomfortable and even threatening to those who hold power and control in authoritarian regimes. The truth can reveal the injustices, corruption, and abuse of power that are inherent in such systems, which can lead to feelings of revulsion, anxiety, and even panic among those who benefit from the status quo. In authoritarian societies, like Biden's USA, the truth is often suppressed or distorted to maintain the power and privilege of the ruling elite.

Those who dare to speak the truth, whether it be whistleblowers, dissidents, or ordinary citizens, are often silenced through intimidation, imprisonment, or even violence. Revulsion is a strong emotional response to something that is perceived as morally repugnant or unacceptable.

In an authoritarian system, the truth can be revolting to those in power because it reveals the darkness and ugliness of their actions and motivations. This can lead to a desperate attempt to suppress or manipulate the truth to avoid confronting the consequences of their actions.

If the melatonin level in mitochondria keeps delta psi between -200 mV and -400mV you can keep heteroplasmy rate stable so aging progresses at a 10% decline per decade. It also helps control delocalization of electrons in the benzene rings of dopamine. When delocalization changes in the molecule charges are moved in the atoms of dopamine and this makes it tunable to photons.

If the delta psi rate of change does not keep up with the heteroplasmy rate in tissues you age faster. This process happens in all tissues with different efficiencies and ultimately this is why aging is not a homogenous process. It is heterogenous and it this is why blue light can cause obesity in one person but ALS in someone else. You are only as strong as your weakest link when it comes to the colony of mitochondria in your body.

[The **sympathetic and parasympathetic nervous systems** have opposite roles](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b1ca868be6743608JmltdHM9MTcxNTk5MDQwMCZpZ3VpZD0yMWIwNjE3Mi0yMzUwLTY1MDEtM2UyOS03NTI1MjIxMzY0ZTQmaW5zaWQ9NTk3OA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=21b06172-2350-6501-3e29-7525221364e4&psq=sympathetic+vs+parasympathetic&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9teS5jbGV2ZWxhbmRjbGluaWMub3JnL2hlYWx0aC9ib2R5LzIzMjY2LXBhcmFzeW1wYXRoZXRpYy1uZXJ2b3VzLXN5c3RlbS1wc25z&ntb=1)[1](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=8ac6902e0f529a79JmltdHM9MTcxNTk5MDQwMCZpZ3VpZD0yMWIwNjE3Mi0yMzUwLTY1MDEtM2UyOS03NTI1MjIxMzY0ZTQmaW5zaWQ9NTk3OQ&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=21b06172-2350-6501-3e29-7525221364e4&psq=sympathetic+vs+parasympathetic&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9teS5jbGV2ZWxhbmRjbGluaWMub3JnL2hlYWx0aC9ib2R5LzIzMjY2LXBhcmFzeW1wYXRoZXRpYy1uZXJ2b3VzLXN5c3RlbS1wc25z&ntb=1)[2](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=ba967cf1f50b6f6aJmltdHM9MTcxNTk5MDQwMCZpZ3VpZD0yMWIwNjE3Mi0yMzUwLTY1MDEtM2UyOS03NTI1MjIxMzY0ZTQmaW5zaWQ9NTk4MA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=21b06172-2350-6501-3e29-7525221364e4&psq=sympathetic+vs+parasympathetic&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9teS5jbGV2ZWxhbmRjbGluaWMub3JnL2hlYWx0aC9ib2R5LzIzMjYyLXN5bXBhdGhldGljLW5lcnZvdXMtc3lzdGVtLXNucy1maWdodC1vci1mbGlnaHQ&ntb=1)[3](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b4e38b373cbc4e20JmltdHM9MTcxNTk5MDQwMCZpZ3VpZD0yMWIwNjE3Mi0yMzUwLTY1MDEtM2UyOS03NTI1MjIxMzY0ZTQmaW5zaWQ9NTk4MQ&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=21b06172-2350-6501-3e29-7525221364e4&psq=sympathetic+vs+parasympathetic&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9ieWp1cy5jb20vYmlvbG9neS9kaWZmZXJlbmNlLWJldHdlZW4tc3ltcGF0aGV0aWMtYW5kLXBhcmFzeW1wYXRoZXRpYy8&ntb=1)[4](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=3277dfdf08ae2b50JmltdHM9MTcxNTk5MDQwMCZpZ3VpZD0yMWIwNjE3Mi0yMzUwLTY1MDEtM2UyOS03NTI1MjIxMzY0ZTQmaW5zaWQ9NTk4Mg&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=21b06172-2350-6501-3e29-7525221364e4&psq=sympathetic+vs+parasympathetic&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9teS5jbGV2ZWxhbmRjbGluaWMub3JnL2hlYWx0aC9ib2R5LzIzMjczLWF1dG9ub21pYy1uZXJ2b3VzLXN5c3RlbQ&ntb=1)[5](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=897899363f78bbfdJmltdHM9MTcxNTk5MDQwMCZpZ3VpZD0yMWIwNjE3Mi0yMzUwLTY1MDEtM2UyOS03NTI1MjIxMzY0ZTQmaW5zaWQ9NTk4Mw&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=21b06172-2350-6501-3e29-7525221364e4&psq=sympathetic+vs+parasympathetic&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9nZWVreW1lZGljcy5jb20vYXV0b25vbWljLW5lcnZvdXMtc3lzdGVtLw&ntb=1):

-   **Sympathetic nervous system**: Prepares the body for the “fight or flight” response during danger.
-   **Parasympathetic nervous system**: Inhibits the body from overworking and restores it to a calm state.
-   Together, they keep the body in balance.

# Thread: 9 yr old - redox, melanin and Mito rx journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-yr-old-redox-melanin-and-mito-rx-journal.28920/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Niki Stanton — 2024-05-18T20:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-yr-old-redox-melanin-and-mito-rx-journal.28920/post-334886>

Sharing in case it helps others and opening up for feedback from all the smart people here.

Our oldest, 9 year old boy, has had lifelong mito issues and symptoms. Dr kruse pinned it down quickly on our consult and we are waiting to hear if we can get in at CHOP for diagnosis.

Meantime!
We are being more intentional about sunlight especially on torso, where he has lowest muscle tone. Endurance issues, postural weakness. Well, 3 days in to this and we are already noticing huge. Huge. Huge. Changes.

This morning I noticed his posture at the breakfast table was upright. Straight! Not slumped like normal.

While watching his bro play basketball for the first time he didn’t try and slump over on us, was able to pay attention to the game, ask about the score. Big stuff!!

Dr kruse wants us to vacation in the tropics and while we save up for that, I scheduled a consult with Anthony at emf safe because our house has high magnetic fields in the bedrooms and want to get that as good as it can be.

Anyway, excited about the future. This is the rundown of stuff we are still facing
\-sensitive to emf
\-super sweater and decreased endurance in this heat
\-demineralized teeth, delayed tooth eruption
\-some leftover stims and facial tics when he’s tired
\-ocd symptoms if all the boxes aren’t checked- hunger, etc
\-anxiety in different environments

Sending encouragement to everyone from south Florida

### Niki Stanton — 2024-05-26T00:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-yr-old-redox-melanin-and-mito-rx-journal.28920/post-335145>

We have been very intentional about his outside time and sun exposure on skin. Happy to report that while he is getting tanner, he seems to be getting stronger, anxiety is decreasing (much fewer questions and repetitions), more independence and willingness to try new things.

Some lingering postural and endurance things.

He seems to not need the nanovi device time as much.

Still need to keep an eye on his teeth and he still has the occasional facial tic or stim.

Going to add in more ice water. He needs stimulation of those nerves. Also need to work on his oral motor skills because a huge goal we would love to conquer is being able to swish and spit water. That would be a huge help and anxiety reducer for everyone. And an important step toward being able to swallow pills, life skill.

Will probably hear back from CHOP Mito team next week.

Onward!

### Niki Stanton — 2024-07-24T15:15:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-yr-old-redox-melanin-and-mito-rx-journal.28920/post-337531>

Great progress to report. We’ve been getting outside more. But we were still struggling with mouth breathing and difficulty calming completely.

Not anymore!

We took a trip to Weeki Wachee FL and stayed in a cabin on an island with acres of swamp and river for its backyard view. Trifield confirmed what I suspected to be very low emf. We did a lot of kayaking and swimming and then day 3 or 4 realized we hadn’t seen any mouth breathing.

We finally weren’t reminding him to close his mouth. The changes are stable for now, but we are actively looking to buy a home with more green space and quadruple our lot size. The emf here isn’t great on our quarter acre suburban lot.

I’m finally grasping more of the pieces of the puzzle. Dr Jacks recent talk with Alexis Cowan helped me to begin to understand the regulation of heart rate, breathing influenced by sunlight.

His posture and strength is much improved also. I’m getting it, Dr. Jack! Our son is our canary in the coal mine. I know we are affected also by this environment.

Thank you again. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### Niki Stanton — 2024-09-19T23:52:28+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=335168)
>
> Keep going

Dr. Jack, we finally had our telehealth visit with the Mito program at CHOP. It felt more like a genetics visit. Their only recommendation was to do whole genome sequencing and in the meantime give Mito supplements. They also said the mitoswab has been found to be unreliable.

Meanwhile we are in a bit of a reverse Florida winter. We’ve had days and days straight of heat warnings and it’s just unbearably hot. I’m also 9 weeks pregnant and my tolerance for that has been pretty low.

I think I can guess next steps: keep going on the outside time, find that low emf house. We probably will increase his NAG dose because I believe we accidentally discovered he does better on 3x daily. Just wish I knew why he needed it.

(Can’t remember if I had posted it in this thread but he didn’t shed a single tear until age 6 when we added NAG. If we miss he gets bad eye irritation).

Still just really grateful for this info.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-24T18:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-yr-old-redox-melanin-and-mito-rx-journal.28920/post-339959>

How do you create higher agency children from low dopamine germlines from parents?

Many ways, but here is an easy one.

Today's lesson is in a thread format on transgenerational epigenetics. If you enjoy it share it.

The difference between "should and will" is a massive chasm for the low agency thinker. Every person in life painfully experiences the chasm between their inner vision and its ultimate expression in reality. The chasm is never completely bridged. We all have the conviction, perhaps illusory, that we have much more to say than appears in our actions when we take control of our beliefs to make better choices. Here is how those choices can be improved daily.

### Niki Stanton — 2026-03-28T22:54:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-yr-old-redox-melanin-and-mito-rx-journal.28920/post-369845>

Update: our son Jacob is 11 now. I had a surprise third baby who presented almost identical to him- horrible persistent newborn “eye goop” and ZERO tears. For context: Jacob didn’t cry a single tear until age six when we started supplementing with N-acetyl glucosamine on a wing and a prayer after I unearthed an article about a genetic condition involving alacrima that was helped with the supplemental “NAG”. I didn’t want to wait six years for this baby to have proper tears so I diy it and gave him NAG and sure enough he started producing tears and no more stuck eyelashes. So we thought surely something would be revealed on WGS. Nada. Consulted the top docs for disorders of glycolysation and it’s a head scratcher.

The baby presents similarly with issues of oral motor and some facial muscle asymmetry though mild, likely untrained eye would not see it. His motor skills and connection are much better than Jacob’s - likely because of all we know about light, avoiding jabs, etc.

I hit an emotional wall with trying to understand the why and the implications of this tear thing. We upped Jacob’s dose of NAG and his night time photophobia went away. But now I’m ready to dig back in and see what we can discover for them both. Jacob’s development is coming along but it’s still a rollercoaster. It seems that he needs every box ticked perfectly to just keep moving and even then he has days where the floor seems to fall out in certain areas. As parents, it’s just tough. To see him struggle and to not be sure exactly what he needs and what’s going on with him metabolically.

We thought pans type ocd flare episodes were behind us and he was doing REALLY well in some areas. We added in a few new activities to his schedule and it seems the emotional and cognitive load tipped him and he tanked and had panic episodes, food aversions, sleep disturbances. So we scaled back and did a lot of nervous system regulation, MNRI (reflex integration) which helps him a ton, though doesn’t seem to stick. And now I’m being more mindful about getting adequate sun exposure on the torso which I believe we had let fall to the wayside.

We are also gearing up for a move to a house in a more rural area with an acre and heavy woods to get him a better emf and noise/light environment. Our suburb has gotten so busy. We will be able to install the emf safe kill switch as well.

As always I’m sharing because I’m open to feedback.

Question about fluorescent lighting: we avoid 99% of the time and are thankful that we can since we homeschool. But for the hour on Sunday during church are there any glasses that would help mitigate some of the effects?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-28T23:10:24+0200

No. I would not put him in any environment that harms him. That includes church. Face dunking & DDW will help and I would tell you to read this thread.

Any DDW you can source on Amazon would fine. I think the lowest amount of ppm you can affrod with solar light to the eyes will augment that N-acetylglucosamine (NAG/GlcNAc) supplementation. Why?

NAG is a primary experimental intervention for **NGLY1 deficiency**, a rare genetic condition where alacrima (the inability to produce tears) is a hallmark symptom. Very similar to the story in the thread I gave you to read above.

From the decentralized perspective of the **human Lagrangian**, this "wing and a prayer" works because it addresses a fundamental **structural and isotopic collapse** at the cellular level.

**1\. The NGLY1 "Clog" and the Deuterium Bomb**
In a healthy system, the **NGLY1** enzyme acts as the "garbage collector" for the Endoplasmic Reticulum (ER), cleaving glycans from misfolded proteins so they can be recycled.

**The Blueprint Failure:** When NGLY1 is deficient, these misfolded glycoproteins cannot be properly degraded. They accumulate as "toxic aggregates," clogging the **S8-Ferredoxin tunnel** and increasing the **Ohmic resistance** of the cell. All of this is discussed in decentralized medicine #100.

**The Isotopic Trap:** This cellular "trash" is inherently "heavy." It traps **deuterium** in the cytosol, ruining the 3rd and 4th "bends" of critical signaling proteins. The result is a **deuterium bomb** that stops the high-speed optical signaling required for tear production.

**2\. NAG as a "Bypass" and Isotopic Exhaust**
NAG supplementation (GlcNAc) provides the raw material to bypass this "clogged" recycling pathway.

**Restoring the GlcNAc-Asn Bond:** By flooding the system with NAG, you provide the building blocks for proper **N-glycosylation** that the cell can no longer "recycle" from its own waste.

**The Deuterium Egress:** When you provide clean, supplemental NAG, you allow the cell to build "fresh" glycoproteins. This helps shift the **dielectric environment** back toward a high-capacitance state (closer to 160). It effectively acts as a **deuterium egress** by allowing the cell to finally "take out the trash" that was previously holding the heavy isotopes hostage.

**3\. Aquaporins and the "Water Gate"**
One of the most critical "victims" of the NGLY1 collapse is the **Aquaporin** channel. This will help improve cognitive and motor skills of the brain where AQA4 gates are big.

**The Secretion Switch:** NGLY1 is known to regulate the expression of **Aquaporins**, the "water gates" of the lacrimal gland.

**The Optical Fix:** Without NGLY1, aquaporin expression drops, and the eye goes dry. NAG helps restore the **post-translational "bend"** in the proteins that control these water gates. By clearing the deuterium "grit" from the **microtubule-aquaporin interface**, NAG allows the "1878 nm harmonic" (red light in sun) to vibrate the tears out of the gland once more.

4\. The Decentralized Cynical Prophet’s Verdict

Alacrima isn't just a "lack of water"; it’s an **optical signal failure** caused by an isotopic "fry-out" in the lacrimal functional unit. The "expert" advice for dry eyes usually involves "chemical noise" (drops), but the **NAG/GlcNAc bypass** targets the **Blue Print**. It un-weights the system, allowing the **human antenna** to sense the need for lubrication and respond with **160-dielectric water**.

### digitald3 — 2026-05-02T17:08:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-yr-old-redox-melanin-and-mito-rx-journal.28920/post-372806>

I’m sure someone has come up with a correct way to do it. But in my head the goal is to reduce D+ consumption. So whatever means you choose is a step in the right direction. I chose to take a 40oz water bottle and dump a 16 oz bottle of 25ppm water in it then add enough filtered water to just under full and that is about 92ppm of ddw water. I did that for a week or so then I moved to a 32oz bottle and that lowered the ddw to about 87 or so. Then you can work down from there. I like working out of a water bottle then I can also keep track of how much water I’m drinking. Not as a goal just a data point. I don’t try to over drink. Just drink when thirsty

# Thread: Aloha, from Maui HI
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-maui-hi.28923/>

### Aylagustafson — 2024-05-20T01:23:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aloha-from-maui-hi.28923/post-334908>

Super excited to be here. My name is Ayla, I have a masters degree in Chinese Medicine and had a fair amount of western medical training. I am currently raising two kiddos on my own, and came across Dr. Kruse about a year ago when facing health challenges with my 7 year old. He had two brain surgeries in 2022 at 5 years old- Juvenile Pylocytic Astrocytoma - luckily benign… but surgeries couldnt remove some that is wrapped around the brain stem. I have been searching for alternative means to support him. Upon finding Dr. Kruse I completely resonated with his way of seeing… and immediately started doing sunrise daily with my kids and wearing blue blockers at night. I also had him start DDW…. Last MRI scan was the end of Oct 2023 and we had about a 1/2 cm shrinkage on all sides of the existing tumor (after 5 months of sunrise, 7 months of Hydrogen inhalation for 3 hrs a day, and 3 months of DDW). Our next MRI is in about a month - hoping we are still moving in the same positive direction. I know how Dr Kruse feels about hydrogen inhalation but would like to hear more of his thoughts given some of the research studies ive briefly looked at, and the success we have seen (tho maybe it has nothing to do w hydrogen ) I also find it incredibly important to work on healing trauma - my son’s tumor symptoms began when he found out his father had passed away, and there was A LOT OF other trauma prior to this… I use a quantum bed daily which has helped to re-train our nervous systems. Healing is tricky if our nervous systems are stuck in fight flight all the time.

Ultimately I can feel so deeply in my being the truth about light and water… my life has changed immensely the more and more I learn.
So happy to be here!
Ayla

After second surgery it does seem to now be just slow growing— and a good sign that we had shrinkage between our march 2023 and october 2023 scans… which was when i started treating it like a mitochondrial dna issue rather than genetic ( they say its a genetic mutation BRAF- KIAA1549 gene fusion). # Thread: TRUST MOVEMENT

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-20T07:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trust-movement.28925/post-334916>

Take a bold and fearless approach to life, trust yourself to use your God given abilities to navigate any challenges that come your way.

-   Embracing uncertainty and taking calculated risks to achieve success.
-   Letting go of analysis paralysis and taking action instead of overthinking.
-   Trusting your inner wisdom and intuition to guide your decisions.
-   Being more proactive and assertive in your life, rather than waiting for others to take action.
-   Developing a sense of self-confidence and self-trust, which can lead to greater success and fulfillment.

Trust movement. Movement happens in the mind and then in the body. Life unfolds at the level of events, not of words. Trust movement, trust instinct, trust yourself. Then dominate.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-21T16:48:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trust-movement.28925/post-334963>

#2 is my biggest weakness from that list

I don't think it is really a trust issue for me though. It stems from desiring the best out of every circumstance in life and then overanalyzing every detail in an attempt to ensure the absolute best outcome.

What sucks is I realize just how much this quality holds me back in life, yet I continue to do it.

My life seems like never ending analysis paralysis sometimes.

### emaret — 2024-05-20T21:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-just-starting-on-my-journey.28926/post-334933>

Hey!
Joined recently and have been enjoying going through all the excellent resources.

I live in London (having moved here from Australia (35 latitude around 6 years ago)). I was diagnosed with Rheumatoid arthritis around 7 years ago and it's been a bit of and up and down journey. I have primarily managed to control the progression of my auto immune disease with diet (primarily carnivore) rather than medication, but in the last year and a half, being strict with my diet is no longer as effective. I am currently making my way through the blogs, listening to the recordings and implementing things such as grounding, getting out for sunrise/sunset, timing of meals but obviously much more to do - including looking at options to move out of London! Really keen to learn and if anyone has any resources, they think would be particularly useful for RA, would love to be pointed in that direction.

# Thread: NIRA bulbs (test pics included) is NIRA false advertising here or are these bulbs worth it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nira-bulbs-test-pics-included-is-nira-false-advertising-here-or-are-these-bulbs-worth-it.28931/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeffgibson2024 — 2024-05-22T02:19:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nira-bulbs-test-pics-included-is-nira-false-advertising-here-or-are-these-bulbs-worth-it.28931/post-334976>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/), anyone thoughts on this? I knew before ordering these that there's **NO WAY these NIRA bulbs could output as much IR as they advertise (steady levels of IR-A/B up to 3000nm as indicated in the chart on the box see bottom left of pic)** and I just confirmed it with spectrometer.

My questions are:
1) is this still enough IR to be biologically active / offset the damage of blue spike that's still present?
2) is it possible that this LED bulb really outputs a decent amount of IR-A and IR-B all the way up to 3000nm?! max measuring ability for device like mine is up to 1100nm so I have no way of telling, but I can't imagine it really outputs that much or it'd be putting off too much heat (it's BARELY warm to the touch after having it on an hour)
3) I realize these are horrible for night-time use, obvs. I only ordered these to test them out bc I was curious and I'm only using them in my office to add IR to help balance my computer screens i'm on all day. Am I better off just pointing my EMR-TEK Firedragon up at the ceiling to keep the balance (and open the window when weather allows) and using f.lux / Iris to mitigate the blue instead?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

FYI, this spectrometer reading is from Hopocolor OHSP-350F at distance of 1ft w/ top of bulb pointing directly at device. Spectrometers normally go up to 850nm or so, but I had this one customized/ modified to read up to 1100nm from Hopocolor on Alibababa so I could measure my RLT devices.
If you want one here's [Link to Hopocolor on Alibaba](https://www.alibaba.com/product-detail/China-factory-OHSP350F-Flicker-Tester-Optical_62285494289.html)\- they'll try to tell you $2,000, you can talk them down to $1850 plus shipping if anyone's interested in getting one of these, they're great.

lea\[/USER\]

42% of outside light is IR, I have a 850nm IR (security) light, in my face, when I have to do computer screen time. No heat. No bright (visible) light to distract from the screen. Not as good as being under the ”big glowy thing” outside but I can’t see my screen outside anyway.

[@Lukassss](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26459/) i wish i'd have known before i ordered my spectrometer...that apparently i could have had the manufacturer modify it to read as low as 230nm also... at least it appears possible for this device, because i noticed other day that Blue Light Diet author has one that measures UV-A/B, wish i'd have gotten 230-1100nm instead...didn't think it was possible to measure UV like this w/out a separate specialized UV device.

to answer your question, i didn't hack my device or anything to modify it myself. i just paid a little extra to have Hopocolor do it, i assume they just upgrade the light sensor component and modifies the software so it displays new ranges, etc.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=334996)
>
> Your spectrometer shows marketing is legalized lying.

Big brands who are in competition with other big brands usually can’t afford to lie, because their competitors come in with a legal team and a class action suit immediately.

Just as info from my times as an advertiser. It works well for a buying guide… in free market economies that is.

As soon as the government sponsors, permits, regulates, or subsidized, that beautiful tool of capitalism is nixed.

Hello all. I left the defense engineering world six years ago and have been working as an ocean rescue lifeguard /EMT since 2019. I am new to this forum and most of the topics covered here, and very interested in learning all I can! I'm especially interested in learning and practicing the key principles of good health as I'm presently working through a not-so-fun health challenge. It appears my enlarged prostate completely shut down my ability to pass urine and I ended up in the ER the other night and now have a catheter and an appt next week with a urologist. I had known about the prostate issue but thought that my 'good' diet and exercise regimen would improve it or at least keep it from worsening. Know better, do better I guess.
Dr. Kruse, I've only recently learned of you (via several podcasts), but I want to say thank you for boldly living and speaking truth, and for making such effort to share it with so many.

### Emmanuela — 2024-05-25T19:46:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmanuela%E2%80%99s-journal.28948/post-335142>

I am working on reaching optimal for me and my little ones, especially my eldest daughter.
This is my backstory:
My mother was raised in the country in Italy - Canale monterano in 42 latitute on a homestead type farm until her teen years when she moved to Montreal (45.5 lat)
I was born naturally, no drugs but in a hospital. I was raised a strict vegetarian (no eggs) and my parents were anti technology. I spent most of my life up until grade 9 outdoors. No computer was allowed and no tv in the house. I moved to Ontario 43lat when i was 11.
i had major menstrual cycle issues from almost the very beginning, all the classic endometriosis symptoms. Extremely heavy bleeding, i could not leave the house. I used herbs to eventually slow it down a bit but when i finished school and started dating, i went on birth control for 9 months at 21. I started developing weakness, fatigue, and my pain got much worse along with my heavy bleeding. When i finally ditched the pill, i had terrible nausea, could not eat anything much for several months. Once my appetite returned, i started my first indoor job which then led to mt first office job. I gained weight for the first time in my life and suffered hypoglycemia symptoms.
At first i went down the food train, started eating seafood then meat for the first time in my life and things got a little better.
I got pregnant and had my first daughter (now 6) just before i turned 28 naturally at home. Long story short, she was jaudiced perhaps due to me being robbed of the first few hours with her due to silly midwives and she got zapped under the blue lights at the hospital. She has struggled majorly with sleep, low appetite, and behavioral issues ever since when exposed to blue light.
I nurses her for 2.5 years

I struggled a lot with restlessness at night and restless leg syndrome. I took liver capsules and that seemed to help my sleep.
I also had major night time hunger.

I gained too much weight when pregnant and my own menstrual and ovulation pain and bleeding got so bad, I scrounged up money and found an endometriosis surgeon in the US and had surgery and all my endo symptoms finally completely went away.
I still struggled with losing my weight, brain fog, low blood sugar at times. Forgetting often, dropping things.
I also started using sunscreen for the first time regularly because i started being vain and bought into the nonsense about sun and aging. I developed tons of dark spots and melasma.
I did a frequent eating program that slowly allowed me to lose almost all my weight save the last 7lb when i had my second daughter now almost 2 at home. My energy was better, but i still could not go more than 3 hours without eating and struggled with being hangry, shaky, and forgetful.
I stopped wearing sunscreen, threw it all out and spent a little more time outdoors and my spots started to fade.

Then i found Jack Kruse’s work several months ago. And i was very skeptical at first but insanely curious.
So far what i have done:
\- i see the sunrise almost every morning unless sick
\-take my kids with me often to see the sunrise
\-tan in the early morning in my kiniki outside and during the day unless i feel i need to seek shade
\- put iris or equivalent on the tv, phones, and my computer
\- quit almost all tv watching
\- work outside unless it’s raining as I am currently a mortgage broker and work on a computer
\- switched every possible bulb to incandescent and added uva bulbs and red lights around the house
\- i keep my led lights off as much as humanly possible unless i am cleaning and it’s raining out
\- wear blue blockers in the evening and when under artificial light

What i need to do:
\- get some labs, i am going through Dr Jack’s articles and posts to make a comprehensive list as I suspect adrenal fatigue
\- do the leptin reset to lose the last 7lb because i want to, maybe i am vain
\- move (this will take some time) and make regular trips south for more quantum yield

Currently:
My daughter (6) has greatly improved her sleep and appetite but still struggles if she is in school (i need to pull her out- working on that)
I am still struggling with fatigue and forgetfulness and stress.
Sleep is better but I am still nursing my almost 2 year old daughter and intend to continue until she is 3 or stops on her own.
Blocking blue light has helped with my nightime hunger but it still happens sometimes
I get very restless when falling asleep occasionally.

I now eat a BAB and can go 5 hours without eating. I struggle with just 3 meals a day though and get wobbly and dizzy if my carb intake drops below 100ish grams

Watching the sunrise and spending my whole day outside helps but there are still missing pieces I am learning to piece together

I will add my blood test results when i get them and add on as i improve and learn new things here.

### Consequences of Low-Carb Diets​

-   Keto diets can provide immediate symptom relief and rapid weight loss, but may worsen health in the long run due to the reliance on **gluconeogenesis**, a less optimal metabolic pathway for producing glucose internally
-   Low-carb diets fail to improve metabolic flexibility, inducing physiological insulin resistance instead, as they do not address underlying issues with glucose metabolism. Essential body parts, including the brain, muscles during strength training, and various organs, require carbohydrates for optimal functioning, highlighting **the inefficiency of forcing the body to produce its own glucose through gluconeogenesis**
-   Relying excessively on **gluconeogenesis** for glucose production is **energetically costly and inefficient**, requiring a significant energy investment for a relatively low energy return, and is stimulated by stress hormones, potentially leading to muscle loss over time
-   Low-carb diets **can hinder thyroid function** by reducing the liver's ability to convert T4 into the active thyroid hormone T3, essential for regulating metabolism, due to a decrease in glucose availability for the liver
-   Reintroducing carbohydrates in a balanced manner can improve metabolism and overall health, countering the negative effects of long-term low-carb dieting by enhancing the body's energy production and usage

\=====================================
I suspect adrenal fatigue
Adrenal fatigue is really very rare.

### Brandon Bazan-Torres — 2026-05-01T07:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmanuela%E2%80%99s-journal.28948/post-372710>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=335142)
>
> I am working on reaching optimal for me and my little ones, especially my eldest daughter.
> This is my backstory:
> My mother was raised in the country in Italy - Canale monterano in 42 latitute on a homestead type farm until her teen years when she moved to Montreal (45.5 lat)
> I was born naturally, no drugs but in a hospital. I was raised a strict vegetarian (no eggs) and my parents were anti technology. I spent most of my life up until grade 9 outdoors. No computer was allowed and no tv in the house. I moved to Ontario 43lat when i was 11.
> i had major menstrual cycle issues from almost the very beginning, all the classic endometriosis symptoms. Extremely heavy bleeding, i could not leave the house. I used herbs to eventually slow it down a bit but when i finished school and started dating, i went on birth control for 9 months at 21. I started developing weakness, fatigue, and my pain got much worse along with my heavy bleeding. When i finally ditched the pill, i had terrible nausea, could not eat anything much for several months. Once my appetite returned, i started my first indoor job which then led to mt first office job. I gained weight for the first time in my life and suffered hypoglycemia symptoms.
> At first i went down the food train, started eating seafood then meat for the first time in my life and things got a little better.
> I got pregnant and had my first daughter (now 6) just before i turned 28 naturally at home. Long story short, she was jaudiced perhaps due to me being robbed of the first few hours with her due to silly midwives and she got zapped under the blue lights at the hospital. She has struggled majorly with sleep, low appetite, and behavioral issues ever since when exposed to blue light.
> I nurses her for 2.5 years
>
> I struggled a lot with restlessness at night and restless leg syndrome. I took liver capsules and that seemed to help my sleep.
> I also had major night time hunger.
>
> I gained too much weight when pregnant and my own menstrual and ovulation pain and bleeding got so bad, I scrounged up money and found an endometriosis surgeon in the US and had surgery and all my endo symptoms finally completely went away.
> I still struggled with losing my weight, brain fog, low blood sugar at times. Forgetting often, dropping things.
> I also started using sunscreen for the first time regularly because i started being vain and bought into the nonsense about sun and aging. I developed tons of dark spots and melasma.
> I did a frequent eating program that slowly allowed me to lose almost all my weight save the last 7lb when i had my second daughter now almost 2 at home. My energy was better, but i still could not go more than 3 hours without eating and struggled with being hangry, shaky, and forgetful.
> I stopped wearing sunscreen, threw it all out and spent a little more time outdoors and my spots started to fade.
>
> Then i found Jack Kruse’s work several months ago. And i was very skeptical at first but insanely curious.
> So far what i have done:
> \- i see the sunrise almost every morning unless sick
> \-take my kids with me often to see the sunrise
> \-tan in the early morning in my kiniki outside and during the day unless i feel i need to seek shade
> \- put iris or equivalent on the tv, phones, and my computer
> \- quit almost all tv watching
> \- work outside unless it’s raining as I am currently a mortgage broker and work on a computer
> \- switched every possible bulb to incandescent and added uva bulbs and red lights around the house
> \- i keep my led lights off as much as humanly possible unless i am cleaning and it’s raining out
> \- wear blue blockers in the evening and when under artificial light
>
> What i need to do:
> \- get some labs, i am going through Dr Jack’s articles and posts to make a comprehensive list as I suspect adrenal fatigue
> \- do the leptin reset to lose the last 7lb because i want to, maybe i am vain
> \- move (this will take some time) and make regular trips south for more quantum yield
>
> Currently:
> My daughter (6) has greatly improved her sleep and appetite but still struggles if she is in school (i need to pull her out- working on that)
> I am still struggling with fatigue and forgetfulness and stress.
> Sleep is better but I am still nursing my almost 2 year old daughter and intend to continue until she is 3 or stops on her own.
> Blocking blue light has helped with my nightime hunger but it still happens sometimes
> I get very restless when falling asleep occasionally.
>
> I now eat a BAB and can go 5 hours without eating. I struggle with just 3 meals a day though and get wobbly and dizzy if my carb intake drops below 100ish grams
>
> Watching the sunrise and spending my whole day outside helps but there are still missing pieces I am learning to piece together
>
> I will add my blood test results when i get them and add on as i improve and learn new things here.

Loved the podcast with jack watched the whole thing was great

next payment (principal and interest)

complete principals of next few payments

Next month the same, and so on.

# Thread: UTI, bladder biofilm, "prostatitis"
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uti-bladder-biofilm-prostatitis.28953/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pebbles — 2024-05-27T19:37:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uti-bladder-biofilm-prostatitis.28953/post-335191>

Patients are treated for above with long term antibiotic treatment, 5-6 month or more, a combination of 2-3-4 antibiotics at ones, and they do not get better. Majority get floxxed. And here it is the reason, why it does not work:

In this video Dr. Harry Horsley, a senior research fellow and principal investigator for the Bladder Infection and Immunity Group (BIIG) at UCL, discusses biofilms. ● Having suffered from recurrent UTIs himself, Dr. Horsley ultimately founded a state-of-the-art biological imaging suite which he currently runs. ● Biofilms in the bladder can not be detected effectively by taking a biopsy. ● At the clinic Dr. Horsley is associated with, patient urine samples are tested for chronic UTI via urine microscopy. ● The presence of white blood cells, or pyuria, in a urine sample indicates an immune response. ● When bacteria is present in the bladder lining (the epithelium), the body sheds the epithelial cells in defense. These bacteria-filled cells are detectable in a urine sample. ● Less sophisticated testing methods classify any sample containing epithelial cells as contaminated because they can’t be proven to originate from the urinary tract. ● Urine microscopy, however, can establish the source and detect the presence of biofilm encasing the epithelial cells. ● This biofilm layer protects the bacteria from the patient’s immune system or antibiotic treatment. ● There are many different types of epithelial cells and not all of them, even of the same type, will be covered in biofilm. This is why biopsy is not an effective method of diagnosis as the sample is too narrow. ● Patients may suspect the presence of epithelial cells in their own urine if their sample is cloudy. Cloudiness, however, can also be caused by harmless crystal formations or mucus. ● The life cycle of bacteria requires the cells to emerge from any biofilm in order to divide. It is during this phase that long term antibiotic protocols may successfully eradicate them. ● If they are not eradicated it is thought that this may be indicative of the cells burrowing deeper into the bladder wall. ● These types of infections are unlikely to be the result of a single bacteria, but rather a community of different bacteria. These communities likely differ from patient to patient, making treatment immensely challenging. ● Dr. Horsley and his team are currently digitally analyzing over 40,000 images of urine samples and comparing them against patient symptoms in order to discern patterns, and shape future UTI treatment and further research.
[
](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pq1K1zBt7Ho&fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAAR38GP0b_VOZIRTVLNqNJYi7Cu6uc6n-fsJluGKsNLIF61a5g8XbUfv4OBM_aem_ZmFrZWR1bW15MTZieXRlcw)

[YOUTUBE.COM
What do Bladder Biofilms Look Like? Imaging biofilms with Dr. Harry Horsley.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pq1K1zBt7Ho&fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAAR21fnFXMUwF5AzTYRTdlRYlY2rjyjZVEnFf8v_0m7GrzhBG2JtS9EJY4Xo_aem_ZmFrZWR1bW15MTZieXRlcw)

# Thread: Transgenerational epigenetics and mtDNA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transgenerational-epigenetics-and-mtdna.28956/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-28T04:49:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transgenerational-epigenetics-and-mtdna.28956/post-335215>

Did you know our Mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is inherited exclusively from our mothers? This finding was discovered by Dr. Doug Wallace. This is important because the environment a mother experiences during pregnancy directly affects the mtDNA health of her baby. An important measurement of mtDNA health and function is something called % heterosplasmy.

As % heteroplasmy rises in mtDNA due to energy deficiencies caused by the environment, so does the prevalence of genetic mutations leading to progressive aging & diseases. When an expectant mother lives in a stressful environment, marked by disrupted light cycles, toxins, and radiation. The baby is born with a higher heteroplasmy rate. This means the baby’s mtDNA could function like that of a much older person, leading to earlier aging and disease. This may explain why children today are getting sicker compared to previous generations, due to our increasingly toxic environments.

This means that although at birth the baby is physically 0 years old, biologically speaking, their mtDNA may be born as a 40 year old, and thus aging and disease will manifest sooner than that of a baby born with a low heteroplasmy rate. This is why our children are becoming more and more sick today when compared to previous generations.

This is because humanity has created a chronically toxic environment. The brain, the heart and the immune system contain the most mitochondria in the body. Is it a coincidence that heart disease, neurodegeneration and autoimmune diseases are booming? Could it be related to high % heteroplasmy rates in our mtDNA? I think so.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2024-05-29T22:45:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transgenerational-epigenetics-and-mtdna.28956/post-335265>

Makes me think of the 9 y/o boy with neuroblastoma that I asked you about awhile back. He passed away in April after a 5 year battle. He was conceived via IVF. The fact that the mother needed IVF to conceive tells me her mtDNA was not good. Centralized medicine encourages overriding these warnings by the body just to make money...and inevitably ending in heartbreak. They had a forever patient before he was even conceived. Still grieving his loss. And I was just his cheerleader. He taught me a lot.

### Aylagustafson — 2024-05-30T01:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transgenerational-epigenetics-and-mtdna.28956/post-335268>

This answers some of my questions around the effect of trauma on children (at least in utero). If a stressful environment, marked by disrupted light cycles, toxins, and radiation causes a higher heteroplasmy rate… I would presume that any stressor (emotional as well) would have a similar effect — and this is on top of the mothers mitochondrial DNA (mine) being weak to begin with...

My son was in utero when his father was leaving us for a younger woman and it felt like my entire world was collapsing. Then his first two years were marked by his grandfather (my father) coming to live with us to help me raise two very young children…until I discovered he was sexually abusing them. Their father (although not in their life), then Overdosed and died and my son started vomiting daily, and losing balance… whereupon we discovered he had a large brain tumor in his cerebellum, also wrapped around the brainstem.

My question is this…. In cases of emotional trauma where its not just fight/flight but severe enough where parts of body tissues go into parasympathetic freeze…. life or death situations for infants like sexual abuse… wouldnt this effect the body’s ability to make UV light in those “frozen” areas…and then those cells would potentially try to migrate to other parts of the body where there is light/energy available? Just wondering about this connection.

In my own experience (with many health issues for most of my life) — until I “remembered” and processed the emotions around my own sexual trauma for example, i was unable to resolve any of the chronic conditions. Then within weeks, things like Candida that i had dealt with for decades resolved on its own…. I heard Dr. Kruse mention that Candida is usually a result of high glucose and high non native EMF’s in an environment…. so can unresolved traumas in and of themselves….created by these freeze states in certain tissues, have the same effect on the mitochondria? Then once “light” or awareness is brought to these areas… resolution can take place.
