# Thread: Christine's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal.28959/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### christine_fp — 2024-05-28T16:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal.28959/post-335228>

Hello,

I haven't posted much on here yet, so I am going to start with my journal section. I'm hesitant and anxious, but not sure what for anymore. I've had a breast cancer diagnosis in the last couple months, I'm 36 and I've been avoiding mammograms up until this point, out of fear and trying to protect myself and control something in a situation I feel I have little control over. An earlier biopsy confirmed cancer in the left side. I had an MRI done and was told that it showed some "abnormalities" in the right side. So, my surgeon has strongly encouraged me to get a mammogram done to see if the right side has pre-cancerous cells or not as the MRI may be misleading and it could just be normal breast tissue. So, I am reluctantly off to go get that done today and a biopsy if one is needed. I'm told it has positive receptors for estrogen and progesterone and that that's "good"...relative to being negative I'm assuming. I've got an ND specializing in breast health telling me to be more vegetarian and stay away from meat and dairy (regardless of anything being organic, grass fed, A2), and consume more seeds, beans and soy, while my surgeon said to stay away from soy. Ultimately I've just been trying to eat what makes sense for my area and that includes (good quality) meat and animal products. Everyone has a different opinion. I know that food isn't going to be the most important thing.

I've had a hard time maintaining any hope for the last little while as I'm questioning everything that may need to be done, how a bilateral mastectomy will impact hormones and put me into menopause. I'm struggling with this a lot and I have some really bad days. My surgeon right now doesn't recommend a bilateral and is thinking I won't need to do chemo, but salvaging tissue and radiating what's left doesn't seem like an ideal option either. I have some strong core beliefs that my body won't be able to heal and I'll always be haunted with this and a few years down the road it'll be somewhere else. This has been hard for me to try and change my language on.

My partner and I have been scrambling to figure out how to move come fall (we're in southern Ontario), and at the very least how to get me moved south and him to move later, we're definitely not rich people and Canada right now is making it challenging to even buy food let alone being able to afford living anywhere. Right now we're searching for a more ideal living situation (getting out of our 7-storey apartment building), and otherwise I've adjusted my diet to include more seafood, and trying to maintain more ketogenic for my location. My last vit D test a few weeks ago was at 80nmol, though it will be higher than this by now given the amount I've been outside plus I've been supplementing at least 10,000IUs per day. I've been making sure I'm getting more full torso sun and getting into morning light. I keep my kiniki on underneath so I can just take my shirt off when I can. I got some kiniki beach wraps and my boyfriend making me some tops out of them so I can wear something tan through all the time. I've always tanned easily and well, though it seems like its one of those situations where I've got a tan but light is leaking out faster than I can put it in. Needless to say I am not surprisingly a huge magnet for mosquitoes. I don't know all the things that make my situation different from someone else's as for the last 5 or 6 years I've made a lot of changes to my lifestyle in terms of sun/light environment, food, grounding, and always slowly improving things and now I feel I've run out of time. I guess that's the whole lesson I'm learning. You never have as much time as you think you do and nature doesn't really give a shit.

### JanSz — 2024-05-28T16:47:21+0300

You are Arborist.
In a way that likely means that you spent lots of time the way we would consider highly healthy.

From what I can tell, breast cancers are now the easiest to live with.
Also, no to bilateral mastectomy, just locally take out what has to be taken out.
\-----

You should learn how steroid hormones work and how to adjust them, so they are optimal as much as possible, including diet.

Eventually you should test there your hormones, but first listen to most of the videos at least once. All available any place in the world that internet and credit cards work. Videos are free to get education.

### christine_fp — 2024-10-22T19:29:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal.28959/post-341393>

Update synopsis. Had a lumpectomy done in July with 2 positive lymph nodes. Cancer was hormone positive, HER2 negative. Suggested follow up was chemo, radiation and 5 years of hormone therapy. To say I'm not okay with these things is an understatement. It took me a long time to be convinced to do surgery. I decided to not take that route. I started taking iverm3ctin in mid September and as of Oct 20th have moved to El Salvador for the winter. I had a CT scan done before I left which was negative for metastasis. A PET scan was booked for November but I did not want to push my flight later as I think getting out was most important. I may see if I can get one in El Salvador.

Vit D level in July was 229nmol, with sun and supplementation. I have stopped taking supplements since I've moved.

I want to make a list of other chronic issues I've had to keep track of what resolves itself:
\- tinnitus for 17 years
\- mild, localized eczema for 17 years
\- pruritus ani for 8 years, only positive pathogen has been blastocystis
\- intolerant to cold...fingers, toes, nose often cold
\- sinusitis for 4 or 5 years
\- hair falls out easily
\- low myopic for 20 years, prescription is -2.25 and -2.50
\- indigestion, gut discomfort, off and on diarrhea since my surgery in July
\- irregular menstrual cycles throughout my 20's but have become regular in mid 30's

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-17T17:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal.28959/post-344029>

ATTENTION MEMBERS: GET SUN OR GET CANCER

When considering your exposure to the sun, make sure it's your choice, not your dermatologist's.

In this paper, which your dermatologist ignores, we see that....

"The findings of the current study confirm solar UV-B radiation is associated with reduced risk of cancer of the breast, colon, ovary, and prostate as well as non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Eight additional malignancies exhibited an inverse correlation between mortality rates and UV-B...."

**This means more sun = less cancer risk.**

The paper goes on to say that....

"**Conclusions:** The results of the current study demonstrate that much of the geographic variation in cancer mortality rates in the U.S. can be attributed to variations in solar UV-B radiation exposure. Thus, many lives could be extended through increased careful exposure to solar UV-B radiation..."

So if you'd like to get cancer faster but have lighter to vampire-like white skin (depending on what skin color you are starting with), by all means, follow your dermatologist's advice and avoid the sun.

But if you would like to extend your cancer-free time here on Earth, maybe do the opposite and follow the mitochondriacs advice that is blasted all over this website.

>
> I haven't posted much on here yet, so I am going to start with my journal section. I'm hesitant and anxious, but not sure what for anymore. I've had a breast cancer diagnosis in the last couple months, I'm 36 and I've been avoiding mammograms up until this point, out of fear and trying to protect myself and control something in a situation I feel I have little control over. An earlier biopsy confirmed cancer in the left side. I had an MRI done and was told that it showed some "abnormalities" in the right side. So, my surgeon has strongly encouraged me to get a mammogram done to see if the right side has pre-cancerous cells or not as the MRI may be misleading and it could just be normal breast tissue. So, I am reluctantly off to go get that done today and a biopsy if one is needed. I'm told it has positive receptors for estrogen and progesterone and that that's "good"...relative to being negative I'm assuming. I've got an ND specializing in breast health telling me to be more vegetarian and stay away from meat and dairy (regardless of anything being organic, grass fed, A2), and consume more seeds, beans and soy, while my surgeon said to stay away from soy. Ultimately I've just been trying to eat what makes sense for my area and that includes (good quality) meat and animal products. Everyone has a different opinion. I know that food isn't going to be the most important thing.
>
> I've had a hard time maintaining any hope for the last little while as I'm questioning everything that may need to be done, how a bilateral mastectomy will impact hormones and put me into menopause. I'm struggling with this a lot and I have some really bad days. My surgeon right now doesn't recommend a bilateral and is thinking I won't need to do chemo, but salvaging tissue and radiating what's left doesn't seem like an ideal option either. I have some strong core beliefs that my body won't be able to heal and I'll always be haunted with this and a few years down the road it'll be somewhere else. This has been hard for me to try and change my language on.
>
> My partner and I have been scrambling to figure out how to move come fall (we're in southern Ontario), and at the very least how to get me moved south and him to move later, we're definitely not rich people and Canada right now is making it challenging to even buy food let alone being able to afford living anywhere. Right now we're searching for a more ideal living situation (getting out of our 7-storey apartment building), and otherwise I've adjusted my diet to include more seafood, and trying to maintain more ketogenic for my location. My last vit D test a few weeks ago was at 80nmol, though it will be higher than this by now given the amount I've been outside plus I've been supplementing at least 10,000IUs per day. I've been making sure I'm getting more full torso sun and getting into morning light. I keep my kiniki on underneath so I can just take my shirt off when I can. I got some kiniki beach wraps and my boyfriend making me some tops out of them so I can wear something tan through all the time. I've always tanned easily and well, though it seems like its one of those situations where I've got a tan but light is leaking out faster than I can put it in. Needless to say I am not surprisingly a huge magnet for mosquitoes. I don't know all the things that make my situation different from someone else's as for the last 5 or 6 years I've made a lot of changes to my lifestyle in terms of sun/light environment, food, grounding, and always slowly improving things and now I feel I've run out of time. I guess that's the whole lesson I'm learning. You never have as much time as you think you do and nature doesn't really give a shit.

Hi Christine, I'm a new member and recently had a 1:1 with Dr. Kruse and he suggested I reach out to you. I've read through your journal. Thank you for sharing your story and being vulnerable on here. Like you I have a breast cancer diagnoses and I'm looking for others on this forum who are going through similar challenges. I've worked very hard to do 'all the right things' however we never know do we? I'm currently also 'scrambling to move south' hoping I also haven't run out of time. I'm curious have you managed to move to the sun? I would love to connect with you. I don't think this is private so I'm happy to reach out personally too. love, Juli

P.S. I just read further and understand you moved to El Salvador in October. You're very brave. I hope this is going well for you. I'm also looking at El Salvador and hoping I can make a move very soon! I have a lot of research to do before making a decision. I'm sure you have some useful insights and experiences to share regarding the process you've been through. I hope to hear from you.

### christine_fp — 2025-03-13T19:32:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal.28959/post-348534>

Update March 2025

I have been in El Salvador for about 5 months now and have 2 more weeks before I go back home to southern Ontario for the summer. My plan is to come back again to ES in the fall. I have been doing and feeling well here overall and am trusting that my body has been renovating and regenerating in this environment. I have a fairly dark tan and have been maintaining it easily.

I copied a list of symptoms/issues I wrote down in my original when I first came down here:

"Vit D level in July was 229nmol, with sun and supplementation. I have stopped taking supplements since I've moved.
I want to make a list of other chronic issues I've had to keep track of what resolves itself:
\- tinnitus for 17 years
\- mild, localized eczema for 17 years
\- pruritus ani for 8 years, only positive pathogen has been blastocystis
\- intolerant to cold...fingers, toes, nose often cold
\- sinusitis for 4 or 5 years
\- hair falls out easily
\- low myopic for 20 years, prescription is -2.25 and -2.50
\- indigestion, gut discomfort, off and on diarrhea since my surgery in July
\- irregular menstrual cycles throughout my 20's but have become regular in mid 30's"

An update on this list:
\- I had my vit D checked a few weeks ago and it was 106ng/ml (265nmol) and this is just from sun, I'm happy with this but still think it should be higher
\- My tinnitus is still present, I know this will take longer to renovate the melanin in the ear
\- eczema is better but still present
\- I will learn more about my cold intolerance once I go back home, but my body has been craving cold the past few months which is unusual for me
\- my fine body hair, which turns gold in the summers, has turned completely white since being in ES
\- sinusitis hasn't been an issue (back home I'd often have a bout of sneezing when I'd wake up and then again once or twice during the day)
\- I've noticed when wearing my low correction glasses for computer work that my vision is the same without wearing them so it seems my vision has improved a little
\- digestion has been good, consuming primarily meat, seafood, eggs, raw dairy and local fruit
\- menstrual cycle has been consistent; had one month where I experienced surprisingly painful cramps for a couple hours which is uncommon for me

The primary thing I have noticed overall is the change in my sleep. I have been dreaming A LOT. Every night I am dreaming all kinds of weird things and can often remember many of them; and wake up every morning with that feeling that I've been somewhere all night. It's nice and feels fulfilling. It's been easy to wake up. Back home waking up was often challenging no matter how much sleep I got, with my sleep being broken and not rejuvenating.

I've had more muscle cramping, bones crackling since being here but that may be that I have been a lot more sedentary than I normally am compared to working back home and climbing trees (arborist) and just in general being more active.

I have also been high-dosing ivermectin the past couple months as per my protocol from Dr. Makis and will be able to have access to mebendazole and CBD oil when I get home. I'm not sure how long I will continue that protocol as I have a great deal of anxiety around lessening it even if my scans are good. I will do a thermogram when I go back home and possibly an ultrasound as well. I felt a couple of small granule/nodule-like bumps near my incision site recently, which feel like they are embedded in the scar tissue. I am trying not to panic and hoping they are granulomas from surgery? But I know nothing is for sure without imaging of some sort.

Grateful to be here, learning and trusting the process!

### christine_fp — 2025-03-17T21:57:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal.28959/post-348791>

Some symptoms updates I missed and family history I failed to include in my original post:

**Family History**
Me:
\- haplotype: U5b3b, predominantly German and French
\- birthed naturally, my mom was 37, there was a miscarriage prior to me where she had a c section
\- not breast fed
\- I had a "touch of bronchitis" as a baby once
\- received whatever vaccine protocol was standard in 1987 for babies, along with childhood vaccines
\- born with internally rotated femurs (pigeon-toed) and wore braces around my ankles attached to a bar to try straightening them
\- raised on a conventional dairy, beef, pork and crop farm; definite pesticide exposure, lots of physical labour, often outside and very active
\- very rarely sick
\- moved to city for university at 20 and started experiencing tinnitus and eczema
\- tested negative for BRCA mutations
\- not jabbed

Mom:
\- born to my grandma at 17
\- said she was sick as a baby once and had convulsions (seizures?)
\- raised on a farm
\- had her vaccines (presumably polio)
\- wore glasses since childhood
\- had an emotionally traumatic event at 22
\- has hypothyroidism, not sure at what age
\- takes meds for high cholesterol for last 20 yrs approx.
\- has had cataracts surgery within last 10 years, glaucoma
\- has had history of bowel polyps since early 40's, gets regular colonoscopies

Grandma:
\- born 1932, smoked most of her life, throughout all pregnancies
\- had hypothyroidism and high cholesterol
\- had a stroke in her 60's, stopped smoking
\- I remember her having heart surgery and the incision was always draining and wouldn't heal
\- continued heart issues and developed dementia before her death at 83

**Other Symptom Updates**
Breast Cancer Situation
\- occasional stabbing pain in left breast (nerves trying to heal?)
\- continued numbness down back of left arm from shoulder to elbow from surgery
\- some recent nerve pain developed on inside of left arm extending to inside of forearm
\- continued numbness at and around incision site of primary tumor and sentinel node biopsy site

Other
\- pruritus ani still persistent; hoping addition of mebendazole will help
\- continued attractant of mosquitoes, though bites do not inflame as much as when I came to ES
\- last 2 periods have been 5 days, normal for me has been 7 days with some stagnancy (a flow pause around day 3 or 4); still dark red rather than bright red

### christine_fp — 2026-02-02T20:21:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal.28959/post-366242>

Long overdue journal update, I will try to be brief.

As a brief synopsis: diagnosed with breast cancer in April 2024, lumpectomy July 2024, declined chemo, radiation, hormone therapy. Spent Oct- March in El Salvador and returned to Ontario end of March 2025. I felt no palpable lesions prior to leaving aside from 2 small bumps near incision that felt like scar tissue.

The first couple months home felt challenging to adapt to the colder than normal weather that was persisting. Very happy though to be with my partner, working again (arborist) and being out climbing and caring for trees.

SUNLIGHT
April-Nov 2025 - I'm fortunate to work outdoors, though much of me is covered due to safety gear, but I was able to work around this a number of times by taking on different tasks where I could wear less and 3 day weekends to do more extensive nude sunbathing. Morning sun and grounding was easier in the summer and then a little harder come fall when I'd be driving to work when the sun is coming up but I always open a window and would often pull over to watch it and ground. Grounding varied but at least 1-3hours a day including time spent in trees, no gloves on.

Something I think that gets lost/forgotten sometimes is that I spent over 10 years working in the basement of a beef factory, no windows, fluorescent lights, I spent all my breaks outside which only amounted to maybe 45min total all day. I wore blue blockers the last few years there, but I think a lot of the damage had already been done. I finally found arboriculture and left this factory job Nov 2022. I first noticed the lump just a few months after I left.

IMAGING
Breast thermogram at beginning of April 2025 just after returning home from ES, TH3 in left (affected side), TH2 in right. Elevated heat signature in left image that was not near incision area, but I was told this was normal considering I'd had surgery. My family doctor brushed off thermogram results and booked me for an ultrasound which I finally had at the end of July. Ultrasound showed a 1.5cm suspicious lesion in left breast (could be felt at this point). I declined mammogram and biopsy.

IVM/MEB
Low doses of ivermectin starting Sept 2024, upped dosage to 90mg/day through March-May 2025. Mebendazole began April 2025 1000mg/day for April-May as per Dr. Makis protocol. Then gradually began decreasing both, mebendazole 500mg/day and ivermectin 24mg/day by the time the ultrasound was done in July. After ultrasound results I briefly increased dosages; failed in multiple attempts to contact Dr. Makis/his team where emails weren't being responded to. I contacted another practitioner that follows ivermectin protocols who suggested taking a brief break from the repurposed drugs to give my liver a break, followed by a moderate does protocol. Started moderate ivermectin and mebendazole dose during November and stopped when I got to El Salvador at the end of November. I have recently started taking both again at the beginning of January 2026.

ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
In the summer I found a TCM doctor that does methylene blue IVs. He recommended a large supplemental protocol as well as biweekly methylene blue and vit C IV's, also an iron IV as well. I could not afford the extent of the protocol so I tried asking for a lesser version with only the methylene blue, but he did not think that was best, so, I did not end up doing any of it unfortunately. It was also hard to justify the biweekly travel to downtown Toronto to sit in a high rise for several hours to do the IV's. Perhaps I'm very wrong on this and should have done anything possible to do it.

While back home I had also taken CBD oil and mushroom extracts.

LABS
Vit D mid August was 141.7nmol/L (56ng/ml). I know it's not even half of what it should be for my situation so I started supplementing vit D (10,000 - 30, 000ius) until I arrived in ES when I of course stopped.

Aug labs also showed slightly elevated MCV (101), slightly low RBC (3.81), low normal hemoglobin (124), ferritin 54ug/L

DDW/DIET
I am not currently able to consume only DDW. If I could, I would. So, from July-Nov I consumed 1-2 cups/day worth of 10ppm. Otherwise my water was local spring water which I salted. In ES its been salted with lime juice. Back home I had been doing monthly multi day fasts, and have learned that that may not be ideal, so I've stopped. There I just ate what made sense and what grows there (meat, eggs, local veg and fruit), aside from seafood that I consumed 4-5 days a week that often wasn't immediately local. Diet currently mainly consists of Fish, meat, eggs, some local fruit, raw dairy.

NNEMF
This has probably been my greatest area of learning. Mostly, it's made me really fucking mad. I learned the house that we moved into in a panic in 2024 to get out of an apartment building, is probably worse than the apartment building. I got a clearer picture of this after I was able to finally get some meters last spring. So, I started sleeping on the bathroom floor in May 2024 as it was the best place I could find (RF: 5-7 microwatts, MF: 0.2-0.3 mG, EF: 0v/m only after my building biologist found a couple spots under the floor I had missed that were 30-50v/m and I elevated everything away from the floor) . We couldn't buy a canopy yet and had no control over the breaker box in the house. We mitigated what we could with grounding and shielding foil. Ultimately we found out half the house isn't even grounded and we're just trying to mitigate a shithole.

After viewing/nnemf testing multiple rentals over the summer we recently found a place in the country where the emfs are drastically lower and the nearest cell tower is several kms away, unlike the multiple that are within 500m of where I currently live back home. I realize this isn't the perfect solution because, well, Canada. But I'm grateful I have a supportive partner as we try and figure out how to make a permanent move or how for me to at least be permanently moved. The financial struggle has been incredible, but we're trying. We've done many side jobs so we'd have something to put into bitcoin and we're both working on alternative entrepreneurial ideas for income to have more flexibility and options. I'm looking to bring my gear to ES in the future so perhaps a future reference for anyone that wants tree, or similar work, done.

LYMPHEDEMA
I only noticed signs of swelling in my left hand after returning home last spring (9-10 months after surgery) and experienced it a handful of times while back home (often after physical work). A lymphatic therapist explained that the nodes on my left side would never be able to work normally again and I needed to "train" the lymph to flow to the other side of my body. Is this accurate that it won't recover? There is a strip of scar tissue that may impede this, but even if I gradually broke this up through massage? The back of the left arm remains numb from surgery with occasional dull pain/sensations that radiate down the arm. I also sometimes experience intense itching in the underarm and breast incision sites.

MENTAL/BIOPSYCHOSOMATIC HEALTH
This doesn't always feel like a welcome topic, but I'm saying it anyway. I think for me there is definitely stored trauma/emotions that have festered in such a way that created this toxic thing I couldn't ignore anymore. In trying different methods to release these things, I find I'm getting more clarity on a lot; what needs to be let go, who needs to be let go of, thoughts, patterns, stories I tell myself about who I am, who I've been and why there's been this unconscious resistance in releasing it. But now its not unconscious anymore so I have no excuse not to change it. An abusive upbringing may be stored in my fabric, but I choose to weave in new threads. Resentment has only hurt me. It's taking time, but I'm learning what it feels like to listen to my body and learn how to feel safe. Like if I let go of patterns, of people, I will be okay.

EL SALVADOR Nov 2025-present
First week mild burning, gut biome adjusting
Second week, base tan established, lots of sweat bubbles covering torso and shoulders
Third week minimal sweat bubbles, tan darkening, gut feeling adapted
Fourth week - present, lymphatic discomfort under left arm/shoulder and re-occurrence of flash-like pain in lower left abdomen , otherwise feeling good, dark tan

Sleep is good for the most part, with occasional nights where sleep is restless, lots of dreaming. Room readings: RF: <1.0 microwatt, MF: 0.1mG, EF: 0v/m

Have used red booklight for a couple hours before sleep, but am changing this to have zero polarized light at all.

I have been here just over 2 months. I so far have not noticed a discernable difference in lesion size, other than it feels less defined. I have experienced occasional aching sensations/pain in left breast and in left shoulder/shoulder blade area. I recently have noticed a swollen lymph node here as well. I have also been experiencing off and on random brief flashes of pain/discomfort in extreme lower left abdomen, down and medial from the iliac crest. This specifically started last summer, I didn't feel it for a couple of months around oct/nov and then I started feeling it again in Dec in ES. It's sporadic, sometimes felt multiple times a day to not feeling it for several days or weeks. I am more sedentary in ES, but have been doing light jogging and occasional sprints to keep lymph moving and now increasing the amount of lymphatic brushing/self-massage.

2 hours of sun and grounding at sunrise, 3 hours midday, 2 hours late aft. It's been challenging finding private places for breast sunning, but I'm able to get at least 2-3 hours midday if I'm creative about where I go and how I do it.

# Thread: We here and introduction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-here-and-introduction.28971/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mamuan — 2024-05-30T21:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-here-and-introduction.28971/post-335294>

Hello all, I am new to this forum and honestly a bit overwhelmed. I have been listening to Jack Kruse podcasts on YouTube and now decided to join this forum. I have few question:
1\. Where do I start( besides reading his book which is coming in the mail)?
2\. How do I join membership?( I followed the link and it only gives me one option, and as I understand there multiple membership options).
3\. As for catching sunrise. I am on hydrocortisone for adrenals( never been diagnosed officially as Addisons, but one doc told me I will be on it for the rest of my life), and catching a sunrise is problematic for me especially now, because I take my hydrocortisone as soon as I wake up and then the 25 mg will not be enough for the whole day if I take my first dose at 5 am. So given that, is it still beneficial for me to catch the morning sun 8-11 am and sunset?

As for my personal health history. I am 44 y.o. Mother of 3. Currently have a whole host of health issues , that started about 13 years ago. I have MCAS, Dusautonomia, POTS, hypothyroidism, Addisons, SIBO, IBS, chemical sensitivity, CIRS, Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, food sensitivities ( I might be forgetting something). I have been eating 4-5 foods for the past 10 years, due to MCAS. Have been trying all kinds of therapies and have seen all kind of doctors. I have been religiously doing brain retraining/ somatics/ bagel toning exercises for the past year and started to finally see some small changes and was able to introduce couple of needed supplements, no foods yet at this point.

I grew up in Tashkent, Uzbekistan. Grew up on the flat of an apartment building. Have always been tomboy and liked to play outside. As a kid always had stomach issues, IBS, eczema and has always had an anxiety and depression later on in my teen and college years.
Got married and moved to the US. Although I was never perfectly healthy the straw that broke my back was when my middle child, my son, was diagnosed with PPD- NOS, basically an autism diagnosis. Ever since my health has been declining and here I am.
I am really excited to start with Jacks principles( I think it will be easy to do leptin reset, since I eat 2 times a day, low carb, and rarely get hungry, although do crave sweets from time to time. ), so now need to get the rest of the ducks in row.

# Thread: Whooping cough in a child
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whooping-cough-in-a-child.28972/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lukassss — 2024-05-30T23:01:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whooping-cough-in-a-child.28972/post-335299>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) needs help. My 7-year-old daughter has whooping cough on the 12th day. The nights are getting worse. The cough is terrible. We spend the whole day outside in the sun, everything is quiet during the day. It's a dark night and we need help. How can we contact you? What shall we do?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-31T02:30:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whooping-cough-in-a-child.28972/post-335306>

You do not need me. You need to see the local doctor for supportive care only. First make sure it is Pertussis bacteria and not a respiratory virus. They should tell you the following: Pertussis bacteria die off naturally after three weeks of coughing. If antibiotics are not started within that time, they are no longer recommended as a form of treatment.

### Pratima — 2024-05-31T05:26:51+0300

I am not sure what restless legs at night means.
From time to time I am getting leg cramps at night. That wakes me up and I have to walk it out, then go back to bed.
When I was dancing, after 2-3 hours of intense nonstop dancing I would have to stop because of leg cramps.

I think I figured out what likely my problem is or maybe.
Likely it is minimally too low ADH (Antidiuretic Hormone), vasopressin.

When I take a minimal amount of desmopressin right when I have the cramps, the cramps go away for over a week or more.
Now I have it by my bed, take it only when cramps come.
\------------
There were discussions here about DDW (started about October 2017).
Eventually I heard dr Boros complaining that most people that he came across and tested their ADH they had it too low.
Too low ADH directs body to keep less water.
So, regardless of how much they may be drinking they are dehydrated all the time.
Peeing out water that they actually could use.
.....

# Thread: Hi from Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-australia.28990/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### K-in-Oz — 2024-06-05T04:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-australia.28990/post-335456>

Hi all,

I'm new here. I'm 31, mum to a little boy (who will be 3 in September) and live with my son and hubby in Bendigo. We moved here in February from Melbourne where I was experiencing suicidal depression, amongst other things, for some time.

I discovered low-carb and then carnivore in 2022, after I was struggling to lose weight from pregnancy (had gestational diabetes during pregnancy). I was able to lose the weight but eventually put it all back on and then abandoned those things as the answer and in the last few months discovered Dr Kruse's website and videos on youtube and I have been devouring as much information as I can. I'm not diagnosed with anything but have been suffering from a range of health issues for many years now. (throat tightness, chest pain, fatigue, palpitations, dizziness, joint pain and recently I have started getting rashes on my face and random burning sensations (in knees, forearms, and in my face when the rash appears).
My poor hubby is not too healthy either, diagnosed with ulcerative colitis, coeliac and primary sclerosing cholangitis when he was around 20, then he ended up with full blown liver failure and liver transplant in 2019 in his 30s and he had a pulmonary embolism in 2022.

Sadly I was ignorant/uneducated/scared during covid and am triple vaxxed, one of those was even during pregnancy which I feel terrible about now. I have made many mistakes in the past but I am trying to pick up the pieces now and I hope that my family and I are able to achieve some level of better health in the future, by following practises recommended hear and learning as much as I can.

I am really looking forward to being a part of this community and to learning more and improving our health as much as we are able.

The days have turned grey in Bendigo now that it's winter and I already feel like it's tanked my energy and motivation even though sunny days were around not so long ago.

I just have to convince my husband it's the go. [@K-in-Oz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31301/) - no worries. ALAN = artificial light at night (i.e. after sunset) & nnEMF = non-native electromagnetic frequencies (i.e. man-made technologies not from the sun) e.g. radio frequencies, electric & magnetic fields etc. It would be of benefit for you to get an EMF assessment if you are concerned, there is a company based in VIC that offer this. Link - [https://spectraldesign.com.au/](https://spectraldesign.com.au/)

All the best.

Hello there and welcome.
I just wanted to encourage you about the sun exposure to the skin causing skin cancer idea. I have been in El Salvador since October. I was very new when I got here, which means I’m still fairly new, but I was also leary of sun exposure because of all the propaganda out there (Australia is inundated with it as I understand…slip, slop, slap and all that). At 15 I was told the ozone layer was no longer protecting us from and we would now get skin cancer if we didn’t protect ourselves and hide from the sun…etc. I now believe this is probably the main reason my health went down the tubes along with nnEMF exposure out the wazoo!
Anyway, I started slowly, but I began exposing myself to the sun and working on getting a “solar callous” by watching the sunrise every morning, exposing as much skin to the sun in the morning, and then slowly getting more and more midday sun as I could handle. I am happy to report that weird skin tags and spots have disappeared off of my face (I thought the opposite would happen based on all I was told) and my vitamin D tripled!!!
Dr. Jack Kruse always says that if the sun gives you cancer then how come cancer patients have super low vitamin D?
Anyway, I hope the very best for you guys

Thanks for sharing and good on you for letting go of the fear mongering garbage people are fed re: sun exposure. We basically live outside and spend as much time as we can outside. Every task where possible I do outside, even cooking over a fire pit on the grass. We always joke about what the neighbours would be thinking and we are known as the red light house at night.. All funny stuff and I try to educate others as much as possible, however, some are truly low agency, so the process is very hard, especially if they are fearful.. This use to impact me quite a bit as I truly wanted them to see the light and get the message, however, I have learnt to conserve my energy with certain people I may come in contact with and keep my opinions to my self if I already know what certain people are about. I only have a very small amount of people in my circle and people that are not like minded (especially with how they raise their kids), boundaries are put in place.

Wishing you guys all the best as well.

Hi India,

Excuse my super delayed response to your message. I appreciate you taking the time to write such an encouraging message. Awesome to hear about your tripled vitamin D!

I absolutely would say I'm on board with the general idea that sun is good for us and that the skin cancer equation is not as straightforward as sun=cancer. I am just still apprehensive about husband's sun exposure as he is on immunosuppressants for a liver transplant which increases the rates of skin cancer astronomically, which I've seen suggested anywhere from 65-100 times normal incidence. I've listened to Dr Max Gulhane talk about this a little and heard him say that it's because the medications makes the skin photosensitive. So unfortunately it seems that some caveats apply to sun exposure in people in situations like my husbands. # Thread: Kate's journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journey.28991/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KN75 — 2024-06-05T07:44:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journey.28991/post-335460>

Now 47, I thought I was doing quite well health wise, although realise I am low redox after being a member here.
I am J haplotype, born in the UK - had a fairly outdoors lifestyle with horses but poor sun quality in Northern England. I then moved to Scotland to study medicine. Some shiftwork was involved until I became a GP 2003 (same as family doctor). Only major health issue was myopia when younger, and started to have some addiction issues late 20s.
I moved to Australia age 32yrs and my job was full time, in front of a computer. I started practicising functional medicine and have had a successful practice in different parts of Australia, now located in Sydney for the past 10yrs. I had 2 children naturally at 38 and 40yrs despite having a low AMH. The last 1-2 yrs I have been having some hair loss which I realise is low redox now.
I have been more consistent with morning light, taking my glasses off and avoiding ALAN the past 2 months with benefits in energy and sleep. I am trying my best with nnEMF although living in a 5G city.
I honestly thought I was doing the best for my patients with functional medicine and I am now relearning and hoping to offer my patients true root cause medicine and do no harm. Aiming to learn the best ways to help people in Sydney who will be unwilling to move due to different reasons.
Any ideas welcome

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-05T19:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journey.28991/post-335471>

welcome. L3 in UK says it all. Change that.

### KN75 — 2024-06-06T00:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journey.28991/post-335485>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=335471)
>
> welcome. L3 in UK says it all. Change that.

I have been in Australia since 2009, initially southern queensland, now Sydney for 10yrs - which I realise is not great either!

### KN75 — 2024-07-01T05:25:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journey.28991/post-336335>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=335471)
>
> welcome. L3 in UK says it all. Change that.

Hi Jack I realised I am a J haplotype (originally came from L) which probably changes lots of things!

### KN75 — 2025-02-26T07:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journey.28991/post-347372>

We have had a good summer in Australia and for the first time in a long time, soaking up the sun. I am a nice chocolate colour and my children are tanned too. Things i have noticed - weight loss, better cycle, hair growth, better sleep and mood generally. My daughter is now dry at night and we have had no viral illnesses despite people around us becoming unwell. Summer is nearly over though..

### KN75 — 2025-05-07T02:14:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kates-journey.28991/post-352065>

adding some maternal history - my maternal grandmother developed MS in her 50's and my mother developed breast cancer in her mid 60's

# Thread: Hello from Quebec city
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 0.66eV Eva — 2024-06-06T21:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/post-335526>

Hi,

I am new on this forum.

I am 33 yo and I live in Quebec, but I am willing to leave as soon as I can. Not sure yet where would be best.

Since 2019, I don't work nor continued my studies. I have constant severe neuroinflammation, brainfog, stiff Dura (?) (I need to crack it constantly).

I fell extremely sick during my studies in Paris during autumn 2019 (covid? nnEMF+++?). I already had a Hx of chronic diseases (including lymphoma at 4 yo) impeding every day, but in 2019 I could not function anymore, at 30 yo. I returned to Quebec for what I still thought at that time to be "medical care".

Since 2020, I have been diagnosed with:

\- Budd-Chiari syndrome (IVC thrombosis probably from chemotherapy received in 1995-1997 for a NHL lymphoma T cell. Massive collateralization, dilatation of azygos and semi-azygos systems, small 3 suprahepatic veins, hepatomegaly (resolved in 2021), severe hepatic fibrosis F3, cap score 268 db/m, portal hypertension, esophageal varices (resolved), minimal ascites, hepatic enzymitis. I had an IVC angioplasty with a Venovo stent in 2020 in Montreal.
From reading my medical dossier from the archives, I discovered that the radiological findings for my Budd-Chiari were already there in 1997: "suspicion of thrombosis of the vena cava", hepatomegaly, steatosis. In 2020, I was put on self injected dalteparine and beta-blockers, then apixaban. Stopped beta-blockers in 2020. Then in 2022 I decided to stop apixaban despite my hepatologist advice.
\- celiac disease
\- hypothyroidism - I refused synthroid
\- dysautonomia

I also have hormonal problems (at 18 yo I was put on Alesse bcp to start my menstruations), premature cataracts (from chemo?), areflexia (from chemo maybe as well), frequent cold sores accompanied by burning headaches and stiff neck, etc. I am very very dehydrated (skin, eye, mouth...). I have pmdd. When I try starchy foods I get extreme indigestions.

I did not take the covid vaccines.

I take Morley Robbins (root cause protocol) suggested supplements since 2022. I get anaphylactoid reactions back when I try to stop.

I want to learn everything I can from Dr Kruse and this community. I hope I can get better.

### 0.66eV Eva — 2024-06-07T17:01:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/post-335550>

I discovered Jack Kruse's work in 2022, through Morley Robbins, who cites him about the danger of artificial light.

In summer 2022 I started grounding, walking barefoot, staying outside all day long, going to bed right after sunset, using a red light bulb in my bedroom and orange glasses, tanning in the sun. At the end of the summer, I could see significants improvements in terms of pain, digestion and mood. In september 2022, I was astounded I had no more pmdd symptoms.

The BIG problem is that I did not realize at that time it was mainly the lifestyle changes that where helping me. I still though it was mainly about the minerals and vitamins I was taking, and the iron/hemosiderin overload I would have to reduce drastically but progressively with phlebotomies. I lost improvements quickly during the autumn 2022 and sinked again in debilitating pain and neuroinflammation, still trying to reimplement a variety of foods and uing my red/IR light panel only for 2x30minutes a day. Tanning beds during winter were helping me tremendously, but only for bouts of a few hours, and I knew the light spectrum/ratio might be dangerous...

I stayed stuck in the vitamin/mineral and diet mindset, chasing one then another symptom until 2024.

Then in winter 2024, when not knowing who and what to trust anymore about my health, I decided to go for a walk during a snowstorm. I thought "I can't miss a snowstorm". But I did not know why I was thinking that. After not even 5 minutes outside in the snowstorm my pain lifted, my mood lifted. Maybe it is the negative ions? The relief did not last long after the walk. I may have severe damage that prevents me from storing electrons. And a lack of DHA to capture them?

Then I learned about deuterium in food and that the absence of UVB here in winter was preventing me from depletinig deuterium. I progressively stopped the carbs. It helped me to reduce pain. I realize that living in Quebec, under a lot of snow in winter, I should have only meat, fish, pine needle tea and not much otherwise...?

I have grassfed pasture raised beef mainly, among other meats from a local farm. But I don't tolerate their chicken very much, especially in winter, and I think it is because they keep their chicken indoor because of the bird flu.....and/or it's because of the grains.

I am on the verge of becoming carnivore right now, with cubes of butter or bacon fat. I am still confused about the risk of acidosis with a carnivore diet (DiNicolantonio JJ, O'Keefe J. Low-grade metabolic acidosis as a driver of chronic disease: a 21st century public health crisis. Open Heart 2021;8:e001730. doi:10.1136/ openhrt-2021-001730).

My favorite is seafood and I would like to be able to afford more of it. Maybe in the meantime a high dose DHA supplement would be useful?

I stop wifi during night. I know it is NOT enough. When I get my own place. I will do wired. I stop the electricity in my bedroom for the night.

My next step is watching the sunrise. It was at 4h51 this morning. I get up naturally only at 7.

Then moving country ? Buying a portable IR sauna? Sleeping outside in a tent? Starting CT (but I need melanin renovation first thing with chronic fatigue syndrome if I understand well...)?

I joined the forum because I need to connect with likeminded people, with Jack Kruse's tribe.

### Sirena — 2024-06-07T17:30:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/post-335552>

Welcome. You'll connect with likeminded people. That's a great start.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-08T03:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/post-335569>

Welcome. With that history getting to a better environment is critical. It seems you already figured that part out which is huge.

### Sandy.Wards — 2024-06-10T20:01:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/post-335646>

I live in Canada as well and we are doing similar things, we also sleep outside in a tent on our rural property. I love the fresh air, waking up naturally to the light and the sounds of the birds.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-10T21:36:53+0300

Thanks! We are quite far from electrical but we sleep on a mattress with no grounding. My plan is eventually to have a new setup sleeping on sheepskins and a mosquito net to cover but I could also look into a grounding sheet. Before the bugs came we did sleep no tent for a bit this spring.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T01:21:39+0300

Happy to hear from a fellow canadian!  In which province do you live? (I apologize for my english, learning still in progress).

I slept outside in a tent for 2 day last week, but there is too much noise and light around, in the city. I hear cars from the bridges bewteen Quebec and Levis, and planes often... The fresh air and the cold were helpful though! I wanted to try camping during winter especially. But I realize from reading [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) that I was maybe too close to the house and electricity poles.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T20:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/post-335695>

The fresh air and the cold were helpful though! I wanted to try camping during winter especially. But I realize from reading [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) that I was maybe too close to the house and electricity poles.

Back yard rule of thumb - be at least 8 feet (12 would be better) from building or electrical wire/pole etc. including underground wires. In densely populated areas the ground everywhere might have ground currents so not be suitable. To connect with nature and Earth’s natural DC energy, it really helps to be in Nature.

### Sandy.Wards — 2024-06-11T20:57:43+0300

But I realize from reading [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) that I was maybe too close to the house and electricity poles.

I am in Alberta near Calgary. We have 20 acres on a big coulee with a wetland and it is super quiet for man made noise and so many birds and other wildlife. We moved here 3 years ago and escaped the city. I think it is much harder to get well in the city but I realize there is not always options. I struggle the most I decided with the constantly changing sunrise and sunset. I can be outside all day on the warmer months and I am trying to plan an outdoor space in winter I can spend a lot of time in that is somewhat sheltered but has unfiltered light and is not freezing … however I do wish the light was more stable. I cold adapted pretty good last winter but I want to take it to the next level where I am an inferno. We also raise a lot of our own food so it is of course local and seasonal.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-12T05:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-quebec-city.28996/post-335716>

> [magnetic said:](/goto/post?id=335710)
>
> Ground currents affecting your grounding is a myth. That's not how it works. You'll be fine grounding near a residence. If your in your place you ground to your kitchen/bathroom sinks copper pipe.

NOT SO . . . If you are close to your building and in an AC electric field or if there is AC current on the water pipe you have a problem. Cows can die standing on the ground because of ground currents (go look it up). Grounding makes you part of the circuit. Grounding (Earthing) in Nature . . . Good - DC to DC. Grounding (making yourself part of the circuit) in your home while in a 60Hz (50Hz) electric field . . . not so good. That 60Hz field will follow the path of least resistance to ground THROUGH YOU. Ground/Earth in the middle of New York and you are not connecting to natures DC ”grid” but NY 60Hz AC grid going back to the power plant. Healthy grounding is connecting to nature, to connect to nature you NEED to be in nature.

> [magnetic said:](/goto/post?id=335729)

Nope. Ground currents are exceedingly rare and practically limited to outdated and inefficient systems(single wire earth return, SWER) in remote areas. The so called dirty electricity is a different issue. It involves rapid transients within the electrical power supply that generate EMFs and it is those EMFs that may disturb some people. Examples: power surges from refrigerator cycling, dimmer switches, or switching on-off of appliances could be disturbing but does not affect the efficacy of grounding. Grounding protects the body.

# Thread: Homocysteine elevated - supplement with methyl folate? mthfr
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homocysteine-elevated-supplement-with-methyl-folate-mthfr.28997/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### K-in-Oz — 2024-06-07T02:00:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homocysteine-elevated-supplement-with-methyl-folate-mthfr.28997/post-335534>

Hi all,

I'm wondering if anyone has experience with elevated homocysteine. I ordered some labs privately and have received some of the results back.

Homocysteine: 14.4 umol/l (elevated)
folate: 31.6 nmol/l (normal)

I am heterozygous for C67TT mutation (mthfr).

I eat a few eggs everyday and meat based so eating things like sardines, tuna, beef everyday.

I've heard some people have success supplementing with methyl folate. I would appreciate if anyone has any experience/advice with this?

I'm still waiting on a number of other markers but in the meantime these are the other results I have if relevant:

hsCRP: 1.34
TPOAb: 48 IU/ml
TGAb: 1.3 IU/ml

I was also told over a year ago I had a small benign thyroid nodule. I will update/post again when I have other results but if anyone has advice on this question for the moment that would be very much appreciated.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-07T02:22:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homocysteine-elevated-supplement-with-methyl-folate-mthfr.28997/post-335535>

means you need more sun not more methyl groups

### K-in-Oz — 2024-06-07T02:43:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homocysteine-elevated-supplement-with-methyl-folate-mthfr.28997/post-335536>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=335535)
>
> means you need more sun not more methyl groups

Hi Dr Kruse,
Given it's currently winter and I've obviously failed to get adequate sun in summer, do you think there is ever any benefit to someone using methyl group supplements as a temporary bridge to an ultimately long term goal of using lots of sun for health?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-09T20:44:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homocysteine-elevated-supplement-with-methyl-folate-mthfr.28997/post-335621>

If you would use a search feature you would have found this post I wrote years ago:

There has been some recent news out about folate levels, sunlight and artificial light. Many people do not know folate is a photosynthetic chemical and it links dark skin (melanin) for natural folate protection.

Vitamin B9 (Folate) was first extracted from spinach leaves in 1941. The term folate comes from the same root as foliage: green and leafy. Folates are vital: they accept carbon atoms and pass them on as needed as the fundamental basis for proper methylation. **This implies that during summer months folate levels should be expected to be at their lowest levels NATURALLY.
Folate biology has a specific and tight seasonal control mechanism linked to light. This is not a food story at all**

_What if there is no seasonal or light controls in place?_
Folate is often given to pregnant women to prevent spinal dysraphisms today. This is something pediatric neurosurgeons deal with and why I know a lot about them. The incidence of these conditions has dropped but it is has done at a COST. Why? but too much folate/folic acid causes cognitive haze and sleep difficulty and may cause neural migration problems in artificially lit environments. This should awaken you. In North America, (CAN/USA) folic acid was added to all grains in 1996. This is only a few decades ago and we are seeing the transgenerational effects of this right now.

In general, it does not appear that even large amounts of folic acid taken orally are acutely toxic in adults. However, given the fundamental role of folate levels in synthesizing nucleotides (including RNA and DNA) and in methylation reactions as a methyl donor, high levels may have inadvertent implications for proper methylation of DNA during times of rapid cell division, such as in prenatal development. You'd think the idea that adding folic acid to the food supply might have caused centralized medicine to expect unintended consequences. Few thought about, much less studied it. I have always been worried about this effect since I learned that B12 was a photoreceptor in humans.

This idea has been speculated in research circles as early as 2005, and specifically speculated to be relevant for the increase in autism in 2011.

An early review of potential problems with mass folic acid supplementation of the food supply was undertaken by Lucock and Yates. Here, they noted that a drastic increase in folates could lead to a selection for the previously rare MTHFR genetic substitution of T for C at area 677 (MTHFR C677T), and that if folic acid is supplemented at doses above 400 mcg that unmetabolized folic acid will circulate in the blood supply at a level largely consistent with the excess dose. In 2005, Lucock and Yates noted that high levels of folic acid in the blood does not generally occur as a result of ingesting natural folates and that “no work has been done so far to evaluate the biological and genetic consequences of excess long term exposure” to these circulating folic acids on DNA/RNA biology. After that review, there were two separate findings of unexpected increases in asthma and breathing problems associated with folic acid use.

It now appears that we have clear data that excessive methyl donor transfer has significant epigenetic effects in humans. This work dovetailed with another review questioning the wisdom of mass folic acid supplementation published in 1996. Smith et al. pointed out that by supplementing the food supply; several hundred thousands of persons are exposed to greatly increased levels of folic acid.

These authors noted that prior research had shown that expectant mothers with low vitamin B-12 (most vegans/vegetarians) AND high levels of folic acid were associated with offspring having an unexpected increased risk for insulin resistance and disease associated with this condition. This is worrisome when you know that blue light exposure does the same thing.

Troen et al. found that some women past childbearing age subjected to high folic acid supplementation may be at risk for reduced immune system functioning causing inflammatory autoimmune conditions to spike. This problem has gotten worse since I first looked into it in 2005.

As a result of the supplementation, the circulating level of unmetabolized folic acid, as well as total folates, has greatly increased over the past generation, probably to levels largely unprecedented in human history.
Folic acid has been shown to be able to epigenetically alter the functioning of the genome and to have long term effects on gene expression as I mentioned above.

# Thread: fiat vs bitcoin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fiat-vs-bitcoin.29001/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Heisenburg — 2024-06-08T20:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fiat-vs-bitcoin.29001/post-335581>

I got thinking about bitcoin, fiat and the relationship to prices of goods and services. the values placed on goods and services given no supply change remains constant. The cost however changes. this change is based on the value of the medium of exchange. As the value of the fiat goes down the cost of goods and services goes up. sounds bad and it is and is called inflation. If the supply of fiat went down this would increase it's value and the cost of goods and services would come down on a relative basis. As bitcoin's value increases, the relative cost of goods and services goes down but the value remains the same. Sounds like a no brainer once you sit down and think the situation through

### Emmanuela — 2024-06-23T14:32:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fiat-vs-bitcoin.29001/post-336033>

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-23T16:53:18+0300

### JanSz — 2024-06-23T19:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fiat-vs-bitcoin.29001/post-336043>

For whatever it means to you.
Now as we speak bitcoin is (kind of) low.
Even better time to buy it.
\-----------
For most people their available fiat currency comes daily or occasionally, so it is better to not think of timing.
That is unnecessary source of stress.
Whatever you would do, in retrospect you could have done it better.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T17:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-2024-jab-update-and-it-is-excellent.29004/post-337068>

The origin of the furin cleavage site (FCS) in SARS-CoV-2 is a critical element in understanding the forensic origins of COVID-19. One theory suggests that the FCS may be derived from feline MERS-related sequences, while another posits it may be derived from human lung-specific epithelial sodium channels (eNaC) identified in Ralph Baric's research.

This theory involves the collaboration between Ralph Baric, Peter Daszak, and Shi Zhengli, particularly through their DEFUSE proposal to DARPA, which was rejected. Despite the rejection, it is possible that the research continued independently at the Wuhan Institute of Virology without Baric's direct involvement, as Daszak and EcoHealth Alliance already had an active NIH grant studying the risk of bat coronaviruses with the WIV, including gain-of-function experiments.

Daszak's eagerness to advance the research and Shi's familiarity with Baric's techniques could have led to the introduction of the FCS using human eNaC as a source.

Another theory posits that the research was driven primarily by the People's Liberation Army, without significant involvement from Daszak, his NIH funding, or his U.S. associates. In this scenario, the PLA would likely have used a feline MERS-related FCS, leveraging the genetic material and knowledge independently acquired by Chinese researchers, indicating potential biotechnological espionage.

Understanding whether the FCS was introduced through natural mutation or artificial insertion has been vital for distinguishing between a natural zoonotic origin and a laboratory-origin scenario. Additionally, it may be crucial for the forensic analysis of COVID-19's origins. This differentiation has significant implications for biosecurity measures, scientific accountability, and assigning culpability for the COVID-19 pandemic.

Acquiring lab protocols from the WIV is essential for further understanding, as it is increasingly established that a lab origin is probable.

### magnetic — 2024-06-09T16:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-335603>

Just a quick question for those who might have experience in weight gain(body fat) while doing low carb or keto. It seems some people actually suffer from this and it's strange because it runs completely counter to the prevailing understanding that you only lose weight. Ray peat crowd likes to use this as ammunition against us. Any thoughts? Thanks

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-09T17:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-335605>

> [magnetic said:](/goto/post?id=335603)
>
> Just a quick question for those who might have experience in weight gain(body fat) while doing low carb or keto. It seems some people actually suffer from this and it's strange because it runs completely counter to the prevailing understanding that you only lose weight. Ray peat crowd likes to use this as ammunition against us. Any thoughts? Thanks

I have this. I don’t know what it is.
I have no stamina to look at Ray Peat. He doesn’t interest me.

I can actually lose weight, or maintain, when I go on an extremely low calorie diet that excludes carbs and fat. I’ve done it three times now.

It’s a 1000 calorie diet with 120g protein, almost no carbs no fat.
Since I know of Jack, I skipped the no fat part and I gained 35 pounds.

If I eat more than 1300 calories a day I gain weight and that is not great for somebody who is 5’7” tall.

I believe now that 1300 calories is still starvation and I need to fix my guts, my melanin sheets, my everything before I can worry about weight.

Recently I ate cake and ice cream, because I was like dang, now I’m fat, might as well enjoy it. Yeah, so now it’s my fault  too much carbs, but that’s just a phase. It doesn’t make me gain more weight at least.

Alas, something is not aligned in my metabolism and I am pretty sure it’s because of where I am living.

I actually think my body wants to tell me something. It might be a protective fat layer, that it wants me to have. Not sure.

It’s an interesting subject. Thanks for bringing it up.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-28T20:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336245>

> [DrEttinger said:](/goto/post?id=336243)
>
> For what this may be worth, here are my two cents on the topic. A person can only explain something with the combined knowledge they possess. The explanation can't be complete if the information is incomplete.
>
> The Keto diet vs. omnivorous diet vs. SAD diet vs. vegan diet builds a different microbiome. Some bacteria extract calories more effectively than others. One hundred calories of apple juice vs one hundred calories of an apple may yield different outcomes in different individuals.
>
> \- Now throw in a sympathetic dominant lifestyle vs a parasympathetic one (vagal tone).
> \- Exercise level, sleep, and light habits.
> \- Consider times of life, infancy, and pregnancy when you can actually create more fat cells if there is too much weight gain.
> \- When and where you eat, and how often you eat...
> \- Leptin, insulin, GLP-1, PYY resistance.
> \- Adrenal cortical hyper and hypofunction, anterior pituitary hypofunction.
> \- Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease due to increased levels of LPS due to leaky gut due to...
> \- Every person is unique and what works for one may not work for another.
>
> This list can go on about why it's hard to lose weight. Diet may be the center of the bell curve for weight control. People who are there aren't complaining. The edges of the bell curve are where people get frustrated because they primarily only understand the food aspect of weight loss.
>
> I have to be a detective in my practice because I don't get to the center of the bell curve patients.

Thank you Dr Ettinger. This is a good reminder that there is not a “magic bullet” when it comes to health (or most anything). We are all uniquely different and what works for me could do nothing or even be disastrous for someone else.

### JanSz — 2024-06-28T23:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336248>

knee joint is particularly vulnerable to injury due to its complex structure and the significant stress it endures
Signs your knee joint is injured include pain, stiffness, weakness and popping or crunching sounds when you move
Common knee problems include **torn ligaments**, cartilage injuries and osteoarthritis
**Maintaining a healthy weight is important, as excess weight may lead to quicker degeneration of cartilage and an increased risk of knee injuries**
Depending on the knee condition, **weight loss**, physical therapy, knee braces, exercise, tai chi and acupuncture can help, as can nutritional interventions for joint health, like bone broth

\=================

> I learn fast. It's important to be like that in life. Not exploring other ideas is detrimental to well being. So anyway I learned what I could here and there a lot of good people here too. I thank everyone's help.

I just had a conversation with an Alaskan native couple yesterday that were looking at buying my house. They are looking at moving from their village a couple hundred miles outside barrow where it is still a hunter gatherer lifestyle. They just finished their hunting season. 2 whales a bowhead and beluga and a lot of bearded seals. They fish for trout and grayling. They gather berries when they are ripe. They can or smoke all of this during the short 3 months of summer. I asked how much berries they collect and eat over the year and they said it varies as much as 100 gallons to none if it were a dry year. This is their diet for the year. Even during a good year eating few handful berries a day is really all they are getting. The woman was in very good shape looked maybe 40 the man had a large pot belly and looked his age. Same diet, I’d imagine same lifestyle choices.
they looked better then the majority of people these days and seemed happier then most as well.

I’ve been keto/carnivore for 10 years now. Personally I feel when my cortisol is high and it’s usually 1 not eating enough fat. 2 not eating eating enough calories. 3 not getting outside enough. I also found cycling clean carbs is essential. Being 0 carb for months or years on end doesn’t work or feel good.

This is also a good example why I don’t understand the sunrise thing about fats. They literally don’t have sunrise for like 2 months but the diet is 90% fat and the Inuit were always very healthy when they lived there traditional diet.

I asked about what evidence changed your mind regarding diet. Your feelings whether justified about Jack or Einstein or irrelevant.

### JanSz — 2024-06-29T00:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336252>

### [Suitable Fats, Unsuitable Fats: Issues in Nutrition](https://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsuitablefats.shtml)

Ray Peat

raypeat.com

disease called **steatitis or yellow fat disease** was found to be produced in various animals that were fed **too much fish or fish oil.**

and a deficiency of vitamin E.

\=================
The cheapness of the seed oils led to their use in animal feeds, to promote growth. By the 1940s, the polyunsaturated oils, **including fish oils**, were known to cause deterioration of the brain, muscles, and gonads in a variety of animals
\=================
The commercial pressure on fat research has created **a new way of writing** research reports, that several decades earlier wouldn't have been acceptable. For example, the effects of a specific fat on a few of the components of a complex process such as clotting are often described in the title, introduction, and conclusion of an article **as if they were revealing** a way to prevent heart disease.
\==================
The suppressive effects of unsaturated fats on mitochondrial energy production have been widely investigated, since it is that effect that makes animal **fattening with PUFA so economical**. Rather than interpreting that as a **toxic effect**,
**Polyunsaturated Fats**:

-   [**Linoleic Acid**:](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**α-Linolenic Acid (ALA**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)**)**
-   [**Stearidonic Acid**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**Eicosapentaenoic Acid (EPA)**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**Docosahexaenoic Acid (DHA)**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**Docosatetraenoic Acid**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)

\====================

### Huck — 2024-06-30T15:08:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336299>

I just read about something the carnivore community calls Priming. The idea behind it is most people are undernourished, so when they start keto or carnivore or any diet for that matter, they are undernourished. Since ketogenic diets tend to diminish appetite, the person may stay in an undernourished state. I think the EpiPaleo diet addresses that by saying to eat until full, and eat foods that are highly nutritious.

Carnivore Priming recommends starting with 1 week of 3 meals a day plus snacks and eating until full, followed by a week of 3 meals a day, followed by a week of 2 meals a day.

I'm not advocating for carnivore, but I think they may have identified an important problem - people cutting calories back while being undernourished.

Probably a better way to say it is people who are electron undernourished have to increase electrons, or they will continue with the same problems.

This is a short segment that starts at 3:26, and you can stop it at 4:12.

Just because you go in the sun and activate the POMC pathway... does not mean you will benefit from the metabolites. The epigenetics of a fucked up mind (your software program that you work off of : beliefs) can be more powerful to some than what can be done via light, food, exercise, and sleep.

I agree, my grandmother in India lived 92 yrs mainly in bombay without any of the diet dogmas. She stayed home unlike what jack says and was the healthiest is our entire family. She almost hated going outside snd since I was born I didn’t see her go for an even a walk. But she did have a good routine, didn’t watch T.V much always liked to sit in silence meditating. Always sat near the window so got light. Loved doing house work till her last years. Was always in the balcony flipping the drying. So again got light. Believed in eating ghee with her wheat and butter on her bread while the rest of India was deep into vegetable oils she always criticised them. Was very lean and no aches or pains. She drank a cup of tea first thing in the morning couple of hours laster 1 or 2 boiled eggs for breakfast and drank a cup of milk. Ate her Indian vegetarian lunch and dinner touched no meat or fish. She loved some savoury and sweet snack once a week but always skipped dinner if she ate those or just drank a cup of milk instead.

I am still figuring out what works for my son in terms of diet and he doesn’t metabolise as much fat as he could probably for his age. And we have been low carb for years. He’s improving but he does need some more carbs that is very apparent now. So 60gms of millet has been added. Eggs whites are not tolerated by him. But he’s probably okay with couple of yolks. Can’t say for sure but we’re not going to remove them since choline is so important and he’s a growing boy. We tried removing them earlier few times and don’t see much difference.

My daughter on the other hand escaped autism because we were already on a the path to eating better and no grains were involved in her weaning. She doesn’t want any carbs at age 7 in the u.k except for fruits, that’s her weakness. And a lick of honey. She loves fatty meat mosty beef and has thrived on raw milk, organs and meats and some seafood. Seafood is her least fav. She is very intelligent probably the most in her class, can read books for hours.

Totally the opposite of my son who went through gluten and dairy intolerance’s and then slipped into severe autism at age 2. He changed our world! His sister benefitted from all this.

Mums diet was very different couple of 3 years before she was conceived. We were low carb but not very low just eating more meats and fish now along with organs and stocks. Adding ghee to everything. Eating sourdough bread and fermented rice pancakes.

My wife is now 46 and starting to feel symptoms of peri menopause but fat surely is her main concern… if she goes low on it she doesn’t do too well. She tried more protein and not much fat to lose some weight which she die but had very heavy periods. She then needed to put on some weight as she was borderline and would be considered underweight when she visited the GP. She then realised and in turn reversed her asthma in the U.K winter eating tons of raw cream with some blueberries even though that’s not ideal to add fruit it still cured her asthma and rid her of the inhaler. She keep fat as her benchmark, carbs are tricky for her if they aren’t fermented. She’s okay with fermented grains but not starches and fruit. She gets sinus headaches. Hence she to be careful about choice of carbs and quantities. She has to go for walks or she puts on weight on higher carbs as fats are also high in her diet. Walking outside is her exercise. She doesn’t do anything else. Kids school runs ensure that she get out multiple times.

She can’t wake for sunrises yet. We began eating a millet porridge recipe from India - 2 tbsp of flour each with 1 1/2 tsp of sugar and 3 tablespoons of ghee with our eggs lately instead of sourdough just rye that we have been eating a couple of years as we were looking too slim on our faces and that’s not healthy either. Our outdoor time has increased post covid and coming across jacks work. He hasn’t given me much feedback when I became a silver member that I could easily understand so I’m not anymore. Was hoping for some good advice from him. But following his advice on DHA and outdoors. Can’t move our location yet. My wife loves raw oyesters now and has mad cravings for them some days. She wishes she had tried them earlier.

We’re all eating slightly differently from each other in one home. You have to find your own balance with food is what I believe in now 100%
