In India, bombay where I’m from its warm all year and just cools down to barely the mid or late 20 degrees Celsius and they will add more fat to their carbs. When it the monsoon season in Bombay we always were always eating deep fried onion fritters and this continues even now. It was not in ghee when I was growing up but sunflower oil in the last few decades which has resulted in horrendous health and in winter always added more fat to food which is also when the festival period also begins. Corn on the cob grilled on coal with butter, lemon, chilli powder and salt is very popular during monsoons. They are now moving back to ghee and traditional oils that have high temp tolerance for frying that were omitted in the last 40 -50 years. There are alot of Indian nutritionists spreading good advice about fats and traditional grains. They also tell expats to continue with the traditional grains like rice or millets and ferment them then eat with protein and ghee in the U.K. especially winters.

### [Cellular Micronutrient Assay (CMA) Test](https://previmedica.com/products/cellular-micronutrient-assay-cma-test)

The Cellular Micronutrient Assay (CMA) tests for insufficiencies in micronutrients (vitamins & minerals), amino acids, & other nutrients within immune cells.

 previmedica.com

/////////////////////////////////

My wife loves raw oyesters now and has mad cravings for them some days. She wishes she had tried them earlier.

Especially when I am overwhelmed by the excessive Linoleic Acid exposure,
I am thinking of **oysters as belonging to a mollusk** category.
That is the only food (I think) that is not exposed to a human feeding it with seed oils.
All mollusks are good food (hopefully low in LA.

oysters are mollusks. They belong to the invertebrate phylum Mollusca, which also includes other shelled creatures like clams, mussels, and scallops.
\======
Mollusks include many different classes and species.

Cephalopods: These are the most neurologically advanced of all invertebrates.
Cuttlefish
Giant squid
Nautilus2
Octopus
Squid
Gastropods: These are the most diverse mollusks and account for 80% of the total classified species.
Abalone
Murex
Nudibranch
Snails
Slugs
Bivalves: These mollusks have a shell that is divided into two hinged parts1.
Oysters
Clams
Mussels
Scallops
Zebra mussel
Ark shell
Scaphopods: These are also known as tusk shells.
Tusk shell
Monoplacophorans: These are deep-sea mollusks with a single, cap-like shell.
Polyplacophorans: These are also known as chitons.
Aplacophorans: These are small, worm-like marine mollusks.
Caudofoveates and Solenogastres: These are small, worm-like marine mollusks.

### AntonisK — 2024-07-03T11:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336498>

Unless you constantly measure, you cannot tell what kind of diet is right for you or anyone else for that matter (by measuring, I mean body fat & lean mass).

There's no such thing as "one size fits all", this should be obvious to all.

We can agree that there are some "foods" that are truly dangerous to all and should be avoided (trans fats, seed oils, ultra/high processed foods), other than that things are quite personalized.

First thing to consider is insulin sensitivity; anyone with insulin resistance should not consume carbs (except some low GL vegetables) to begin with. As Charles used to say "you must deserve your carbs".
Prioritizing animal protein is the first step to make sure you preserve (at least) or increase (ideally) your lean muscle mass by stimulating muscle protein synthesis (MPS). Muscle mass and strength are shown to be linked to longevity.

Good fats are the second thing to include (EVOO, omega 3 etc.) and then some seasonal vegetables.
Also, there is no need to count calories if you do things right, the plan is to make life easier and simpler (less stress), not put another pain in the neck in our - already filled with stress - life.

Knowing that the vast majority of people are overweight and/or obese - which means they have low insulin sensitivity - we can safely say that they should start by limiting carbs and increasing protein.

Light exposure and all that great and interesting facts that are discussed here should be complementary to a fixed and balanced diet, they are not going to solve the problem alone, that's by belief.

Also, I believe that keto and carnivore diets should not be forever eating plans for the majority of people, they are useful tools for the practitioner when properly implemented.

Also, you see the evidence of fats all over in the U.K, strawberries and scones with clotted creams in the summer, roast potato’s, Yorkshire puddings in goose fat, sweet puddings made with suet in winter during Christmas, seafood in supermarkets especially wild salmon in spring , cheese platters. where I come from in Bombay monsoons, there’s cloud cover like the U.K and even though warmer temperatures fats go up in the diet while usually the carbs have remained the same but different sources are used like millets and corn etc . and wheat is lesser used. Fruit is not eaten in monsoon. How much carbs can one eat anyway or need is also dependent on outdoor activities. They usually complement your meal in a small portion unless you are addicted to processed food. Whole meal flours fermented for 24-48 hrs are not going to push you very high carb even when eaten all year round. Unless you go too low in protein and are also low fat I guess? When you have gut issues, neurological or autoimmune conditions simply thinking carbs is detrimental. You get sicker by experience in the U.K. and DHA is king for sure

I wanted to understand more but there is only so much time that I could put into. My son is growing older, I see time ticking fast and I want to make fewer mistakes that prolong his healing. Kids do need food to grow not just light. The light part is being looked at but food is important too.

If I was an expert already like you are I wouldn’t need to be here. I am here because I need to learn. And for that we need response.

Details require your focus. If you don't focus you'll hear it from me. I'd suggest you read this Jims ---->

RULES TO PROTECT YOUR INNER PEACE AT THIS WEBSITE

1\. Cut off toxic people in your life. Although it is not easy to do it, for you to have peace of mind, you have to. This includes members who pay you.

2\. Distance yourself from people who only give out negative thoughts and energy. They will never help you grow into a decentralized thinker.

3\. Choose your battles wisely. Not everything requires your energy and reaction. Details matter. It requires your focus.

4\. Be careful in letting people be a part of your life. Not everyone is a friend. Most students are not.

5\. Unwind. When everything starts to feel tiring, go on a vacation to recharge yourself. You deserve it.

6\. Learn when to walk away. If it is no longer giving you peace of mind, it is not worth it. Sometimes I have to push members into retirement with a ban.

7\. Always make time for yourself. Being alone feels therapeutic at times. Silence allows you to think

8\. Have less time for social media and more of starting a new hobby. Be productive with your time. Stop wasting it on the superfluous.

9\. Be adaptable to change. Always brace yourself for endless possibilities. This subject requires your awareness.

10\. Knowing your triggers is your responsibility. Not all people will adjust to your temperament. Deal with that appropriately. By design, I have fed many trolls here for a long time. Once I realize they add nothing, I will adjust rapidly.

### 1oevets — 2024-07-09T08:38:34+0300

After looking at the forum more that seems to be e case. Do you think it's because of the massive supplement and pharmaceutical usage?

From what I saw there was no talk of real root causes. They are very myopic on diet and hormones. I was there looking for ideas on how to help my kids ulcerative colitis. Most suggestions were based on progesterone pregnelone cyclosporidine and numerous other drugs/supplements not something I really want experiment with on my 6 year old.

They never took it to that next level and would ask why is this or that low or high.
They would just find a supplement or pharmaceutical to try to fix it. It’s not so different than most drs. I think we can agree that this is the wrong approach.

Most of the dietary ideas are very contradictory to what I’ve seen help most in me and my family. I found keto do to what a dr said would be career (firefighter) ending arthritis in my knee if I didn’t start taking drugs. I was 25 at the time. Eating keto/carnivore I was able to not only continue as a structure firefighter but I got into wildland firefighting for a few years. I’m 40 now and still climbing mountains hunting sheep and packing 150 lb moose quarters on my back. If I were to eat peaty id never be able to do the things I love. I’ve tried Saladino animal based approach but even that will eventually cause my knee to ache.

What I really find valuable with Kruse’s point of view is he really has taken a huge 1000 foot view on why humans are getting sick. Emf, Food, water, light, magnetism and has shown why or how the body uses these mechanisms and how we are breaking these laws of nature making ourselves get sick.

Whether he is 100% right about everything he saids I’m not sure. I am sure that he has a better understanding of how the human body works and how our environment affects its function than any other person I’ve come across.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336863>

> [1oevets said:](/goto/post?id=336862)

Most suggestions were based on progesterone pregnelone cyclosporidine and numerous other drugs/supplements not something I really want experiment with on my 6 year old.
>
> They never took it to that next level and would ask why is this or that low or high.
> They would just find a supplement or pharmaceutical to try to fix it. It’s not so different than most drs. I think we can agree that this is the wrong approach.
>
> Most of the dietary ideas are very contradictory to what I’ve seen help most in me and my family. I found keto do to what a dr said would be career (firefighter) ending arthritis in my knee if I didn’t start taking drugs. I was 25 at the time. Eating keto/carnivore I was able to not only continue as a structure firefighter but I got into wildland firefighting for a few years. I’m 40 now and still climbing mountains hunting sheep and packing 150 lb moose quarters on my back. If I were to eat peaty id never be able to do the things I love. I’ve tried Saladino animal based approach but even that will eventually cause my knee to ache.
>
>
> What I really find valuable with Kruse’s point of view is he really has taken a huge 1000 foot view on why humans are getting sick. Emf, Food, water, light, magnetism and has shown why or how the body uses these mechanisms and how we are breaking these laws of nature making ourselves get sick.
>
> Whether he is 100% right about everything he saids I’m not sure. I am sure that he has a better understanding of how the human body works and how our environment affects its function than any other person I’ve come across.

Excellent reply. I agree with everything you said. That's quite the fitness level you have. Just curious have you ever tried a ray peat diet and if so how did you fare?

### coho3131 — 2024-07-10T01:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336899>

I would not trust people recommending certain foods for the whole general population.

These people don't realize the importance of sunlight, circadian rhythms, local environments, seasons, and latitude.

I also think eating non-native fruits at high latitudes in an environment with nnEMF is a recipe for disaster in any season.

### 1oevets — 2024-07-10T03:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336906>

the ray peat diet was pretty close to what I grew up eating as a kid until I was 16 and gained some freedom. My mom cooked every meal, brought my lunch to school until 9th 10th grade. Worst thing I ate according to peats ideas would be peanut butter and jelly sandwiches for lunch and Friday night pizza with the family. My mom saw through the vegetable oil marketing and never used them and didn’t let us eat fast food or processed food. I was pretty healthy but I definitely was on the chubby side. 16 yo 5 8 170 playing jv football. I’m 5 9 now, Biggest I got eating sad was 210 after my first kid was born. Eating keto carnivore I can lean out to 170 and have some ab definition with a little work but most the time I’m 180-185.I tried Saladino animal based approach which is pretty close to peat a few years ago but quickly put 15-20 lbs on was always hungry. Aches and pains started again and generally felt like crap. Personally I found every couple weeks if I have a light to moderate clean carb day I do pretty well. Going long periods 0 carb you can feel the cortisol rises and I don’t like it. I’m interested experimenting with adding more carbs to my diet when we move to Texas and see if this approach works…..

### Brent Patrick — 2024-07-10T05:51:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336907>

Carbs are highly charged they have alot more blue light photons which is why they enter cytochrome one and not cytochrome two which is for fats.
When you eat out of season mainly rely on fats you are missing out on cell signaling in cytochrome 1 you have lower redox and reduced superoxide burst.

**"In the ubiquitination series, I showed you a loss of cell signaling in cytochrome 1, means redox becomes reduced at cytochrome 1 and lowers superoxide burst and affects cardiolipin release. Cardiolipin release changes the size and shape of mitochondria indicating a redox shift has occured. This tells us ultraweak biophoton emission and oxygen consumption has changed metabolically indicating to us a redox shift in metaobolism has occured. This changes the phenotype of the organism by altering the electronic state of the tertiary and quaternary bends in semiconductive proteins.

What are the implications of losing this pulsed superoxide in a zip code of mitochondria? You lose the ability to collect and move electrons in the brain’s neurons using DHA. This means the mitochondria in these neurons cannot harvest those electrons of their excited light energy." JK**

\= Seasonal diet with plenty of seafood!

# Thread: Any UK people here? What does your diet look like?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MatthewT — 2024-06-11T17:44:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335690>

Just seeing if there’s any fellow UK people in here and would love to hear what you eat in a day if so.

I want to gain weight as I’m really slim and the way it seems to me is a bit of extra weight is protective against things like emf, I don’t want to get fat or build a ton of muscle just a few more lbs probably a bit of both lean muscle and fat.

If I’m to follow the advice of epi paleo and I’m not misunderstood I would be eating super low carb all the time in the UK as we don’t get very much UV, with this in mind what foods could I utilise to put on the mass?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T20:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335696>

Guinness (liquid bread)

### Jims — 2024-06-12T20:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335745>

> [MatthewT said:](/goto/post?id=335690)
>
> Just seeing if there’s any fellow UK people in here and would love to hear what you eat in a day if so.
>
> I want to gain weight as I’m really slim and the way it seems to me is a bit of extra weight is protective against things like emf, I don’t want to get fat or build a ton of muscle just a few more lbs probably a bit of both lean muscle and fat.
>
> If I’m to follow the advice of epi paleo and I’m not misunderstood I would be eating super low carb all the time in the UK as we don’t get very much UV, with this in mind what foods could I utilise to put on the mass?

Hi, I’m in the U.K too and still learning what will work food wise. We introduced finger millet flatbread and pancakes in a small quantity, eaten with ghee or coconut oil for our son who is also in need of some help with weight gain. He doesn’t do well on potatoes yet even the small ones which are supposed to have less starch. We will try again when it warms up Have also used buckwheat flour in the past but prefer the millet. We were too low carb looking back now. It has helped with his grip and focus but he is getting a lot of hayfever symptoms which creates its own challenges with ADHD, although better than last summer but still bad.

### rtd — 2024-06-12T21:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335749>

Raw milk. Start with 1 gallon a week. In my unscientific N=1 trials, raw cow milk adds mass faster then goat milk. FYI: I prefer raw goat milk

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-03T13:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336502>

I was born in the UK
What's your BMI currently?

I'm here this summer prior to getting to the tropics permanently.

My current day to maintain weight and bear in mind I'm 54 with 45% water in the body is to have a light breakfast of fruit and ham and a dinner of eg 10 Oz of lamb with roast potatoes (ghee) and ketchup
But I'm not a healthy youngster like yourself
I remember putting on weight eating chocolate. Because chocolate comes from the tropics and whilst it stifles melanin it's full of deuterium.

What exactly is your body shape ? Ectomorph ? Mesomorph ? Frame ?

### MatthewT — 2024-07-03T17:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336512>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=336502)
>
> I was born in the UK
> What's your BMI currently?
>
> I'm here this summer prior to getting to the tropics permanently.
>
> My current day to maintain weight and bear in mind I'm 54 with 45% water in the body is to have a light breakfast of fruit and ham and a dinner of eg 10 Oz of lamb with roast potatoes (ghee) and ketchup
> But I'm not a healthy youngster like yourself
> I remember putting on weight eating chocolate. Because chocolate comes from the tropics and whilst it stifles melanin it's full of deuterium.
>
> What exactly is your body shape ? Ectomorph ? Mesomorph ? Frame ?

My BMI is 18.6 at the moment so just out of the underweight range and I’m certainly an ectomorph.

It’s hard to find foods like you say that are low in deuterium and high in calories, there’s only so much meat I can stomach so it’s just finding the additional calories outside of that.

I’m eating in the morning at the moment beef mince with eggs cooked in ghee then turkey steaks, sardines sometimes some organic dark chocolate with some almond butter for lunch and another meat based meal for dinner whether it be salmon, prawns, more beef or turkey and oysters sometimes. I am still not eating in a big enough surplus to gain weight.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-03T18:06:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336522>

Just ask a local farmer what foods are native to your seasons at this latitude. Why is this so hard for people to do? It is quite simple.

What is your age? I could not gain a pound from 16-20 despite eating like a pig. Was also very active and lifted. Father time allows weight gain, you get stoked (as was I), several plateaus ups and then it can go off the rails. I'd say enjoy your leanness while it lasts.

### MatthewT — 2024-07-03T19:38:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336532>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=336522)
>
> Just ask a local farmer what foods are native to your seasons at this latitude. Why is this so hard for people to do? It is quite simple.

I’m perfectly aware of what food is seasonally available where I’m from, it is an issue however because even in the farm shops and farmers markets I go to they sell produce from all over the world it’s never local.

I use mostly a farm shop where it is labelled the country it is from and I buy strictly only English produce.

I won’t get very far trying to put on weight with just meat and lettuce though, that is why I ask on here, I hoped there would be other people from where I am from who have faced a similar issue and overcome it and would offer me advice, I’m not willing to mess up my health to accomplish this as I have other issues more important to me so reaching out to wiser people seemed to be the best option to me - I assume this is what this forum is intended for right?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-03T21:13:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336544>

> [MatthewT said:](/goto/post?id=336532)
>
> I’m perfectly aware of what food is seasonally available where I’m from, it is an issue however because even in the farm shops and farmers markets I go to they sell produce from all over the world it’s never local.
>
> I use mostly a farm shop where it is labelled the country it is from and I buy strictly only English produce.
>
> I won’t get very far trying to put on weight with just meat and lettuce though, that is why I ask on here, I hoped there would be other people from where I am from who have faced a similar issue and overcome it and would offer me advice, I’m not willing to mess up my health to accomplish this as I have other issues more important to me so reaching out to wiser people seemed to be the best option to me - I assume this is what this forum is intended for right?

How much exercise do you do ?
As one person mentioned if you are young and have a low BMI and eat till you are full and don't feel hungry then I wouldn't worry about it too much. Seriously

I could not put weight on as a16-18 year old at all. I was stuck at 10 stone 8 not knowing at the time that that was ideal for my height

I know that one friend of mine at university was stick thin and remains pretty much under the 25-6 range of BMI, at uni he was 18-19 to the point of graduation.
He has run the coast to coast in South island new Zealand, a couple of times. He took up triathlon in his 40s and now he's still thin. But muscular I think he doesn't eat enough fat. I stayed with him in Tasmania for 4 weeks and the meat was devoid of fat that he would buy.

I actually felt hungry

I wouldn't worry about your weight. The moment you hit thirty you'll put on weight. If you're in your 20s now then enjoy being able to eat and respire and not put weight on.

Roast local potatoes in lard or tallow and eat those. If you still cannot put weight/ muscle/ fat on then accept that your pathways are optimal

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-03T21:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336546>

Remember that carnivore is for winter only when nothing grows except the odd herb or salsify as a root vegetable. In the summer use the potatoes, that are new

### rtd — 2024-07-03T21:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336548>

>
> I won’t get very far trying to put on weight with just meat and lettuce though, that is why I ask on here, I hoped there would be other people from where I am from who have faced a similar issue and overcome it and would offer me advice, I’m not willing to mess up my health to accomplish this as I have other issues more important to me so reaching out to wiser people seemed to be the best option to me - I assume this is what this forum is intended for right?

Anyone who wants to add mass: one gallon of milk a day. There is a reason young men who are really skinny use this to add mass. It is not for older people. It is not permanent.

As not a young man, when raw milk became legal in my state I started consuming it. Loved it! Too much. At one point I was crushing 3 gallons a week. Guess what? I packed on weight really fast. Why? I was consuming 10 days of average calories in 7 days. And that was if I was following the US recommendation for men having 2500 calories/day. Now I am working on losing all that weight.

### Annelieuk — 2024-07-05T18:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336675>

If you have awful health you CAN NOT recover in the U.K! I live on this rainy damp island too and it's hard even if you're not suffering with ill health. I know it's overwhelming to make these changes but I took my two small girls to Costa RIca as my eldest was showing signs of autism. After 1 year she fell into her body and became a perfectly normal little girl, of which I am certain was due to living off grid, being grounded and eating seasonally. It was NOT easy to up and leave. We did 5 years in C.R and came home 5 years ago. 2 years into being home she started to show signs of OCD. She is now 17 and gorgeous and healthy but has had to work REALLY hard on her mental health, managing EMF;s phone use, blocking blue light etc,, however she is desperate to go back to the tropics - she's half Iranian and knows when it's time to have a good bout of sun stability. However, I also KNOW that we must all move back to the equator soon for my kids and husbands health, as they just desperately NEED it. It's so much harder when you have a family but when you get your mind right about the environment side of things and the high need for light stability to heal, it just becomes an adventure. Top tip is to try and get to the Canary Islands for few weeks over the winter (ideally when there isn't a Calima) - we do this every Feb and it's been a life saver!

Thank you for sharing your experience. We loved Gran canaria, we were there last summer for two weeks and my wife and kids loved it and didn’t want to go back home. The resort we stayed was on the beach and spent most of the time swimming and outside. They had amazing sea food and it was so easy with the kids. I could see my son thriving more by the second week and it was hard to leave. We are heading to Turkey for a couple of weeks end of July.

I can’t pack up and leave but if my wife and kids could get away for longer it is worth exploring. We are both from Mumbai in India and lived in England for more than 20 yrs. My daughter who is 7 loves her school and she’s too social to be home schooled. She’s in fact terrified of it after we began homeschooling my son. We do have a home there but it’s not off grid by any stretch. Where do you go in the canaries? Thanks for the tip! Getting another holiday in winter would be great but don’t have the finances sorted yet. Hopefully we can get away again this year

### Sue-UK — 2024-07-07T11:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336790>

Hi Matthew

If you put methylene blue in the search and Jack's name it will show you what he has said about it. Its not something to mess with if you don't know what you are doing. Its not a decision based on finances but I've chosen to stay in the UK, based on my own n=1. All my food comes from the supermarket. I eat carbs all year, just much less in winter. PM me if you want to know how I shop for food, but bear in mind its based on my experience, my n=1, and I know nothing about yours. My interest is finding ideas that are more affordable for those who don't have a lot in terms of financial resources, (and the savings I make help fund trips to the sun in the winter.). While you work towards being able to move abroad if that's what you decide and it becomes possible, squeeze the low hanging fruit until the pips squeak while you learn more and are able to apply new knowledge safely based on your context. Its all very well if its possible to move abroad, but I don't accept that if I was financially constrained or for any other reason based on my situation couldn't do that I would be doomed. If CT is a metabolic back door, there's others to find and tap in the UK, and finding them is my challenge.

### Sue-UK — 2024-07-07T12:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336791>

> [MatthewT said:](/goto/post?id=336789)
>
> potatoes were a thought they’re always in season but I’m sure I heard they’re high in deuterium that’s why I ask so I can be pointed in the right direction, the reason I imagine why this forum was created.

Deuterium is a huge rabbit hole. I see it as a nutrient, but like any nutrient, you can have too little or too much. There are nuances. Deuterium is not the enemy unless its in the wrong place eg mitochondria, or excessive in the wrong season such as winter, or when avoiding available UV by living indoors during the summer, sunscreen etc. There are also deuterium depleting pathways (such as during REM sleep), which is another reason why getting circadian rhythm sorted and prioritising good sleep is important. I'm not sure if REM sleep depletes it, or if its necessary for REM sleep to happen and its used rather than depleted, but years ago, unrelated to deuterium, when I reported I was dreaming and remembering my dreams Jack said that was a good sign. UV plus deuterium is not the same biological response as deuterium in the absence of UV whilst living in the warm lane in winter, which is why I live in the cold lane in winter, eating enough carb to get good sleep. Zero carbs screws my sleep, n=1.

### Sue-UK — 2024-07-12T12:59:43+0300

I've seen mention of it by Jack a number of times. It may be called "photonic pressure" or "radiation pressure" by physicists. How it works in biology, though, is what I'm most interested in. Thanks!
>
> Cheers,
> Dennis

Hi Dennis

Musings

Not _how_ UVA pressure induces deuterium to make UVC, physics and equations leave me like a deer in the headlights., but it being in the emission spectrum is something I can get my head around. The problem with biology research on zoo or lab animals is that it wouldn't replicate how it happens in nature where I think the "recipe" could be unique for each mammalian species.

In his book Cross Currents, Becker said

_It appears that, over 2 billion years of evolution, living things have taken advantage of the two portions of the electromagnetic spectrum that could be depended upon to always be present: the geomagnetic field and visible light. In this view, it is no more surprising that life developed specific organs to sense the geomagnetic field and to derive timing information from it than that it developed specific organs to sense and derive information from light."

"More recently, it has been shown that the pineal is also sensitive to the daily cyclic pattern in the Earth's magnetic field. Melatonin secretion in human subjects may be changed at will by exposure to steady magnetic fields of the same strength as the geomagnetic field. **Apparently, nature determined that biological cycle activity was too important to be left to one environmental signal alone. "**

"Nature intended the pineal to simultaneously receive the same signals from the daily pattern of day-night and the same rise and fall in strength of the geomagnetic field. Obviously, when one or both signals are abnormal, the pineal does not respond in the normal fashion, and the body's biological cycles become disturbed - with important clinical consequences."_

Bears go into hibernation grounded and in the cold, deuterium loaded from berries and don't poo or pee for months. Humans have a circadian cycle for body temperature. If dying cells emit light, that is a possible source for a predator at the kill site, even if the prey is low in deuterium..... and so on....

So far, for me UVA is just one potential "pressure" and nature provides more than one, or mammals wouldn't live in places where there's no sunlight for months at a time, or if they are nocturnal. They would presumably rely more on the geomagnetic field rather than sunlight for circadian input. There's cold, there's diving to great depths, etc that could provide the deuterium "squeeze" - then there's electricity, and there are electrogenic microbiome bacteria. I wonder if its possible that if a microbiome electrogenic bacteria's electricity hits deuterium, deuterium's emission spectrum provides the UVC ? and if it could also split off a proton to provide to the host making it part of the deuterium depleting pathways?? Then as poo contains a lot of dead bacteria, its what frequencies of light they emitted when they were active and when they were dying ....

JK - _**"Dopamine is made in the eye by sunlight and** i**t can be made in the gut by your microbiome.** **People forget that bacteria release 5000 times more light than eukaryotic cells and this light is capable of making dopamine from the aromatic amino acids in the gut.** This is why serotonin and phenylalanine and tyrosine are stored in the enterochromaffin system there."_
So can the microbiome (fed by the photosynthetic food web linking it back to sunlight) produce the UVA which in turn puts pressure on deuterium leading to an emission of UVC ......

CT can increase dopamine, as can sunlight and the microbiome. Melatonin can increase by a change in the earth's magnetic field. How it works in biology type research would need to be mind bogglingly complex to imitate what nature is doing effortlessly.

But from a UK biohacking point of view I try and look for how different mammals obtain or produce UVC from deuterium in cold dark and boring situations, and for nocturnal mammals. If CT is a metabolic back door, its unlikely its the only one .....

### ajohnson90 — 2024-06-11T18:57:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d-25-hydroxy-labs-despite-being-outside-all-day.29016/post-335692>

I’m located in SLC, UT, and am outside pretty much all day. I wake up with the sun, crack the windows and get ready for work. Drive to work with the windows down and from about 7:30-9 I am inside but then Im outside from 9-7:30. Then when I go home I have the windows open until the sun sets.

That said, only my arms, legs, head, and neck are exposed to the sun while I’m at work. I do expose my torso to sun on my days off

I went in for some bloodwork and was surprised to see that my Vit D labs were at 33. From what I understand 50 is the baseline for real health. I have also read that the conventional vitamin D lab may not be the best method of measuring vitamin D in the body as there are multiple forms and they differ between active and stored, iirc.

I’ve listened to some of Dr Kruses interviews where he talks about how EMFs can disrupt vitamin D synthesis, but it’s not something I entirely understand on a practical level.

Any insight or links to blogs I should read or podcast I should listen to?

Thanks!

I was born at 37N, and after 21 years moved to 35N, that’s when I started experiencing health issues. Moved to UT after 8 years at 35N and have been here for for 4 years. I’ve spent a lot of time outside, especially growing up in the country, and particularly since discovering Jack in 2017. Even moreso since moving to UT, where the weather is always nice enough to go out. But I’ve been working an outdoor job since the end of last year and am outside practically all day, and have open windows when at home or in the car.

I work for the postal service as a postal carrier. Lots of walking and spending all day out doors. I take shade when I can just to not get overheated.
I have to wear shirts, shorts and shoes due to the job but rarely wear any sunscreen. I’ve only worn a mineral based sunscreen a handful of times very recently when I felt like I had too much sun but couldn’t get out of it due to work. Before that I hadn’t worn sunscreen since I was a child in the 90s. I’m gonna get a big hat to mitigate that.

I would like to get a tan-thru shirt, as I know I’m only harming myself by not exposing my gut and torso.

# Thread: Viruses as micro-organisms or something else?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chlo_e — 2024-06-12T22:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-335754>

As someone who's been through traditional medical training, I never thought I'd be questioning the nature of 'viruses'. In fact, when I first heard alternative theories to viruses as microorganisms I lost my head and got into a totally unnecessary online argument. However, over the last couple of years there have been too many instances that have made me pause and reconsider this topic. What if viruses are not microorganisms at all?

I know JK's position is that viruses are obligate intracellular pathogens. I would like to offer a different perspective based on a broad range of research and observations. What if viruses are actually a bodily response to changes in native or nnEMF? What if viruses are actually frequencies and the effects we observe in people and/or animals are the reactionary cellular and biochemical effects of electromagnetic information that the body is struggling to process or function with? What if transmission can occur between people or groups, but not because of transfer of microorganisms or 'infected' cellular content, but because of the transfer of fluid containing water that carries electromagnetic information? I.e what if there is no germ theory in relation to viruses and it is all happening at an EMF level with downstream biochemical and cellular dysfunction causing clinical signs and symptoms? With the host response then being determined by individual characteristics such as nutrition, sunlight exposure, genes and other variables?

I can't find any anti-viral treatment, vaccine, medication or otherwise, that actually targets a microorganism. They all arrest or interfere with cell function in one way or another- i.e OUR OWN CELL FUNCTION.

Are we completely chasing shadows and we've shoe-horned germ theory onto viruses because we couldn't explain them in any other way? And of course we humans cannot deal with uncertainty and so we are often willing to accept explanations for things that may not actually have a good or solid explanation as yet. I'm posting this in curious good-faith to see what other people think, rather than trying to be overtly contrarian.

### Chlo_e — 2024-06-27T12:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-336182>

> [Jingpaw said:](/goto/post?id=336173)
>
> As my brother Alec Zeck once said :
>
> “Here’s how freaking simple it is:
> Virologists add snot from a sick person (that is to contain “viruses”) to viral transport medium (VTM), and then add this mixture to a foreign cell line, alongside antibiotics, nutrients for the cells, and sometimes other stuff.
> The cells break down, experiencing what’s called the cytopathic effect (CPE). This is not a piece of evidence — .
> We did the same steps without any sample of snot (meaning no possible source of any “virus”) and achieved the same results . This means we achieved the same results in which virologists claim the presence and pathogenicity of a virus without any possible “virus” present.”

Yes, thank you for outlining this. I agree that the methodology used to 'isolate' viruses does not show anything resembling what is then interpreted as a virus. I am incredulous that we have built entire professions and systems out of such a flawed concept - although I suspect it has been engineered that way rather than accidental

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-27T17:33:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-336191>

I think my Viral marketing post tells you exactly how I see viruses in an evolutionary framework. This thread on X covers just how important decentralized ideas are for humans. [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131)

### Chlo_e — 2024-06-27T18:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-336196>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=336191)
>
> I think my Viral marketing post tells you exactly how I see viruses in an evolutionary framework. This thread on X covers just how important decentralized ideas are for humans. [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131)

Your thoughts on this are interesting. I wonder whether- if the word virus were to be substituted with energy, water memory, or electromagnetic information, we may still have a cogent rationale that doesn't involve micro-organisms. It would probably be a moot point if we were all living by Nature's laws, but it seems we have strayed so far from Nature that we must now unpick everything to find our way back. Like trying to undo a very large, complicated knot with our toes!

### rtd — 2024-06-15T18:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-335817>

Edit: 23 JUNE 2025
Please feel free to interact with my journal. I no longer want to be an isolationist here.

This is my space to records thoughts, topics and ideas I am interested in health wise. Somewhere I can jot down stuff and go back to as I learn. I have been reading through the forums in chronological order, it is interesting to see how posts evolved from 2012 to now, along with the types of replies. In some forums the number of female to male posts is high, like the early on "Optimal Labs" section. As a male, I found myself where all the other male lab posts? Just an observation. I am still going through in chronological order.

Feedback:
~If I am inquiring for feedback, I will post I am looking for feedback. Outside the man himself, Jack, please do not assume a question or topic I write is an invite to chime in.~ Respectfully I would use "Ask Jack" after researching the topic. (Minus something timely, like a current event)

Current thought explorations:

Bloodwork lower/upperbound data ranges: If derived from an "unhealthy" population, what are optimal values? Sex based or all inclusive?
Male: LDL Cholesterol upper limit?, HDL; age dependent?
Peripheral neuropathy from physical posture
Exogenous vs natural- when to temporarily supplement, time frames
CBC Panel: Red blood cell (RBC), magnesium RBC and General Chemistry magneisum: don't see Mg RBC option in panel
Meyer Cocktails: local vitamin bar via IV?
Fournier effect at Water Spas
**If your redox level is low you should avoid all alcohol no matter what**

### rtd — 2024-06-21T16:31:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-335972>

\*The joys of renting and shoddy landlord work. My black out curtains caught a breeze in the middle of the night, pulling out the "screwed" in hardware keeping the curtain pole up. There was no anchor in the wall; have to fix ASAP as tonight is a full moon.

\*Coronary CT calcium scan or coronary artery calcium (CAC) scan (add to annual physical)

> "[**_Sufficient_**](https://jackkruse.com/what-is-peripheral-neuropathy/) dietary iodine is crucial for proper ketogenesis in our nerves, liver and in our brain. Ketogenesis is critical for myelination of all nerves in humans. These are just three of many tissues that diabetics have massive trouble with. When iodine is low, estrogen levels tend to be higher in both sexes. This also is associated with elevated SHBG on testing.
>
> Estrogen also blocks the absorption of iodine from the human gut. So any cause of leaky gut can also be an etiology for neuropathy even in a non diabetic. I see this many times a week when some one comes in with a normal MRI and classic radicular pain they think is from compression or from a disc. This is why women have higher rates of neuropathy, MS, and hypothyroidism than men. It is also why they have less myelin than men in adulthood naturally. Myelination is a proxy regeneration because of the loss of the regenerative DC current below the myelin level and outside the axon of nerve cells.
>
> Artificial blue light in your environment destroys iodine absorption due to poor melatonin signaling in the brain. When iodine is low in nerves over time it causes [lower levels of vitamin D](https://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-7-vitamin-d/) and [vitamin K2](https://jackkruse.com/osteoporosis-two-the-vitamin-k2-story/) and atherosclerosis usually develops in the blood vessels that feed the vascular supply to nerves. You might be shocked to learn loss of melatonin signaling in the brain and in nerves also increases estrogen levels and, this in turn, further lowers iodine absorption from the gut in both sexes to cause peripheral neuropathy."

### rtd — 2024-06-26T19:44:32+0300

Haven't gotten enough morning sun, sleeping in lately. Feel fatigued. Installed an air conditioner (window) and it is a gift from heaven. My apartment gets hotter at night even though outdoor temp is 10 degree cooler. That with dew point over 70%, stagnant air is a recipe for poor sleep.

Capturing health stuff:
"[Bruxism](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bruxism.18609/#post-202976) is a precursor symptom to sleep apnea and poorly discolored teeth and REM sleep disturbance. Putting a device in your mouth does nothing until you change why bruxism occurs to begin with."

### rtd — 2024-07-02T21:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-336478>

Got 30 mins morning sun (0650-0720) despite sunrise at 0514. The AC is a double edge sword. Non humid sleep + waking up more congested vs humid, hot low quality sleep and being a zombie. I cleaned the filter too. There is something musty in the air, inside and out. My eyelids are inflamed, eyes a bit scratchy and have some eye junk upon awakening. My dog has a slight wheeze and morning sniffles. Definitely something in the air.

### rtd — 2024-07-03T22:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-336553>

Inflamed uvula! Very uncomfortable. Happened from accidentally inhaling concentrate, green mold particles. How? While doing dishes. I opened a glass food container containing a little leftover rice that was under my car seat in hot weather. Above my sink are two windows, both were open with a nice wind. Zoned out while doing the dishes I open the lid and WHOOSH! Like a puff ball mushroom exploding, I got a big whiff. I could taste it and started sneezing. Shortly after I felt something in my throat I did not feel before- my uvula. As it got bigger I though I may need to go the hospital. My throat and mouth were dry, felt unquenchable. Sipping water hurts (still does some). I gargled Black Seed oil several times through out the night and the swelling has subsided some, yet is not fully gone. Sore throat too. Was not expecting that!

### rtd — 2024-07-06T19:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-336739>

Uvula happy again, took several days and all soreness is gone. This morning was 100% humidity with 70% dew point. You just sweat and everything is damp. Even my dog does not want to play much. This weather makes me not hungry and I have not been hitting my daily minimum protein intake. Mid day I'll cook outside if not raining and try to eat 1lb of hamburger with mixed in beef heart and liver. A cold plunge (CT) would be amazing, I am going to buy an inflatable tub and try it on my deck. I have a hose and a 5 lbs ice block maker. Renting an apartment has upsides and downsides and I am thankful I can afford it.

### rtd — 2024-07-10T19:23:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-336922>

I've been reading "Ask Jack" from oldest to newest and what a bunch of great information! I love the rapid fire form, I call it "Krusing". Even more so I enjoy when someone posts to long and Jack responds, "too long, closed" hahaha. Reading posts regarding dairy, I decided to stop consuming raw milk until I reach a desired percentage body fat. In mean time, have to finish my Skyr yogurts (especially @ $2.49/ea) and a few small bottles of Goat Kefir ($8.99/ea). Then I will stop those too.

Septoplasty with turbinate reduction is in near future. The damage is structural from a car accident. Over a decade I cannot breathe much from one nostril. The damage was confirmed via digital imaging. The ENT is supposedly top in my state and is linked to Brown University. I delayed the surgery years ago because we had an vocal disagreement. After confirming the damage was structural and no signs (at that time) of inflammation, he stated I have to do a nasal steroid spray for a month to see if there is any improvement.

I repeatedly asked why a steroid to reduce inflammation is necessary given the information he provided. That the data does not show inflammation, how would a steroid change/alter the structure permanently. Finally he loudly said it has to be done for insurance reasons prior to doing a surgery. I said ok, that I understand. Why did not you say that in the 1st place. I left and did not follow up.

Ordered a 23 and Me kit today. Three days to get here, advertised 3-4 weeks for processing.

Purchased a Polar Tub for CT. Old Second floor deck, hope it doesn't collapse

### rtd — 2024-07-17T20:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-337267>

First visit with my new PCP was positive. We spoke for about half an hour while I was verbally providing my medical history (not much), health goals, lifestyle, etc. I did not speak about quantum health as I wanted to get a feel for the person. He is very fit for his age, his staff not so much and that is ok. This visit primarily was on boarding and to address a respiratory issue I have had since late Jan. Will see were it goes from here. Physical in 4 weeks. He wants me to stop dairy intake and go on a Mediterranean diet for now. I do not want to stop yogurt intake. Milk and cheese I will forgo. I'll do seasonal veggies with fish, pork, and beef; along with local fruits only (in few days raspberries, black berries and blue berries). Peaches next month, fall are pears and apples.

Portable Polar Tub arrived. No way can it be inside my apartment due to leak risk. Locating it on the deck I am having second thoughts. Think I have yard spot that will work (hose accessible and some shade) and have to discuss with tenant below me. Regardless, I started making 6 lb ice blocks

### rtd — 2024-07-27T16:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-337629>

Down 12 pounds in past few weeks. Dietary and light changes only, not exercising other then walking the dog. Wild raspberries and blueberries I find when dog walking are a nice treat.

23 and Me kit- have not submitted it yet. Still have privacy concerns.

Home ownership - More homes are hitting the local market. Two properties I scouted were rental properties with not much up keep. Checking tax records and cross referencing, the owners passed away and do not live in the area. No skin in the game. I am interested and am okay with fa ixer upper to a point. Both I saw have foundation problems (sinking and cracking), along with antiquated electric panels. One place I could smell mold. The property values are worth more then the structures. I cannot afford to purchase, tear down, rebuild.

Mold - common word in my posts. There was a water leak awhile back and it was not fully repaired (occasionally leaks with heavy rain). I am more convinced the respiratory issue I have had maybe linked to that. Gotta look into an air quality test.

Downsizing - donated items I like yet do not fit my current living space, mostly cookware, clothes and a few appliances. Books are next; however, I love reading and get attached to my books. I do not like moving books and if they sit on a shelf and no one has access to them, is it wise to keep them? Certain books are keepers, like reference books. A few are out of print. I saw a two volume set on Ebay for $900 that years ago I purchased for $75. A part of me thinks having real books is important because a digital edition can be altered easily. Plus I can read them in the sunlight or by candle light.

Bought all new linens, comforter and undergarments. Cut up old t-shirts and boxer shorts to make cleaning rags. Forced to cleaned out my pantry as the wall shelving failed and broke many jars and cans etc. Whomever installed the shelving system did not secure the hardware to any wall studs. A few sheet rock anchors were used. Joys of renting. Thankfully the pantry has a door, preventing my dog from walking in broken glass and eating the food on the floor.

Purged old cooking spices. Organized my half of a garage bay to make space for a bicycle. Freezer chest is next.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-27T17:50:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-337634>

> [sunman said:](/goto/post?id=337383)
>
> How did you get access to "ask jack"? Im silver but still unable to access the "ask Jack" forums.

I believe Ask Jack is restricted to Gold members.

### rtd — 2024-08-24T17:02:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-338858>

Busy past 4 weeks. Its been difficult to get up early and watch sunrise, often I slept in. For me- sleep, feeling rested, good brain function is more important for me right now. I do not have an option to go back to bed in the morning or an afternoon nap if I want to remain employed, lol.

Home ownership - prices are falling. The property adjacent to me has dropped by $50k to $625k and needs alot of work. The land value and location is the main cost IMO.

Diet has included tinned small fish, eggs, and burritos. Started caffeine again, green tea and occasionally a diet coke.

Trying to find a dog walker and a cleaning service. I am jaded because a previous cleaning service employee (bonded) stole from several clients and it was difficult to get a resolution. The person went to jail. My previous dog walker I knew.

Am keeping on, grinding. Barring any financial or health issue, in one year I will be debt free. My cash flow will improve. Seems so far away.

### rtd — 2024-08-29T22:26:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-339063>

Two steps forward, three steps back. I am my own worst enemy at times. Been eating too many late night carbs. Kraft Shells and Cheese was so rewarding. Stopped caffeine again based on how my stomach felt with it. There is a new pizza place near me that does NYC style slices, hot cheese $3.50 and open until 2am. Only one other place that does slices and it is for lunch only.

Potentially new dog walker two work days a week. The person is semi-retired and am uncertain about their reliability. Yet I am I happy to see what pans out. Working on getting a Saturday dog day sitter. Work mode is my priority. Need to be debt free and bank cash.

Received a work award- bonus time off! 8 hours, I am thankful. My team works well and meets deadlines. Monetary awards are taxed, time off is not. A work software suite was upgraded to latest version, over 13 years from last upgrade. Big refactoring is coming; all those years of compounding technical debt.

An Indian Curry restaurant opened, first one on the island. I am craving buttered chicken, which I can make. Yet it is easier to order. My version uses cleaner ingredients but then I have enough for 4 people and binge eat. Thinking ordering goat biryani. I'm my own worst enemy.

Off island and a few towns away is a Pakistani/Local fusion restaurant. One block away is a small performing arts theatre. I need to leave the island for an evening. Catching a show and a trying the restaurant would be good.

### rtd — 2024-09-21T16:18:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-339793>

I, hypocrite.

My body is giving me signs my lifestyle is not good and I know that. Knowing Jack's work, being on this blog, and not doing well makes me a hypocrite. Why is walking the walk difficult at times? I have resources just not a lot of time. Balance is required as my focus has been getting debt free ASAP. What good is that if my health declines?

I, hypocrite.

I treated my self to fresh orange juice. I drank too much, too fast. I have had issues with eating and drinking quickly, more so when I am alone. Shortly after I had an anxiety episode and my teeth felt dirty. I had do controlled breath work. Thinking all the sugar to quickly. It was an impulse purchase. Have not had juice, minus aloe vera cactus juice, in a long time.

Never did the 23 and Me kit, and all of the companies independent directors stepped down.

I will do better, starting with a scheduled daily health routine and more meal planning. Self bootstrapping.

### rtd — 2024-09-28T16:32:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-340117>

Better week food wise, did meal planning and prep. Steaks with salad, a butternut squash with kale and tahini soup (instant pot dish), baked a full chicken, steamed carrot and asparagus, lamb and veggie for next several meals. Two small mushroom quiche and two small fruit tarts from a French bakery (imported flour), delicious. I noticed I tend to drink majority of my daily water in the evenings, I am planning to spread it out. Small wins: zero pizza slices this week (technically 9 days), zero soda, zero milk. I did re-introduce goat kefir with no issues.

Sleep seemed better as an aggregate. I've been waking at 3am regularly to use the bathroom (evening water consumption) and take my dog out too. I shoot for 4:30-5:00am to wake up. Now using NuCalm's "Rescue" during that time slot in the living room, laying on the floor. Sometimes with the fireplace on with windows open, getting a mix of cool and hot air circulating around me. It is very peaceful time of the day. Plus the dog loves the fireplace. (I did exactly this today. Post NuCalm session, I drifted off with vivid dreams (evidence of REM sleep?) and awoke 20 min before official sunrise time for my area. Felt a little groggy, think that was due to the room being warm).

### rtd — 2024-10-04T23:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-340426>

Ordered a pair of Ra-Optics day light blockers (yellow'ish lens) for screen work. I have been using Spectra 479s, in the evenings, and I like them. Trying something new.

Nice sunrise walk the doggo this morning. On opposite side of street, I saw a coyote trotting up the sidewalk in a densely settled area. Mofos are everywhere.

Virtually no appetite last three days. My protein and over all caloric intake is way down. During this period, my left and right Achilles have been randomly pulsating. Crazy thirst. So much so I added supplements to the water to prevent getting flushed out. Other than that, I feel pretty darn good!

NuCalming daily, using the "rescue 120" starting at 9pm. This track is wild and I like it. Sometimes I am sleeping during it, other times I'm in altered conscious dream like state. My body feels heavy yet if I think hard I can lift a limb.

Officially candle season! I use tall bees wax candles. Wearing the Spectra479 with the candle creates such a chill ambiance. Take the glasses off and the candle looks so much brighter! What a difference.

### rtd — 2024-10-09T20:37:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-340706>

Received my Ra-optics and am wearing them as I type, I like them. In half a day of comparison, my spectra's seem too much for day time usage. Night time and early AM pre sunrise, perfect. Went with the Maxwell style frame.

[HDL](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-anchorage-alaska.18840/post-205127):

> "What is HDL to a quantum biologist? It is another proxy for how much sunlight or lack of sunlight is buried within the plasma of your blood. That redox stimulus in the blood plasma is what signals the liver to make HDL or not too. Increasing your exposure to sunlight, especially in the morning, and maximize darkness at night will raise your HDL tremendously and benefit your heart because it will shrink your respiratory proteins in mitochondria to make them increase energy flow. When energy flow in the heart is well the rhythms it generates are proper. A fib is another proxy for a body devoid of light. Just by sleeping with an eye mask at night and try to avoid all artificial light after dusk. New electronics put out unreal amounts of light, and completely destroy our normal circadian cortisol/melatonin cycles and lower HDL more than any other behavior we engage in, including obesity or a carp diet. You must “tighten” your retinal exposure to sunlight, because this sharpens our brain’s perception of our circadian cycle of melatonin in the pineal-thyroid neural pathways in the brain. This is why HDL rises. It is a primary redox chemical of the blood plasma. If you blood flowed in you car TRIPLE AAA would pay attention to its HDL level to decide who needs a jump of their battery. When you pay attention to the AM light cycle, it helps tremendously optimizing your appropriate diurnal cortisol levels during the day, and minimizes cortisol production at night. It is critical to avoid living and working in semi-darkness during the day. This correlates with HDL levels and cariac function. For example, a radiologist who works in a dark room looking at films all day is at big time risk for cortisol problems and very low HDL unless they do something to offset it. People who work in mines during day break hours carry the same risk. People who work in semiconductor clean rooms under bright light at night carry the same risks as well. Following the light cycles for cortisol is a critical point that really should be followed strictly by all patients who are clinically trying to optimize their own cortisol levels daily. I also do not advocate wearing sunglasses in the daytime because of the circuit, no matter the season we are in. Humans tend to be very sensitive to loss of light in the winter months because of a lack of sunlight. Those humans at high latitudes around fake light have the lowest levels of HDL I have ever seen. They also have low sex steroid hormones and tend to be depressed until they see the sun. Then they see life in a new way."

### rtd — 2024-10-09T21:07:33+0300

### rtd — 2024-10-19T18:20:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-341247>

Was ill for a few days mid-week. So was a co-worker; I did not telecommute! Appetite slowly coming back. I am avoiding saltines, which has been a life long go to when ill and stomach not feeling well. Being ill is always mentally tough on me as I live alone. Sure, my dog is great yet I have to take care of her too. Makes me think about retirement homes and places like that. I do not want to end up in that style of living.

I keep telling myself that feeling down energy wise is temporary. Just have to dig down deeper and pull myself up and out. A positive is I did not turn on my television or use my personal computer. I did use my cell to get podcasts, then set it to airplane mode. Not a big win but I take what I can get at.

Still enjoying the Ra-Optics, my coworker's comments be damned.

Hi, How are you? Any better? I also had a slight fever - just 37,5°C - and headache and decided just to let it be - didn't stress about it thanks to all I've learned now. It's gone. Take care!

### rtd — 2024-11-13T21:12:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-342317>

A Wellness Center near me just added cold plunge only to their services. Before it was linked to a sauna session (marketed as "Fire and Ice"). I contacted them via email a few times asking for just cold plunge, no sauna, for 10 mins vs 30mins- never received a response back.

This morning I saw the Wellness Center's store front sign advertising for cold plunge only: US$20 non members, $15 members. Includes shower and towels. So-so hours yet it is workable. Am thinking 3 days a week/min.

### rtd — 2024-11-19T15:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-342628>

Some thoughts.

My WIFI router has been off for two weeks, I have not noticed a difference in sleep quality. I can see more SSIDs, signal strengths are low, now that foliage has fallen. A new thing is my cell phone is now getting 5G signal strength in the house on the south side. The strength is about a bar and drops outs. I also think that is foliage related.

My home heating system is all electric; every room has a thermostat. I do not heat several of the rooms. For my bedroom, I purchased a room humidifier as the air is very dry now. Arrives soon. I feel extremely dried out each morning I wake up despite the amount of water I consume. I sip water through out the night. I consume minimum 96 oz from 0500-1600. Trying to get a happy balance of hydration and not waking up in the night to use the bathroom. Also purchased a WaterPik. The base unit is small, yet my bathroom is smaller. It does not fit on the bathroom counter.

Evening light has been beeswax candles. They burn cleaner. Getting day light M-F is more challenging. Weekends I am outside alot. The dog and I nap in a field often facing the sun, often shirtless. The ground is far from frozen and my region has been in drought for several months.

### rtd — 2024-11-22T23:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-342813>

"Hello darkness my old friend"... Am finishing up at work and it is dark out (1615). Keep telling myself it is only for a few months, yet is it a heavy weight. I have grow lights on at my home for my plants and my dog. Candles when I get home. I no longer work weekends, which is nice.

### rtd — 2024-11-25T20:16:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-342902>

The past weekend was very off health wise. I felt out of breath from doing basic cardio AND that followed to resting time periods. I use an oximeter to track my oxygen and beats per minute. O2 was lower and BPM high. Chest felt awkward too. I scheduled a Calcium heart scan. Previous EKG and Chest X-ray were fine.

### rtd — 2024-12-03T21:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-343248>

T-minus 3 days until my heart scan. I have not had an accelerated heart beat since, also have not done any elevated cardio- minus brisk walking. I hope whatever chest discomfort I am experiencing is detected.

Thanksgiving was nice. Visited family. Prime rib instead of turkey. I avoided desserts and drinks. Did not over eat. I did hit the charcuterie spreads pretty hard pre meal. Love me cured meats with fruits and cheeses. There was a melted brie with apricot spread.

I am tired. To bed early and up early. The 2pm hour hits hard; especially sans afternoon caffeine or sugar. To nap would be glorious; damn these social constructs and hours. Productivity. That is how output should be gauged, not just hours. I am having dinner at friend's home tonight and would rather nap in sweat pants. However, it is partially my initiative. I ordered a cake and asked my friends if they want to get together to share. They pulled the dinner card. I just wanted to share cake, maybe with a cup of tea. No dinner- JUST CAKE. I do not know what they are making.

Switched to a softer tooth brush head; can feel the difference on my gums. Changed deodorant brands from Old Spice Classic to Nivea Ocean- TBD. Turned on the electric heat last night in bedroom (50F) and bathroom (65F). Now wearing socks and winter hat to bed (only). Head and feet warm, I am warm. This morning, the dining room thermometer was 46F. I keep the heat off in that room. Main room I keep at 56 F with propane.

I've been walking my dog pre sun rise then off to work. Ground is freezing. Tough time of the year for AM sunlight at 42N

### rtd — 2024-12-06T19:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-343444>

Few hours until my heart scan, I am nervous a bit. A positive is there is an oyster bar near the medical office. A dozen oysters, 1/2 dozen little necks and 1/2 dozen shrimps is a go to.

Bitcoin broke $100k and fell. Bitcoin has been a great trade, it is not (currently) money. It is a tradeable asset class. I do not like the ledger, every exchange being trackable; defies the idea of liberty to me. Still think US deep state created it: [https://archive.org/details/CryptographyOfAnonymousElectronicCash/mode/2up](https://archive.org/details/CryptographyOfAnonymousElectronicCash/mode/2up)

Yesterday I was very vibrant, good quality sleep. I am very tired today, not enough sleep. No caffeine so far. Aloe Vera juice and water. Beef shoulder cuts with horseradish and grilled vegetables. I am craving onion soup.

Below freezing temperatures here. With wind chill, feels like 20F. I like it, wakes me up. Flip side I would enjoy napping by a fire until I feel rested. Saturday I can do that. No set plans outside regular chores.

### rtd — 2024-12-20T23:02:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-344187>

My heart scan did not happen for personal health reasons. Perhaps a nnEMF blessing in disguise. During the work week I am using red light therapy in the AM and evenings. Good chance of snow this evening. Most likely it will melt and be slush then ice.

Outer order, inner calmness. I am looking forward to the weekend to rest and continue parsing through my possessions. Many books to go through that I have irrational sentiment to retain. For example, lots of cook books and I have Paul Stamets' complete mycology set. I know I will have to move sooner then later and ideally will be downsizing my living area. Cookware too. I no longer have back yard get togethers so a giant pot, server platters, etc. Coffee mugs- how did I get so many!? My camping gear, two tents, stove, etc- have not been in a long time. I no longer backpack either nor plan to. All good shape. Same with two guitars. Items can always be repurchased or rented. Possessions truly own you.

### rtd — 2024-12-27T15:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-344384>

The end of the year is close. Looking ahead at S&P500, 2025 most likely will be a down year (correction) and a buying opportunity. Media heads have been talking about a crash or bubble, which can generate a fake out.

This morning I meditated for about 30 minutes (pre sun rise). I did an inner check on my body and concluded I really need to stretch my hamstrings. I still have much difficulty eating within 30 mins of waking up. Caught maybe 5 mins of sunrise before leaving for work. The rat race...

### rtd — 2025-01-16T20:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-345293>

An appointment has been set with a new ENT surgeon. This surgeon is the one I wanted; he is highly regarded in his field and has done surgeries on several local, professional athletes. The first appointment is on boarding, then will have to get a new scan; to be able to breathe through both nostrils will be glorious.

Heard of a local mutual that does ocean polar bear plunges. I am trying to meet the person. Ocean temp is 39ºF (4ºC), air temp 25ºF (-4ºC). The surfers are present year round.

Physical in about two weeks with new Primary Care Physician.

Long weekend coming up. Looking forward to naps and leaving the island.

### rtd — 2025-01-24T21:18:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-345705>

I relapsed. Resumed consuming raw milk; it is addictive to me and a local, raw food. $9.50 a gallon. Ran the numbers:
