Local eggs $0.50/each. Eating a dozen a week

### rtd — 2025-06-04T20:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-353897>

Long time since I posted anything or been on the forum. Tapering non-work screen time usage. Daylight PC is coming this month.

I am watching the sun rise daily. Still struggle with eating 50-70g protein afterwards. I have lost about 30 lbs. From my all-time peak maybe a year ago, down about 50lbs. I got a DEXA scan in April. The plan is to get a follow scan up in November where I hope to be down another 30 lbs. I am more concerned about visceral fat in the abdomen area.

Bloodwork in a few weeks to see where I am at. An ENT surgeon has approved me for a septoplasty with turbinate reduction; the damage is structural from a car accident over a decade ago. I regret not getting it repaired when it happened. Working on the dates, shaping up to be either Sept or Oct.

I am a little tanner. Not much. The UV index and weather overall has been low and wet. Kinikis were on sale and I ordered a few. I still go outside in the AM for at least 30 minutes. Today and tomorrow look great to be outside.

Recently purchased and received a Body Voltage Meter and cornet meter from [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/); have some testing to do this weekend.

Hired a home cleaning service that also walks my dog! This is so huge for me help wise, love it. It is every other week. I still clean and tidy daily, it is much easier. I dislike dusting and cleaning the tub/shower.

Beaches are open and being maintained. Probably purchase a beach pass and taking nice afternoons off for beach time

### rtd — 2025-06-05T18:43:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-354017>

Mixed feelings and emotions today. A friend passed away, that is all I know. No details yet. Nothing online. I received a phone call about it. We were close for years until we both moved away for work. The sun is actually shining today, I told myself to get out in it. Took the dog for a longer walk and went into work late. Feeling down in the sun is better than feeling down indoors. Time carries on for the living.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T06:01:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-354078>

Time is a function of the light you imbibe and transform. Your friend did not assimilate that message well.

Will you?

### rtd — 2025-06-06T17:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-354105>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=354078)
>
> Time is a function of the light you imbibe and transform. Your friend did not assimilate that message well.
>
> Will you?

The past few years I have had apathetic durations where I did not care about time/life. Low dopamine, indifference, whatever.

Death certainly cuts through bullshit and the ego. A reminder to the living to actually live. To enjoy life.

Certain aspects I am living better than before yet worse off in other aspects. Regardless, I am active to be healthier
physically, mentally, and spiritually in my current environment. I believe and think I can progress only so far
in my current environment. Which means change. Massive uprooting, starting new somewhere. Still processing.

I do enjoy your GIF posts with sagely wisdom or inspirations. Thank you for those.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T17:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-354106>

**I want my Tribe to be a threat to the status quo.**

### rtd — 2025-06-10T17:46:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-354432>

Ordered a pair of Rhizal shoes. Certain areas I work require steel toe (mandatory safety protocols). Minus that, the plan is to wear them when I cannot be barefoot.

Next Monday I am taking leave. I am traveling to see my parents and other family. Almost the middle of June and the weather here at 41N has been bad- rain, cool, not much sun.

Am back to eating stricter. 200lbs is close. Looking forward to permanently leaving the heavyweight class. I think I should be in the 150-170lbs range, depending on muscle mass.

A friend of mine, maybe 15 yrs older than me- was sick. He lost his strength and balance and fell. Hit his head, slight fracture, concussion. Got out of the hospital last night. I am going to give him a set of night time blue light blocking glasses I keep in my automobile for night travel. I have a second evening pair I use and keep in my home. At this pace, I am gonna run out of friends \[insert macabre laughter\]

### rtd — 2025-06-12T17:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-354560>

Had an abdomen ultrasound this morning. Results will take a few days. The outcome "is what it is". Perhaps a future post with more context.

Made and provided my injured friend and his wife some food- bison steaks and a teriyaki pork tenderloin. Made another friend beef teres major cuts with a horseradish mayo.

A second, human skull fracture related story this week, a dog park acquaintance. She is recovering from a skull fracture. In fact, she said it was the first time she has driven since her fall (about a year ago). She talked about her neuro recovery, the therapy sessions- movement, holding items, speech, what she remembers of the fall, current issues, short comings, etc.

Her conversation was a timely teaching moment. Entanglement. I may ask her if she would be willing to speak with the wife of my friend that just got a skull fracture. (I received a voicemail from him and he sounded really different and out of it. Figure a little of medication + a lot of fracture) .

Additionally the conversation updated my personal reference frame, such as re-evaluating my personal health issues. Also it made me wonder if I have the internal drive to overcome and persevere. Living solo away from family, a small incapacitation as non-fatal as broken ankle would rock my physical world. Never mind a neurological issue.

I feel blessed.

### rtd — 2025-06-16T19:51:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-354711>

About 75% through the June 2025 Pow Wow recording. I was listening while driving and could not take notes. I have much to read and do.

When it comes to sharing my personal information with others, l lean towards closed off and guarded. Less is more. I must change this to take advantage of the journaling here; a tool I am under utilizing. I will open up. Perhaps not full "open kimono" at first. More like "kiniki beachware" exposure level.

My abdomen ultrasound results were graded RADCAT2: routine result. Small mobile stones in the gallbladder, no Murphy sign. I have a colon guard test to complete. Bloodwork coming up- the ever always coming blood work that does not happen because the doctor has not prescribed the panels I requested- and I want a Calcium Heart Scan.

I went for a calcium heart scan December 2024. I was on the table, wired up. My pulse rate would not drop below a 100bpm. The technician would not perform the scan due to this.

Getting my passport renewed is low hanging fruit. I used to love to travel. It is time to research and get boots on the ground in
search for new a start.

In parallel, develop a business/time plan. Perhaps the half dozen or so times I tried buying a house the past two years and getting out bid was a blessing in disguise.

### rtd — 2025-06-23T14:05:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-355076>

My friend who passed, I was informed it was due to an unknown neurological issue; he was working with physicians for the past few years.
I did not know that. I was asked to write a little something for his celebration of life. This has been weighing on me not because of his passing, because it is forcing me to face my own mortality given my current known health issues.

I've been listening to 2012 webinars and I am concerned. I would like to get blood work soon and have been updating what I want testing based from the webinar information. The information Dr. Kruse provides amazes me and frightens me. His intro to the EpiPaleo Diet webinar (2012) about having a survivor's attitude, is inspirational. Currently I do not have much fight in me or a good attitude.

Currently I work in engineering and tech. I got my first computer at 6 yrs old (TI-99). I learned TI BASIC language at a young age. As a pre-teen I ran dial-up Bulletin Board System (BBS). My dad helped and encouraged fixing things. At 13, I was fixing Nintendo consoles through a local video store; this was pre Block Buster Entertainment stores. I was building computers and was asked to fix computers via word of mouth. I was into CB radio and dabbled with a HAM radio. Eventually I went into a tech career. Flip side I spent time outside camping, hiking, fishing, gardening, and mowed a half dozen or so neighborhood lawns.

So yes, I have been blue light dominant most of my life with ever diminishing outdoor time. I have taken many steps at my apartment to mitigate nnEMF and not use tech as much outside of work.

I do not have much community. I live alone with my dog. No children or spouse, and I am turning 46 yrs old in a few months. Even going out, I am isolated and alone. Most my life I have been ok being alone. Now it hits different. I feel like I am in a rat race. That I am not really living life. I do not have many joyful events in my life. Day to day, my dog is all I have that I really care about. She is 5 yrs going on 6yrs. I do not want to feel like a Debbie Downer.

I know I have to grind out an Epi-Paleo diet to fix my brain (low dopamine), reverse type 2 diabetes (I am not on insulin) and eliminate
hypertension. I have pretty much no libido and feel like I am in andropause; that I am cardiac event waiting to happen. In March I started metformin, metoprolol, and linsopril. My new PCP also wanted me on a statin, which I declined. I have sleep apnea. Recently I had a few cavities, which I haven't had in quite awhile. All these things, the signs are not good and means poor redox. Life changes and quarterly blood panels to measure and monitor my progress. I know this may take years to fix. I hope I have the time.

To wrap up on a few positives, I am down 30 lbs since March, viewing sun rises, and doing what I can to be outside in the sun. Eating high protein breakfast, mostly fish, is still challenging. I can do 30-40g protein ok and I feel full.

### JenniferM — 2025-06-23T16:04:56+0300

You can only go as fast as the pace allows. Hang in there and keep your head up!

### rtd — 2025-06-24T18:29:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-355170>

Woke up naturally at 0440. Hit the bathroom, drank 16 oz of water, woke up the dog and watched the sunrise. Post, I ate a mackarel tin (about 30g protein). Air temperature is high and I do not feel like eating. I weighed in sub-200 lbs for the first time in about two years (199.8 lbs). Progress.

I ran my window AC through the night on low fan, setting 3 of 10 (10 is coldest). The analog hygrometer was 72. Before I went to sleep, it was 89. Bedroom temperature at wake up was 74F. My guest room has an AC yet it is off. Windows open in rest of my apartment, temperature (before sunrise) was 85F.

Was in bed at 0930, after I finished a 1.5 mile walk with the dog. Evening and early AM are the only times I can get my dog to walk in the current weather.

Tomorrow I am cycling off caffeine (again) to improve sleep and because I speculate I have high cortisol (based on the mirror test). One or two nights of poor sleep is all it takes for me to lean on caffeine to get me through a work day.

Wednesday is rubbish collection. Old freezer chest foods are going out, 5 gallon buckets of water to freeze for ice baths are going in. A concern I have is not having a second person around just in case something happens- the good old buddy rule.

I listened to the 2012 Cold Thermogenesis (CT) Webinar (I am in process of listening in chronological order). I will listen again this evening and take notes. My current approach is to listen to it once and take mental notes. Then listen again with the pause button, a pad and pen. Doc stated people on beta blockers medication ending in "-olol", will take longer with CT. I am on metoprolol.

Septoplasty surgery date: first Friday of the month. Next available dates are August and September. Originally August was not available. I am leaning towards September yet I could make August happen. I have to decide soon. A mental/personal goal has been to weigh 185 lbs or less for the surgery. Recovery, I was told, is a week.

### rtd — 2025-06-25T15:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-355245>

CT prep: two five gallon buckets, filled 3/4ths, into freezer.

Small win: used small amount of paid leave on top of lunch break to sun my self (in kinikis). May do again tomorrow.

This heatwave may break, potentially a 20 degree temperature drop. I would like the dew point to fall, it is in the 70s. There is an air quality alert.

### rtd — 2025-06-26T14:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-355308>

Woke up naturally few minutes before 0500. Took my dog out for sunrise. My dog sits by me on the ground or on my lap.
Truly a tranquil way to start the day. Overnight the dew point fell almost 20%, huge relief! Rain showers forecasted.
Breakfast was eggs, coffee and water.

I listened to more webinars from 2012 and 2013, so much info. My gratitude for this content is unbound.

My evening dog walk was detoured. There is a movie being filmed in downtown- "Remain" with Jake Gyllenhaal
and Phoebe Dynevor, directed by M. Night Shyamalan. The homeless and bums were forced away from their spots for a bit.
Nothing like a town having a seasonal, tourist based economy and being a sanctuary city.

### rtd — 2025-07-21T16:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-356731>

Septum surgery is less than two weeks away. I will have up to two weeks off for recovery; which will include a lot of sun maxing.

My weight is below 200 lbs (197 at 5'9"). Since Memorial weekend I have not been strict epi-paleo diet given fresh, local fruits. Especially wild raspberry harvesting this past weekend.

My goal is not a total weight number. My focus is reduced mid section visceral fat with working leptin/insulin sensitivity. Losing weight around the neck would be great too. My sleep apnea days are in the crosshairs.

An April DEXA scan showed my mid section fat was 6+ lbs with an ideal range <= 1 lb. My lean muscle calculation was 133 lbs. Maybe overall 150-160 lbs range to achieve this. That means CT'ing to eliminate my white fat and build my brown fat.

Octopus has been my latest "fruta del mar".

For the past week I had more days sleeping in past sunrise by an hour than I would prefer. Regardless, upon awakening I take the dog outside
and we sit in the grass. My dog likes it too, often receiving belly rubs. A Vermont pastured pork chop for breakfast with a side of octopus.

Supplements: Ubiquinol, Resveratrol, and L-Acetyl Carnitine

I returned my CPAP machine.

### rtd — 2025-07-25T14:43:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-356947>

Slow mornings lately. Tired/groggy upon awakening and wanting to sleep in. I woke up at 5:01 no alarm, dozed off until 5:30. Last night I feel asleep on the couch, post dog walk, listening to the wind and mother nature. My deck has several bird houses and there are chatty occupants.
The sun was setting when I dozed off. Woke up around 10pm to move to bed. Technically my dog woke me up and was growling at me to
go to bed. She is funny like that.

Season produce is a nice change of pace. Eating wild berries, had a premature peach (fuzzy, lightly colored skin with green flesh). Have to admit, this week I had more carbs consisting of cold rice/protein and a few desserts. Then there were Masa Chips. Good thing they are all gone and I will not get more.

Waiting on my pre-admission blood work results. Post surgery I am looking forward to full nasal breathing again. Six days with nasal packings post surgery, 100% mouth breating during that time. Aug 1st is the day. I arranged a dog sitter for the first two days. I am going to miss my dog those two days.

Sunrise this morning. Pain and discomfort manageable. Canned herring for breakfast. Appetite is so-so; cotton mouth from meds and breathing through my mouth. All this will pass, greener pastures ahead.

### rtd — 2025-08-05T20:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-357649>

Two more days with nostril stents. The pain dropped off greatly today. No sense of taste. I enjoy smoked blue fishes robust, bold flavor and could not taste it. Lots of sleeping. Indoors I'm not bleeding much, outside I bleed. It's ok though. I'd rather bleed outside and replace bandages then been inside more with less bleeding. My ENT visit is going to be a glorious launching of snot rockets. Today was last day of antibiotics. Good opportunity to refocus on gut health.

### rtd — 2025-08-12T19:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358055>

Stents out, healing continues.

Half of a pork loin for lunch, man did that hit the spot (get that methionine). Mid day sun too.

First day back to work. Going better than anticipated. Not even half way through and I feel I've maxed my screen time. Using Ra Optics.

The past 4-5 days, I've must have listened to Jack's Nourishing Traditions Vermont talks three times each. Plus the Optimal Reset Leptin talk.

Years ago I was gifted a book on Cal Ripken Jr., "The Iron Man". A dominant theme was "Consistency". Jack's teachings/protocols, I have not be consistent enough. Given my situation, what I can control, 70-80% is not good enough.

Tired? Does not matter.

Mindlessly checking phone? Enemy.

Being consciously conscious.

Resolute. Focused. Stalworth.

Consistent approach and execution.

Iron Man approach to change my heme iron.

### rtd — 2025-08-13T16:32:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358109>

Woke up naturally about 30 mins before sunrise. Overcast/hazy day.

45 minutes outside, BAB afterwards - bistro steak, two egg omelette, chourico link.

I could use a post breakfast nap. Canned crab for lunch. I am reducing my daily caffeine intake, again. Coffee. On then off then on again is my caffeine dance. I stop because my consumption goes to high, I stop and feel better, then I re-start with here an there, then too much, repeat. No more cold brews or afternoon coffee. Had an afternoon headache yesterday.

Physically breathing ok. There is an air pollution warning.

### rtd — 2025-08-14T16:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358227>

Another day waking up naturally (0513) before official sunrise time (0556), dawn light and bird activities awakened me. There is a chickadee bird nest with babies below my 2nd story bedroom window. Rather than returning to sleep, I got up and did minor cleaning and organzing without any lights on, followed by providing belly rubs to my dog. Then we were outside for an hour or so. When out, I have to keep alert for skunks and coyotes ending their night shift. Many animals traverse the property as it provides an access path to a slope that goes to a wooded area.

Breakfast was eggs and ham with a coffee, plus water. Plus some wild black berries from a shaded briar patch; berry season is at the tail end.
Dogwood berry season is happening; the lack of rain is causing fruit to fall prematurely. This berry is like a lychee; I live in a Zone 5a/5b agriculture area.

Continuing reading through older blogs, mostly after work for 20-30 min. I printed the latest blog to read outside.

Going to bed 30 mins after sunset (1947) this time of year is easy with getting up early. I dread the return of winter hours.

An observation: my cell phone is receiving 5G on my property. Previously, no 5G inside my home- weak LTE signal at best, which is preferable. I made a note to check for new towers. Or perhaps there is no new tower, yet a tower or telephone poles were updated (new node, repeaters). There is a new apartment complex being constructed about a mile away.

National Grid, the local electric company, is planning to move to Smart Meters.

Update: I changed my work schedule to have more AM time. Necessary for me at 41N. No afternoon coffee. Had two measured cups from a Nespresso machine before 0830. I ordered a 24 hour saliva cortisol DHEA test kit.

### drdan — 2025-08-14T20:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358238>

> [rtd said:](/goto/post?id=335817)
>
> Edit: 23 JUNE 2025
> Please feel free to interact with my journal. I no longer want to be an isolationist here.
>
>
> This is my space to records thoughts, topics and ideas I am interested in health wise. Somewhere I can jot down stuff and go back to as I learn. I have been reading through the forums in chronological order, it is interesting to see how posts evolved from 2012 to now, along with the types of replies. In some forums the number of female to male posts is high, like the early on "Optimal Labs" section. As a male, I found myself where all the other male lab posts? Just an observation. I am still going through in chronological order.
>
> Feedback:
> ~If I am inquiring for feedback, I will post I am looking for feedback. Outside the man himself, Jack, please do not assume a question or topic I write is an invite to chime in.~ Respectfully I would use "Ask Jack" after researching the topic. (Minus something timely, like a current event)
>
> Current thought explorations:
>
> Bloodwork lower/upperbound data ranges: If derived from an "unhealthy" population, what are optimal values? Sex based or all inclusive?
> Male: LDL Cholesterol upper limit?, HDL; age dependent?
> Peripheral neuropathy from physical posture
> Exogenous vs natural- when to temporarily supplement, time frames
> CBC Panel: Red blood cell (RBC), magnesium RBC and General Chemistry magneisum: don't see Mg RBC option in panel
> Meyer Cocktails: local vitamin bar via IV?
> Fournier effect at Water Spas
> **If your redox level is low you should avoid all alcohol no matter what**

as far as bloodwork: lipid panels are incomplete and doctors use them to banter patients into statins... lifestyle and exercise should be used, then herbs (remembering that red yeast rice is a mycotoxin from which Lipitor is made...), then reassurance. Dr Perlmeter of grain brain fame, says that aborigines in Australia walk around with cholesterol of 350-- no strokes or heart attacks. as long as the oxidized LDL is normal, i let my patients go up to 275 easily. more likely to have issues with genetic Lp(a) or with high triglycerides than high cholesterol. all levels are age fluctuating and sex (especially menopausal or manopausal) since estrogen and testosterone are in the ATP binding complex of the G1 and G6 positions in the gut that kick the cholesterol back out that gets absorbed from bile as it helps get fat soluble products into the body.
thyroid: TSH should be .5 to 2.5... higher gets one on antidepressants, hair loss drugs, weight loss drugs... if higher, use iodine products, seleniuum in brazil nuts, and sunshine (vitamin D3) to improve the thyroid. in women, most hypothyroidism (over 4.5) are associated with autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto's). check TPO and anti-thyroglobulin antibodies. usually due to gluten issues or EBV virus problems.
This is a start. good luck
Dr Dan

### rtd — 2025-08-15T17:51:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358281>

Woke up naturally at 0430. Too early. Went back to sleep.

Woke up naturally at 0650. Too late. Felt much more rested.

Cannot let perfection be the enemy of good.

30 minutes outside in the sun on grass with the dog.

Small breakfast (one egg) and coffee. Gonna have a bigger lunch.

Weekend goals:
As much CT as I can manage.
Unthaw and cook a lamb 4 lb lamb roast.
Setup new Linux environment to support Nvdia Jetson Orin Super Development controller.
Configure new SVN repository.
Create property and household drawings to mark EMF test points.
Collect property EMF data.
Finish reading Decentralized Medicine #66.

Decentralized Medicine #66: I printed out the PDF with wider margins for manual note taking. I definitely absorb more using paper and pen vs reading a screen. The print out was one-sided, more room for notes. I am four or five pages into and lots of note taking, specifically looking up biology and chemistry terms. Talk about drilling down. For example: protein -> peptides -> amino acids -> atomic structures/groups -> bond types -> valence shells -> electrons. I'm 27 years out from my last chemistry class. Note taking is aiding my recall and relearning.

Plasticity.

Thanatos is the personification of death, often depicted as a winged god or demon. He is the son of Nyx (Night) and Erebus (Darkness), and twin brother to Hypnos (Sleep). Thanatos is responsible for carrying souls to the Underworld after their death, a role that is both crucial and often feared.

### rtd — 2025-08-17T16:40:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358374>

A solid morning. Awakened naturally about 10 minutes before sunrise. Water and coffee outside for an hour. Ate 60g of protein. Went to grocery store, canned wild crab on sale - $2 off. Score! One can is 40g of protein. Cooked lamb, set for a few days. Heading to a park soon to hang out outside. Ordered a new hard drive for the Nvidia board.

Last night I failed by snacking around 10pm. I had cheese and ham. I ate dinner around 5:30 and knew I did not eat enough. I felt really full post dinner and feel asleep reading a book. Woke up to let the dog out and that is when the hunger hit. I drank water first, waited, and caved.

As I finish this, there is 10 hours, 3 minutes left until sunset.

### rtd — 2025-08-18T17:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358411>

Got up 0600 (sunruse 0557) naturally. Outside for an hour. I am tired this morning. Last night I had a sleep disturbance that awakened me and kept me up for awhile. I felt hot and had to use the bathroom stat (#2), which is an odd time for that. My pulse was elevated (upper 90s-low 100s) for a bit and I felt anxious some.

Based on my June ER visit, the lab results and diagnosis from that ER visit, and feeling a lesser version of that experience, I decided to drink water with added potassium and magnesium. I turned on the AC to cool the room down. I waited and did control breathing. My pulse returned to the low 70s, the anxious feeling dissipated, and I feel asleep.

Yesterday I was outside a lot and was sipping water through out the day. The constant breeze with overcast was nice given the humidity. I did reach my protein goals and did well on meal times. That evening I feel asleep just after official sunset (1941) while reading. I woke up around 9pm to let the dog out and head to bed. The sleep disturbance was 2345-0030 time frame.

### rtd — 2025-08-19T15:47:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358481>

Slept on the floor by choice, my body wanted a very firm surface. The floor is carpeted so it had some give. Naturally woke up with my dog pretty much exactly as sunrise. Overnight the temperature dropped significantly to low/mid 50s. Took my dog for a walk downtown, doning a light winter hat and sweat pants, which took about an hour.

No big breakfast today, which I regret. I did not pre make one and needed to get to work earlier. AND I forget my bring my lunch.

Kruse's forum post on worlds oldest lady re-plucked a chord that has been internally vibrating in me for quite while, amplifying it. I am thankful for what I have yet question what I really have.

### rtd — 2025-08-21T16:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358578>

A nice morning. Naturally woke up at 0500, about an hour before sunrise. I sat in bed and gave my dog belly rubs for a solid 30 mins. Was outside for about an hour and a half. Big breakfast.

Fired up the sous vide cooker and plopped in pork tenderloin (not pastured, from grocery store on sale). In a second bag, venison. A third bag with sausage link. Cook temp set to 135F. I am planning to buy button and shitake mushrooms to make a side dish and sauce.

I have not been in the woods looking specifically for mushrooms yet. August and September is the start of mushroom foraging season. Yesterday and last night was steady rain, which is good for the mycelium to "fruit". Common are maitake and chicken of the woods. Biggest maitake, aka hen of the woods, I found to date was 23" in diameter. Good eating and dehydrated about half of it. The smell of a fresh maitake is an earthy, sweet aroma that is very inviting. A store maitake aroma is nothing like the wild ones I find, IMO- makes sense.

### rtd — 2025-08-22T16:36:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-358614>

Physically a good morning. Woke up within 20 mins of sunrise, outside, breakfast. Mentally, so so. Gotta grind it out, keep my head up.

This weekend I will be reviewing my finances, as well as blood work requests. My PCP made two slips to get work at two different locations covering different tests and not including any of the sex hormones I requested. Plus there was pre-surgery bloodwork; I could not get the different doctors on the same page for one blood draw session for all the tests.

Living in the smallest state, almost by default I live near a state border. One script is for a specific out of state location. I prefer to plan the trip to see if I can get the tests I wanted performed. I mention this because my state requires a physician's request to get blood work. Medical oppression IMO.

Gonna pause on the journaling, collect and process my thoughts offline with pen and paper. In parallel process more Optimal blogs and audio content offline. I have physical print outs and downloaded PDFs for the DayLight Computer (DC1). I have always been a slow reader. For me, Kruse's blogs are dense reading in that I have to look up alot of stuff, realize it, then re-read a sentence. Flip side, the how and what to do for a protocol is not conceptually difficult and that too leads me to taking a break from online journaling. Focus on action and time to improve. Learn and understand because I am an autodidact yet DO THE WORK to improve, where the latter is the goal.

Keep It Simple Stupid (KISS) and do.

### rtd — 2025-09-14T16:44:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-359498>

One week zero caffeine (Leaky Gut item C).

Working the Leaky Gut steps:

### [The Leaky Gut Prescription](https://jackkruse.com/the-leaky-gut-prescription-2/)

Readers Summary: What is a leaky gut? What can cause a leaky gut? What are the leaky gut associated conditions? How might one consider treating a leaky gut? Well, we have talked about the gut in three other blogs so far. (1) Could It Be the Gut?: An Introduction to the Brain-Gut

 jackkruse.com

Been napping this weekend. My Blood Pressure readings past two weeks were low/normal range and I contacted my PCP office several times with out getting through. The diastolic was lower and I have been feeling ligh headed and sometimes dizzy. Pulse was lower

Decided to halve my metoprolol dosage. After a few days, my BP was still normal low range. Eliminated Lisinopril, BP in normal range, diastolic improved from low readings. I'm measuring my BP 3 times a day. I feel better. Still tired

My left thumb has been spasming a bit too. Enough to notice it's not a one off. Leg twitches by my right ankle. I had diarrhea few nights ago after a dinner party. It hit me within an hour after eating while driving home. I knew I would last the car ride home. Fortunately I knew of a port a potty I saw while walking the dog and I stopped to use it. The walk from my car to the port-a-potty was a brisk, awkward gate consisting of clenching my sphincter and mentally pleading with it, as I worked undoing my belt. Barely made it.

In addition to continuing eat epi-paleo food sources, I'm interested in using magnesium supplementation vs taking a beta blocker. There is a Drip Bar in the area that offers a Meyer's Cocktail IV. Can add CoQ10 to it too. (I expressed interest in this before being diagnosed with hypertension, T2D, sleep apnea... and glad its locally available if need be).

I read that serum magnesium testing is not an accurate measurement. That intracellular RBC test is a better test... something along those lines. Have to sus all that out.

No supplements in last two days (Magnesium powders- malate and oratate, D-Ribose, KCl, reservatrol) and keeping it that way until the blood work is done. Eating for nutrients is ideal; I am not against supplements as an aid. A supplement is not a permanent.

Previous blood work spring 2025 was limited. However the results showed I have T2D. No CRP was performed. I have been implementing the Leptin Protocol and am not on insulin. I have been taking Metformin (2g) daily since the T2D diagnosis. I recall Metformin is part of the EPCOT Rx yet i want to be off that too. Steadily I will improve my health. Reduce inflammation, high DHA consumption, regain leptin sensitivity.

> Marker of disease and poor energy utilization.

(I have sleep apnea)

> To detox the high level, Vit B6 and Mg are required.

Mg intracellular RBC test results pending

> High levels of homocysteine clinically is a marker of disease and poor energy utilization. Other conditions which have been linked to high homocysteine levels include: Cardiovascular disease, Multiple sclerosis rheumatoid arthritis, spontaneous abortion, placental abruption, renal failure, and type II diabetes.

Am T2D. Most likely have some form of cardiovascular disease.

> Homocysteine levels are related to depression in several ways. The methyl group metabolism provided for by the pathway of homocysteine (when correctly functioning) is necessary for the production of depression-relieving neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine. The B vitamins are also crucial in the direct synthesis of the brain neurotransmitters. Aside from the fact that they are needed (especially B-6, B-12 and folate) for the homocysteine pathway to provide methylation, they are essential to the pathway of making these neurotransmitters"

Lab results still pending. Second blood draw this Saturday morning. I know my energy metabolism has been and still is sub par. Expecting low sex steroids based how I have feel- listless, dull, low libido. As well as low HDL, low DHEA, low T, and high triglycerides. I'd expect a low CO2 value. Vit D- I would be happy with a value in the 60s knowing a solid lower bound is 70; great than 50 I would hope. I have a tank top tan. Not a great tan, but there is a noticeable difference. Yet I know a tan does not imply Vit D.

Gotta keep working the protocols and improve. I 100% know I feel better now than I did in early spring.

protocols. Plus there is also IV Meyer Cocktail. I would be able to pay for treatments using my HSA.

I feel much better without caffeine. Sleep comes easier. I miss the ritual, the smell and socialization aspect. I am not going to do decaf coffee.

Two tests I need to get are saliva based. I ordered kits BUT had issues with my payment and shipping address being a PO Box.

In the meantime I've continued reading the blogs in chronological order and have taken a pause to read the book, "Stop the

be adrenal related. Hence one of the saliva tests - 24hr, 4 sample cortisol test I am ordering. A suspicion I had of having low DHEA was
confirmed via blood work:

> Range: 61-442

\-> SHIT LIGHT ENVIRONMENT AND NOT ENOUGH SUN

2) Second round of blood work results come in (give me time to read more)

I am pleased with my progress yet am not optimal. Outside of the mirror test and how I feel (symptoms or lack of), I like having lab results
to gauge where I am currently and have quantifiable indicators to monitor progress. The framework is here.

Getting positive results, like my A1C dropping out of T2D range, and stopping two blood pressure medications motivates to do more, to be better, to physically feel better.

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td><b>URIC ACID (mg/dL)</b></td><td></td><td><b>CALCIUM (mg/dL)</b></td><td></td><td></td><td><b>UREA NITROGEN (BUN) (mg/dL)</b></td><td></td><td><b>CREATININE (mg/dL)</b></td></tr><tr><td>Value = 5.5</td><td></td><td>Value = 10.2</td><td></td><td></td><td>Value = 22</td><td></td><td>Value = 0.95</td></tr><tr><td>Range: 4.0-8.0</td><td></td><td>Range: 8.6-10.3</td><td></td><td></td><td>Range: 7-25</td><td></td><td>Range: 0.60-1.29</td></tr></tbody></table>

[Source:](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parathyroid-hormone.27122/):

> "Most people can avoid calcium stones by reducing/eliminating high calcium food sources or making sure they eat natural foods with Vitamin K2 (raw milk cheeses and charcuterie). We can easily increase our citrate intake UP to a limit TO PREVENT CALCIUM AND URIC ACID STONES.
> Citrate is an intermediate in the TCA cycle which is the central metabolic pathway for animals, plants, and bacteria. Citrate synthase catalyzes the condensation of oxaloacetate with acetyl CoA to form citrate. Citrate then acts as the substrate for aconitase and is converted into aconitic acid. Aconitase is where metabolic water is recycled and this makes it vulnerable to deuterium kinetics. This is why processed food is dangerous for humans because its hydrogen atoms have no protection from the photosynthetic pathways to make sure of the fidelity of the isotope of hydrogen around specific carbons. The cycle ends with the regeneration of oxaloacetate in the TCA cycle.

After all the tests, I realized I missed one lab: IGF-1. I thought it was included in a bundle I ordered and I was wrong. Still waiting on more results.

A 24-hr saliva cortisol test from ZRT labs is on its way. Looking at the Thorne test kit for melatonin.

### rtd — 2025-10-07T16:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-360571>

I have many acquaintances; I do not have many friends. This is not a bad thing! One has a finite amount of time, energy, and availability to allocate.

A local friend will be permanently moving away. The move has been expected and the person is prepping their home to go market.

The majority of my local friends- people who live within 30 miles- are two decades older and retired. Several are or have been snow birds- traveling south for the winter months, returning in spring and staying through the fall.

I do not do well with remote or long distance relationships. Mutual activity to communicate is required and natural tapering off occurs. Either "we" are in each others orbit or not. Minimal status maybe one or two phone calls a year, maybe some text messaging if we share a similar sense of humor.

I do not purposely burn bridges unless it is a must. For those few individuals, I made it clear to those individuals- no lame ghosting someone.

Tying into all this, next year maybe the first year I send out a holiday card to say hello to those who are not in my local sphere. I am thinking St Patrick's Day. That would stand out amongst the typical Christmas and New Years cards.

I listened to the HRT Webinar available to gold members and took a lot of notes.

Before I go down the HRT path, I want to complete both 24-Hr Cortisol and melatonin saliva tests. I am waiting on the home kit deliveries (ZRT Labs and Genova).

In an early blog post I wrote, I thought maybe I had high cortisol. Since I was 100% leptin resistance in the spring (mirror test + symptoms like having T2D), and learning "after leptin, cortisol goes"- I have a strong confidence my cortisol is low.

Some thoughts:
1) Purchase the EMF Boot Camp Series
2) Buy local, fresh oysters.

I used to live near several salt ponds. Occasionally when I went clamming I'd get an oyster. There are many oyster farms now. Which reminds me, a former neighbor (Jen) is the founder of an oyster farm: [https://www.quonniesirenoysters.com/](https://www.quonniesirenoysters.com/) ....

I just texted Jen. Jen is a very interesting person. Used to work and live in Manhattan. Great at design, organic farming, now oystering. When she started her business, I went kayaking to see the floating dock spaces to grow the baby oyster seeds. She is now on Quonochontaug Pond.

Ya know, I would work for her part time if I could manage it. Get paid in oysters, outside, in cold brackish water. An issue would be the commute. 45 minutes with no traffic each way from where I currently live. Perhaps a part-time weekend schedule. Cannot hurt to inquire.

Fun fact for any fans of the TV-show, "The X Files": Moulder's mother lived in Charlestown, RI, in the village of Quonochontaug. (Southern Rhode Island has many villages within larger towns. I used to live in Village of Green Hill, which abutts Quonochontaug).

Previously I wrote about acquaintances versus friends. This "issue" is half me. That half of me is 100% me. Skepticism, distance, barriers, moats- low dopamine plays a role. Energy is the ability to do work and using it requires a potential. There are energy sources and energy drains, and it is all about reference.

I need to be more of a generator.

### rtd — 2025-10-10T20:53:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-360844>

Brain Gut 16:

> Thus, the PVN, unlike any other brain site, has direct influence over both sympathetic and parasympathetic outflow. Furthermore, the PVN receives direct sympathetic and parasympathetic afferent inputs from trigeminal pars caudalis (sympathetic) and the nucleus of the solitary tract (parasympathetic). _This is why we use face dunks in the Cold Thermogenesis Protocol_. It is the fastest way to signal an inhibited a broken PVN. (a bad ASI) The PVN therefore is the only brain site in a closed efferent-afferent reflex loop with both the sympathetic and parasympathetic nervous systems. **This implies that the PVN is the key portion to study in the brain in all cases of adrenal fatigue. The system breaks when signaling is broken here. The PVN is a collector of environmental inputs. So if we can’t sense our environment well the output of the PVN will not be reflective if what our brain is really experiencing in life.**

### rtd — 2025-10-27T21:37:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-361701>

My E2 (estradiol) is in range yet towards the top of the range. I have early gallbladder stones.

Blog Post: Do You Need A Gallbladder?

> People with gallbladder disease tend to be estrogen collectors in their tissues and have many estrogen dominant diseases. Estradiol is normally conjugated in the liver by sulfate and glucuronide formation and, as such, is excreted via the kidneys. Some of the water-soluble conjugates are excreted via the bile duct (think gallbladder) and partly reabsorbed after hydrolysis from the intestinal tract. This enterohepatic circulation contributes to maintaining estradiol levels.

Another indicator of not enough sun light/poor light environment.

### rtd — 2025-10-28T21:19:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-361772>

Pictures from my trip to Quonnie Siren Oyster Farm (Charlestown, RI - Quonochontaug Pond)

Nice autumn day. This pond circulates the water about every 24 hours, flushing out and replenishing nutrient. The salt pond above it, Ninigret, I was told takes 3 days to circulate.

Jen, the owner, shucking an oyster. My dog Charlie

Oyster shells carry a premium. Used in construction from crushed driveways to stone walls. I recall the Romans used crushed oyster shells in their cement/mortar.

Young oysters

I purchased two, 100 hundred count bags and gifted the majority. I've eaten 3 dozen the past two days. (Lots of ticks- deer ticks- on the shoreline, small ones. I pulled a few off me and about 8 off my dog. Lyme, Connecticut is not that far away)

### rtd — 2025-10-30T23:15:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-361913>

As Yogi Berra once said, "Deja vu all over again". Same poor sleep pattern last night.

I found a tick embedded into my arm; no bulls eye ring around the bite area yet it hurts. The head maybe stuck in. Did a body sweep and didn't feel any other ticks. It was a small tick.

Finishing up the last of the oyster haul. Will make a stock from the shells with a mirepoix.

I swear my weight jumped over 8 pounds in the last few days. I attribute it to water weight from lack of sleep.

This morning I caved and had an espresso. I regret it. My caffeine intake has been near zero for over a month.

Am trying to foster and maintain a positive attitude yet I am very tired and have a lower tolerance.

Good news is I have fresh oysters to go home. Have to focus on the positives; reframing.

And before I return home, a quick trip to the grocery store to purchase lamb loins for me and the dog. There is a local farm I need to visit for lamb. I am not sure of the lamb seasons. ### rtd — 2025-11-04T22:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362165>

This AM I spent one hour in the sun. I believe this "shift" I am experiencing is light based, going to a poorer seasonal light environment. CT and red light therapy. I am craving rice and beans on the regular, being good by abstaining. I am eating local gourds from a farm down the road. Rutabagas are yummy. Made a hash with them.

My weight has jumped 15 lbs. Not good. Always room for improvement. Light cycles are real.

Going back to protein/fat for the winter. I bought a salmon fillet and two pounds of ground bison. The ground bison is not local, although there maybe a bison rancher in the Amherst, MA area. Its been awhile since I have been there, Amherst- my collegiate stomping grounds.

Looking forward to walking with my dog today. My hip flexors and/or hamstrings feel tight. Crazy high wind levels.

### rtd — 2025-11-06T23:51:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362265>

To say my sleep has been poor this week is an understatement. I am very tired, jumpy, anxious. My resting pulse is flirting near 100 BPM; this morning it was low 70s and my BP at one point was 150/90. I meditated and took another BP reading: 127/80. If I shut my eyes and breathe, pulse lowers to the mid 80s. In September my BP and pulse were lower; at times I felt faint.

I am too much in my head from not sleeping. At this point I am open to taking a sleeping medication although I live by myself and don't want to take it and be alone. A previous housemate was on sleep medication and this person was active on it, cooking, cleaning and even drove once. Scary.

This too shall pass. I know other people have bigger problems than I. Writing this down is helpful to my journey. I know I can get better. I was doing it for months. Autumn and winter hit differently. Sometimes we need someone to lean on. I know I can stay at a friends house on their couch if need be. As an adult, I am ashamed to feel the way I do. Being up the past few nights, all I have are my thoughts and my dog. My dog is good companion yet she needs to be taken care of too. I am her care giver, her human.

I've been subpar at work this week, and part of last month when I was feeling ill. This a stressor too. I read the headlines and see layoffs rising, government shutdown, etc. Life and timing. I have some savings I could tap into if I take medical leave, if that is possible. The cost of living is high. I dunno. Babbling now.

Protocols. Follow them. Provide the best inputs to my body I can. Stay positive. Embrace the suck.

### rtd — 2025-11-07T15:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362282>

I went to bed around 1845. Slept off and on through the night. Blood pressure was higher than normal. I think I had a panic attack at one point. Cumulatively I got more sleep last night than the past few days. I woke up with the sunrise and felt more human.

### rtd — 2025-11-07T21:55:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362297>

Two more hours to go until my weekend starts. A better day today, productive. No appetite though. Had half a can of tuna fish. Also I did not drink as much water as normal. Maybe make a water schedule. Hydration is key. I do have a tired/sleep headache though. That is why I am thinking dehydration (plus skin is getting drier). Maybe a Saturday Drip Bar session for a Meyer's cocktail IV as a hydration hack ($150).

I can feel my sinus passages drain now and then and can successfully blow my nose. Prior to the septum surgery, that was not happening. I am thankful for that. In fact, I learned a co-worker has a deviated septum and has poor sleep. I referred the surgeon I used.

I weighed myself this morning, 212 lbs. Two mornings ago, 218 or 219 lbs. Aug 1st, 195 lbs. I had a mid November goal to be 180 lbs. Lets see how close I can get to that by New Years.

Reading several books on oysters. Planning a trip back to farm to talk with Jen, see how I can assist. Thinking maybe this Tuesday (Veteran's day).

Finishing up "Adrenal Fatigue: 21st Century...".

Keeping a Chroma SkyPortal 2.1 on in my main room for my dog and for when I get home. (I like this light, maybe I'll post a picture of it). Although she sleeps mostly in a different room, she has several window places in the living room to perch and look outside. Last night she did not want to walk! When I showed her leash she ran and hid under furniture. Granted, it was very high wind and she is so low to the ground. I changed out of my work clothes and sat near her. She came out of hiding for play and belly rubs. She had bison and carrots for dinner; I had water (Topo Chico 18oz). Then a quick, no leash, outside for her to go and we went to sleep. She woke me up three times to use the bathroom and I am ok with that. That is why the before bedtime long walk is key, she'll sleep through the night after a long walk.

The house temperature is mid 60s F. The bathroom is colder, I have the heat on in their at 60. Who likes sitting on a cold toilet? Summertime maybe. I am going to order new bed pillows, [cariloha bamboo pillow](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillows-sleeping.31811/post-361906). The red light above my kitchen sink went out. Its not even a month old, low hours too. Anyways- just clearing out my thoughts. What else, hmmm.

Was thinking how nice it would be to have a small wood stove or propane in the garage. That is where my exercise bike is located. (Not ideal, is what it is. I need something at my residence in the winter yet if I do have pregnenolone steal happening, I question if I should exercise. I probably can do low HRV zone bike pedaling. In better weather I can wheel it outside).

If I owned, definitely would have something like that. Maybe this weekend I will look at new apartments; currently I have no signed lease. There really is no good place EMF wise here. I think I stated before that I believe living here I can improve my health to a point, better than now yet I don't know how high.

Gotta push harder to find an endocrinologist to explore HRT. A place near me does the pellets; I do not want that technique. Perhaps they have other therapy options not listed online. Or perhaps this is my body telling me I have to relocate. A move cannot be lateral and certainly not a step back. My state most likely will turn to be more socialistic because that's the demographics. Choices, choices....

For the heck of it, just checked my resting pulse: upper 70s. I think tonight is going to be better sleep. One blood pressure reading last night, 172/98, pulse in the 90s. In September my evening blood pressure was 90-120/55-80, pulse in the 60s, sometimes mid 50s when drifting a sleep.

### rtd — 2025-11-08T04:56:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362317>

Pre bedtime blood pressure measurement: 123/70, pulse 81. Had a nice walk this evening.

### rtd — 2025-11-08T14:51:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362327>

Didnt sleep much as I wanted. Fell asleep 2-3am, woke up around 4am feeling odd. Went through some health checklists and determined my blood sugar was probably low based on symptoms. No glucose monitor to check. I didn't eat much past two days. First I drank a glass of water and waited, nodded off for a bit.

Awoke with same symptoms. Put a little honey under my tongue and waited. Symptoms went away. I did skip dinner as I wasn't hungry and was pushing through to wait until post sunrise to eat breakfast, to which I did. I was up and outside for the sunrise. Eggs with mushrooms, and chourico. Estimating I fell short of my 70g protein goal by 30g. (Note to self, buy more eggs).

I recall reading night time low blood sugar is an adrenal fatigue symptom. Will have to work out my evening dinner time, starting by eating dinner for number one. Even if it is an earlier dinner, have to make sure it's enough protein and fat.

I did have a few glorious farmer snot rockets outside while walking the dog. It's a new dirty pleasure. The instant relief, the ease of nasal breathing. It's up there when you have to pee really bad and then are able to go. I don't think that will ever stop being a good feeling.

Looking at notes, the last good, full, night of sleep I had was Oct 11th. So what happened? I was sick for a week. Started flu like symptoms- aches, chills, fatigue. I took unplanned time off from work that week. And looking back a year, I got sick around that same timeframe. Interesting.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-11-08T15:53:35+0200

Reading your last couple of updates, I'd be almost certain you've got an ALAN/nnEMF problem in your sleep environment AND you are not getting enough sunlight, particularly AM. Even if days are only 7-8 hours of sun ATM I think being out there for all of it is probably necessary. Grounded. T shirt, shorts, grounding shoes if the ground is frozen.

I think when you get beta oxidation back online from proper AM clock signals you stop seeing that low blood sugar at night because if you can beta oxidate you deplete liver glycogen slower than if you can not. nnEMF destroys this though.

Ate a big, early dinner of lamb loins. Cooked mushrooms for tomorrow. I feel ok despite lack of sleep.

Evening blood pressure: 114/67, pulse 89 (post household chores, laundry going as I type)

Tuesday I see Jen the oyster farmer. I ordered 8 dozen. Gifting half while I have the means. Work uncertainty, paying into the karma cup.

# Thread: Difficulty tanning the top part of my head
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/difficulty-tanning-the-top-part-of-my-head.29035/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2024-06-17T03:30:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/difficulty-tanning-the-top-part-of-my-head.29035/post-335831>

Hi everyone,

First time posting here so if this is the wrong area to post this I apologize.

Have been following Jack's work for around 2 years now. Generally aim for as much sun throughout the day as possible. I've built up quite a solid tan this year, but unfortunately I've been unable to build a tan in the places that my hair has fallen out on the top of my head. I often feel toasted/slightly sun burnt in the area because I'm having trouble building melanin there. I was wondering if anyone else had gone through this problem and had any solutions they could recommend.

Current routine while working outside is sunrise -> 11:30 am outside in the sun w/ shorts on. 11:30-12:30 make lunch, eat outside. 12:30 - until I need a break, outside in peak UV (occasionally will cover the top of my head with a shirt if it starts to feel like it's getting burnt). Shade outside until 5-6pm then outside until sunset. Light out by 9pm. Been hitting a good 10-12 hours of sun throughout the spring/summer time. Diet is mostly steak/eggs/local goat dairy/sardines/mackerel/oysters. Then sweet potatoes/sourdough bread for lunch. Fruit and deserts occasionally, always eaten outside.

All light bulbs in the house are red/UV-a or incandescent and are rarely on past dark Iris on computers/red light setting on phone. I've removed most of the negative ALAN.

nnEMF is still a serious issue where I live and probably not something I can solve without moving (in the works)

Attached two photos of the top of my head.

This seems like something red light maybe be able to help with but I thought just seeing tons of AM/PM sun would solve the problem but unfortunately is has not.

Any advice would be appreciated.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-17T19:44:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/difficulty-tanning-the-top-part-of-my-head.29035/post-335846>

Tells you need more red light exposure of head and neck and your brainstem around trigeminal nerve is why you are stuggling. That part is likely devoid of melanin so when you make it on your face and top of head your pulling it inside = poor brainstem redox from bad environment.

You need to test your environment.

You need a real human pic up. This thread will be locked until you comply.

# Thread: Ovarian follicles, fertility, inhibin, and biophoton creation = transgenerational risks to offspring
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-follicles-fertility-inhibin-and-biophoton-creation-transgenerational-risks-to-offspring.29042/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T00:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-follicles-fertility-inhibin-and-biophoton-creation-transgenerational-risks-to-offspring.29042/post-335884>

_**Ovarian follicles, fertility, inhibin, and biophoton creation = transgenerational risks to offspring**_

Ovarian follicles are small, fluid-filled sacs in your ovaries that each contain an unfertilized egg. You’re born with a set number of eggs and ovarian follicles — usually anywhere from 1 to 2 million — and the total declines over time.

By the time you enter puberty, about 25 percent of these follicles remain, around 300,000. At menopause, fewer than 1,000 ovarian follicles are typically left.

Your follicles themselves go through various stages of development. First, primordial follicles will [form in a fetus’s ovaries](https://www.whattoexpect.com/pregnancy/fetal-development/fetal-sex-organs-reproductive-system/). They remain that way until puberty, at which point they begin to develop — just like the rest of you — and become primary follicles.

Right around the time of ovulation, some of these follicles begin to swell. One will become dominant, and the others that started growing — called antral follicles — will recede.

The dominant follicle then breaks open and releases an egg from your ovary, which travels through your fallopian tube toward the uterus. This egg will hang out for about 24 hours, creating an opportunity for [fertilization to occur](https://www.whattoexpect.com/getting-pregnant/fertility/how-fertilization-happens.aspx). The cycle continues each month until you go through menopause. Menopause happens when inhibin in the blood rises.

The antral follicle test is a transvaginal ultrasound that measures how many antral follicles you have. An ultrasound technician or doctor uses a probe inserted into the vagina to examine and count the number of antral follicles on the ovaries. It only takes a few minutes, similar to a pelvic exam.

Antral follicles are very small — between 2 to 9 millimeters in diameter — but you can still see them on an ultrasound as hypoechogenetic regions on the screen. The number of antral follicles visible can give your doctor a ballpark sense of how many total eggs (including the many follicles still too small to see) you have remaining in your ovaries.

While it’s often done on the third day of your menstrual cycle at the same time as other fertility tests, in reality it can be done at any time.

How many antral follicles are normal? It depends on your age and heteroplasmy level in your ovary. If you’re older, you’ll have fewer antral follicles as someone who’s younger. On average, women in their mid 20s to early 30s have around 12 to 30 antral follicles, while women aged 35 to 40 may have between 8 and 15 and women aged 41 to 46 may have somewhere between four and 10. If you have fewer than five antral follicles at ANY age, your doctor may advise certain fertility treatments, like trying in vitro fertilization (IVF) or use donor eggs.

What about women with PCOS? Follicle number does not mean fertility. PCOS is proof of this. Women with PCOS have more antral follicles than women without PCOS. It’s not unusual for a woman with PCOS to have more than 30 antral follicles total. Women with PCOS produce above-average levels of the hormone testosterone, which hinders ovulation. The follicles struggle to release eggs, so they hang out instead of going through the ovulation process. Women with PCOS lack solar redox because testosterone is reduced by sunlight.

Sunlight is stored in mammals at the electronic levels in cells. The light stored gives you the life you get. This is true for you and your perspective baby.

Light needs electrons to work = photoelectric effect.

When we lose electrons for any reason back ot our environment our biomolecules become oxidized and our cells liberate light as a result. This light is called ultraweak biophotons. The oxidation of biomolecules occurs by **_hydrogen abstraction_** by superoxide anion and hydroxyl radicals or by the cycloaddition of singlet oxygen initiate a cascade of oxidative reactions that lead to the formation of electronically excited species such as triplet excited carbonyl, excited pigments and singlet oxygen. The abstraction of hydrogen is defined by the **removal of a hydrogen atom or group from a molecule by a free radical**. Hydrogen/deuterium atom abstraction is often confused with deprotonation, which is the removal of a hydrogen atom (i.e., a proton) by a base in an acid-base (proton transfer) reaction.

When deuterium is abstracted out of our biomolecules by light, cells begin to need to use more UV light to complete this process. The light used in deuterium abstraction needs to be more powerful due to the kinetic isotope effect of the extra neutron in deuterium. As a result of this cost of mass, our cells need more VUV-UVC-UVB-UVA light to abstracted the heavier isotope of hydrogen because of the extra neutron. This means as we age, increase our heteroplasmy rate, or accumulate more deuterium in our tissues for any reason, you are losing the most powerful part of light in our cells and as a result things that rely on UV light become defective.

Generally, the way oocyte selection occurs in mammals is by hydrogen abstraction. The oocytes with the lowest atomic mass are ejected first in a woman's life. Her best egg is usually the one with the lowest deuterium content. The eggs with the most deuterium and exited last. This means the older a pregnancy becomes the more light is needed in the transgenerational process. In this way you can see now why high maternal age and transgenerational epigenetic diseases occur. It is also why more chromosomal abnormalities occur as mammals age. It begins to explain how childhood cancers actually occur when you understand the biophysics. It also explains why migration problems might happen in morphogenesis in the fetus. If the UV light is expended abstracting deuterium in eggs overloaded with deuterium there is less light left at the elctronic level to drive the cell cycle past the mitosis level. This is the key reason why many childhood cancers begine. It also explains why this is a huge problem in infertility, cancer, and in morphogenesis (autism).

One of the first steps in a fertility workup is to measure markers that indicate [how many eggs you have](https://www.whattoexpect.com/getting-pregnant/ovulation/ovarian-follicles). One of those markers is anti-mullerian hormone (AMH). What does AMH tell you about your fertility? AMH is a hormone released from eggs and the higher the number the more eggs you likely can make. The mistake most owmen are making today who delay childbirth is not to harvest and store their eggs earlier in life. In a tech world this decision should be made before one even has a relationship especially if you know your career is going to delay family. In an electromagnetic driven world this is even important to consider.

If you do get pregnant late in life or you have high heteroplasmy rate you might want to consider an Inhibin A test.
The inhibin A test is done to measure the amount of this [hormone](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/health-encyclopedia/he.sth149942#sth149942-sec) in a pregnant woman's blood to see if the baby may have chromosomal abnormalities like [Down syndrome](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/health-encyclopedia/he.std120885#std120885-sec). Inhibin A is made by the [placenta](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/health-encyclopedia/he.stp1633#stp1633-sec) during pregnancy.

The level of inhibin A in the blood is used in a maternal serum quadruple screening test. Generally done between 15 and 22 weeks, this test checks the levels of four substances in a pregnant woman's blood. The quad screen checks alpha-fetoprotein (AFP), human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG), a type of estrogen (unconjugated estriol, or uE3), and the hormone inhibin A. The levels of these substances—along with a woman's age and other factors—help the doctor estimate the chance that the baby may have certain problems or birth defects.

Inhibin can also be used in the fertility perimenopause menopause transition. In fact, in the early in the menopause transition, when cycle irregularity is first observed, the initial event is a decline in circulating inhibin B levels in the early follicular phase. In the late perimenopause, levels of estradiol and inhibin A also fall, inhibin B levels remain low and FSH is markedly elevated. Inhibin B is a peptide hormone that is produced primarily by growing antral follicles that are responsive to FSH. As the follicle continues to grow and become dominant, inhibin B secretion declines. As the growing pool of antral follicles decreases during aging, levels of inhibin B are diminished.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T00:32:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-follicles-fertility-inhibin-and-biophoton-creation-transgenerational-risks-to-offspring.29042/post-335885>

Inhibin B is a peptide hormone that is produced primarily by growing antral follicles that are responsive to FSH. As the follicle continues to grow and become dominant, inhibin B secretion declines. As the growing pool of antral follicles decreases during aging, levels of inhibin B are diminished. Studies in women have demonstrated that decreased inhibin B secretion is associated with elevated FSH levels and decreased oocyte quality and fertility potential. Thus, inhibin B has been used as a predictor of ovarian reserve. However, the late appearance of inhibin B in the menopausal transition has led to the suggestion that it may be a better indicator of ovarian activity than of ovarian reserve, due to its direct link with growing follicles.

In children who are born Inhibin B is used differently.

Inhibin B is a heterodimeric glycoprotein and similar to AMH, is a product of Sertoli and ovarian Granulosa cells under the stimulus of FSH. Both _in vitro_ and _in vivo_ studies have demonstrated the important role of inhibin in the negative feedback regulation of FSH \[59,60\].

In male infants, inhibin B concentrations are measurable in the postnatal period and rise during the first week of life. Unlike AMH which decreases with pubertal onset, inhibin B increases progressively as boys approach the onset of puberty, increasing during pubertal stages G1 to G3 and declining slightly thereafter. Less is known about the role of inhibin B in girls during childhood and puberty, however like their male counterparts they follow a similar pattern of secretion, albeit at significantly reduced concentrations. In contrast to AMH in females, inhibin B concentrations vary across the menstrual cycle, with higher levels found in the follicular phase, peaking after the FSH surge, followed by a decline during the luteal phase. Clinical studies have suggested inhibin B as a useful marker to help distinguish between CDGP and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (HH) in both males and females, where the absence of stimulating FSH in HH results in low inhibin B levels.

Constitutional delay of growth and puberty (CDGP) is classified as the most frequent cause of delayed puberty (DP).

# Thread: Mitochondria makes water that acts as a highly reflective mirror for visible light in cells.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T21:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335905>

By careful choice of the type and thickness of the dielectric layers in matter, one can design an optical coating with specified reflectivity at different wavelengths of light. Dielectric mirrors are also used to produce ultra-high reflectivity mirrors: values of 99.999% or better over a narrow range of wavelengths can be produced using special techniques. Alternatively, they can be made to reflect a broad spectrum of light, such as the entire visible range for water. That is what Nature did when she forced a bacteria and archea to merge and become a mitochondria. She did it to create a dielectric mirror for visible light to build complexity in eukaryotes.

All cells emit ultraweak UV biophotons via mitochondrial metabolism. So this means the light created from cells needs to be made useful in some way. Creating a dielectric mirror in mitchondrial water produced at cytochrome c oxidase is one of the things that does this.

Dielectric mirrors are very common in optics experiments in physics. What do these mirrors do? Examples of their applications include laser cavity end mirrors, hot and cold mirrors, thin-film beamsplitters, high damage threshold mirrors, and the coatings on modern mirrorshades and some binoculars roof prism systems.

Water is a dielectric material. ... The surface of water can also act like a mirror and change the path of light.

**CITES**

1.

2\. [https://www.sott.net/article/328936-How-electrons-affect-our-lives-Dr-Jack-Kruse](https://www.sott.net/article/328936-How-electrons-affect-our-lives-Dr-Jack-Kruse)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T21:53:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335906>

Why is a high reflective mirror needed in cells?

As a charge or an electric dipole in water approaches an interface with proteins or lipids, the electric field lines of the charge extend into the protein with less damping than in water. This means dielectric mirrors concentrate light's effect in proteins and lipids inside of cells. As the field energy in the protein rises, the charge is repelled from the protein-water interface as a consequence of the laws of physics.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T21:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335907>

When your redox potential drops you cells have more frustrated hydrogen bonds? What does that mean in terms of light and frequency?

As water molecules get near a large protein molecule in a cell they can't form all four of the hydrogen bonds that they normally form with other water molecules in cells. Each frustrated hydrogen bond costs an energy of about 1 kcal/mol or EH = 0.04336 eV per molecule. In other words, a loss of redox not only costs you energy, a loss of negative charge, and increases the numbers of frustrated hydrogen in cells and this decreases the ability of the cell to achieve quantum coherence in life.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T22:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335908>

Water has a low refractive index but a very high dielectric constant due to the different physical properties that these characteristics are associated with.

1.  Refractive Index:
    \- Refractive index is a measure of how much light is bent, or refracted, when it passes from one medium to another. In the case of water, it has a relatively low refractive index compared to many other substances. This means that light does not bend as much when passing through water as it does through some other materials.
    \- Water's low refractive index is due to the fact that its molecules are relatively simple and uniform in structure, which results in less bending of light as it passes through water.

2\. Dielectric Constant:
\- The dielectric constant, also known as relative permittivity, is a measure of a material's ability to store electrical energy in an electric field. Materials with high dielectric constants are good insulators and can store large amounts of electrical energy.
\- Water has a very high dielectric constant compared to many other substances. This is because water molecules have a significant electric dipole moment, which allows them to orient themselves in response to an electric field and store electrical energy effectively.
\- The high dielectric constant of water is important for many biological and chemical processes where water acts as a solvent, facilitating the dissolution and interaction of charged molecules.

In summary, the low refractive index of water is due to its simple molecular structure, which causes less bending of light, while its high dielectric constant is a result of its ability to store electrical energy efficiently because of the electric dipole moments present in water molecules.

**Toxoplasma gondii** is a common single-celled parasite that can infect various tissues in the body. Recently, researchers discovered that it can also infect the prostate. Here’s what we know:

1.  **Infection in Mice:**
    -   In experiments with mice, **Toxoplasma gondii disseminated to the prostate within two weeks after infection.**
    -   Once in the prostate, it remained in the form of latent tissue cysts for at least 60 days and could persist for the host’s lifetime.
    -   [The presence of these parasitic cysts led to **chronic inflammation** in the prostate](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate)[1](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate).
2.  **Human Implications:**
    -   If Toxoplasma gondii triggers chronic inflammation in the human prostate, it becomes significant due to the parasite’s prevalence in the human population.
    -   [Up to 22% of Americans are infected with Toxoplasma gondii, which is **transmitted through oocysts from infected cats or ingestion of raw/undercooked meat**](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate)**[1](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate).**
    -   Researchers found similarities between the pathology triggered by this parasite in mouse prostates and features observed in humans suffering from BPH.
    -   [BPH affects nearly half of men by age 50 and is associated with lower urinary tract symptoms](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate)[1](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate).

So if you are "young healthy 20 something" who works out in a blue lit gym, or a blue lit Harvard lab, you by definition, have a lowered redox potential because you DESTROYED POMC signaling via alpha-MSH. Dehydration of cells due to mitochondrial damage is the key initial starting point for ME. It can lead to a local loss in physiologic control over certain muscles too, just by a lack of red light from the skin above the muscles. The goal is not to avoid foods, molds, or toxins, with this issue, but to fix the structure of the tissues and gut by replacing the electrons lost to build the capacitor in cell water back. This turns your energy engine, the mitochondria, back on to protect your cells once again. Here is how I figured it all out.
