### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-30T20:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/post-357218>

Today's lesson on heat sinks and quenching radicals is linked directly to the GOE.

UV light on the skin photolyzes nitrite (NO₂⁻) and nitrate (NO₃⁻) in the epidermis into NO, a potent vasodilator. This increases venous oxygen saturation and lowers blood pressure by relaxing vascular smooth muscle. Research (e.g., Liu et al., 2014) confirms UV-induced NO boosts cutaneous blood flow by ~60% within minutes.

NO regulates stem cell proliferation and differentiation in niches (e.g., bone marrow, skin). Low NO levels impair stem cell function, while UV-induced NO enhance photorepair mechanisms of 380nm light via mTOR, linking to tissue health and immune modulation.

Corrin rings in vitamin B12 bind NO, quenching its bioactivity to limit diffusion and ensure localized effects (e.g., vasodilation, immune signaling). This binding modulates NO’s global impact, preventing excessive vasodilation or ATP depletion. Improving blood flow while stopping ATP production defines the action of a 'heat sink" This idea came directly from the GOE when oxygen was causing a HOLOCAUST on earth.

The GOE (~2.4 billion years ago) introduced oxygen, creating toxicity risks. NO’s role in reducing ATP production (by inhibiting mitochondrial respiration) evolved as a protective mechanism against oxygen overload, a legacy preserved in modern blood and tissue physiology. This aligns with my decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis that disrupted CCO (e.g., by nnEMF) mimics GOE stress, amplifying chronic disease running rampant on Earth today.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/post-357390>

Of course. That picture of mitochondrial nanotubes defines ER failure.

Tie the lessons together Mat of the last 20 blogs.........CAN YOU DO IT?

Start with this paper of Martin Picard on nanotubes. Then add my wisdom to what he has missed.

[https://www.cell.com/trends/cell-biology/pdf/S0962-8924(17)30146-0.pdf](https://www.cell.com/trends/cell-biology/pdf/S0962-8924\(17\)30146-0.pdf)

**Light/dark and Charge Control:** Have you ever asked yourself the of question how light and dark affecting nanotube formation. The manner in which it happens strongly suggests a photobioelectric response. A lack of daytime light (e.g., UV-A, red/NIR) or darkness at night disrupts circadian mitochondrial function, increasing ROS and charge leakage (e.g., proton gradient collapse). Nanotubes likely fuse mitochondria to increase surface area, acting as a larger capacitor to stabilize bioelectric charge. This is supported by Liu et al., who note nanotubes enhance membrane potential (ΔΨm) under stress. This is why GDF15 isused in mitoception.

**ER Failure and Lipid Loss:** in cell membranes loaded with DHA: Prolonged nanotube activity, driven by defective mitochondria (e.g., from blue light), overtax the ER, which synthesizes lipids (e.g., DHA) for mitochondrial membranes. Loss of melatonin (nighttime antioxidant made 95% in mitochondria), melanin (photoprotection), and DHA (membrane fluidity) impairs ER function, leading to protein misfolding and stress, linking to diseases like autism, neurodegeneration, and EDS (Perkins et al., 2017, Journal of Cell Science).

**Oxygen Toxicity and Capacitance:** Nanotubes reflect mitochondrial MRO2 use and are reflection of the cell acting to reduce oxygen toxicity by buffering ROS, building a larger capacitive network to control current. This aligns with the GOE lessons of evolution, early life adapted to rising oxygen via light-sensitive structures (e.g., aromatic rings in prokaryotes, but upgraded tomicrotubules in eukaryotes), a precursor to mitochondrial nanotubes. Light is driving this all, not electrical current. Electrical currents are down stream light and dark effects. Time to wake up Martin. Ask better questions.

Look at the photorepair slide in those blogs of the last series............I dare you to.

Biophotons are ultra-weak light emissions produced by living organisms, thought to arise from metabolic processes like oxidative reactions in mitochondria. Centralized scientists and AI bots hypothesized them to play a role in cellular communication, potentially influencing gene expression or signaling pathways. FIAF, also known as angiopoietin-like protein 4 (ANGPTL4), is a protein produced by tissues like fat and the gut, and it’s a key player in lipid metabolism and microbiome construction. Neither understand how UPEs control FIAF. If the UPE is coherent then FIAF inhibits lipoprotein lipase, affecting how fats are stored or broken down, and its expression ramps up during fasting. The microbiome, meanwhile, is the community of gut microbes that can shift in response to diet, fasting, or host metabolism, influencing energy balance and health. If the UPEs in mitochondria is not coherent, the UPE changes and mental illness becomes more probable. You cannot burn fat so it changes neural signaling.

Centralized scientists and technocrats who build AI systems will say no direct study says “biophotons control FIAF to affect the microbiome,” but we can hypothesize based on related mechanisms. This is patently false. Why?

FIAF has known absorption and emission spectra to light frequencies. Because of that, a study is superfluous because each chemical in the world has an optical fingerprint. Just because a scientist has not published the work is immaterial to this BASIC biophysical fact in spectroscopy. This was what I brought Ray Peat over ten years ago, and he was impotent enough to answer my critiques of his work. This podcast covers these details.

### Redox and resonance are tightly coupled in Nature.​

When we see waves coming toward us ashore we are actually seeing the energy of the sun powering wave action. Wind causes the see to pulsate so that a certain rhythm of waves occurs. This is exactly how the sun and Earth act to create the Schumann pulse as the solar wind crashes into the magnetosphere and is distributed to the ionosphere and Earth below in Aurora and lightning.
When living things are screened \[blocked\] from this geomagnetic field, they don't develop correctly. This tells us that redox and resonance are deeply coupled to morphogenesis in way we still are yet to understand. This diminishes the fidelity of the signal. This changes the waves that begin in our thalamus to create the life we get.

Today, not all life vibrates at exactly the Schumann resonance because man made waves oscillate at many different frequencies in all the zip codes on Earth modern man has created. Researcher now suggests that while early life was synced at around 7.83 Hz, the cellular activity in animals slowly drifted to other frequencies as the environment changed, with different frequencies being used for different types of activity in the brains of living things. For example, specific frequencies in human brain waves have been linked to specific mental states such as alertness, dreaming, and deep sleep. It is also linked to HRV. The Schumann resonance is closest to frequencies found in humans' deep relaxed state, suggesting primordial life could have been in a state similar to deep relaxation.
